# Thread: Do Cell Phones always emit EMF even with everything turned off??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-cell-phones-always-emit-emf-even-with-everything-turned-off.26272/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ossa — 2021-08-08T02:43:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-cell-phones-always-emit-emf-even-with-everything-turned-off.26272/post-302076>

I notice when I turn the data, wifi, bluetooth off, I can't detect any radio frequency coming off my cellphone. Does this mean turning everything off without airplane mode has the same effect without losing signal? Or does it only output a very small signal. Any advice appreciate thanks.

### Crimson Funk — 2021-09-06T07:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-cell-phones-always-emit-emf-even-with-everything-turned-off.26272/post-303362>

It is still connecting to towers so you can receive calls, so it's still emitting and receiving EMF. I believe that even in flight mode, the phone's battery would still create a field.
Happy to be corrected if I'm wrong, but my hierarchy of phone safety is:
1 - turn it off where possible
2 - flight mode

When in use:
1 - phone set to prioritise 2g/3g towers, use speaker mode with device preferably 2 feet from body / air tube earphones

Note, could also hardwire your phone to router (wifi off) and use Skype type software for calls

### Aussie Chris — 2021-09-06T09:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-cell-phones-always-emit-emf-even-with-everything-turned-off.26272/post-303364>

I agree. Even without the radio frequency signals, an “on” electric device will still emit small amounts of electric and magnetic fields. Reading Robert Becker suggests these are just as detrimental as RF. Best practice is distance or off.

Careful with any headphones. The magnetic part of the speaker will emit magnetic fields within mm of your brain (or in the case of airtubes, your torso/chest) . I had a set of DefenderShield headphones that had magnets to attach them together. I measured the magnetic field and it was significant (though I think they don’t use magnets anymore at the ear plug).

### Sean Waters — 2021-09-06T23:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-cell-phones-always-emit-emf-even-with-everything-turned-off.26272/post-303407>

> [Crimson Funk said:](/goto/post?id=303362)
>
> Note, could also hardwire your phone to router (wifi off) and use Skype type software for calls

Yeah this the best way - also remember to **Ground your router** that the ethernet cable is plugged into. This will pull a lot of the RF and Dirty Electricity away and out to the grounding plug/ copper pipe (whichever you choose). If not, those Ethernet cables and adapters can give you some nasty voltage to your hands.

I also agree with all your points, solid advice - the only phone I keep ON these days is an old Nokia phone that only has 1G capability (900 Mhz). But, if I get a message through to it, the RF is insanely high for a few moments. So, be aware of that too. I would never keep a smartphone ON, always airplane or WiFi if using while in transit or emergency.

# Thread: read the last paragraph...FATF=Financial Action Task Force
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/read-the-last-paragraph-fatf-financial-action-task-force.26287/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2021-08-11T19:52:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/read-the-last-paragraph-fatf-financial-action-task-force.26287/post-302208>

**[Blog](https://www.armstrongeconomics.com/blog)/Upcoming Events**
**Posted Aug 10, 2021 by Martin Armstrong**
Spread the love
[](https://www.armstrongeconomics.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/2018-Orlando-WEC-R.jpg)

Our 2021 World Econo0mic Conference that Nigel Farage called the Alternative to Davos, will be held **[November 5, 6, & 7, 2021 in Orlando](https://www.armstrongeconomics.com/conferences/). While Davos has been about trying to control** Tickets are available for the in-person event and virtual. Like last year, the virtual attendance will be limited since this will be a live stream worldwide so our clients can attend globally and summit questions. Those attending the physical and virtual will receive the materials as usual which will provide a look at the entire world – stocks, bonds, commodities & Currencies including Cryptos.

[](https://www.armstrongeconomics.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Reports.jpg)

Included in the materials this year will be the special reports on all the forecasts on population and the cycle of disease that comes into play in 2022, the Pawn of Financial & the Monetary Crisis Cycle, and the Money Crisis including the Cryptocurrencies & the Know Your Client Regulations to be imposed with the stealth rise of a new world regulator from the G20 outside of all national jurisdictions – Financial Action Task Force.

[](https://www.armstrongeconomics.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/FATF.jpg)

While the Crypto World is about to find out who FATF really is, the reality behind all of this is straightforward. This is the first truly international form of government that was NEVER elected and over which we have no right to appeal and they will argue that they have immunity from any lawsuit whatsoever. More significantly, where Fauci’s emails were obtainable under the Freedom of Information Act, that only applies to US agencies – it does not apply to quasi-governmental bodies that were created by the G20.

The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is the global money laundering and terrorist financing watchdog. The inter-governmental body sets international standards that aim to prevent these illegal activities and the harm they cause to society. As a policy-making body, the FATF works to generate the necessary political will to bring about national legislative and regulatory reforms in these areas.

FATF is not even under the United Nations. This grew out of the G20 and the definition of money laundering has been so loosely defined to include simply have a stash of cash you might have in a safe deposit box. They assume you did not pay taxes so you are **GUILTY UNTIL PROVEN INNOCENT** which is the foundation of French law – not English Common Law.

So welcome to the real **INTERNATIONAL REGULATOR** on money that even your local MP or Congressman do not understand and had no idea that they now sit on top of the world. Any country that does not comply with their demands is greylisted or black listed. So what happens is they can be removed from the SWIFT system which will deny them total access to the world financial markets to be able to buy or sell.

It was FATF that pushed the “money laundering” threshold from $10,000 to $3,000 and they even wanted to lower that to $250.

The implications of FATF upon the global markets and capital flows will only increase as we head into the years ahead. How will this impact the investment in the world ahead? It has been impacting gold but the cryptocurrencies are now in their targeting objective.

# Thread: Living in a Tipi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-a-tipi.26288/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kimberly P — 2021-08-11T20:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-a-tipi.26288/post-302211>

Hi friends, I’m currently living in a gazebo in the country. Sounds crazy, but it’s enclosed with electricity, like a beautiful little room without running water. I share this gazebo with my siblings and while most of them have never made use of it, I need to open it up so my family can come and visit when they want to without me and all of my things occupying it. Long-story short - I need to move out of the gazebo and I plan on purchasing a 26-ft tipi. There will be a traditional fire pit for heat. I have to decide if I want the tipi cover to be treated with fire retardent. Otherwise, it will be organic cotton. I’m very happy to be moving into a tipi, even closer to nature than I am now, but I can’t decide if I want those chemicals sprayed or not. One good thing is that there is an opening at the top of the tipi, of course, so there will be airflow. I need to put this order in soon, so I would welcome your thoughts. What would you do regarding the fire retardent? Use it? Or keep it organic? Thank you!

### Richard Watson — 2021-08-12T00:47:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-a-tipi.26288/post-302216>

The use of linseed Oil is option to treat the tipi cover, plenty of info online about how to go about it

### Kimberly P — 2021-08-12T21:45:47+0300

I share this gazebo with my siblings and while most of them have never made use of it, I need to open it up so my family can come and visit when they want to without me and all of my things occupying it. Long-story short - I need to move out of the gazebo and I plan on purchasing a 26-ft tipi. There will be a traditional fire pit for heat. I have to decide if I want the tipi cover to be treated with fire retardent. Otherwise, it will be organic cotton. I’m very happy to be moving into a tipi, even closer to nature than I am now, but I can’t decide if I want those chemicals sprayed or not. One good thing is that there is an opening at the top of the tipi, of course, so there will be airflow. I need to put this order in soon, so I would welcome your thoughts. What would you do regarding the fire retardent? Use it? Or keep it organic? Thank you!

I should note I’m usually extremely organic-minded. I don’t use commercial chemical cleaners in my home and I put mostly naturally-derived things on my skin. So I was very happy to find a tipi made with organic cotton - but I’m also weary of the risks considering I’m not the most experienced with open fires in my living space.

### Marko Pollo — 2021-08-13T01:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-in-a-tipi.26288/post-302279>

I think one angle to consider is how much wood are you going to burn during the winter. The people who lived in tipis couldn't have dry split wood delivered, didn't have chain saws, hand saws, axes, splitting mauls, tarps, ect. To heat a tipi constantly to modern standards would, I believe, make it very different than a traditional Native American home. A cob stove might work, but i dont think that a tipi usually had a roaring fire like we are accustumed to.

### Sean Waters — 2021-08-11T20:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iris-software.26289/post-302212>

Considering I'm using a PC everyday, with IRIS on 0 kelvin or "sleep" mode.... why is it that the screen still has blue in it?... **if you use IRIS at night, and you look at the laptop from the side, it is an Icey Blue glow!** Especially compared to my Red Light.

Maybe I don't understand how Screens work, but to me it seems like a lie because I still get symptoms of blue light toxicity when using it, such as Red eyes, twitching, irritability and low mood. I get **a lot less than normal,** but I would imagine 0 Kelvin = you cannot see a single thing on the screen, so there has to be blue light in it still, **so it is just a marketing gimmick for the app to say "0 kelvin"?** Does anyone have a spectrometer to test it? or has anyone tested it?

It makes a big difference between regular screen blue light, so I always use it, but I think we need to be aware of what the spectrum is so we can re-align our use of technology to our n=1. **It may not be as "okay" as we think it is, as a community, and we may be complacent in our use of tech and thinking we are doing everything possible while this issue undercuts our gains.**

### Dan2 — 2021-08-13T08:01:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iris-software.26289/post-302287>

"Yeah lowbluelights.com also sells this stuff [https://lowbluelights.com/product/imac-blue-light-filter-acrylic/](https://lowbluelights.com/product/imac-blue-light-filter-acrylic/) .... I think I am going to have to purchase some or some similar acrylic plastic."

That says $99. I don't know about a spectragraph of what gets through generic art supply store acrylic compared to that, but you might be able to get different shades of red and maybe a dark one would be as good as the expensive one. If I'm thinking of what you're talking about, it's a glow that doesn't get dimmer as the screen is dimmed, and it's a greyish-blue? Which means you can more easily see it when the screen is dimmed and there's only dim reddish light in the room. I have an 11W opaque red lightbulb on at night ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lightbulbs-and-kids.25020/#post-291383](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lightbulbs-and-kids.25020/#post-291383)) and if I dim the screen with Iris to say 15%, and I have just this 11W red bulb on several feet away, I can still see the glow through the acrylic a little, but it's dimmed by the acrylic and doesn't feel stimulatingly bright to my eyes even though it's color is still greyish-blue. Maybe that sounds like okay then the acrylic isn't doing enough. But a small candle flame puts out a lot more light between orange and blue than the monitor surface glow through the cheap medium-red acrylic does. I don't know what makes the monitor surface glow so I don't know if you might need a dark shade of red or more than one layer for some monitors.

The art store acrylic cost me about $3 for enough to cover a 17" monitor. I cut it with scissors and taped it on and keep it on. If you make a clip-on panel to put on and off the monitor, if you put the acrylic positioned on the panel so it's as close to the screen as it can get, it might get close enough for there to not be glares, blurriness etc. I haven't tried a badly made clip-on panel that has some space between.

\_\_\_\_\_

"And then no one here is talking about the flicker effect... you might want to try Ben Q monitors - they purport to take care of the flicker in hardware - flicker ages your eyes and therefore your brain at an exponential rate..."

If the monitor is set to 100% brightness on its hardware settings, and Iris is being used, is there still a flicker that's different from the refresh rate of the monitor?

The refresh rate can be adjusted on some monitors to frequencies that can be beneficial:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ot-a-disease-damn-it.25242/page-9#post-294530](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/aging-is-not-a-disease-damn-it.25242/page-9#post-294530)

### Daulatwant — 2021-08-13T21:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iris-software.26289/post-302314>

The backlight can be replaced with any light source you have access to that is bright enough. Eve the sun can light up an lcd display.

**Step 1 -** remove lcd backlight like so

**Step 2** \- make a wide black border around the display so it does not get blown out by too much brightness of daylight

**Step 3** \- point the back of the monitor at the sun and you have a monitor that is backlit by the sun

This will work even on cloudy days. Maybe even better so.

I bought this old monitor for 5$
It was quite easy to take apart, since it's old it was made to be taken apart so that is something to keep in mind. New monitors may be a challenge to do the same with.
Rotated the lcd upside down to expose the backlight due to the limited length of the cables attached to it. Used a drill, some scrap wood, screws and bolts to attach it in this new position and voila.

Or throw one of these in the back as backlight

If you are handy with soldering and electronics then you could take apart an led monitor, replace the white led inside with red led and make it look like a totally normal monitor on the outside.

### Daulatwant — 2021-08-14T09:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iris-software.26289/post-302342>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=302323)
>
> That's dope. But I have no confidence in doing any of that - I don't even have a monitor with me.
>
> Question - would you run IRIS on top of that? Would you be able to see anything?

**incandescent bulb**
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/_mg_9942-jpg.19033/)**

**here I used my phone as a red light just to show what happens**
**[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/_mg_9943-jpg.19032/)
Here is what happens when you use a red light, the blue or green pixels simply are not visible and appear as black.
Iris or other software then can be used to make it so that the green and blue is displayed as shades of red instead of plain black. Your native display drivers software can be sufficient to achieve a perfectly usable image.

The pixels in an lcd are filters for light, they can't turn red into blue or green.

### Aussie Chris — 2021-08-14T10:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iris-software.26289/post-302343>

Excerpt from _Deep Work_ by Cal Newport (highly recommended). p191 referring to technology
**
"The Craftsman Approach To Tool Selection**

Identify the core factors that determine success and happiness in your professional and personal life. _Adopt a tool only if its positive impacts on these factors substantially outweigh its negative impacts_ (emphasis mine).

Notice that the craftsman approach to tool selection stands in opposition to the "any-benefit" approach. Whereas the any-benefit mindset identifies _any_ potential positive impact as justification for using a tool, the craftsman variant requires that these positive impacts affect factors at the core of what's important to you and that they outweigh the negatives."

Whereas the any-benefit mindset identifies _any_ potential positive impact as justification for using a tool, the craftsman variant requires that these positive impacts affect factors at the core of what's important to you and that they outweigh the negatives."

### Dan2 — 2021-08-17T00:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iris-software.26289/post-302467>

[@Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/)
"and Desert.life - that does not surprise me! So, it's all one big pile of BS then.... Acrylic sheets being the way forward, or these Ben Q monitors that Penny suggested. But really, the point comes back to this - Technology is not safe, at all."

"all one big pile of BS then" I think Daniel Georgiev, the inventor of Iris, knows what he's doing with the software capabilities and monitor physics. Desert.life said, "I have iris on my pc, and it doesn't eliminate the flicker on my monitor. Took a phone and made a video and could still see the flickering" -- isn't that just the refresh rate? The refresh rate of the monitor and the frames per second of the camera's recording aren't equally timed and so the camera shows like an imposed polyrhythm, like a wave interference pattern, between them? And so that's not a flicker Iris caused, or can fix.

About the Ben Q monitors -- I don't know much about this but I think there's several brands that have models with eye health-related designs/features. Sometimes it requires a specific graphics card, like to adjust the refresh rate of some monitors, like in post #18 I made on the first page of this thread.

[@Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/)
"Does that run on a Pi?"
(replying to: "I got it to work on a desktop with Linux Mint. I have to use an older version -- version 0.9.2.2 -- and open the file called "Iris-0.9.2.2.AppImage" (included in the folder when it's downloaded) to start it.")

I don't know. Did you look at the Github forum where there's threads about troubleshooting? [https://github.com/iris-technologies/iris-user-issues/issues?q=is:issue+is:eek:pen+sort:created-desc](https://github.com/iris-technologies/iris-user-issues/issues?q=is:issue+is:eek:pen+sort:created-desc) I searched "raspberry pi", "pi", and "raspberry" there and no results. :\\

> [View attachment 19227](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/19227/) Hopefully this image shows up:
>
> Just got the screen filter from WAKN. Flicker free screen plus filter makes for a really nice experience.
>
> This is red which is pretty dark for daytime. Would probably get one in Amber (for daytime) and one in Red next time.
>
> Very positive experience working with Phillip from WAKN--personalized, friendly service. Attention to detail to get the custom measurements right to make sure it's a good fit.

If the blue and green colors appear as black. You may not see certain elements on the screen anymore.

That was an issue for me when trying to use a monitor while wearing red glasses.

If this is an issue for anyone, in graphics driver settings, adjust the green and blue channels to the lowest setting. Then adjust the saturation/vibrance to zero.

I was able to do this with intel and invidia drivers.

Using iris can achieve this also.

### SurthesAmy — 2022-12-08T15:58:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iris-software.26289/post-318243>

Iris can be installed on all versions of MS Windows as stated on the official website, as well as on android. But I had a problem with the installation, or to be more precise, with the correct operation with respect to startup time. Since I am a fan of different health tracking software, I decided to use Iris in conjunction with [https://www.exposit.com/solutions/healthcare/](https://www.exposit.com/solutions/healthcare/) app. There I look at the time to see the signal of visual impairment, and in my program already use the prompts to work comfortably for a long time at the laptop. I can't say they aren't compatible, but Iris often confuses the time when I need to take a break. In the near future I am trying to give it up, I want to check how convenient it will be to use one program for disabled people like me.

# Thread: great transcription - thank GOD - of Dr. Peter McCullough M.D....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-transcription-thank-god-of-dr-peter-mccullough-m-d.26292/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2021-08-12T20:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-transcription-thank-god-of-dr-peter-mccullough-m-d.26292/post-302249>

Peter McCullough M.D., transcribed incompletely by Dick Eastman

I'm, Dr. Peter McCullough and I'm an internist and cardiologist and academic physician, professor of medicine at Texas A & M College of Medicine on the Baylor-Dallas campus.

I think this whole pandemic from the beginning was about the vaccine. So I think all roads lead to the vaccine and what it means. There are already places in Southeast Asia and Europe \[where\] they are laying the groundwork for a compulsory vaccination. I mean compulsory. That means someone pins you down to the ground and puts a needle in you. That's how bad stake holders want vaccination. Listen there's not a cost. You don't have to pay for it. It's all provided. There are people or stake holders, they do want a needle in every arm. This needle in every arm is a very important moniker. Why? Why do you want a needle in every arm? Let's take Covid recovery: Can't get the virus, can't receive it it has nothing to do with -- why would they ever want a needle in the arm of a recovered patient? Why? Three studies show higher safety \[fail\] events. You see, the tension that Americans are feeling right now as they try to keep their jobs and go to work is they know that they can die of the vaccine. That's the problem. If the vaccine was like water and you just got it with no side-effects who wouldn't take it, say, I'll comply, they've got my social security number anyway and the data base, I'm already marked, I'll just get marked. But no, there's something unique about this vaccine. There something about injecting something into a body that is so important to stake holders that that doesn't matter. Kids 12 years old told they can make their own decision on this and it could be their fatal decision? Think about that. North Carolina passed that that the law, kids 12 years old can decide on their own. There's over 4,000 dead Americans, there's over 10,000 dead people in Europe that die on days 1, 2 and 3 after the vaccine. Why are we pushing this in a way where people's jobs and their education and their livilihood depend on a decision that is potentially fatal. The tension, you can cut it with a knife. There are parents who say, listen, I want my kid to go to college this year but I don't want to lose him to the vaccine. They know what is going on. The internet is full of these cases, blood clots, strokes, immediate death. Now I'm fortunate. I have not lost a patient directly to the vaccine. I've told you, most got vaccinated in December, January, February. Based on the safety data now I can no longer recommend it. I can't recommend it. It's passed all of the thresholds to being a safe product -- it's not a safe product. None of them are. It's not just Johnson & Johnson. In fact more of the safety events in the United States have occured with Moderna and Pfiser. There are now papers written by prominent scientists calling for a world-wide halt in the program. There are prominent virologists, many of them, including Nobel Prize winners, who have said, if we vaccinate people and we create a very narrow incomplete library of immunity, which is what the vaccine is -- the vaccines are all targeted to the original Wuhan spike protein which is long gone, that's extinct. Patients are getting vaccinated against something that doesn't even exist any more. That Wuhan spike protein is gone. We are hoping the immunity covers the other varients but that narrow immunity is a setup! It's just like giving everybody a narrow spectrum antibiotic. If you did that, what would happen. We would grow up super bugs. There are warnings out there saying Don't do this! Don't vaccinate the entire world -- all we're going to do is set ourselves up for a super bug thats going to really wipe out populations. So for many reasons indiscriminant vaccinations is a horrendous idea. It's a horrendous bioweapon that's been thrust unto the public and it's going to cause great personal harm which it already has. Thousands of people have lost their lives. I've never lost a direct patient but I've had my patients lose their family members, lots of them. I've filled out a safety report on a patient who developed blood clots after one of the Pfizer Moderna vaccines and I'm telling you, it took half an hour to do it; there was many pages and each page said "warning," "Federal offense punishable by severe fines and penalties" if I falsify the report -- all those thousands of Americans who have died with the vaccines, the hospitalizations in the data base I think are real. and they are far beyond anything we've ever seen and as a doctor and as a public citizen I am extraordinarily concerned about the vaccine. The vaccine center right down the street here is empty -- I drive past it every day. Americans know. They're talking to each other. The vaccine is not safe and now effort is the vaccine stake holders want kids without parental guidance and now they want to be in the church. Americans and people worldwide should be extraordinarily alarmed.

Q:Why has there been such a relentless focus on mass vaccination as the ONLY way back to normalcy?

McCullough: I think its about what the vaccine means. And Whitney Webb gets credit for this. Back in April she said, "ah ha, I've figured this out. This is what globalists have been waiting for. They've been waiting for a way of marking people. You get a vaccine, you are marked in a data base and this can be used for trade, for commerce, for behavior modification, all different purposes. And you see it right here in Dallas. They've announced you can't go to a Dallas Maverick's game unless you are vaccinated. You've had people say, Listen, you have to have passports. You have colleges announce today that they are not going to give any credit to natural immunity. Every scientist in the world knows that the natural immunity is way better than the vaccine immunity. If its about Covid, why don't we have Covid-recovered go to the Maverick's games? Why don't we have Covid recovered people free to go to college? Why do we have to have faulty vaccine immunity be the priority and have natural immunity not count?

You see these types of things make me think that Whitney Webb is correct. This is actually about marking. The vaccine is a way of marking people. It is a way of starting to assert efforts to create compliance and behavior control. Don't forget, the vaccine is just a starter. Now there's going to be updates. There's going to be boosters. They are already prepping people for this. There's going to be more. The vaccine manufacturers are linked. They are all indemnified. What medical product is there indemnification. If something happens to you you have no recourse. You know a woman gets vaccinated, a pregnant women, she has no maternal fetal rights. If something happens to her baby she is out of luck. This is extraordinary what Americans are doing, it is absolutely extraordinary what's being thrust upon us now.

Q: How is the development and deployment of COVID vaccines a departure from previous vaccine development and safety review?

McCullough: Well, U.S. FDA regulatory guidance in vaccines, and there have been modern vaccines, you don't have to pick the old ones. I mean we've had modern vaccines, Shingles vaccines, Hepatitis B vaccines, Meningococcal vaccines demand a minimum of two years of safety data, two years by regulatory \[word inaudible\], written and codified into the regulatory rules for the manufacturers. That was all thrown out. Two months. For Covid, two months. So two months of observational data. This idea that we could vaccinate people that were not even tested in the trial, that has never been done before. We have never just thrown a vaccine at somebody without having any data. None. So the very first pregnant woman that was vaccinated here in the United States, it was done with no knowledge of safety and no knowledge of efficacy. And the argument that we've heard is, "Well, Covid 19 is a bad illness, 600,000 people have died, the vaccine can help them, we should 'give it a shot," come one, we should just give it a shot, while that 600,000 died. I've already told you that 85% of that was preventable with early treatment which was actively suppressed and squashed. And not only that, is if this vaccine can help them, the vaccine better be safe, it better be safe." And my comments on the vaccine are, Safety. Safety. Safety. Let's see it. Let's see it. And Americans should have been getting weekly updates on vaccine safety. Very important. Weekly updates from our federal officials on safety. Super important. Those two things are probably the largest acts of malfeasance in all of medical regulatory history. It will go down in the history of malfeasance, wrongdoing by those ....

### John Schumacher — 2021-08-14T19:18:58+0300

### Penny — 2021-08-13T01:27:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-pfizer-emplyee-confirms-poison-graphene-oxide-in-the-jab-transcript-thank-god.26296/post-302277>

[https://principia-scientific.com/fo...ntl+(Principia+Scientific+Intl+-+Latest+News)](https://principia-scientific.com/former-pfizer-employee-confirms-poison-graphene-oxide-in-vaccine/?utm_source=feedburner&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=Feed%3A+psintl+%28Principia+Scientific+Intl+-+Latest+News%29)

### John Schumacher — 2021-08-13T06:00:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-pfizer-emplyee-confirms-poison-graphene-oxide-in-the-jab-transcript-thank-god.26296/post-302283>

PEGylated nano-graphene oxide is a nanocarrier for drug delivery - [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-59624-w](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-59624-w)
The drug delivery efficacy was enhanced through the introduction of dual-drug-loaded PEGylated nano-graphene oxide (pGO).

Advanced functionalized nanographene oxide as a biomedical agent for drug delivery - [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0014305720318395](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0014305720318395)

The field of nanoscience has blossomed over the last two decades and the importance of nanotechnology has increased in areas such as biomedicine.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Graphene nanostructures, as highly conductive materials, acting as substrates for Fe3+/Fe2+ ions.
The utility of GO can extended to gene delivery and biological sensing considering the quenching properties of fluorescence-labelled DNA.
Gene delivery using these materials has already been tested in a variety of cells, through different graphene-based materials functionalization.

# Thread: Fed Ct Orders FCC to reexamine 1996 safety limits
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fed-ct-orders-fcc-to-reexamine-1996-safety-limits.26303/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2021-08-15T14:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fed-ct-orders-fcc-to-reexamine-1996-safety-limits.26303/post-302390>

Today, the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit [ruled in](https://www.cadc.uscourts.gov/internet/opinions.nsf/FB976465BF00F8BD85258730004EFDF7/$file/20-1025-1910111.pdf) the historic case EHT et al. v. the FCC that the December 2019 decision by the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to retain its 1996 safety limits for human exposure to wireless radiation was “arbitrary and capricious.”

The court held that the FCC failed to respond to “record evidence that exposure to RF radiation at levels below the Commission’s current limits may cause negative health effects unrelated to cancer.” Further, the agency demonstrated “a complete failure to respond to comments concerning environmental harm caused by RF radiation.”

[https://ehtrust.org/in-historic-dec...NsFLM4btFkxqDp5stgKcct4AIs-rAhwZbt0ykFSK3LPp8](https://ehtrust.org/in-historic-decision-federal-court-finds-fcc-failed-to-explain-why-it-ignored-scientific-evidence-showing-harm-from-wireless-radiation/?fbclid=IwAR2Mh-dYRBhrraNsFLM4btFkxqDp5stgKcct4AIs-rAhwZbt0ykFSK3LPp8)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Nigella Sativa plus honey blows away HCQ in a Pakastani study...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nigella-sativa-plus-honey-blows-away-hcq-in-a-pakastani-study.26306/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2021-08-16T20:05:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nigella-sativa-plus-honey-blows-away-hcq-in-a-pakastani-study.26306/post-302452>

[https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.10.30.20217364v2](https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.10.30.20217364v2)

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8106451/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8106451/)

COVID-19 has caused a major global health crisis, as excessive inflammation, oxidation, and exaggerated immune response in some sufferers can lead to a condition known as cytokine storm, which may progress to acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDs), which can be fatal. So far, few effective drugs have emerged to assist in the treatment of patients with COVID-19, though some herbal medicine candidates may assist in the fight against COVID-19 deaths. Thymoquinone (TQ), the main active ingredient of black seed oil, possesses antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antiviral, antimicrobial, immunomodulatory and anticoagulant activities. TQ also increases the activity and number of cytokine suppressors, lymphocytes, natural killer cells, and macrophages, and it has demonstrated antiviral potential against a number of viruses, including murine cytomegalovirus, Epstein-Barr virus, hepatitis C virus, human immunodeficiency virus, and other coronaviruses. Recently, TQ has demonstrated notable antiviral activity against a SARSCoV-2 strain isolated from Egyptian patients and, interestingly, molecular docking studies have also shown that TQ could potentially inhibit COVID-19 development through binding to the receptor-binding domain on the spike and envelope proteins of SARS-CoV-2, which may hinder virus entry into the host cell and inhibit its ion channel and pore forming activity. Other studies have shown that TQ may have an inhibitory effect on SARS CoV2 proteases, which could diminish viral replication, and it has also demonstrated good antagonism to angiotensin-converting enzyme 2 receptors, allowing it to interfere with virus uptake into the host cell. Several studies have also noted its potential protective capability against numerous chronic diseases and conditions, including diabetes, hypertension, dyslipidemia, asthma, renal dysfunction and malignancy. TQ has recently been tested in clinical trials for the treatment of several different diseases, and this review thus aims to highlight the potential therapeutic effects of TQ in the context of the COVID-19 pandemic.

### DLO — 2021-08-18T03:42:46+0300

THURSDAY, August 19th 2021

8:00am PST | 11:00am EST | 4:00pm UK

This week Susan Sweetin, Founder & CEO of FTA, will be talking to Cambridge University educated, Solicitor David Adelman, aka [The UK People's Lawyer](https://www.freedomtravelalliance.com/so/47NjEGFvc/c?w=fevLbR5duo2xBzr8TCAh3YziBacS_MLLvvwWpg7e7go.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly90aGVwZW9wbGVzbGF3eWVydWsuY29tL2Fib3V0L2RhdmlkLWFkZWxtYW4vIiwiciI6IjkxMWFkOGE2LWJiNGQtNDc4Yi1iNWM0LWNkNjIwNTJkOGNiNiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjM4OTZjZjVhLTgwMDEtNDk1OS05ZDc0LTI0MmZiZWY0MDUyMyJ9).

He turned his back on conventional work and office incarceration as a young man, to explore life through experience. He has recently published a book: [School - No Place for Children](https://www.freedomtravelalliance.com/so/47NjEGFvc/c?w=U1gwQBBam_kc9e1W4rhd0hwlspaCzNkgYUB8I2qllT0.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvLnVrL2RwLzE5NzgzNzAwMzIvcmVmPWNtX3N3X3JfY3BfZXBfZHBfRHZGRkFiN1dYV0FDVCIsInIiOiI5MTFhZDhhNi1iYjRkLTQ3OGItYjVjNC1jZDYyMDUyZDhjYjYiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiIzODk2Y2Y1YS04MDAxLTQ5NTktOWQ3NC0yNDJmYmVmNDA1MjMifQ) which was inspired by his childhood experience in the UK educational system.

David provides guidance, training and talks on Universal Law and on protection from medical tyranny, law enforcement tyranny and so much more.
His aim is to upgrade people's general sense of their own power.

Covid cut David's nationwide tour on education short right after he gave the keynote speech at the Glastonbury Symposium in July 2019.

We are delighted he will be joining us.

[► Join the Live Chat](https://www.freedomtravelalliance.com/so/47NjEGFvc/c?w=v1yspmJr3h9UKp098-5ccO_BGKpAoPv2RtVD_AVmd08.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZnJlZWRvbXRyYXZlbGFsbGlhbmNlLmNvbS9mcmVlZG9tdGFsa3MiLCJyIjoiODBmMGQ2MTItYWU0Ny00MzEyLWFkZGMtYjMyMWI4YWJlZDU2IiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiMzg5NmNmNWEtODAwMS00OTU5LTlkNzQtMjQyZmJlZjQwNTIzIn0)

# Thread: Cannabidiol Inhibits SARS-CoV-2 Replication and Promotes the Host Innate Immune Response
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannabidiol-inhibits-sars-cov-2-replication-and-promotes-the-host-innate-immune-response.26325/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2021-08-20T01:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannabidiol-inhibits-sars-cov-2-replication-and-promotes-the-host-innate-immune-response.26325/post-302619>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7987002/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7987002/)

### Dan2 — 2021-08-20T11:43:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannabidiol-inhibits-sars-cov-2-replication-and-promotes-the-host-innate-immune-response.26325/post-302630>

I think the way to ingest weed to get the most variety of cannabinoids (maybe dab punks have figured out some new thing that's 130% THC, but) is 190 proof alcohol extract, "Rick Simpson Oil". With shake or trimmings (CBD strain?) and Everclear it's pretty easy to make lots of at home. Legal growers who grow outside in the sun with no pesticides usually sell at a low price point. And I think the best emulsifier to help mix the RSO into a drink or something is honey. RSO didn't emulsify for me into vodka or egg yolk, did a little bit into 151 proof or lecithin, and emulsifies easily into honey and then the honey can be mixed into milk or something and the RSO mixes into that smoothly too. And I used raw honey; maybe the enzymes or something in raw honey makes it emulsify RSO but pasteurized honey won't.

### JanSz — 2021-11-23T02:05:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannabidiol-inhibits-sars-cov-2-replication-and-promotes-the-host-innate-immune-response.26325/post-306549>

> [Dan2 said:](/goto/post?id=302630)
>
> I think the way to ingest weed to get the most variety of cannabinoids (maybe dab punks have figured out some new thing that's 130% THC, but) is 190 proof alcohol extract, "Rick Simpson Oil". With shake or trimmings (CBD strain?) and Everclear it's pretty easy to make lots of at home. Legal growers who grow outside in the sun with no pesticides usually sell at a low price point. And I think the best emulsifier to help mix the RSO into a drink or something is honey. RSO didn't emulsify for me into vodka or egg yolk, did a little bit into 151 proof or lecithin, and emulsifies easily into honey and then the honey can be mixed into milk or something and the RSO mixes into that smoothly too.

RSO=????

.

### Dan2 — 2021-11-23T02:08:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannabidiol-inhibits-sars-cov-2-replication-and-promotes-the-host-innate-immune-response.26325/post-306550>

Rick Simpson Oil. Other cannabinoids help support and complement the effects of CBD; isolated CBD isn't as effective as an extract that has other cannabinoids too. RSO can be made with a weed strain that has relatively little amounts of THC, and relatively a lot of CBD and other non-psychoactive cannabinoids, but the mixture of cannabinoids other than CBD, including some THC, helps the CBD effects. I don't know what the weed laws are in New Jersey and New York, but in states where THC strains are legal, RSO is available in stores.

### Dan2 — 2021-11-23T02:25:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannabidiol-inhibits-sars-cov-2-replication-and-promotes-the-host-innate-immune-response.26325/post-306552>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) If you try it, be careful about starting with a small dose. The first time I tried it (made with a strain that had lots of THC, so the RSO had about 80-90% THC in it, compared to most flower having about 15-25%), I hadn't smoked weed much recently, so didn't have much of a tolerance to THC, and I used about 1/10 of a gram of RSO, and slept for about 30 hours haha. Or maybe it was a lot of weed butter that I ate that time; I forget. But my point is, eating THC metabolizes into a different chemical that makes slower-onset and longer-lasting effects, and a lot of people say is a different psychoactive feeling than THC, and eating cannabinoids might be more medicinal some ways than smoking. RSO is usually eaten, but can also be smoked. Weed butter is also about the same medicinally as RSO and easier to make.

# Thread: How to know if fatigue is insulin resistance or adrenal fatigue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-know-if-fatigue-is-insulin-resistance-or-adrenal-fatigue.26329/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ossa — 2021-08-20T21:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-know-if-fatigue-is-insulin-resistance-or-adrenal-fatigue.26329/post-302646>

Do i have to test to figure this one out?

### Sean Waters — 2021-08-23T05:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-know-if-fatigue-is-insulin-resistance-or-adrenal-fatigue.26329/post-302768>

You could test, but you probably don't need to. 2 Qs:

-   Are you having a "crash" in energy post-carbohydrates or large protein meal?
-   Or are you having a crash whilst in ketosis, or after waking up?

Post meal would indicate it's IR. That would then be Blue light toxicy as the issue.

Ketosis, fasted, anytime Cortisol rises (stress) like exercise would be Adrenal fatigue typically. That would then be Blue light toxicyt as the issue.

dense blog, but really good for Adrenal fatigue:
[https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/?print=print](https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/?print=print)

# Thread: WEF your heartbeat is your new digital identity.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wef-your-heartbeat-is-your-new-digital-identity.26334/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2021-08-21T20:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wef-your-heartbeat-is-your-new-digital-identity.26334/post-302689>

**[https://www.infowars.com/posts/worl...nce-turning-your-heartbeat-into-a-digital-id/](https://www.infowars.com/posts/world-economic-forum-pushes-new-form-of-surveillance-turning-your-heartbeat-into-a-digital-id/)**
**World Economic Forum pushes new form of surveillance – turning your heartbeat into a digital ID**
**This [tech has already been developed by the Pentagon](https://www.technologyreview.com/2019/06/27/238884/the-pentagon-has-a-laser-that-can-identify-people-from-a-distanceby-their-heartbeat/), that has produced a device to identify what reports refer to as a person’s cardiac signature. This was done at the request of US Special Forces, who wanted to be able to pinpoint their enemies from as far as 650 feet away.

But in the future, as predicted by the WEF, the “enemy” will be everybody – not only security targets, but also, for instance, “opted-in shoppers as they enter a store, to personalize their visit.”

The device, an infrared laser, will also help with bypassing the problem of people being told to wear masks, but then many mass surveillance facial recognition systems ending up “stumped” by them.

Then there’s the “advantage” of these lasers’ long range use. Today it’s 650 feet way – but as Pentagon’s Combating Terrorism Technical Support Office’s Steward Remaly remarked in 2019, “I don’t want to say you could do it from space, but longer ranges should be possible.”
**

### JanSz — 2021-08-21T20:40:59+0300

> **World Economic Forum pushes new form of surveillance – turning your heartbeat into a digital ID**

Is it ok to use that when voting?

....

# Thread: Cell Phone - Mitometer reading - YIKES ! Mitochondria being fried?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-mitometer-reading-yikes-mitochondria-being-fried.26341/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jackie Nelson — 2021-08-23T07:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-mitometer-reading-yikes-mitochondria-being-fried.26341/post-302779>

I bought a small mitometer and just tested my new cell phone ... HOLY MOLY ... it is intermittently blasting the highest levels of RF frequencies ... into the red bands ... this can't be safe. Are there any phones that don't do this or is there some way to protect myself from this? Does airplane mode help? Any comments appreciated.

I bought my meter from a now defunct site, but it is the same as shown here ... might be better places to purchase ... don't know:
[https://mitometer.com/terms-conditions/](https://mitometer.com/terms-conditions/)

### DLO — 2021-08-26T15:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-mitometer-reading-yikes-mitochondria-being-fried.26341/post-302943>

Mitometer? LOL thats just jason bawden smiths rd10 rebranded. Use a landline if you want to avoid pulsing. Or you can do what mercola does have a selfie stick turn it on and leave it maximum distance from you while it downloads then go airplane mode and read everything on it but your still getting hit with magnetic when the screen is on.

### halvibe — 2021-09-05T22:20:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-mitometer-reading-yikes-mitochondria-being-fried.26341/post-303341>

When you make, or take a call, put it on speaker and place the phone at least3 ft away from you.

### Tarun — 2021-09-05T22:57:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-mitometer-reading-yikes-mitochondria-being-fried.26341/post-303342>

No real way to protect afaik, you can get some kind of ethernet cable to your phone so you don't have to use 4G.

I recommend trying the meter ouside too, and also to check people's airpods.

### Marko Pollo — 2021-09-11T17:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-mitometer-reading-yikes-mitochondria-being-fried.26341/post-303589>

Doesn't make sense to worry about cell towers if our biggest threat is in our own pockets. We can live without these toxic pieces of crap.

# Thread: EMFs - European Parliament with a new study report 22/7 - 2021?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-european-parliament-with-a-new-study-report-22-7-2021.26342/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Johan Lindøm — 2021-08-23T11:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-european-parliament-with-a-new-study-report-22-7-2021.26342/post-302783>

_**Current state of knowledge of 5G-related carcinogenic and reproductive/developmental hazards as they emerge from epidemiological studies and in vivo experimental studies**_

The following are noted:
1) Power:
FR1 (450 to 6000 MHz) (mobile masts 800-3600 MHz, wifi 2400 MHz): EMF is probably carcinogenic to humans, especially related to gliomas and acoustic neuromas;
FR2 (24 to 100 GHz): Sufficient studies have not been performed on the higher frequencies;
2) Reproductive developmental effects:
FR1 (450 to 6000 MHz): These frequencies clearly affect male fertility and possibly female fertility.
They may have potential adverse effects on the development of embryos, fetuses and newborns;
FR2 (24 to 100 GHz): Insufficient studies have been performed on the non-thermal effects of the higher frequencies. links in the comments field ...

[https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2021/690012/EPRS\_STU(2021)690012\_EN.pdf](https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2021/690012/EPRS_STU\(2021\)690012_EN.pdf)

### Pablo — 2021-08-23T17:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-european-parliament-with-a-new-study-report-22-7-2021.26342/post-302792>

> [Johan Lindstrøm said:](/goto/post?id=302783)
>
> _**Current state of knowledge of 5G-related carcinogenic and reproductive/developmental hazards as they emerge from epidemiological studies and in vivo experimental studies**_
>
> The following are noted:
> 1) Power:
> FR1 (450 to 6000 MHz) (mobile masts 800-3600 MHz, wifi 2400 MHz): EMF is probably carcinogenic to humans, especially related to gliomas and acoustic neuromas;
> FR2 (24 to 100 GHz): Sufficient studies have not been performed on the higher frequencies;
> 2) Reproductive developmental effects:
> FR1 (450 to 6000 MHz): These frequencies clearly affect male fertility and possibly female fertility.
> They may have potential adverse effects on the development of embryos, fetuses and newborns;
> FR2 (24 to 100 GHz): Insufficient studies have been performed on the non-thermal effects of the higher frequencies. links in the comments field ...
>
> [https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2021/690012/EPRS\_STU(2021)690012\_EN.pdf](https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2021/690012/EPRS_STU\(2021\)690012_EN.pdf)

This is... very good news, isn´t it???? I mean, in public and institutional awareness.

Until this document I believe the EU regarded nnEMF´s at current levels quite safe (only the Council of Europe, which is not part of the EU, had recommended caution and reducing legal emf limits in its resolution 1815/2011, but the EU was super permissive).

Time will tell... thanks!

### JanSz — 2021-08-28T23:01:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/critically-thinking-with-doc-p-and-t-aug-19-eggsellent-info-at-minute-35.26366/post-303037>

[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33278625/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33278625/)

doi: 10.1016/j.ijid.2020.11.191. Epub 2020 Dec 2.
**A five-day course of ivermectin for the treatment of COVID-19 may reduce the duration of illness**

Ivermectin, a US Food and Drug Administration-approved anti-parasitic agent, was found to inhibit severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) replication in vitro.
A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial was conducted to determine the rapidity of viral clearance and safety of ivermectin among adult SARS-CoV-2 patients.
The trial included 72 hospitalized patients in Dhaka, Bangladesh, who were assigned to one of three groups:
**oral ivermectin alone (12 mg once daily for 5 days),**
oral ivermectin in combination with doxycycline (12 mg ivermectin single dose and 200 mg doxycycline on day 1, followed by 100 mg every 12 h for the next 4 days),
and a placebo control group.

Clinical symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat were comparable among the three groups. Virological clearance was earlier in the 5-day ivermectin treatment arm when compared to the placebo group (9.7 days vs 12.7 days; p = 0.02), but this was not the case for the ivermectin + doxycycline arm (11.5 days; p = 0.27). There were no severe adverse drug events recorded in the study.

**A 5-day course of ivermectin was found to be safe and effective in treating adult patients with mild COVID-19.**
Larger trials will be needed to confirm these preliminary findings.

### JanSz — 2021-08-28T23:12:44+0300

[https://www.drugs.com/medical-answers/hydroxychloroquine-effective-covid-19-3536024/](https://www.drugs.com/medical-answers/hydroxychloroquine-effective-covid-19-3536024/)

**Hydroxychloroquine was given as 800 mg once,**
**followed by 600 mg in 6 to 8 hours,**
**then 600 mg daily for 4 additional days**.
Patients started treatment within 4 days after exposure, defined as being in close contact with a COVID-19 patient for more than 10 minutes without protection.

### Dan2 — 2021-08-29T05:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/critically-thinking-with-doc-p-and-t-aug-19-eggsellent-info-at-minute-35.26366/post-303048>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)

"12mg Ivermectin
I am interested in the dosage of Ivermectin pills
.......
While at it dosage of hydroxychloroquine 200mg

While at it dosage of quinine sulfate 200mg"

I think the 200 mg HCQ dosage recommendation is from Vladimir Zelenko's protocols:

[https://vladimirzelenkomd.com/prophylaxis-protocol/](https://vladimirzelenkomd.com/prophylaxis-protocol/)

For prophylaxis:
"...Hydroxychloroquine (HCQ) 200 mg 1 time a day for 5 days, then 1 time a week..."

[https://vladimirzelenkomd.com/treatment-protocol/](https://vladimirzelenkomd.com/treatment-protocol/)

For immediate treatment of SARS-CoV-2 infection:
"...Hydroxychloroquine (HCQ) 200 mg 2 times a day for 5-7 days and/or
**Ivermectin 0.4-0.5 mg/kg/day for 5-7 days.**
Either or both HCQ and IVM can be used, and if one only, the second agent may be added after about 2 days of treatment if obvious recovery has not yet been observed etc...."

So there's Zelenko's immediate treatment recommendations about dosages of both IVM and HCQ.

And with the prophylaxis HCQ dosages, I calculated (using an approximate half-life duration of HCQ in the blood) how much HCQ is being maintained in the blood from (quoting Zelenko's prophylaxis protocol) "200 mg 1 time a day for 5 days, then 1 time a week".

2nd link here:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-301291](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-301291)

(links to this: )

[https://raypeatforum.com/community/...-to-do-exactly-that.40134/page-24#post-669303](https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/transmissible-viral-vaccines-how-the-shots-are-affecting-those-who-havent-taken-it-they-may-be-designed-to-do-exactly-that.40134/page-24#post-669303)

The Zelenko prophylaxis protocol will keep about 900-1100 mg of HCQ in the blood day to day when you're on the weekly doses (if I'm not misunderstanding the pharmacokinetics).

And also in that post I estimated how much quinine is maintained in the blood from 500 mg every 12 hours (using 12 hours because that's an estimate of quinine's half-life, and makes the calculations easy). So using 200 mg every 12 hours instead of 500 (because you said quinine sulfate 200 mg), that'd be (amount of quinine maintained in the blood considering quinine half-life):
Day 2: 350 mg
Day 3: 387.5 mg
Day 4: 396.865 mg
and then it stays at about that.

So because of quinine's shorter half-life compared to HCQ (HCQ about 22 days, quinine about 10-18 hours), if you're using quinine sulfate instead of HCQ and you want to maintain about the same amount of quinine in the blood as is maintained by Zelenko's HCQ prophylaxis protocol, you'd want to dose about 500 mg every 12 hours instead of 200 mg.

I don't see a recommendation from Zelenko about IVM dosage for prophylaxis, but if you want to mix the immediate treatment and prophylaxis protocol recommendations, then from the immediate treatment protocol: "Ivermectin 0.4-0.5 mg/kg/day for 5-7 days"

And so for a 200 lb person...
200 lbs = 90 kg
90 kg x 0.45 mg = 40.5 mg IVM per day for 5-7 days

And then what's IVM half-life, pharmacokinetics...

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2751445/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2751445/)

"Absorption
In healthy subjects that received **12 mg of ivermectin as oral solution, tablets or capsules, it was shown that the solution had approximately twice the systemic availability as either of the solid forms (tablets and capsules showed similar systemic availability**; Table 1). The oral solution was given as an ethanolic solution. This may affect bioavailability and could explain why the solution resulted in a twice as high availability as tablets and capsules. Nevertheless, **the rate of absorption was similar in the three cases** (11).

...

**Data concerning excretion can be observed on Table 1**. No differences in the elimination half-life were detected among healthy and onchocerciasis subjects (12). It was also suggested that the **kinetics of ivermectin (its elimination half-life is around a day) were somewhat disconnected from its pharmacodynamics (antiparasitic events persisting for several months after a single dose of the drug**) (11)."

I don't know how to interpet the units of Table 1 of that paper. So...

[https://www.drugs.com/pro/ivermectin-tablets.html](https://www.drugs.com/pro/ivermectin-tablets.html)

"...The plasma **half-life of ivermectin in man is approximately 18 hours** following oral administration."

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3043740/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3043740/)

"The **half-life of ivermectin in humans is 12–36 hours**, while metabolites may persist for up to three days."

So if we say 24 hours IVM half-life;
and the Zelenko immediate treatment recommendation for a 200 lb person would be 40.5 mg IVM per day;
and you're using 12 mg tablets, so let's say you take 3 tablets per day...
How many milligrams is being maintained in the blood during a week...

Day 1...
take 3 tablets = 36 mg

Day 2...
Day 1's dosage has become 18 mg
take 3 more tablets, new 36 mg
total = 54 mg

Day 3...
Day 1's = 9 mg
Day 2's = 18 mg
take 3 more
total = 63 mg

Day 4...
Day 1's = 4.5
Day 2's = 9
Day 3's = 18
take 3 more
total = 67.5 mg

Day 5...
2.25 + 4.5 + 9 + 18 + 36
total = 69.75 mg

Day 6...
add 1.125 to 69.75
total = 71 ish

I don't know if Zelenko's immediate treatment IVM dosages should be maintained longer than a week. But if they are then you'd be keeping about 70 mg IVM in the blood.

And so if you want to combine Zelenko's immediate treatment IVM dosage with the prophylaxis HCQ dosage (200 mg 1 time a day for 5 days, then 1 time a week), you'd have about 70 mg IVM and 900-1100 mg HCQ being maintained in the blood.

(HCQ 900-1100 mg: [https://raypeatforum.com/community/...-to-do-exactly-that.40134/page-24#post-669303](https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/transmissible-viral-vaccines-how-the-shots-are-affecting-those-who-havent-taken-it-they-may-be-designed-to-do-exactly-that.40134/page-24#post-669303) )

And about this (that you posted, post # 5)...
"[https://www.drugs.com/medical-answers/hydroxychloroquine-effective-covid-19-3536024/](https://www.drugs.com/medical-answers/hydroxychloroquine-effective-covid-19-3536024/)

Hydroxychloroquine was given as 800 mg once,
followed by 600 mg in 6 to 8 hours,
then 600 mg daily for 4 additional days"

HCQ half-life is about 22 days, so those dosages will build up in the blood to be about 2000 mg that then slowly decreases (compared to Zelenko's prophylaxis HCQ dosages maintaining about 900-1100 mg).

[https://raypeatforum.com/community/...powers-wont-allow-it.40014/page-2#post-669118](https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/we-have-a-covid-lifeline-the-powers-wont-allow-it.40014/page-2#post-669118)

The ivermectin paste I bought doesn't have ingredients listed on the box or on the information insert, and I think it used to; I'm pretty sure when I bought it a couple months ago there were ingredients on the box. Now at the store, taped to the shelf, there's a notice from supposedly the horse dewormer manufacturer saying there's been talk on the internet about using it for humans but it's not for that and so don't do that. So I don't know if the CIA or CDC or whatever talked to the people who make ivermectin horse paste and told them to stop telling people the ingredients? And if they wrote that notice? I saw something online a few months ago, a screenshot from Twitter, I think, about the mRNA vaccine product info insert being blank. I don't know if that was disinfo but if not then maybe something's being put in the horse dewormer (a horse anti-psychotic?), or maybe it's a joke of making a paranoid person like me more paranoid about that.

### Katie Durham — 2021-08-30T15:10:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/critically-thinking-with-doc-p-and-t-aug-19-eggsellent-info-at-minute-35.26366/post-303092>

The protocols have changed over time. Delta is much more infectious and aggressive. 12 mg daily for five days is NOT the recommendation any more. The best protocol I have seen is I-MASK+ at flccc dot net. Should be two or three times that dosage for as long as it takes, accompanied by a number of other supplements or drugs. Hard to OD on ivermectin, but taking too little isn't going to help.

The veterinary product must say only ivermectin and not include any other active ingredients like praziquantel. Still available for a reasonable price online at valleyvet dot com and chewy dot com. I would trust veterinary horse paste far sooner than Asian pills for humans (although I do have a stash of those too). Horses are expensive.

# Thread: Resources to protect son from WiFi at college dorm room
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-to-protect-son-from-wifi-at-college-dorm-room.26372/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jacks — 2021-08-30T01:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-to-protect-son-from-wifi-at-college-dorm-room.26372/post-303078>

Hi - my son just moved into college dorm and we were dismayed to discover the head of his bed is about 3 feet from a WiFi emitting device which is on the other side of the wall. It is a 50's era building ? First off he decided to move his head to the other end of his bed to increase distance from his head to the device. Just wanted some ideas on any additional tips of things to do to help. This is where we are at and some ideas:

1) we wrote to college maintenance folks to ask them to move the device to the bathroom area but Crickets so far
2) You can buy emf blankets - do these work and are they worth it as quite pricey?
3) He could hang such blanket in 2) on the wall behind his bed for protection
4) Cookie sheet against the wall near where the device is on other side of the wall.
5) Ask to move to another dorm.

Thanks

### Dan2 — 2021-08-30T04:51:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-to-protect-son-from-wifi-at-college-dorm-room.26372/post-303083>

Ask these people for a specific recommendation depending on what frequencies the wifi device puts out (the mesh size affects what frequencies can get through it):

[htthttps://www.swiss-shield-partner.com](https://www.swiss-shield-partner.com/america/)

Or just cover the wall with solid copper foil because solid copper foil will guaranteed block wifi router signals (but might be more expensive than a shielding mesh):

[https://basiccopper.com/1milcopperfoil.html](https://basiccopper.com/1milcopperfoil.html)

To do it cheap, a couple layers of aluminum foil will also block wifi-range frequencies, but also might bring up associations for other students of tin foil hats and crack dens whereas copper foil could be pretty fancy lookin'. Or just use aluminum and cover it with a giant portrait of himself to let the other kids know who's boss.

And the foil will reflect a range of frequencies (not sure which) coming from the side of the wall he's on, so still not a good idea to sleep with his head right beside that. Maybe a specific mesh size would minimize the (common telecom device range) frequencies that reflect back on his side of the wall, by trapping the signals in the mesh and the metal in the mesh then gradually converts the signals to heat instead of the signals bouncing back out? (And then as long as the wifi router power isn't increased too much the wall probably won't catch on fire... probably. Just kidding, it wouldn't get that hot.) Ask [https://www.swiss-shield-partner.com](https://www.swiss-shield-partner.com)

More options than what Swiss Shield makes: [https://www.lessemf.com/fabric5.html](https://www.lessemf.com/fabric5.html)

### Jacks — 2021-08-30T19:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resources-to-protect-son-from-wifi-at-college-dorm-room.26372/post-303105>

> [Dan2 said:](/goto/post?id=303083)
>
> Ask these people for a specific recommendation depending on what frequencies the wifi device puts out (the mesh size affects what frequencies can get through it):
>
> [htthttps://www.swiss-shield-partner.com](https://www.swiss-shield-partner.com/america/)
>
> Or just cover the wall with solid copper foil because solid copper foil will guaranteed block wifi router signals (but might be more expensive than a shielding mesh):
>
> [https://basiccopper.com/1milcopperfoil.html](https://basiccopper.com/1milcopperfoil.html)
>
> To do it cheap, a couple layers of aluminum foil will also block wifi-range frequencies, but also might bring up associations for other students of tin foil hats and crack dens whereas copper foil could be pretty fancy lookin'. Or just use aluminum and cover it with a giant portrait of himself to let the other kids know who's boss.
>
> And the foil will reflect a range of frequencies (not sure which) coming from the side of the wall he's on, so still not a good idea to sleep with his head right beside that. Maybe a specific mesh size would minimize the (common telecom device range) frequencies that reflect back on his side of the wall, by trapping the signals in the mesh and the metal in the mesh then gradually converts the signals to heat instead of the signals bouncing back out? (And then as long as the wifi router power isn't increased too much the wall probably won't catch on fire... probably. Just kidding, it wouldn't get that hot.) Ask [https://www.swiss-shield-partner.com](https://www.swiss-shield-partner.com)

### John Warner — 2021-08-31T02:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keratoma-in-horses-foot.26375/post-303119>

Hello don’t know if this is the right place for this, or if anyone has any info on horses. Have a horse with a keratome in left front, wondering if anyone has used red light therapy. Thinking it would penetrate the hoof wall, and if anyone has tried this before?

### Martha Ray — 2021-12-05T23:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keratoma-in-horses-foot.26375/post-307120>

I don't know if _red light therapy_ would help or not. Sorry no one noticed your question with a reply on that.
....I was looking for human foot health (zero drop grounding shoes) when I found your post.

Keep asking about the _red light therapy_....maybe _CT therapy_ too and you might also look into _magnetic therapy_.

I've had horses before and I know when someone uses the saying, '_**Healthy as a Horse**_' they don't know much about what can go wrong with them. Horse owners go to great lengths to keep them healthy, athletic and beautiful ...like humans they are complex and all the weight they carry only adds to the possibility of numerous muscular skeletal issues.

I do believe the Churchill saying,
**“There is something about the outside of a horse that is good for the inside of a man.”**

**_Hope you already found some help._**

# Thread: Sleeping in a tent
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-a-tent.26376/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mdavid — 2021-08-31T18:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-a-tent.26376/post-303145>

Mind was wandering the other day, was thinking of purchasing a tent and sleeping in it one or two nights per week esp with fall coming and those crisp cool nights. The issues I have thought of are the chemicals in the tents, both plastics and fire retardants, as well as the possibility that the "floor" of the tent might prevent earthing at least to some degree. I am willing to try to sleep on the ground with no pad etc, just a sheet on the "floor" of the tent. I would prefer a completely enclosed tent with a floor as I don't necessarily want bugs from the ground crawling all over me or mosquitoes.

Before I spend time digging into this I figured I would post here and see if anyone has gone through this and come out with a good solution.

### Daulatwant — 2021-08-31T19:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-a-tent.26376/post-303152>

> [mdavid said:](/goto/post?id=303145)
>
> Mind was wandering the other day, was thinking of purchasing a tent and sleeping in it one or two nights per week esp with fall coming and those crisp cool nights. The issues I have thought of are the chemicals in the tents, both plastics and fire retardants, as well as the possibility that the "floor" of the tent might prevent earthing at least to some degree. I am willing to try to sleep on the ground with no pad etc, just a sheet on the "floor" of the tent. I would prefer a completely enclosed tent with a floor as I don't necessarily want bugs from the ground crawling all over me or mosquitoes.
>
> Before I spend time digging into this I figured I would post here and see if anyone has gone through this and come out with a good solution.

Here is a rough example of what I would like to try.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/dugout-shelter-png.19240/)

Dig out a place, make raised edges and cover over the raised edges with a tarp. So no rain can get in and gets redirected somewhere else.

The deeper it is likely, the warmer it will be during cold months since deeper ground maintains ~54F year round.

It is important to pick an elevated area to make shelter to begin with so you keep away rain, dew and moisture.

Fog will flow down into lower areas and can make you wet even when there is no rain.

Vegetation transpires water and if you put your tent or other shelter on top of vegetation like grass it will create a wet warm area where bugs will gather.

### Dan2 — 2021-08-31T23:36:49+0300

### mdavid — 2021-09-02T17:49:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-a-tent.26376/post-303200>

Thank you both for your suggestions, I had not thought of a framed wall tent, I will check out some more of the videos, at least for now I would like to find something small for 1-2 people made of canvas with some ventilation netting, something I can easily assemble and disassemble in a few mins by myself.

My father lives out in the country so I will likely use the tent there, he will probably think I am nuts and wont want anything permanent on his property, my house is just outside of town but I am on a fairly main road with not too much property so I don't love the idea of. I also have a condo but they would likely frown upon a tent outside, however there is a sandy island that is a quick ride on the boat from there that I may also set the tent up on, I believe it is not allowed but if I get there late at night with a small tent and pack up early AM I can't see anyone hassling me.

# Thread: Thoughts on the biocharger - PEMF and energy device
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-biocharger-pemf-and-energy-device.26380/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101

### mdavid — 2021-09-02T17:55:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-biocharger-pemf-and-energy-device.26380/post-303201>

I had posted this in the ask Jack forum the other week not realizing there was a biohacking forum, since I didn't get any responses there I figured I would give it a shot here:

This past weekend I was able to test out a biocharger which they call a "subtle energy revitalization platform" It uses principals from Tesla and Rife in its design. I felt incredible afterwards, well balanced, great energy, and it seemed to have knocked the small lingering effects of my corona from 3 weeks ago. Interested in your thoughts on the device if you are familiar with it or if anyone else has used one.

My main concerns with this is it could be emitting negative EMFs along with possibly some good frequencies. Am also concerned about mismatching these frequencies at the wrong times in my circadian rhythm potentially throwing something else out of alignment.

These are promoted by Tony Robbins, Ben Greenfield, Luke Storey, and Dave Aspery.

"The BioCharger NG utilizes four different energy types – Light, Voltage, Frequencies & Harmonics, and Pulsed Electro-Magnetic Fields (PEMFs). " [https://biocharger.com/science-behind-biocharger/](https://biocharger.com/science-behind-biocharger/)

### John Schumacher — 2021-09-02T19:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-biocharger-pemf-and-energy-device.26380/post-303207>

From the testimonials, it will definitely give you a charge; something similar to exposing yourself to a highly "static" air atmospheric ion charge.

For example: going for a hike in the Flatirons of Boulder Colorado for the day during 60 mile an hour winds. The air is very dry, the air atmospheric pressure is low, the ion charge is very positively charged. Your skin (derma) has the capacity to take in this charge differential.

However, my understanding of the BioCharger is that it is producing a negatively charged ion field, radiating at frequencies that Rife found to be "healing".

Since the price point for the product is a bit high for home use, practitioners are incorporating it into their clinic. ex:

[https://www.naturalhealinghawaii.com/biocharger-ng/](https://www.naturalhealinghawaii.com/biocharger-ng/)
[https://www.transformationalhealinguniverse.com/services/biocharger](https://www.transformationalhealinguniverse.com/services/biocharger)
[https://omninaples.com/services/biocharger-therapy/](https://omninaples.com/services/biocharger-therapy/)
[https://wellnesssolutionspt.com/wellness-services/biocharger/](https://wellnesssolutionspt.com/wellness-services/biocharger/)
[https://www.drbobseiler.com/biochargerng/](https://www.drbobseiler.com/biochargerng/)
[https://www.thesanctuarycapecod.com/biocharger.html](https://www.thesanctuarycapecod.com/biocharger.html)​

Can elector-dynamic frequencies be used therapeutically? -> Clinical studies need to be done.

[https://sci-hub.se/10.1101/cshperspect.a034140](https://sci-hub.se/10.1101/cshperspect.a034140)​
