**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sue-UK — 2021-11-06T11:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-double-life-of-atp.26597/post-305817>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2877495/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2877495/)
The double life of ATP

Interesting, particularly in relation to the senses, and possibly the heart and vax/clot risks?

### JanSz — 2021-11-06T16:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-double-life-of-atp.26597/post-305821>

Hi [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)
Bladder, capacity, and tendency to urge.

In my younger days, my bladder capacity was on the large side.
That was highly helpful in the job I had. (Lots of dressing and undressing between bathroom trips).
Eventually, I had BPH benign prostatic hyperplasia. That made peeing highly difficult, so I decided to have Green Laser Therapy, to drill a bigger hole.
Next, about three years was hell, I had urges to pee sometimes every 15 minutes.
Now, the situation is improved but I never know when the urge comes, luckily it is rare. Mostly my bladder behaves in normal ways.
But my bladder hardly ever holds more than 200cc.

Reading this article, and possibly from other sources, **are you getting some ideas on-howto improve bladder behavior, capacity, and tendency to urge?**

How urge to pee is created?
The normal way would be the presence of a high volume of urine.
But my current urges are sometimes out of nowhere. In the bathroom, I may produce a rather small volume of urine.

### Sue-UK — 2021-11-07T12:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-double-life-of-atp.26597/post-305862>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=305821)
>
> Hi [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)
>
> Reading this article, and possibly from other sources, **are you getting some ideas on-howto improve bladder behavior, capacity, and tendency to urge?**
>
> How urge to pee is created?
> The normal way would be the presence of a high volume of urine.
> But my current urges are sometimes out of nowhere. In the bathroom, I may produce a rather small volume of urine.

I don't think its the presence of the high volume of urine on its own, the brain would need to get a signal that the bladder is full - a sense-ation. So the senses section of the article might be relevant, although its easier to relate to something like taste or hearing. So its possibly a loss of top down control from the brain, or a hyperactive signal to the brain from the bladder that causes the brain to issue the signal to pee too early, or a combination of both, or intermittently moving along a continuum between both states dependent on the (ATP?) context in the now.

It can be a signal to do something, or a signal not to do something. If a signal is overridden for long enough, there might be some sort of chronic downregulation, because resources wouldn't be put into something that is never used. Or downregulated to stop the system being fried ... (Sort of use it or lose it but don't wear it out ....) In a healthy state, the brain can override the need to pee for a time, which acutely might have survival value, but done chronically would be a different matter. Then there's the effects on ATP production or ATP signalling if the desire to avoid peeing because of say someone's job leads them to drinking insufficient water.

Whatever your n=1 context at the time, something led to you needing surgery. I'd not discount the effects of trauma from the procedure, because there's expectation and how that affects the brain signalling circuits. For example in phantom limb pain, there is a signal of pain from a limb that is no longer there. You not only had the surgery, but 3 years of hell after it, peeing sometimes every 15 minutes. "I am always aware of the presence of bathrooms nearby".

Musings

### JanSz — 2021-11-07T14:35:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-double-life-of-atp.26597/post-305863>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=305862)
>
> I don't think its the presence of the high volume of urine on its own, the brain would need to get a signal that the bladder is full - a sense-ation. So the senses section of the article might be relevant, although its easier to relate to something like taste or hearing. So its possibly a loss of top down control from the brain, or a hyperactive signal to the brain from the bladder that causes the brain to issue the signal to pee too early, or a combination of both, or intermittently moving along a continuum between both states dependent on the (ATP?) context in the now.
>
> It can be a signal to do something, or a signal not to do something. If a signal is overridden for long enough, there might be some sort of chronic downregulation, because resources wouldn't be put into something that is never used. Or downregulated to stop the system being fried ... (Sort of use it or lose it but don't wear it out ....) In a healthy state, the brain can override the need to pee for a time, which acutely might have survival value, but done chronically would be a different matter. Then there's the effects on ATP production or ATP signalling if the desire to avoid peeing because of say someone's job leads them to drinking insufficient water.
>
> Whatever your n=1 context at the time, something led to you needing surgery. I'd not discount the effects of trauma from the procedure, because there's expectation and how that affects the brain signalling circuits. For example in phantom limb pain, there is a signal of pain from a limb that is no longer there. You not only had the surgery, but 3 years of hell after it, peeing sometimes every 15 minutes. "I am always aware of the presence of bathrooms nearby".
>
> Musings

### JanSz — 2021-11-07T14:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-double-life-of-atp.26597/post-305864>

**[https://health.clevelandclinic.org/5-ways-to-deal-with-phantom-limb-pain-after-amputation/](https://health.clevelandclinic.org/5-ways-to-deal-with-phantom-limb-pain-after-amputation/)**

**Five techniques for managing post-amputation pain**
The success of treatment for post-amputation pain depends on your level of pain and the various mechanisms playing a role in causing the pain. While your surgeon may suggest beginning small exercises or managing your stress levels, there are five treatment techniques that go beyond the basics.

Here are five of the most effective treatment techniques:

1.  **Mirror box therapy**: The patient actually watches in a mirror while receiving physical therapy to re-map the brain’s neural pathways to register that the limb is no longer there. They feel the [imaginary movement](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3468806/) of the removed limb behaving as the normal movement through a mirror. This type of physical therapy with proven efficacy for patients with post-amputation pain.
2.  **Local injection therapy**: The physician injects a local pain-blocking agent at the amputation site. This can calm the painful signals sent by the nerve endings to the brain.
3.  **Non-opiate analgesic:** These prescription pain medications slow or limit how the painful nerves send signals to the brain.
4.  **Deep brain stimulation**: In this technique, a surgeon places tiny electrodes directly on the surface of the brain to help attenuate pain with electrical impulses.
5.  **Neurostimulation**: This technique involves placing an electrical device near the nerves traveling to the amputated extremity. Rather than blocking the nerve signals, it sends a pleasant signal through the nerve. Patients can control the neurostimulation unit via a small wireless remote control.

Is your bladder hyperactive or is it that the mind perceives it as hyperactive, reinforced by the behaviour changing hypervigilance of "I am always aware of the presence of bathrooms nearby".

"They feel the [imaginary movement](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3468806/) of the removed limb behaving as the normal movement through a mirror."

Despite the removal of a limb, this seems to be using (out) sight, to imagine (image in) movement and (signalling) behaving as normal. A visualisation technique that alters brain chemistry and signalling.

If its a brain perception issue, because you can't physically see your bladder, you could adapt the visualisation technique and use in-sight to image-in the bladder behaving normally. Over time with regular practice it might increase the volume you can hold or reduce hypervigilance.

> b. add to that energy produced by disassociation of water molecule by melanin (that represents 90%of total energy). So the energy in point a. represents 10%

Where my thought experiment is taking me ...

For simplicity, assuming 7400 liters at rest divided by 24 hours = 308 liters per hour divided by 60 minutes is just over 5 liters per hour, then 0.085L per second.

With the melanin hypothesis, a proportion of that 0.085L would be almost instantly unmade (to provide a source of hydrogen and oxygen) then what's left reformed back into water, and on and on .... The melanin could be stimulated not only by photons from the sun when its visible, or stored light from when it was visible, but also the invisible frequencies available 24/7 at any latitude, and also by the heat and light emitted by fluorophore proteins and the mitochondria themselves.

But theoretically with more mitochondria, or more efficient mitochondria, and maintaining good melanin levels, whilst consuming foods to maintain mass, we could make more ATP. In terms of energy, the ratio might still be 90% to 10%, but that extra ATP could take us beyond "at rest" in terms of signalling, for example a small spike of dopamine with ATP might encourage movement towards increasing whatever stimulated the initial dopamine. An animal might get a small hit of dopamine picking up a scent of prey, (ATP needed for the sense of smell) but enough ATP to signal movement could be the difference between starvation (and tanking dopamine) and lunch, with the full dopamine reward, whilst also ingesting food to maintain mass. ....

Before orgasm, levels of _vasopressin_ rise in men; after orgasm levels of oxytocin rise in women. These hormones are known to cause attachment, and probably contribute to the feelings of closeness from sexual intercourse.

[https://dana.org/article/brains-do-it-lust-attraction-and-attachment/](https://dana.org/article/brains-do-it-lust-attraction-and-attachment/)
​

Vasopressin is associated with the increase in desire that comes with the formation of an erection. After ejaculation, a man’s vasopressin levels decrease, along with the man’s sexual desire.

[https://vitalforceal.com/uncategorized/is-testosterone-the-only-sex-drive-hormone/](https://vitalforceal.com/uncategorized/is-testosterone-the-only-sex-drive-hormone/)​

We know Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA, C22:6n-3), and palmitic acid (C16:0) are the predominant PUFA and saturated fatty acids, respectively, in human sperm cells. It is the rich fatty acid content of the sperm cell membrane is critical for sperm function.

​

[https://www.endocrinologyadvisor.co...may-benefit-testicular-function-in-young-men/](https://www.endocrinologyadvisor.com/home/topics/general-endocrinology/omega-3-fatty-acid-supplements-may-benefit-testicular-function-in-young-men/)
In this study, the authors found that intake of fish oil supplements during the past 3 months was associated with higher semen volume, total sperm count, and testis size; lower FSH and LH levels; and a higher free testosterone to LH ratio among young men compared with no supplement intake. Interestingly, a dose-response association was found, as men with intake of fish oil supplements on 60 or more days had higher semen volume, higher total sperm count, lower LH level, higher free testosterone level, and a lower free testosterone to LH ratio than men with fish oil intake on fewer than 60 days​

_Bottomline, if this is one, -> Doubling our life's mitochondria ATP production is a **good thing**_

The frequency may not be accurate for humans, but it gives the idea .... As an organ with regenerative capacity, theoretically I could improve mitochondrial function in the liver, but if it doesn't then do what its supposed to do regeneration wise, the result may ultimately be the same .....

**"Hepatocyte growth factor (HGF)**
HGF is a cytokine that regulates cell proliferation, motility, morphogenesis and exerts anti-apoptotic and anti-inflammatory activity during hepatic regeneration. The activation of its transmembrane tyrosine kinase receptor, called Met receptor, leads to autophosphorylation of tyrosine residues and phosphorylation of downstream signaling molecules, such as PI3K and MAPK pathway proteins. Araújo and co-workers observed that, after 632.8 nm PBM, hepatectomized animals showed an increase in the expression of HGF followed by increased phosphorylation of Met and its downstream signaling molecules Akt and ERK. This indicates that PBM could enhance liver regeneration after hepatectomy"

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5215870/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5215870/)

### JanSz — 2021-11-11T16:26:25+0200

The hospice nurse is likely the last job I would want to do.

I came across 3 hospice nurses. Actually couple more.
Two cases. My Mom, the other my wife.
After my Dad died (in Poland), my Mom lived with us for 25 years. 1980-2005
When I brought my dying Mom (95yo) to the emergency room, (she was in the wheelchair in my van).
I did not realized that Mom was dying. Her death was the last thing on my mind.
It was the end of March, many people had colds then, she had a cold. We have been thru colds a month earlier.
I was quietly waiting in rather a long line in ER. When the nurse saw us she took us in in a hurry.
While other people were taking care of Mom, the nurse was taking care of me but she never told me that my Mom was about to die.
Mom had a hard time breathing. She had an oxygen mask over her face. That was getting being not enough.
But I still had eye and voice communication with my Mom.
Then the nurse is asking me if I want to permit them to put a tube in her throat.
All the time I am thinking that Mom is in temporary trouble.
I agreed to the tube in her throat.
Mom was fighting violently against the tube.
That fight used the rest of her energy, and she was done.
She had to go thru an unnecessary last hurdle. I had to watch that.
That nurse was ok all the time, but she should have told me that my Mom was dying.
That tube was unnecessary.

\------
Story with my DW for another time.

..

### JanSz — 2021-11-12T01:33:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-double-life-of-atp.26597/post-306064>

**Why Have an Advance Directive?**

By: Andrine Redsteer
Updated July 27, 2017

Advance directives are an important estate-planning tool. The three most common forms of advance directives are
durable powers of attorney for health care, (got it, written by a lawyer)
living wills, (got it, written by a lawyer)
and do not resuscitate orders. (not sure if the lawyer got that, but talked to my GP general practitioner, about it)

All three function to let family members and health care providers know a patient's wishes in the event he is unable to communicate or becomes incapacitated.

**Why Have an Advance Directive?**
Making an advance directive gives many people peace of mind. Often, when a family member becomes terminally ill or incapacitated, other family members have the heavy burden of deciding on appropriate end-of-life care. Advance directives let family members and health care providers know exactly what types of medical procedures a patient wants administered or withheld, taking the responsibility out of the hands of others. Some people make advance directives because they fear becoming a financial strain on their families, as end-of-life care and prolonging death through medical procedures can be costly.

**Durable Power of Attorney for Health Care**
A durable power of attorney for health care gives others guidance as to what end-of-life procedures a patient wants. Unlike a living will, a durable power of attorney for health care directs a specific person — often called a health care proxy — to make decisions on the patient's behalf. Durable power of attorney documents typically direct a health care proxy to communicate to physicians exactly which end-of-life care to withhold or to have administered. Examples of end-of-life procedures include artificial respiration, hydration, nutrition and medicines that might suppress respiration and hasten death.

**Living Will**
Living wills typically address many of the same things as a durable power of attorney for health care. The difference between the two is that living wills simply communicate the maker's wishes regarding end-of-life care. Living wills do not give power to another person to make decisions on the patient's behalf. Living wills merely communicate a patient's preferences regarding end-of-life medical procedures.

**Do Not Resuscitate Order**
Do not resuscitate orders are another form of advance directive. However, do not resuscitate orders are more limited in scope. Such orders only address whether a patient wishes to have cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) if he stops breathing or his heart stops. Without a do not resuscitate order, health care providers such as emergency medical technicians will administer CPR. As with living wills and durable powers of attorney for health care, give a copy of the do not resuscitate order to the patient's primary care physician.
**
Got it from (rather young) my neighbor (Indian Indian).
He has that he allows whatever high technology for only three days.
After that, disconnect all tubes and wires.
Then see how it goes.
My GP commented that for younger patients he thinks more in terms of two weeks.**

Hi everyone,

I'm currently living in central New York. So UV light on the body is starting to dwindle. My eyes are getting plenty of sunlight - mostly infrared and visible, but I decided to buy an emr-tek Firewave to compensate for what the NY country winter brings. As far as nnEMF goes, this is the best environment I’ve probably ever been in.

I’m not sure when/where to use this device. How many hours and what times of day do you think I should be using it?
I’m currently healing from a chronic illness of chronic inflammatory response syndrome, biotoxicity & neurotoxicity, mycotoxin toxicity, and mast cell activation syndrome.

Thanks for any information, all!

Taylor

### Tfreeman — 2021-11-20T18:57:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/suggestions-for-emr-tek-firewave.26600/post-306451>

So I’ve been using the Emr-tek Firewave for about a week. Sitting in front of it makes me feel like I’m sitting on the beach at sunrise, without the warmth. There is a day/night switch, only using the COB switch during the day is recommended.

I’d say the Firewave so far has boosted my mood, depression, and anxiety slightly, and I feel more good things are to come from using it.

I use it first thing in the morning with most switches on, in the afternoon when the sun has set, and then at night at a night light without the COB turned on (as recommended).

I guess the only thing that I’m missing now during a NY/fall winter is much UV light, but maybe that is ok as our ancestors did the same?

For me, it is important to respect the light cycles locally. I only use artificial light if it is bright outside. Preferably in the middle of the day. Using such artificial light after dark affects my sleep. I also notice that artificial light is not optimal. I miss the sun a lot at this time of year. I live at 59 degrees latitude. Kids and wife act as an anchor in my life. I love them, they need me. I hope it will be easier to travel soon.

[https://rumble.com/vobzao-plague-of-corruption-with-judy-mikovits.html](https://rumble.com/vobzao-plague-of-corruption-with-judy-mikovits.html)

32:11 --
Judy Mikovits: "We had our silver masks, which are at least anti-microbial, and we can breathe through them, and we can protect ourselves. There are a lot of people \[exhaling synthetic viral particles from the modified spike protein vaccines, and exhaling other microbes and pathogens\]."

35:15 --
Dr. Darrell Wolfe: "...about where to get the silver masks; I think it's tru47.com, right, Judy?"

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks](https://tru47.com/collections/masks)

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/silver-mesh-cup-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/silver-mesh-cup-mask)
SILVER MESH
"- Two layers of 99.99% pure ionically plated silver mesh
\- Silver is known to have antimicrobial, antibacterial and anti-fungal properties
\- Highly breathable
\- Reusable and long-lasting
Disclaimer:
TRU47 masks are not N95, medical or surgical coverings..."

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/heavyweight-mesh-cup-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/heavyweight-mesh-cup-mask)
HEAVYWEIGHT MESH
"- Interior and exterior layer of 99.99% pure ionically plated silver mesh
\- Middle layer of jersey modal woven with 99.99% pure silver thread"

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/pure-silver-cup-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/pure-silver-cup-mask)
PURE SILVER
"- Highly breathable, however, more dense than our mesh masks"

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/trifecta-cup-silver-mesh-copper-face-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/trifecta-cup-silver-mesh-copper-face-mask)
TRIFECTA COPPER/SILVER
"- Interior layer: 99.99% Pure Silver mesh
\- Middle layer: 99.99% Pure Copper
\- Exterior layer: 99.99% Pure Silver mesh
\- Silver is known for its antimicrobial, antibacterial and anti-fungal properties
\- Copper is known to have antiviral properties"

Maybe that one with copper would be useful the way wearing the masks are supposed to be.

\_\_\_\_\_\_

Maybe putting oils with antiviral qualities onto one of those masks would be an improvement.

Blending essential oils and a carrier oil to put in the nose to prevent infection from inhaled spike proteins:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300109](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300109)

Details of blending essential oils into a carrier oil so they can be safely put in the nose
Second to last link in this post:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300344](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300344)

One of the things I've been doing to prevent infection from inhaled spike proteins instead of wearing a mask; last paragraph here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-3#post-304550](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-3#post-304550)

\_\_\_\_\_

Critiques?

### Dan2 — 2021-11-15T22:22:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/masks-made-of-silver-and-copper-and-adding-antiviral-oils-to-a-mask-masks-that-are-more-helpful.26608/post-306250>

I was thinking, because the mesh is less dense than N95, even though most spike proteins would get through still, maybe the ones that do get stuck in the mask would react with the silver or copper to somehow weaken or break the structure of the whole protein, or especially maybe do something to the S1 subunit? And so then even if the particles that do get stuck in the mask are inhaled later, however much it might do that would be a little more useful than other masks that aren't N95. Or I was hoping it could be better than N95 somehow but I don't know how. When some places require a mask and I don't want to have to not go in that place, and even though a lot of people don't want to wear one but the people who own the business and the doorman don't want to deal with someone trying to fine them if someone goes there trying to find a reason to fine them, maybe this would be useful against the spike proteins too to some extent, better than the free disposable ones or buying a cloth one, or ideally better than N95 somehow. And if it is then maybe I'd wear it in grocery stores or other places where they don't enforce it too. So other than the mismatched filtration size -- or let's say then if it was N95 filtration size -- what advantage might there be with the silver or copper?

This is a caution to everyone out there in youtube land......

I got a serious dose of zinc overload. I was taking 100mg daily for about the last 10 days.

My DH has been taking the same amount but he is really fine with no issues.

Zinc has been mentioned so often by so called experts in the potential early treatment for covid.

Sort of on a must have list of preventative vitamins and minerals.

Apparently too much Zinc can have very serious side affects.

This was no fun ....we spent a few hours really worried and not knowing what to do.

### DLO — 2021-11-21T03:22:23+0200

### Dan2 — 2021-11-21T03:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zinc-overload.26637/post-306483>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)

Supplementing a different form of zinc than gluconate might help.

[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18271278/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18271278/)
"The aim of the present study was to compare the oral bioavailability of zinc bis-glycinate (a new formulation) with zinc gluconate (reference formulation). A randomized, cross-over study was conducted in 12 female volunteers... Bis-glycinate administration was safe and well tolerated and **bis-glycinate significantly increased the oral bioavailability of zinc (+43.4%) compared with the gluconate**."

[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29457347/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29457347/)
"The oral absorption, distribution, excretion, and bioavailability of zinc sulfate (ZnS), zinc gluconate (ZnG), and zinc-enriched yeast (ZnY) in rats are fully and systemically compared... ZnS, ZnG, and ZnY show equivalent bioavailability based on plasma and tissues zinc levels, although **ZnY was statistically more absorbed and retained than ZnS and ZnG** based on the excretion amount."

[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17916952/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17916952/)
"The objective of this study was to demonstrate whether Zn was absorbed better by healthy male volunteers when given supplements where the mineral is found organically bound in yeast or as a salt gluconate form... Zinc gluconate gave higher Zn concentrations in the blood in the first 6 h but also showed greater losses in the feces. Zinc yeast also increased in blood with time but showed significantly less loss in the feces. Thus, **the net Zn balance after 48 h for Zn yeast was 9.46 but for Zn gluconate it was -2.00, indicating that Zn gluconate supplementation contributed to a net loss of Zn**. It was concluded that organic Zn yeast supplements are more biologically available than Zn gluconate salts."

Or considering that, maybe just not supplementing gluconate would allow net increase from zinc in food without a supplement.

Some other options:

Glycinate chelate

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-3#post-306245](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-3#post-306245)

[https://store.emeraldlabs.com/products/zinc-25mg?\_pos=1&\_sid=fb0243ca6&\_ss=r](https://store.emeraldlabs.com/products/zinc-25mg?_pos=1&_sid=fb0243ca6&_ss=r)

Picolinate

[https://www.thorne.com/products/dp/double-strength-zinc-picolinate-60-s-1](https://www.thorne.com/products/dp/double-strength-zinc-picolinate-60-s-1)

Standard Process brand zinc chelate

[https://www.standardprocess.com/products/zinc-chelate](https://www.standardprocess.com/products/zinc-chelate)
(That says it's chelated to a rice amino acid. It used to be chelated to a beef liver amino acid. Not sure if it's the same amino acid still. I sent a message on Standard Process's website asking which amino acid it was and is now chelated to. I'll edit this when they reply.
The answer from Standard Process:
"We don’t specify a particular amino acid in either the rice chelate or the former liver chelate. The process of chelation results in zinc bound to amino acids from rice protein. The previous version was zinc bound to amino acids from liver protein.")

[https://www.amazon.com/Standard-Process-Zinc-Chelate-Digestion/dp/B08FMX4MSZ](https://www.amazon.com/Standard-Process-Zinc-Chelate-Digestion/dp/B08FMX4MSZ)

\_\_\_

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) What were your zinc overdose symptoms?

### caroline — 2021-11-21T04:26:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zinc-overload.26637/post-306484>

extreme nausea and vomiting and diarrhea and cramping and bloating.

This happened over the weekend - Sunday today in Oz.

I wonder if I should see a Doctor tomorrow? Will my system get rid of unnecessary zinc on its own? or will it be bound up in my system and I will need help to eliminate it?

The zinc we bought ......Now brand Zinc 50 mg \[from 450mg Zinc gluconate}

### caroline — 2021-11-21T04:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zinc-overload.26637/post-306485>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=306480)
>
> Welcome to alzhiemers/dementia.

why do you say that????? please explain

### DLO — 2021-11-21T04:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zinc-overload.26637/post-306487>

Copper/zinc balance
[https://duckduckgo.com/?q=too+much+zinc+alzhiemers&atb=v238-1&ia=web](https://duckduckgo.com/?q=too+much+zinc+alzhiemers&atb=v238-1&ia=web)
This is the same kinda shit that happens with ivermectin people thinking it's great when it's not indicated.

### Dan2 — 2021-11-21T06:57:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zinc-overload.26637/post-306490>

[@DLO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19314/) (Maybe you were joking but) She's not gonna get dementia, dude lol. Balance of zinc and copper helps prevent dementia, yeah, but short-term zinc supplement overdose causing dementia is silly.

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) "Will my system get rid of unnecessary zinc on its own?"
Because the amount you supplemented adds up to only about as much as is okay to eat in about two weeks or a little more instead of about ten days, depending how much zinc is in the foods you eat, it'll get used or removed soon. Most of it probably already has been and that's why there were those symptoms.

[https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/326760#treatments](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/326760#treatments)
Zinc overdose treatment:
"Unless a poison control representative or a healthcare professional provides alternative advice, the person should drink a glass of milk. The calcium and phosphorus in the milk can help bind the excess zinc and prevent the stomach and intestines from absorbing it."

It's probably a pretty serious problem if the common advice is have a glass of milk. People don't get long-term zinc poisoning after supplementing a normal amount again (depending on diet too) because of how much can be used nutritionally compared to other metals and so how fast something can be done with a short-term excess.

You might be okay supplementing 50-100 mg with another form than gluconate. Maybe the gluconate form doesn't digest as well for you as another form would. 100 mg a day, from any form of supplement, might still be a bit much depending what you're eating. A pound of beef would be about 20-40 mg.

People don't get long-term zinc poisoning after supplementing a normal amount again (depending on diet too) because of how much can be used nutritionally compared to other metals and so how fast something can be done with a short-term excess.
>

Thanks Dan.
I eat a lot of beef, lamb seafood as I am mostly carnivore-ish with some sweet potatoes and local green beans lately and some local blueberries.

Thinking back this last 10 days .....when I ate oysters I really wasn't feeling well.

\===================================================================

**Product description**
**Zinc Tally** is a simple screening method for **evaluating zinc status.** By placing two teaspoons of Zinc Tally in the mouth, **a lack of taste or a delayed taste perception suggests a possible zinc insufficiency.**

**If Zinc Tally tastes bad you are overloaded with zinc (or rather you don't need more zinc).**

### JanSz — 2021-11-21T14:06:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zinc-overload.26637/post-306497>

> [Dan2 said:](/goto/post?id=306483)
>
> [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)Supplementing a different form of zinc than gluconate might help.

Since I do the testing, I try to minimize variables as much as possible.
So, I rather eat one or two more additional pills.
Hopefully that will be enough.
Additionally, the testing I do, the Spectracell, allows me to test twice a year only.
So, starting with new product would actually set me back.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussie-army-taking-your-positive-case-and-your-contacts-to-a-camp.26640/post-306542>

The Australian army has begun forcibly removing residents in the Northern Territories to the [Howard Springs](https://aecom.com/projects/manigurr-ma-village/) quarantine camp located in Darwin, after nine new Covid-19 cases were identified in the community of Binjari. The move comes after hard lockdowns were instituted in the communities of both Binjari and nearby Rockhole on Saturday night.

“Residents of Binjari and Rockhole no longer have the five reasons to leave their homes,” said Northern Territory chief minister, [Michael Gunner](https://www.facebook.com/TerritoryChief/posts/435285761296933), referring to the country’s five allowable reasons to avoid lockdown (buying food and supplies, exercising for up to two hours, care or caregiving, work or education if it can’t be done from home, and to get vaccinated at the nearest possible location

# Thread: near infrared bulbs and "ordinary" light fixtures?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-bulbs-and-ordinary-light-fixtures.26647/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Charmane — 2021-11-26T19:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-bulbs-and-ordinary-light-fixtures.26647/post-306687>

Ah Black Friday... This bulb looks totally affordable. Any thoughts? I can't afford the whole sauna set up, and I'm considering putting this bulb into an ordinary lamp. Would that be safe? I also have a bathroom with an overhead "heat lamp" on the ceiling... but it's from the 1980s and it's never had a heat lamp in it as long as I've lived here. Interested in safety issues... Thanks!

### John Schumacher — 2021-11-26T20:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-bulbs-and-ordinary-light-fixtures.26647/post-306691>

> [Charmane said:](/goto/post?id=306687)
>
> Ah Black Friday... This bulb looks totally affordable. Any thoughts? I can't afford the whole sauna set up, and I'm considering putting this bulb into an ordinary lamp. Would that be safe? I also have a bathroom with an overhead "heat lamp" on the ceiling... but it's from the 1980s and it's never had a heat lamp in it as long as I've lived here. Interested in safety issues... Thanks!

I use infrared bulbs & UV bulbs for ordinary lighting; however, they draw more electricity and run much hotter. They can burn out ordinary lamp fixtures and can add up to too much amperage usage on a circuit. So you will need to learn

-   How much heat does a given bulb produce
-   How many amps does each bulb consume
-   How far is the heat throw &/or UV radiance of each bulb.

I prefer the nature heat feel and light "color" of NIR & UV bulbs in the winter.

### Matt Wales — 2021-11-29T16:34:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/near-infrared-bulbs-and-ordinary-light-fixtures.26647/post-306794>

I've got a 'DIY' set up in my shower room. Didn't cost me much. I recommend looking at Matt Justice's site. He's really honest and seems to really care about helping people. [Certified Saunas.com – Safe Low EMF Infrared Saunas](https://certifiedsaunas.com/)

# Thread: The Lions Among Men
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lions-among-men.26653/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matt Fowler — 2021-11-28T02:51:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lions-among-men.26653/post-306739>

Fearless.
The Lions among men.
Blues and twos - gasping, I'm there in a swift flick of a big cats tail.
Venturi masks are the order of the day. By night, flickering recessed lighting burns continuously - saving the planet but doing much less for its keepers.
A whirring of lifesaving gadgetry and intense symphony of repetitive beeps - sometimes in unison - but mostly conflicting aggressively. Come visit the night club at respiratory ward 7.
Have you had your vaccine sir? Eyes drop nervously at my unfavourable reply. Have some antibodies.
Whatever you say Dr. Patel. I want to live.
The sound of assisted respiration fills the semi dark ward - Darth Vaders here to stay.
A green and black, glass fronted helmet complete with tubes and whirring fans startles me through the artificial twighlight - can I check your sats? Be my guest. Ridley scott would be proud.
6 days in - venturi mask weaned and titrated. I still can't breath but Im on my way - Its gonna be a cold winter.
Big cats with huge, brave hearts - too old for their years - broken mitochondria starting to show - risking life and limb up to their eyeballs in contagion 24/7.
Selfless.
I saw a medical paper - Cytokine induced Acute Respiratory distress syndrome doesnt happen with a vit d level over 50.
Mines 30, hers is 70.
Im going home. My mothers in the ITU - ventilated.
She devoted her life to others.
"As long as space remains" as His Holiness likes to pray.
Will she survive to breath another day?
Nobody knows, nobody knows.
...
Not even the Lions among Men.

### JanSz — 2021-11-28T03:08:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lions-among-men.26653/post-306742>

................

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3

### Martha Ray — 2021-12-04T06:09:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrooms-light-from-glass-windows.26678/post-307040>

Researching and looking at homes and have come across several with **sunrooms**\----
what could be done with with a sunroom...maybe make it into a greenhouse?
**
Am I right that sunrooms are NOT healthy for humans?**

My family loves to sit near the windows of our great room in the winter and I'm thinking that is not healthy.
**Am I right?**

**I also have no ideas about what** glass choices for windows **are best for health. I am pretty sure the great room wall of windows are tinted to protect the furniture. Also in that room two window pannels beside the fireplace need to be replaced and the whole room needs the blinds replaced..we did not know and put unhealthy blinds up year ago before we knew to be careful with materials.**

Realators, home remoldeling and decor sites bill sunrooms as health and a place to sunbathe?

How should we even interact around our home windows?

**Isn't sunlight through glass windows home and cars harmful (unhealthy and possibly carcinogenic?) for humans?

What think you all...?**

>
> **Isn't sunlight through glass windows home and cars harmful (unhealthy and possibly carcinogenic?) for humans?
>
> What think you all...?**

Note that [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) advises keeping the window in the car crack open while driving.
Light goes in thru the crack, bounces off surfaces,
now inside the car you have all the information from the light that you need (if you take the clothes off).

Information from light----->>100% need to stay alive
Energy from (real time) light ---->is minimal, close to nothing (on equator, during noon)(when compared to the energy of matrix water synthesis and its decomposition in the presence of melanin)

\----------------------------
Do the same with the sunroom. Keep some windows crack open. Preferably windows that are directly in the sunlight.

# Thread: Environmental Health Consultant coming Indoor air+
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-health-consultant-coming-indoor-air.26679/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2021-12-04T06:58:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-health-consultant-coming-indoor-air.26679/post-307041>

I set up an appointment with an **Environmental Health Consultant** to talk to us about some concerns I have about home health and about his services.

His specialties include:

-   Asbestos-containing Materials
    -   ACM Identification
    -   ACM Management
    -   ACM Abatement
    -   Asbestos Project Design and Monitoring
    -   Asbestos Regulations Consulting
-   Lead-Based Paints
    -   LBP Inspections
    -   LBP Risk Assessments
    -   LBP Regulations
-   Indoor Air Quality Investigations, Testing & Inspections
    -   COVID-19 (coronavirus) testing and air exchange rate measurements
    -   Mold
        -   Mold Inspections
        -   Black Mold Testing
        -   Mold Assessments
        -   Mold Remediation Design and Consulting
    -   Radon
        -   Radon Gas Measurements
        -   Radon Testing for Home and Residential
        -   Radon Inspections for Commercial Properties

I will most likely test the Indoor air the day he comes ($80.00). I ask him if there was anything I should do before he comes and he said to not open the house up or run the air purifiers 24 hours before our appointment.

For the past 12 years we have been using _Austin Air_ brand STANDARD purifiers almost 24 /7. They use charcoal filters. I used to have one of there large high end units that after I researched one of the materials I found it may not good for thyroid health. When we replaced that large unit filter last I went with their plain standard charcoal filter and have been please with them. The small EMF meter I have does not register the units as high but I still also wonder about running them 24/7.

Not sure this consultant is all that holistic but he has a very good reputation in town, he come highly recommended by people I think well of and he does not personally provide any remediation services himself. His fee is $225. for this appointment.

**Have any of you had experenice with _Environmental Health Consultants_ and can you please share your experiences, as well as any** **questions I might consider asking him**....

**I'll report back after I see him....**

### JanSz — 2021-12-04T14:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-health-consultant-coming-indoor-air.26679/post-307050>

Be careful.
You may not be able to sell your home (until after you clean up the mess he is going to write about), and send a copy of it to your town.

.........

Going somewhere by Andrew Marino reccomended by Jack repeatedly over the past several years makes a mockery of the scientific process used by many around the world. These same studies are being used to justify extreme interference in the lives of individuals. Having read that book I think it's preposterous that a person would go along with what these fraudsters dictate.

I just want to celebrate that book, thank Jack for recommending it, and to ask anyone else here who has read it to vouch for how prescient and relevant to our current circumstances it is.

### Pablo — 2021-12-04T17:23:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-somewhere.26680/post-307060>

Yeah, it is freaking great, great wake up call on how science has been corrupt for years. IMO solution to this comes from individual responsability, waking the fuck up, always asking questions, not delegating authority, pushing in self improvement as a way to change the world, etc. However, most people get in the path we are at only through some traumatic exprience, and I find it is fucking hard to wake people up to this reality otherwise, since most people are just comfortably numb.

Lets celebrate that book, Andrew Marino, Jack Kruse, and so many other heroes, known and unkown, who had fought for truth, stayed strong under huge pressure, and not succumbed to mediocrity, self-deception, pure selfishness and other lower sentiments in detriment of the human species.

# Thread: Screw the metaverse just get straight to it
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/screw-the-metaverse-just-get-straight-to-it.26691/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2021-12-07T03:13:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/screw-the-metaverse-just-get-straight-to-it.26691/post-307211>

**First-ever ‘Suicide Pod’ Approved, Promises No Choking Feeling**
[by RT.com](https://www.rt.com/news/542307-assisted-suicide-sarco-nitschke/)
December 6th 2021, 5:00 pm
3D-printed pod, intended for use in assisted suicide, successfully passed legal review in Switzerland and should be ready for operation next year, says creator.
Euthanasia is legal in the European country, with 1,300 people having had recourse to the procedure in 2020, according to data from its two main assisted suicide organizations.

Service-users’ lives were ended through the ingestion of liquid sodium pentobarbital, which puts patients into a deep coma before their passing, but Dr Philip Nitschke, the developer behind the sci-fi-evoking Sarco capsule is suggesting another approach, promising his clients a swift and peaceful death without any drugs.

His pod achieves its goal by being filled with nitrogen, thus rapidly reducing the concentration of oxygen and killing the person inside through hypoxia and hypocapnia.

The user of Sacro _“will feel a little disoriented and may feel slightly euphoric before they lose consciousness,”_ Nitschke explained, in an interview with website SwissInfo on Saturday. He also said death is brought about in 30 seconds through this method, and that _“there is no panic, no choking feeling.”_

The machine is activated from the inside, and the person in the capsule can press a button _“in their own time”_ without needing assistance, the Australian doctor said. The pod is also mobile and may be transported to any location of the patient’s choosing – be it a special assisted suicide facility or an _“idyllic outdoor setting.”_

The legal review of the technology, which started last year, has now been completed and, Nitschke said, _“we’re very pleased with the result, which found that we hadn’t overlooked anything … There are no legal issues at all.”_

The third prototype of the Sarco pod is currently being 3D-printed in the Netherlands and _“should be ready for operation in Switzerland in 2022,”_ he added.

In a promo clip last year, Nitschke [said](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HUp6aowxPTE) that he was planning to make the technology free. The program to print the capsule would be made available online and _“that would allow us to spread the idea around the world,” he explained._

# Thread: How do I restore my cognitive function
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-restore-my-cognitive-function.26704/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joshben — 2021-12-11T01:05:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-restore-my-cognitive-function.26704/post-307354>

To go into my history it started with taking a statin pill and I had a bad reaction my memory was never the same I believe statins cause mitochondrial mutation. Anyway It was never that bad compared to what it is now, but I had dysautonomia, gaps in memory short term but nothing major. I think some blood brain barrier issues as I would struggle with raw butter, milk etc maybe related to bacterial load? Maybe my vagus nerve, as I had inappropriate sinus tachycardia. I don't know but I never had those issues before that statin and it messed me up pretty bad.

I learnt to deal with it and improved alot of my symptoms but never got back to being able to run around freely due to my heart rate, syncope etc.

4 years on my memory got quite bad in April I decided to take bovine brain supplements under the belief of like for like. What ensued was horrendous neuro symptoms, after a week of 6 tablets I started to hear ringing when there wasn't any, my cognition was so poor I'd forget what I was just doing, leave doors open, I developed visual snow, eye floaters, headaches everyday, muscle weakness, tremors when lifting anything, no stamina, transient hearing loss in one ear, slower processing. It's now December I've tried a few different things but still pretty much the same in many ways. I seriously worry about having a prion disease.

I wasn't even aware it could be contacted like this but then when I did enough research after I came to the conclusion it was at the very least possible. I wonder with my blood brain barrier issues if it was this the onset could be rapid somehow. I see saladino has just launched a bovine brain supplement also and when asked about prions gives a cryptic answer that there has never been a case in NZ and that they do third party testing which is actually for microbes. I think this is really dangerous as far fetched as it seems. It just feels to me that the thing that was so devastating was trust the alternative health world and it's done more damage than big pharma ever could to me. From peoole who receive such praise for there health orientated supps and promotion. I should have researched more but blindly trusted them.

Anyway I live in a cold climate UK but there is no 5 g where I am. I'm giving up hope in a sense of I really felt my health was improving or at least aspects of it then took this supp and it destroyed it I had been walking and building stamina doing body weight routine very paced for around a year to get fitter. I hadn't tapped into the light stuff yet tho or EMF.

Anyway I'm open to all suggestions and thoughts.
.

### caroline — 2021-12-11T02:48:09+0200

Hi ......EMF and light stuff is hugely important. Jack tells us not to take a statin.

But you are kind of screwed if you stay in the UK.

Jack always tells us that we can't get well in the place we got sick in!

You need the equator yesterday!

Check into Photbiomodulation

What are you eating? Do you eat seafood?? Oysters are a must.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Jack a health history - also your Mom's and Gran's.

What do you do for work? Where do you live exactly?

Have you considered a consultation with Dr. Kruse? Would you be financially able to become a farm member?

You have a lot on your plate but you are young .....Good Luck

### Martha Ray — 2021-12-16T03:26:48+0200

# Thread: How does one naturally boost Glutathione levels?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-one-naturally-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tfreeman — 2021-12-14T00:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-one-naturally-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/post-307472>

My GGT is always high on my bloodwork. The redox rx says a high GGT = low glutathione. How do I raise them naturally?

### Dan2 — 2021-12-14T01:20:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-one-naturally-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/post-307473>

Do you already eat a lot of foods that have lots of the amino acids that are precursors for it, like meat or eggs or dairy? If you do, maybe it's some liver function that could be improved. Milk thistle seed tea would be a good thing to try for generally improving liver function (the seeds ground to powder in a coffee grinder or not much will be extracted into the water; about a teaspoon or tablespoon a day, simmered to make a decoction (decoction means simmering for about 15 minutes in a saucepot instead of just pouring hot water over it and letting it steep like a tea bag)).

[http://glutathionepathway.com/glutathione/](http://glutathionepathway.com/glutathione/)

"GLUTATHIONE SYNTHESIS

It’s true that your body produces and recycles it’s own supply of glutathione. Unfortunately this synthesis only occurs if you have the correct pre-cursors and ingredients to do so. The process of glutathione synthesis is highly regulated by two major enzymatic steps which both disease and environmental factors can disrupt.

A tripeptide of glutamine, cysteine and glycine, the most common limiting factor in producing glutathione is the semi-essential amino acid Cysteine. The glutamine and glycine are generally not lacking in a typical Western diet.

SYNTHESIS OF GSH

Two ATP requiring enzymatic steps synthesize GSH from amino acids.

L-glutamate + L-cysteine + ATP → γ-glutamyl-L-cysteine + ADP + Pi
Gamma-glutamylcysteine is synthesized from L-glutamate and cysteine via the enzyme gamma-glutamylcysteine synthetase. (This is the rate limiting step, again often due to lack of available cysteine.)

γ-glutamyl-L-cysteine + L-glycine + ATP → GSH + ADP + Pi
Glycine is added to the C-terminal of gamma-glutamylcysteine via the enzyme glutathione synthetase.

MITOCHONDRIAL GSH IN THE LIVER

Even though glutathione is synthesized from precursor amino acids in virtually every cell, the liver has your highest concentration because it’s hepatocyte cells have unique GSH synthesis abilities.

The ability to convert methionine to cysteine through the transsulfuration pathway, and the regulation of GSH biosynthesis based on rate of export into plasma, bile, and mitochondria. Short answer, the liver is responsible for mitochondrial glutathione."

### John Schumacher — 2021-12-14T02:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-one-naturally-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/post-307477>

If I may add [@Dan2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20918/) -

Most of the time when addressing GGT it is approached from a dietary perspective

[https://drhyman.com/downloads/Functional-Diagnostics-Redefining-Disease.pdf](https://drhyman.com/downloads/Functional-Diagnostics-Redefining-Disease.pdf)
(gluten, IgG food allergies, poor diet, yeast overgrowth)​

It has been well established that glutathione metabolism (synthesized from cysteine) occurs in the liver thru the trans-sulphuration pathway.

Doi.org/10.3390/antiox10030364
[https://sci-hub.se/10.3390/antiox10030364](https://sci-hub.se/10.3390/antiox10030364)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The study of cytosolic synthesis & disposal ->

Role of the Liver in lnterorgan Homeostasis of Glutathione and Cyst(e)ine
[https://sci-hub.se/10.1055/s-2007-1007167](https://sci-hub.se/10.1055/s-2007-1007167)​

Liver and Intracellular Glutathione Compartmentalization, in the nucleus, mitochondria and endoplasmic reticulum. mGSH: mitochondrial GSH; mGSSG: mitochondrial GSSG.​

Ok, cool - So how do you improve that? Can you supplement your way to health?

If glutathione metabolism is synthesized in the liver, perhaps our question maybe -> So how's your liver functioning?

-   High levels of _gamma_\-_glutamyl transferase_ (_GGT_) shows poor liver function.

What has Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) recommended for improving liver function?

We know he's talked about the liver's role in "vitamin" D3 production; but what has he said specifically on how to improve it?

So far... crickets...​

But my study of _light-water-magnetism_ shows there are treatments using photobiomodulation.

-   Reference book "Handbook of Photomedicine" page 505 photobiomodulation therapy is used on primary liver cancer.

    -   Light delivery using laser-pumped dye (630 nm) three treatments spots per session. A light dose of 220 J/cm^2 was deliverd at fluence rates of 300-350nW/cm for each spot.
    -   The best results came from four or more sessions with a 75% rate of decrease in the alpha-fetoprotein levels.

-   From the Journal of Photochemistry and Photobiology "Mechanisms and Mitochondrial Redox Signaling in Photobiomodulation" - [https://sci-hub.se/10.1111/php.12864](https://sci-hub.se/10.1111/php.12864), [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/pmc/articles/PMC5844808/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/pmc/articles/PMC5844808/)
    -   Photobiomodulation has a marked effect on stem cells, and this is proposed to operate via mitochondrial redox signaling.

Nitric oxide can inhibit this process by binding to CuB, and it is proposed that red or NIR light can dissociate this noncovalently bound NO increasing the rate of respiration and ATP production.

What is of interest for us is -> reduced glutathione (GSH), superoxide dismutase (SOD), and catalase (CAT) activities increased
​

Ok nice - so what's the therapeutic dose for increasing our liver's synthesis of glutathione?

_don't know
_​

Here's a guess -

Go to 9:30 for dosage in Joules​

The sweet spot is usually between 10–20 joules per square centimeter (10J/cm² — 20J/cm²), although this “sweet spot” can vary depending upon the body part and the treatment.

-   More superficial tissues, such as your skin, teeth, or superficial blood vessels, need a low dosage of 3-10 Joules
-   Deeper tissues, which include your muscles, joints, organs, or bones, need between 20 and 60 Joules. Most studies actually stay around the 20-40 J dose, every few days.

Since we maybe interested in liver function, we may want a dosage of 20-40 Joules on our liver area.

_Remember the article above: "A light dose of 220 J/cm^2 was deliverd at fluence rates of 300-350nW/cm for each spot."_
So a dose between 20-40 Joules is well below.​

Dose Analysis of Photobiomodulation Therapy - [https://www.hindawi.com/journals/ecam/2020/8145616/](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/ecam/2020/8145616/)

### Tfreeman — 2021-12-14T22:10:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-one-naturally-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/post-307506>

Thank you all for this info!

I do have almost every single symptom of a glutathione deficiency, so I'm hoping that my EMR device will start to work along with being outside often.

My liver is completely over burdened which would make sense why my GGT, AST, ALT, cholesterol, triglycerides and more won't go down even on epi paleo. I'm trying to work on increasing my glutathione naturally, and Jack's redox rx seems to be one important component as it says:
"Meyer cocktails, HBO therapy, and [infrared therapy](http://www.vielight.com/product/810-infrared/) will improve your redox potential. You will need the help of your doctor to gain access to most of these therapies. They need proper context to your specific issues. There are several additional useful biomarkers for identifying patients in whom glutathione is low. One such indicator is elevated Gamma-glutamyltransferase (GGT), the enzyme that breaks down glutathione from the blood to allow its importation into the cells. **Up-regulation of GGT has been observed in cells deficient in glutathione**."

Therefore, infrared therapy for me is extremely important, I just don't know what amount of time I should be spending with my EMR firewave.

My main issues are mast cell activation, POTS, histamine and inflammatory responses, and what those in the functional medicine world called mold toxicity and biotoxin illness. I believe the mold issues are stemming from a lifetime of a lack of sunlight as well as a glutathione deficiency.

Due to my symptoms and sensitivity, light, water, magnetism are taking quite a while to heal it, even in my remote location. I use SUN, activated charcoal, bentonite clay, and chlorella to reduce my histamine and lower th2, and also am in the process of trying to raise th1.

I was listening to a podcast last week on betterhealthguy blogcast with Dr. Frank Shallenberger who said that vaccines literal purpose is to stimulate the th2 immune response to create antibodies, he said "Vaccines are – the definition of a good vaccine is it stimulates Th2. It stimulates Th2. So I’m thinking the biggest single reason we have people walking around now, especially said the under 40 crowd, the under 40 crowd has been exposed to something like – it’s more than 10, it’s 10, it’s 20, it’s about 20 times more vaccinations as children than the over 40 year old group. So I am quite sure that a lot of the problems that we’re seeing in society today from the allergies, to autoimmune disease, to the Lyme disease, to all these kind of stuff, especially in these young people. And I can’t tell you how we see stuff in the under 40 group like stage four cancers that I never saw before. I never saw – that would be like weird if I’d see something – It’s like every day normal now. And I’m pretty convinced that the very heart of it is this over vaccination that we have gotten into since what, the late 70s, early 80s."

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-wars-are-bankers-wars-incredible-read-history-of-the-central-bank.26721/post-307609>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=307608)
>
> [www.whatreallyhappened.com/WRHARTICLES/allwarsarebankerswars.pdf](https://www.whatreallyhappened.com/WRHARTICLES/allwarsarebankerswars.pdf)
>
> Or the movie:
>
> [https://topdocumentaryfilms.com/all-wars-are-bankers-wars/](https://topdocumentaryfilms.com/all-wars-are-bankers-wars/)

Interesting threads of history including:

As President, John F. Kennedy understood the predatory nature of private central banking. He understood why Andrew fought so hard to end the Second Bank of the United States. So Kennedy wrote and signed Executive Order 11110 which ordered the US Treasury to issue a new public currency, the United States Note.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Kennedy's United States Notes were not borrowed from the Federal Reserve but created by the US Government and backed by the silver stockpiles held by the US Government. It represented a return to the system of economics the United States had been founded on, and was perfectly legal for Kennedy to do. All told, some four and one half billion dollars went into public circulation, eroding interest payments to the Federal Reserve and loosening their control over the nation. Five months later John F. Kennedy was assassinated in Dallas Texas, and the United States Notes pulled from circulation and destroyed (except for samples held by collectors).​

# Thread: How to talk to the jabbed people in our life.......

### caroline — 2021-12-21T03:42:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-talk-to-the-jabbed-people-in-our-life.26731/post-307771>

How do you explain, in simple terms, to the brain washed and jabbed people in our life ... why these covid injections are a complete world wide humanitarian medical disaster of epic proportions.

I want to get the science right but I want to be brief and to the point.

I get angry and emotional - which, of course, doesn't help.

I feel devastated that the doctor in our family is totally onboard with the clot shot, and they hold her up as the authority on this medical matter. After all - there is a doctor in our family and we should be listening to her and forget about this world wide conspiracy theory.

There is a world wide conspiracy alright .....not a theory.

I feel so angry and hurt that the folks in our lives try to insist that we should get the jab and that we are being selfish and ridiculous ...... but they have not done any due diligence what so ever.

When did our government become so trustworthy????

Apparently Aussies think that Dr. Norman Swan is the gold standard for medical/covid advise and every word he utters is gospel .....God help us all!

Trust me - I'm a doctor......

What do you say to the people in your life?

I don't think we can sway them in the least but, at least, we need to know what we are talking about in clear and concise terms.

We need to be able to verbalise the courage of our convictions in no uncertain terms

The rest is up to them.......

### Liam Taylor — 2021-12-21T12:54:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-talk-to-the-jabbed-people-in-our-life.26731/post-307773>

I've been telling people about my concerns around the globalist agenda for almost 10 years now. Not once have I received an apology for the fact I told them something which was publicly available information at the time, which they called conspiracy theory, and is now common knowledge which they accept. Nothing in my experience suggests we're able to convince other people through conversation. Unfortunately I suspect the only way is via subtle manipulation -planting seeds etc. etc. - which most ethical people object to doing and find rather exhausting to attempt.

Alan Watt of cutting through the matrix (died earlier this year, very suspicious), has been barking on about this for 20 years via his podcast. He advised about **all** of this. He didn't predict it, he simply reported what organisations like the council on foreign relations (hi ) had made publicly available.

I need to reply in the other thread, but I'm too damn depressed about all of this to do it right now.

### Sue-UK — 2021-12-21T13:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-talk-to-the-jabbed-people-in-our-life.26731/post-307774>

I now see it in terms of energy ....

I've decided its not up to me to justify my decision. My body, my choice. If I actually do a thought experiment to weigh up the probability that I'll change a particular family member' or friend's mind to the extent that they see my point of view or regret having had the shots (and now the first booster ) and that probability is pretty much zero, what is the point? Why leak energy to them?

I stop anyone trying to coerce me now, I see it as energy vampire behaviour, unintentional though it might be.

### 5G Canary — 2021-12-21T17:32:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-talk-to-the-jabbed-people-in-our-life.26731/post-307786>

We cannot change people but we can let Mother Nature/Life be their educator.

We also cannot force an awakening especially on someone who is not ready. Kind of the same approach as when someone is sleepwalking.... we aren’t supposed to wake them abruptly but gently guide them back to bed. As we guide them it has to happen naturally and on their own.... the best you can do is to be an example of your higher self. You also don’t need words... your thoughts create reality. Think positive thoughts toward them and for them.... collectively praying/thoughts is very powerful. That is also why a prayer chain is so powerful.
**_

### caroline — 2021-12-23T03:25:06+0200

I don't really feel like loving others right now.......I feel quite uncharitable actually.

We intend to save our own skin first - This is going to take every ounce of energy - both mental and physical - for us to do what we need to do.

The people in our life that matter to us think we are idiots ...so be it.

I, personally, am crossing them off my Christmas card list....and I am not going to forget or forgive their subtle mental abuse.

I won't be a bitch ......but I have lost all respect for people that can't or won't do any critical thinking of their own.

I am putting this away now ....time to move forward with positivity and excitement and thankfulness.

We are both so grateful for everything we learn here....

100% agree with you and Sue. It is all about energy, collecting as much as we can and using it wisely... I'm not wasting it with close minded people, life is too short, but I do with people in the middle ground, if I feel like it. Use energy wisely, it' s imposible to save the world, and maybe it is not even desirable/ how the world works, and we just need to accept that fact.

Lets see the good side of this shit: covid is allowing us to rearrange the poeple we want to spend time with, trust, listen, to become our priority... and the people who we need to ignore much more... with a revolving door, as Jack says, I'll keep the door open for people when they start getting more wise and less stupid, everything and everyone is changing.

Actually I'm finding a good community of people in the protests against coercion, passports, etc. I'm rearranging my social network and it feels so damm good, people in similar vibe, with whom you can connect and talk "crazy shit". ### Sue-UK — 2021-12-23T13:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-talk-to-the-jabbed-people-in-our-life.26731/post-307841>

> [Pablo said:](/goto/post?id=307839)
>
> Actually I'm finding a good community of people in the protests against coercion, passports, etc.

Seeing the protests has kept a lot of people from going under I think, - including me - knowing we are not alone .... whilst waiting for enough mistakes to be made to trigger the tipping point ...

The saying "Never interrupt the enemy when he is making a mistake" comes to mind.

After a constant drip for months of one rule for them and one for us, attitudes are generally changing in the UK thanks to leaked photos of the garden parties in Downing Street last year when it was banned for us, and the admitting that the modelling predictions for infection rates and hospital admissions weren't done to inform decision making of new government policy, but done to fit whatever policy decision had already been made.

More stories hitting the MSM media, and more criticism. There's a definite change of mood. Normally if I go round a supermarket without a mask I get a fear vibe from some of the other shoppers, but yesterday ....none. Even though very few were not wearing masks, there isn't the hesitancy, no sense of disapproval. Been to the pub 4 times recently, everybody behaving normally, no masks, no social distancing. Conversation. Laughter.

At the last Parliament vote on minor restrictions, it went through with support from the opposition, it was nearly 100 of Boris Johnson's own MPs who voted against. Apart from risking the anger of his own MPs by bringing in more restrictions, (whilst giving the opposition a chance to get rid of him) he's now risking far more non compliance if he tries ....

The invisible is being made visible. By them.

Thought this was perfect from Jack’s Christmas 12/20/2021.....

“**_THOUGHTS MATTER DEEPLY_**”

“Every thought we have is like an electromagnetic bar code we see at a grocery store. Those bar codes encrypt its energy, information, and vibration. That fearful thought that you have negative energy and causes physical and mental stress in the body. It thrives on your attention. The more you spend time processing and dwelling on the thought, the more you feed it and the faster it expands and manifests into your life. You unintentionally attract it into your life by focusing on it and feeding it from your time and mental energy.

**_If you fear something, take a step back, rebalance yourself and look at it from another perspective.”_**

Unfortunately, when we focus upon only one thing, and it then uses up most of our ENERGY, we don’t have much left over to deal with all the mis-INFORMATION we receive on a daily basis!

All our ENERGY needs to focus upon accurate real-time INFORMATION!

### John Schumacher — 2021-12-22T20:16:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chinese-lockdowns-trigger-panic-across-supply-chains-prepare-your-self-for-shortage-of-everything.26725/post-307828>

> [Johan Lindstrøm said:](/goto/post?id=307790)
>
> Thanks. I do not understand many of your biological messages. They're flying over my head. Good for you. I'm miles behind you in biology knowledge. What have you done to get such a high and tidy level?

Thank you - [@Johan Lindstrøm](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22853/) - From your posts, I see you've been into this health thing for a while now as well. I love your workout room pictures!

I has never a smart kid like Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - who has a photographic memory.

I was born with retardation. I failed kindergarten and first grade. The only reason I advanced through the grades was because I was a “good” kid, trying my best. Each year from grade school through junior high, I found myself a different school. My mother was an alcoholic and cycled through four husbands during her life. I was also physically uncoordinated; I could not through, hit nor catch a ball. I ran so slow the PE teacher asked if I was pulling an anchor. It felt like it. I was poor at everything academic – reading, writing, math and science. I entered remedial classes beginning at Junior Community College(s) since I scored below 3rd grade level for reading and writing. My academic goal was to be able to read and write at a college level. Using my health practices to assist in this effort, I completed College English, Calculus, some Physics; then overtime, since I could only afford a few classes per semester, I finally graduated with my BS in Computer Science thirteen years later.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/)​

Since my grandparents _(for which we get our longevity genes mRNA from)_ had died in their thirties and early forties, my wife and I decided to live on less so I could retire, spend sometime together before I walk through the exist; _no one has made it out of here alive_. So, September 2020, I ending my second career which was in "Data Processing".

Now I'm pursuing the education for my third profession - that is to become a Health Practitioner. Of course, I don't have any of the pre-med courses. So, I've started a self study of chemistry and biology in Sept 2020. I found right away that it frustrated me greatly. It seemed "so wrong"; but I didn't know why. So I've been studying Plasma Physics, and Electrical Engineering to augment my studies in chemistry and biology. It has really helped. Now, I have a better idea why I was so frustrated with chemistry and biology - _how these were "half right"_.

From a biochemical perspective, we _(our current understanding of things)_ are like "Flat-Earth" thinkers still stuck in 100 year old mentality about Quantum Physics & Biology. It is my hope, we will be able to engage the mind of Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) to think beyond the current paradigm. He has done a great job so far!

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...is-it-wrapped-in-our-human-environment.25380/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-health-a-supplementation-of-multiple-interventions-or-is-it-wrapped-in-our-human-environment.25380/)
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...entality-about-quantum-physics-biology.26715/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/will-we-ever-develop-past-our-flat-earth-100-year-old-mentality-about-quantum-physics-biology.26715/)

We want to be able to describe things like: How do our hormones work? -> _from a light-water-magnetism perspective_
