# Thread: Windchill vs. cold water - can it be effective and safe?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windchill-vs-cold-water-can-it-be-effective-and-safe.27272/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2022-10-25T23:10:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windchill-vs-cold-water-can-it-be-effective-and-safe.27272/post-316905>

I ride by roadbike to work. In the summer months this can be a heat issue (I experienced that at 37°C). Now its autumn and it already has got really cold like 2-3°C on some mornings here. Because I create much heat while driving I couldnt stand long bike bibs and long arm shirt so Ive done it mostly short-short. I recently switched to a compression shirt for the low temperature and found it very pleasing. I noticed after 25 - 30min (which is my time to work) I have quite red legs and also red chest/stomachs with a little numbness, so its like doing CT with water.
Now is the question how effective CT this way can be if we compare it to water, when we have a strong windchill factor. And can it be even more dangerous than using water?
Because riding a bike with a certain speed is like having a wind with the same speed against me, more heat is taking away and the conduction must be much greater than in air without movement.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I seems windchill lowers the temperature not so much, but how it effects the conduction? Basically water is 24 times more effective than moveless air. But was if air moves rapidly or something is moving through air? I ride 25-30 km/h with my roadbike. My red skin is telling me something is going on but quantifying it would be interesting.

### Marko Pollo — 2022-10-26T18:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windchill-vs-cold-water-can-it-be-effective-and-safe.27272/post-316934>

I wonder if you need beware of frostbite. While the air might not remove heat from the body core as fast as a liquid, the actual temp at the upper epidermis and in the extremities maybe pretty extreme. Last week it was 75F and I was riding 50mph shirtless, now it is around freezing and I wear mittens, tuque and parka and am keeping it under 20mph. I am not generating heat like you as I'm on a motorcycle. Ride safely!

### Butters — 2022-10-27T09:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windchill-vs-cold-water-can-it-be-effective-and-safe.27272/post-316961>

For now we're talking only until ground freezes. So far it should be ok for that time. Eventually I will switch to one layer long. When ground freezes it's to dangerous for me because I can't use spikes.
But I would like to know how the effectiveness changes with air flow. I already noticed hand blood flow change. Starting without gloves it gets warm in the middle of the ride with blood flowing more into the hands.

### Butters — 2022-11-26T09:53:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windchill-vs-cold-water-can-it-be-effective-and-safe.27272/post-317872>

I have to say since I was riding bike outside, mainly to work, I havent had a big cold for months. So its the 5th months now and I havent been a little sick over the whole time. It was the summer months, with sunshine etc. yes, but I think this is a big plus. I have 1 hour almost every workday that I spend outside more (despite clothes and glasses, because I have to see  ) but thats is 1 hours exposed to the elements with light endurance and sometimes intervall training. And I dont have any EMFs like inside of a car.
So I can say: that is a simple biohack for me this year and I plan to do this everytime when its possible. When I dont do it, I miss it.
And because we have snow on the streets and frozen streets max. 10-20 days per year, I can do this pracitcally year around.

# Thread: How did you land here at jackkruse.com? ....tell us
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-you-land-here-at-jackkruse-com-tell-us.27275/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2022-10-26T18:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-you-land-here-at-jackkruse-com-tell-us.27275/post-316935>

What compelled you to join this group of eccentric folks?

I don't really think we are all that eccentric .....we are the ones who have our sh\*t together.

Right from the beginning - the three legged stool made so much sense to me ....not even a stretch.

Of course - now we have the four legged stool \[bitcoin\] - and that makes even more sense.

I have never been a sheeple and neither has Tony....fate at work I guess. We met 8 years ago and Tony says I changed his life - so not true. He was 90% there.

Together we are stronger but also more vulnerable to each other.

Love and belonging and trust and faith, in whatever form it takes, is so very important for all of us.

We are misfits to our families and friends ...... Hardly anyone understands why we had to leave Oz.
Why we insist on seeing the sunrise and sunset everyday.
Why we need to be near the ocean
Why we wear blue blockers etc etc etc.

Leaving Australia was really hard but necessary.

We often wonder what compelled us to start a new chapter in our life at this age - it would have been so much easier to have stayed put.

Our life together has been a huge adventure - we continue to move forward.
As I see it - that is the most important thing ...to keep dreaming and following our instincts.

I am really proud of us........

# Thread: The Dark Origins of the Davos Great Reset

### Johan Lindøm — 2022-11-02T12:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-dark-origins-of-the-davos-great-reset.27287/post-317210>

_"Important to understand is that there is not one single new or original idea in Klaus Schwab’s so-called Great Reset agenda for the world. Nor is his Fourth Industrial Revolution agenda his or his claim to having invented the notion of Stakeholder Capitalism a product of Schwab._

_Klaus Schwab is little more than a slick PR agent for a global technocratic agenda, a corporatist unity of corporate power with government, including the UN, an agenda whose origins go back to the beginning of the 1970s, and even earlier. The Davos Great reset is merely an updated blueprint for a global dystopian dictatorship under UN control that has been decades in development. The key actors were David Rockefeller and his protégé, Maurice Strong._

In the beginning of the 1970s, there was arguably no one person more influential in world politics than the late David Rockefeller, then largely known as chairman of Chase Manhattan Bank.

**Creating the new paradigm**
At the end of the 1960s and into the early 1970s, the international circles directly tied to David Rockefeller launched a dazzling array of elite organizations and think tanks. These included The Club of Rome; the 1001: A Nature Trust, tied to the World Wildlife Fund (WWF); the Stockholm United Nations Earth Day conference; the MIT-authored study, Limits to Growth; and David Rockefeller’s Trilateral Commission."

### SHANNON SELL — 2022-12-06T16:43:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-look-for-when-purchasing-black-lights.27289/post-318205>

what are you trying to achieve with UV lights? Vitamin D? Circadian biology?

Lights are important but mainly you want to get out and see direct AM sunshine every morning as naked as possible (your skin has receptors too) use screen filters on devices to take out blue light, and use candles/oil lamps at night and blue light blockers + cover your skin from artificial light at night.

Try to have a window cracked during the day too to let light in through a screen or open air and not through glass.

Before using more artificial lights to correct a bad light environment, I would start by mitigating the bad and using the natural light available to you, track labs or how you’re feeling and go from there.

### Marko Pollo — 2022-12-11T18:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-look-for-when-purchasing-black-lights.27289/post-318293>

This is a warning for all light fixtures. I had a short fluorescent blacklight that was in a house we moved into. tested it for magnetic emf and it was in the 1000s of gauss, ten times higher that readings next to microwave or even inside a hydro electric generating plant. believe it was caused by faulty ballast. Worthwhile to check all fixtures from time to time.

### JanSz — 2022-12-11T19:38:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-look-for-when-purchasing-black-lights.27289/post-318297>

Worthwhile to check all fixtures from time to time.

The idea is that we need to have exposure to UVA and UVB
but the man-made sources use AC (alternating current) to power it.
AC creates EMF.

There are two ways to avoid this type of EMF
one can use incandescent light bulbs (problem here is that they emit very small power).

The other way is to use AC into DC converter, I think EMR uses that in their red lights.

Any manmade equipment is always short of ideal, which is sunlight.

And [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) is always unwilling to use B or C solutions if A is the best.

/////////////////

### 5G Canary — 2022-12-13T01:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-look-for-when-purchasing-black-lights.27289/post-318310>

Don’t forget to add people to your list... people are a form of light too! There is a reason friends are number two after the Sun on Jack’s list. Gold at both ends!

Just my rambling thoughts after reading QE #21...

QUANTUM ENGINEERING #21: POLARIZATION OF TISSUES... “The frequency signal in sunlight creates biological truth cells need to function. The frequency noise in man’s light is what distracts cells from their biological potential. Man made light creates lies, and these are lies in timing. This affects how the eye clock measures time. If you say you don’t have time to do things correctly, what makes you think you’ll have time to do it over?”

I tend to be super sensitive to everything in my environment. When I became light sensitive I realized that I grew up like a moth drawn to the flame. The flame being man made light. And I had to learn how to reconnect with nature’s light.

I feel like we have been so badly lied too about man made light that we now can’t comprehend these aren’t just bad but they are also used as programming weapons against us. Most of us are no different than “the moth” with our light choices- Shakespeare’s “moth to the flame” was truth in plain sight. They/we fly straight into the fire. The only way to protect ourselves is by learning the truth. But truth only protects us if we choose too act on it.

Moths are supposed to use stars to navigate through the dark but they are easily distracted by man made lights. Nature wasn’t meant to have all these bright lights at night... the moths get lost and disoriented. They get so confused that they become addicted to mans lights unknowingly losing the connection to nature. If you’ve ever watched them... they will repeatedly bang their heads into the light bulb until they die. They unknowingly cut their time short never being able to reconnect with the stars and nature as intended. They never see the truth because they are stuck in the lie. Are we not doing the same thing as the moths if we don’t act? It’s time to find our light in nature and shine it on others before it’s too late.

Want to save a moth turn off the light!

This is actually truth in plain sight... deceptively clever too!

### ekarge — 2023-07-08T03:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-look-for-when-purchasing-black-lights.27289/post-323416>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=323385)
>
> Why would someone be seeking to do this?

Potentially for shift workers who are trying to get back on a normal schedule/normalize their circadian rhythm. Or similarly, in my case, trying to normalize my circadian rhythm by simply going to bed earlier and waking up earlier after an unhealthy long stretch (years) of going to bed super late/sleeping in super late. Without going into too much detail with my n=1, due to depleted dopamine and high adrenaline, my best/deepest sleep occurs after my dawn phenomenon hits in the morning (debatably somogyi effect at times). By using a sunrise lamp (Hatch is good brand) I have the light automatically pop on for 5 minutes at 4:45am (roughly an hour and half before sunrise) to try and phase advance my internal clock and get the dawn phenomenon to hit a little earlier for more deeper morning sleep. Huberman talks about a particular study in one of his podcasts on light, where parents diabolically flicked on the lights early in the morning (when it's still dark) for a period of time and it ultimately led to kids going to bed earlier and getting more sleep. While of course there's no replacement for actual morning sunlight, mimicking the warm wavelengths of light could ostensibly help a circadian phase advance. He also talks about breakfast (as does Kruse), exercise, and increasing internal body temp (via cold plunge/CT) as other methods to help phase advance.

Huberman talks about a particular study in one of his podcasts on light, where parents diabolically flicked on the lights early in the morning (when it's still dark) for a period of time and it ultimately led to kids going to bed earlier and getting more sleep. He also talks about breakfast (as does Kruse), exercise, and increasing internal body temp (via cold plunge/CT) as other methods to help phase advance.

DUTCHtest.com

### [DUTCH Plus® - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/product/dutch-plus/)

The DUTCH Plus® takes hormone testing to a new level. In addition to sex hormones and their metabolites, the DUTCH Complete™ looks at the overall diurnal pattern of free cortisol, and the total and distribution of cortisol metabolites. The DUTCH Plus® adds the Cortisol Awakening Response (CAR)...

 dutchtest.com

[https://dutchtest.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Sample-Report\_DUTCH-Plus\_Female\_RevB1-062022.pdf](https://dutchtest.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Sample-Report_DUTCH-Plus_Female_RevB1-062022.pdf)

Deals with this problem testing The Cortisol Awakening Response (CAR)
Then, in their freely available webinars they advise how to make corrective actions.

**The Cortisol Awakening Response (CAR)** is the rise in salivary cortisol between the waking sample and the sample collected 30 (as well as 60) minutes later. This "awakening response" is essentially a "mini stress test" and is a useful measurement in addition to the overall up-and-down (diurnal) pattern of free cortisol throughout the day

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-08T18:39:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-look-for-when-purchasing-black-lights.27289/post-323437>

I do remember Huberman talking about phase advancing circadian rhythm now.

Ultimately you gotta do what you gotta do.

Just so long as ppl realize there is no replacement for the sun when it comes to circadian entrainment. Artificial "circadian" lights don't even come close and should always be used as an absolute last resort.

### ekarge — 2023-07-08T23:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-look-for-when-purchasing-black-lights.27289/post-323453>

I wouldn't say last resort as much as simply a secondary supplemental tool in my case, as I get as much natural sun as possible, but that's just semantical. I wholeheartedly agree with the sentiment that sun is king and absolutely irreplaceable. But the fact that artificial UV blacklight can make meaningful contributions to human health in darkness, and that we can grow healthy plants without a modicum of natural sunlight is a testament to the potency of artificial light (for better or more often for worse) and I think is a fascinating human paradigm.

### Marko Pollo — 2022-11-10T00:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pemmican.27290/post-317463>

Great article on pemmican, Dan. I don't think people realize how little carbs or fat were available to the plains Indians. the amount of fat on a wild duck,goose, deer, elk, bison is nothing like a corn fed pig or cow. Martha, I remember when I first went KETO. I had read Phinney & Vollick but was just starting to believe. I went on a long, hard hike, probably 12 miles with 5000 feet elevation gain. I took some bananas and apples just in case I crashed. Never touched them. I still remember the elation I felt the last couple of miles. I was high stepping and felt the best I'd felt in forever. Remember that if you are going low carb, you don't have to force feed yourself constantly and one of your super powers is the ability to fast without turning into a crying baby. When travelling I like to carry pocket steaks, lamb chops or bacon, precooked, sliced thin and wrapped in plastic. Keeps me from having to compromise. Good luck!

### Dan2 — 2022-11-12T01:36:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pemmican.27290/post-317531>

Cocoa butter might be good for making pemmican with.

For texture, cocoa butter instead of tallow would stay solid at higher temperatures, and so it might fix mushy pemmican.

And it has about 35% stearic acid, compared to about 15% in tallow. The stearic acid apparently stimulates metabolism better than other fatty acids common in foods that are shorter-chain and saturated or longer-chain and unsaturated:

[http://commonsenseketogenics.com/what-fats-to-eat-to-increase-your-mitochondria-metabolic-rate/](http://commonsenseketogenics.com/what-fats-to-eat-to-increase-your-mitochondria-metabolic-rate/)

From that article:
"When fats are broken down for use in the mitochondria they are broken apart at carbon bonds. The longer the chain of carbon bonds the more high energy electrons they create. These high energy electrons enter the Electron Transport Chain of the mitochondria and as they move through it there is one part that has a limit of electrons it can accept. If they carbon chain is long enough it will provide enough electrons to hit the limiter in the cell and at that point it bounces back anything else. This means no more energy can enter the cell and it cannot continue to grow (fat cells for example). This also means that the body has excess electrons to deal with. The length of the chain that seems to do this is 16 carbons long. The fatty acid Palmitic acid has 16 carbons and is referred to as C16. Stearic acid has 18 carbons and is referred to as C18. Stearic acid would be better for this process as it has more carbons and will create more of the high energy electrons needed to overcome the cell and pop off the signalling molecules.

These electrons that get kicked out of the cell function as signalling molecules to increase mitochondria to better deal with the excess. This is how you increase metabolism.

Note that this only works with saturated fats as unsaturated fats have additional hydrogen bonds in the chain so it stops the process of creating the high energy electrons and the limit is never reached in the Electron Transport chain and you never create the signalling molecules needed to create more mitochondria. An example of this is Palmitoleic acid which is a 16 carbon monounsaturated fatty acid. This means it has 16 carbons but it has 1 extra hydrogen bond in the chain. This hydrogen bond breaks the cycle of creating the high energy electrons that go into the mitochondria so it does not create enough to overcome the flow into the mitochondria and create the signalling molecules. Dr. Eades goes over this in depth in the video above."

### Inger — 2022-11-12T13:24:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pemmican.27290/post-317537>

Martha,
I have made pemmican many times, its super easy

Just slice (pure muscle meat without fat) meat thinly and dry it in a dehydrator on about 40 degree C. Then grind it in a mixer to flour.

Then take your fat of choice and mix with it. Put it in muffin forms or other container whatever you like. Ready

I have used rendered beef fat, or melted coconut oil. Bet you could use butter too. For emergency use it is great!
I also just dry thin slices of beef in my dehydrator on low heat, and use that to dip into butter with or whatever other fat you have available on your trip. I have even used olive oil

Dried meat is wonderful to take on trips, you dont need a fridge and it takes very little space and weight almost nothing

I dont eat it at home though. Way too easy to over eat on because it is soooo good

# Thread: In the dark about light and sight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-the-dark-about-light-and-sight.27291/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2022-11-09T00:37:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-the-dark-about-light-and-sight.27291/post-317416>

My family calls our home the Red Light District but i insist on using Red Light Bulbs
I am using True Dark light bulbs [https://truedark.com/shop/truelight-led-light-therapy/luna-red-sunset-bulb/](https://truedark.com/shop/truelight-led-light-therapy/luna-red-sunset-bulb/) and amber salt lamps through out the house.
I've been wearing these [Twilights](https://truedark.com/shop/twilights/twilights-classic/) blue blockers the fit overs in the late afternoon and the classic style at night [https://truedark.com/product-category/glasses/twilights/](https://truedark.com/product-category/glasses/twilights/)

I had an eye exam and was told I have cataracts last July. We were all sick with The Flu the whole month of August but since then I've had hearing loss and stinging lower back pain. Those two issues were uncommon to me and while not gone they are somewhat better but now my eye's hurt and I am having accelerated vision decline. I am still getting up for the sunrise but with the time change last weekend 11 6 22 I'm experiencing SAD and concerned with my vision. Last 3 days sad about not naturally getting up in time for exact sunrise like I had been.

I'm wondering if I'm doing harm using the wrong light bulbs or glasses ?

I've been cutting out tons of supplements but still take [Astaxanthin](https://draxe.com/nutrition/astaxanthin-benefits/) for vision health. Have used Brite Eyes III eye drops by Life Extension [https://www.lifeextension.com/vitam...tm\_term=4583382968809908&utm\_content=Ad+group](https://www.lifeextension.com/vitamins-supplements/item00893/brite-eyes-iii?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=LEF-SHOP-BING-SPAG-EN-SMART-EyeSupport&utm_term=4583382968809908&utm_content=Ad+group)a few times. I'd tried it about 7 years ago but it was so uncomfortable to use I returned it...did not have a reaction using the drops in the morning.

Did have a great early Oct highlight with a week at the beach when I felt wonderful!!! but the recovery benefit must be fading as I'm SAD now.
Woe is me...will be better tomorrow.

cataracts
\---------------------------
Make (duble or triple) sure that you have checked pressure in your eyeballs (glaucoma).
I am finding that many ophthalmologists neglect checking that.
But if one asks for that, they gladly do the work. (How nice).
Recently (couple weeks), my girlfriend's brother almost lost his eye, because of it.
By then his doc was able to a complicated surgery and (likely) saved his eye, (how f\*\*n nice).
\----------------------------
I had my cataracts removed and lenses installed. (And do not feel bad about it.)

\--------

I am still getting up for the sunrise but with the time change last weekend 11 6 22 I'm experiencing SAD and concerned with my vision. >
> I'm wondering if I'm doing harm using the wrong light bulbs or glasses ?
>
> I've been cutting out tons of supplements but still take [Astaxanthin](https://draxe.com/nutrition/astaxanthin-benefits/) for vision health. Have used Brite Eyes III eye drops by Life Extension [https://www.lifeextension.com/vitam...tm\_term=4583382968809908&utm\_content=Ad+group](https://www.lifeextension.com/vitamins-supplements/item00893/brite-eyes-iii?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=LEF-SHOP-BING-SPAG-EN-SMART-EyeSupport&utm_term=4583382968809908&utm_content=Ad+group)a few times. I'd tried it about 7 years ago but it was so uncomfortable to use I returned it...did not have a reaction using the drops in the morning.
>
> Did have a great early Oct highlight with a week at the beach when I felt wonderful!!! but the recovery benefit must be fading as I'm SAD now.
> Woe is me...will be better tomorrow.

There's risks to using unopposed red lights chronically, because visible red light increases IOP, and even if it has a source of IR, its never on its own with natural sunlight, the balancer for red in IOP is green. I also don't think "same" lets the pupils act normally by responding to _changes_ in a natural environment, (like your week at the beach). As a signal, for me blue light is not the enemy if its in the right amount within the recipe, and at the right time. Blue light has been used for treating SAD, so your indoor light set up may be contributing to deficiency as outside levels seasonally drop.

After a scare earlier in the year, I've just had to have a retina checked, which involved drops to paralyse the pupil and bright light .... Normally it leaves my eyes feeling traumatised and frightens me about what damage the test itself is causing, both the intensity and the blue, but I hacked spirulina and chlorella during the 3 week notice I had. If you google spirulina or chlorella and either eye health or specific eye diseases, there is a lot of interesting stuff, albeit either in culture or animal models.

This is one

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6871545/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6871545/)
**Dietary Spirulina Supplementation Protects Visual Function From Photostress by Suppressing Retinal Neurodegeneration in Mice
**
**T**he interesting thing is the 20 per cent of diet supplementation, but that seems to be what was tested, it may not be necessary to use such a high amount. So I took it to bowel tolerance, and on the day of the test took a handful of spirulina and chlorella tablets before and after the test, fasted apart from that. It was a completely different experience to last time. When I got home I rested my eyes in a darkened room whilst lying on a grounding mat connected outside for a couple of hours, though I didn't feel I needed to IYKWIM. I also protected my eyes until the drops used to keep the pupil open wore off. But that was it. No discomfort. The best part is that the hospital doesn't need to see me again ....

I haven't seen the full paper but this is an abstract of a study on phycocyanin

[https://www.researchgate.net/public...\_of\_Cataract\_Preventive\_Action\_of\_Phycocyanin](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/281335404_Evaluation_of_Cataract_Preventive_Action_of_Phycocyanin)

In the natural world wading birds needing to protect their eyes from the reflection of light off water are eating the whole algae, often in large quantities, high in either phycocyanins or chlorophyll as well as other possible useful compounds. So any supplement that focuses on one or even a few nutrients may be missing a trick ...

# Thread: Magnetic Underlays in the Southern Hemisphere
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-underlays-in-the-southern-hemisphere.27299/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### StevieJ — 2022-11-13T01:07:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-underlays-in-the-southern-hemisphere.27299/post-317553>

Hi all,

Looking into magnetic underlays and trying to find the best value underlay that still works. Magnetico seem the best, however they are very expensive including costly international postage due to their weight.

Has anyone owned for over a year either a Magnetic Experience underlay (Australian made 3000gauss) or Biomag underlay (New Zealand made 1000gauss)? Are your experiences good and would you recommend the product?

I understand that some underlays cause imbalance long-term, whilst initially feeling beneficial. Hence, owning an underlay for over a year and still feeling benefit seems like a good tell that it's working.

Feedback is appreciated, thanks.
Steve

### Martha Ray — 2022-11-14T06:46:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-underlays-in-the-southern-hemisphere.27299/post-317572>

I've wanted a _**Magnetico Sleeping Pad**_ for some time and saw your post.
My concerns are that I am sensitive to nickel plus a whole lot of other things and I am not sure if I would react to a magnetic pad and my husband has been in remission from Afib for 9 months now and I do not know if it might set him back out of rhythm. He has no devices, no pacemaker etc. Also our king bed is complicated. It is very heavy and very high. From the bottom of our slat foundation then through the **_AVANA_** _7" LIFT Under Mattress Incline_ _bed therapy foam WEDGE_, to the top _**Cozy Pure**_ dunlop latex mattress... measuring at the head of the bed is 21" The two twin slat foundations have about 10" legs under them at the corners and in the middle so the **overall height is about 31"**. Not at all sure where the _Magnetico_ would be best positioned to not be too far away or if it would be too heavy on top of the slats.

I have a mind to take the legs off the foundation so that the bed would be be closer to the concrete slab to see if that would work best for now. But the heavy latex bed may need bottom air circulation.

The 3 _Magnetico_ choices for the strengths each adding more expense adds even more complexity.

**I would like to know more about the possibility of underlays causing imbalance long term.**

Late now going to enjoy my current bed set up.

# Thread: Wedges under Mattress to elevate head?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wedges-under-mattress-to-elevate-head.27302/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nintendo 1889 — 2022-11-13T18:48:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wedges-under-mattress-to-elevate-head.27302/post-317562>

I always sleep with my mouth open even though I have eliminated 90% of dairy for my diet, use breathe right strips, nose snoring clips that press the acupoint inside the nose (Google it), and use mouth tape (I have used many different kinds and covered my mouth many different ways and even try cutting a small slit in the tape. I almost always rip off the tape in the middle of the night).

Symptoms: ADD, low t, hair loss, cavities for no reason, 16 hypothyroid.symtoms, 11 iodine deficiency symptoms. Have never been a morning person.

So the newest hack I read about on the lowcarber.com forums is elevating the head of your mattress. It's even being promoted as a way to eliminate SIDS.

My problem is that my bed is too heavy to be able to elevate with corner raising blocks or cinder blocks. Should I get one of those full length wedges that goes under the mattress or a half length wedge that is put over the mattress, on the head/upper side? I'm even considering getting Dr Peter Martone's neck nest, since I do have spine issues ever since the age of 12 - an old playground injury that was never corrected.

I almost always rip off the tape in the middle of the night).

Should I get one of **those full length wedges** that goes under the mattress or a half length wedge that is put over the mattress, on the head/upper side? I'm even considering getting Dr Peter Martone's neck nest, since I do have spine issues ever since the age of 12 - an old playground injury that was never corrected.

> [Nintendo 1889 said:](/goto/post?id=317562)
>
> I always sleep with my mouth open even though I have eliminated 90% of dairy for my diet, use breathe right strips, nose snoring clips that press the acupoint inside the nose (Google it), and use **mouth tape** (I have used many different kinds and covered my mouth many different ways and even try cutting a small slit in the tape. I almost always rip off the tape in the middle of the night).
> Symptoms: ADD, low t, hair loss, cavities for no reason, 16 hypothyroid symtoms, 11 iodine deficiency symptoms. Have never been a morning person.
> So the newest hack I read about on the lowcarber.com forums is elevating the head of your mattress. It's even being promoted as a way to eliminate SIDS.
> My problem is that my bed is too heavy to be able to elevate with corner raising blocks or cinder blocks. Should I get one of those full length wedges that goes under the mattress or a half length wedge that is put over the mattress, on the head/upper side? I'm even considering getting Dr Peter Martone's neck nest, since I do have spine issues ever since the age of 12 - an old playground injury that was never corrected.

RE: Mouth tape **Hostage Mouth Tape** [https://hostagetape.com/](https://hostagetape.com/) is strongest I've tried. The product is really great (But personally I did not like it that the _after sales pitch_ tricked me into over buying.) while it is a very comfortable product my favorite is **SomniFix Mouth Strips** [**https://somnifix.com/products/mouth-strips-snoring-sleep-aids**](https://somnifix.com/products/mouth-strips-snoring-sleep-aids) They have a built in slit. I've been mouth tapping everynight for 5 years. Great improvement in energy right away.

RE: Full length Wedge
**_Avana_** has a reasonable wedge they call it:
**_LIFT Under Mattress Gentle Incline Elevator_**
[https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html](https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html)
_W_e have two 7" (but they also sell a 5") with the additional canvas cotton covers and I have an additional bottom sheets as a double cover over them. I am very sensitive and was concerned about product being made of high-density polyurethane foam. I aired out both very well even though neither had any smell right out of the box. We have been happy with both only wish we had tried the 5" on the master bed because our bed was pretty high to begin with...hope to change that up in the future after we remodel to get closer to the slab. The Magnetico Sleeping Pad is still on my want list.

I know of at least two other threads covering bedding.
**Lets talk about optimal beds**
Discussion in '[The New Monster Thread](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/the-new-monster-thread.23/)' started by [freshveggies](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/freshveggies.25/), [Mar 7, 2013](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/).
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/)
And
**Elevation of Bed Suggestion**
Discussion in '[Feedback/Suggestions](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/feedback-suggestions.20/)' started by [Martha Ray](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/martha-ray.22506/), [Oct 7, 2019](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/elevation-of-bed-suggestion.24332/).
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/elevation-of-bed-suggestion.24332/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/elevation-of-bed-suggestion.24332/)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### 5G Canary — 2022-11-21T17:01:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.27315/post-317740>

> [Kari Dunlop said:](/goto/post?id=317723)
>
> Curious if anyone has found anything in addition to the sun and red light therapy helpful for reversing hair loss?

The body does what it needs to do to survive a bad environment. Hair acts as an electromagnetic antenna. In a bad environment your body will ditch the hair/antenna. Trying to grow it back in a bad environment isn’t always a good idea. Your body is part of nature and wasn’t made to connect with all these alien signals.

Years ago when I had no clue what our environment was doing to our health I observed strange things... my husband had a wifi router on the floor under his desk and as a result he lost all the hair on the front of his lower legs next to the router. He used to wear a wireless headset and lost a patch of hair in his sideburn along with a nice large lump. We both experienced hair loss but for a man society has completely normalized it. I have ridiculously thick hair so doctors never took my concerns very seriously. My youngest daughter who also has very thick hair was little at the time and had a smart meter behind her bed... she used to actually pull her hair out at night.

Eventually becoming very sick I learned the hard way what a bad environment can do. We quickly then moved to a better environment... the hair not only came back but it came back thicker, healthier and it grows very fast again. We didn’t use a single product... just a better environment and a better connection with Mother Nature. It’s really all about reconnecting with nature.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2023-01-03T15:05:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.27315/post-318669>

Working on the same thing, but I did have hair transplant surgery

# Thread: Low Histamine Bone Broth to brake fast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-histamine-bone-broth-to-brake-fast.27334/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2022-11-29T20:07:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-histamine-bone-broth-to-brake-fast.27334/post-317976>

**Anyone here have a recipe for Low Histamine Bone Broth?**

I'd been trying to find one from '[Angi Fletcher](https://angi5.com/change-starts-here)' but not able to locate it yet.
I am **new to _Instapot_** and have only used it about 5 times so far for steaming veggies, stews and wild rice. It is a 6 QT _Instapot_\- not sure it that is going to be too small....I need a recipe of 'how to'. I have lots of well raised large animal bones in my freezer waiting on me to try something.

Due to my histamine intolerance flare I may not even be able to do the Bone Broth but might be able to use very fresh frozen Fish, Beef, or chicken for broth from those. I've wanted to make bone broth to get those large bones out of my freezer and want broth to drink for one day after water fasting to break the fast.

I got the bones last month before my histamine bucket overflowed recently.

Maybe I should just fast and brake with greens and lettuces.

You just simmer the meat and bones for a short amount of time.
There are lots of advise on line about eating and cooking for low histamine.

**Thank you!**

**?So back to DGL Mastic Gum, L-Glutamine, cabbage juice, slippery Elm grul + about 5 or more remedies I used in the past for 6 months to a year?**
**I've not gone back to work on my gut because this histamine thing comes and goes. I just happen to be having a spell of it now. Also I do have some genetic predisposition to it... will have to dig out my notes on that and on healing the mucosal lining.**

**What do you suggest?**

### caroline — 2022-12-03T02:12:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-histamine-bone-broth-to-brake-fast.27334/post-318107>

Really watch what foods you are eating.

When we came to El Salvador we were in an airb&b for about 3 months. We didn't have proper cooking facilities so we were eating a lot of the local wonderful tomatoes and pineapples and mangoes and avocados and bananas and ham and salamis etc etc.

All high histamine foods - my bucket definitely overflowed!

All of these things that I mentioned above I rarely ate in Australia.....I ate mostly protein and very limited veg.....usually a little carrot or sweet potatoes or mushroom.

I didn't know I had a histamine problem until we moved here.

Looking back over the years I can now see that I did have a histamine problem .....way way to much cheese and my feet and tummy would swell. UGH

At one point I spent a fortune on probiotics trying to fix this - but they never helped except I had the runs all the time.

### JanSz — 2022-12-12T19:47:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-you-consider-these-free-t3-results-low.27357/post-318307>

When supplementing with thyroid hormones
be aware of your heartbeat at rest.
At certain point the resting heartbeat maybe too fast.
Not sure of the number, but definitely 100 and over is too high.
Good heartbeat is somewhere 50 - 80.

Lack of potassium tend to raise heartbeat.
\=========================================
Get double decimal digit, thermometer.
keep it within your reach next to your bed.
When you wake up, do not move, be still, get the thermometer.
Put it under your tongue, deep in your mouth.
You are ok with your thyroid FT3 if you read within
36.6C and 37C

35.6 C=97.88 F
37 C=98.6 F

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nintendo 1889 — 2022-12-23T22:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-outdoors-on-a-hill-with-a-system-of-mirrors-shining-on-my-taint.27369/post-318503>

Like the guy from Arizona who built the moonlight collector, I want to build something like this ([https://www.reuters.com/article/us-...arizona-light-collector-idUSN0463343020071205](https://www.reuters.com/article/us-moonbeams/moonstruck-flock-to-arizona-light-collector-idUSN0463343020071205)) but on top of a hill and exclusively for waking directly in the morning sun - but then how would I be able to keep moonlight from making me a LUNAtic? **Would the glass would have to be Quartz to get the full spectrum of visible and invisible light or would it work with regular glass, since the reflective material is behind the glass?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**

I need to study the angles of the moonlight and sunlight and thankfully there are AR apps that make it easy and the angles at different dates. If you are camping out and have an android "sun's path" is the one I use.

I suppose I could start simple and just have a basic platform on a hill and a tent on a platform and then build it from there.

My thesis is that the morning light is the most healing, and does no damage. Would it be safe to receive concentrated amounts of morning light? I would be building a low tech version of the powerful near/far IR device that Jack has in his clinic.

I would also sleep nude in the dirt on top of a hill where this light would hit me from both the sun and the mirrors (obviously I would burn the dirt and/or add potash to sanitize it and have a metal barrier for the worms etc...) but still have a grounding cable.

Another thought was building a faraday cage to sleep in. I asked Jack on clubhouse about this and he said that there's a stanford study where people went crazy being buried underground (but I would prefer to sleep outdoors, maybe on a hill or platform for maximum sunlight\[1\]) - also if EMF ever reached my residence/healing center I could build a metal box that blocks most RF in most directions but still be in the dirt. I asked Jack if it is because a faraday cage disconnects us from the schumann resonance and he said yes.

I'm **seriously** considering moving to **El Salvador** and am trying to save enough to have a portable house/RV etc, and do whatever I want since there's little regulation on starting a business: sell keto coffee on the beach (yes DA is a jerk). rent out jetskis, build a health retreat, make a farm, teach bitcoin to anyone, build a bitcoin mine/miner hosting/wireless isp, help to wire up bitcoin city, etc.... anything to get away from modern life. It sucks that I work in computers and IT, but health is my true passion, I just happen to be good at computers. Plus with the abundant virgin volcanic soil and mostly agrarian society I'll fit right in as an anti-EMF and offgridder.

# Thread: A cautionary tale of Vitamin A
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-cautionary-tale-of-vitamin-a.27404/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Warren Canavan — 2023-01-15T14:23:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-cautionary-tale-of-vitamin-a.27404/post-318960>

I have been feeling rick sick since having a flu/fever about a month ago. Turns out I am feeling on the mend after taking cod liver oil. I think I had a vitamin A deficinecy, I have been suffering with light sensitivity for a while now. I was taking large dose vitamin D, on a low fat diet doing gerson therapy. It turns out that you need to take vitamin A and D together, taking one will deplete the other. It also turns out we don't really convert beta carotene into vitamin A, especially when not taking fat at the same time. So don't listen to people who say eating carrots is enough for your vitamin A. Get extra virgin cod liver oil that has its vitamin A and D intact.

Also since taking more healthy fatty foods my teeth have been yellowing less. You need A, D and K together in whole foods, liver and cod liver oil being one of the best. I don't think butter cuts it because they are fed hay which has less nutrition in winter.

[https://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/abcs-of-nutrition/vitamin-a-saga/#gsc.tab=0](https://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/abcs-of-nutrition/vitamin-a-saga/#gsc.tab=0)

tells calcium where to go, bones or cardiovascular surfaces.
Supplementation with K2-MK7 removes calcium from already existing cardiovascular surfaces.
Status and progress can be checked using test:
Coronary CT calcium scan
Calcium Agatston score
This test is not paid by insurance.
It does not need doctor's script.
They do that on request, cost $100

In my neighborhood:
Princeton Radiology
973-696-5770
You can call them asking for other places.

### Warren Canavan — 2023-02-23T16:16:37+0200

> I remember being at a health conference about forty years ago in California and going to a booth that was all about
> Hulda Clarke and her health theories. I looked and listened but didn't think much of it back then. Still can't remember
> exactly what it was about. Think she had a ZAPPER of some sort? Anyways, still amazing how so many ideas come full circle. When the student is ready, the teacher arrives.

Yes basically she says she discovered that rather than a specific frequency a positive current, rather than an alternating current, at a low frequency will nullify alot of bacteria but not human cells , which she says operate at a higher freuquency. This is probably the same effect the sun has with its low frequencies. The rife theory was basically like how glass will shatter when a certain frequency hits it, the same will happen to bacteria; Rife called it a Mortality oscillatory rate (MOR). Its the same idea with engine in cars, they have to be designed so they do not vibrate too much at the frequency of the car engine, it would cause weakeness and potentially distrupt the funcitoning of the car.

However there are theories that bacteria are innocent bystanders, they only consume dead tissue. The underlying problem is cellular toxicity, produced by the chemical industry: DDT, glyphosate, dioxins(the train derailment in ohio). Which also is what Hulda said was causing disease, she mentions benzene, and plastics particularly disrupting the thymus and other organs. The thymus is where our immune cells are produced; a malunctioning immune system allows cancer cells to get out of control. Hulda also proposed cancer as a form of parasitic infection, and them forming cysts in the body.

I have been reading some newer stuff about how plants take bacteria up the root tip, eat some and release some back out to the soil, which then communicate the plant's needs to other bacteria in the soil, encouraging the mining of soil bound nutes and minerals. Communication by light and emf. This is a totally different mechanism than the mycorrhizal fungi pathways. The point I'm making is, bacteria are not just by standers, but active symbionts in all biomes, including our own. We are more of an ecosystem than an individual.
Other point is that glyphosate does not breakdown quickly as claimed and the residue chelates micronutrients and locks them up indefinitely, causing deficiency even when soil tests may show plenty of copper, boron, selenium, moly, etc. Plant tissue and sap analysis show what isn't making it upstream. Foliar feeding can help. Do chemicals like glyphosate bind with nutrients in our bodies, showing up on blood or tissue tests as plentiful, but unavailable and deficient for bodily functions?

# Thread: Harmoni Pendant
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harmoni-pendant.27454/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2023-02-09T18:56:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harmoni-pendant.27454/post-319522>

Hi All,

Does the Harmoni Pendant really work. Is it worth using if it does work.

Thank you

### Billybats — 2023-02-09T18:57:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harmoni-pendant.27454/post-319523>

[https://naturallivingfamily.com/harmoni-pendant-review-personal-emf-protection/](https://naturallivingfamily.com/harmoni-pendant-review-personal-emf-protection/)

The link above talks about it.

### Billybats — 2023-02-09T20:14:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harmoni-pendant.27454/post-319524>

[https://consciouscopper.com/pages/e...cUMCqj0ODijvYnM1GJB4Ek-hbvDPlI6RoC3bYQAvD\_BwE](https://consciouscopper.com/pages/emf-protection-for-home?gclid=CjwKCAiA0JKfBhBIEiwAPhZXD2Nc-hws9Q9paFU5E5LmRMcUMCqj0ODijvYnM1GJB4Ek-hbvDPlI6RoC3bYQAvD_BwE)

Any thoughts on this, link above.

### Sue-UK — 2023-02-10T13:55:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harmoni-pendant.27454/post-319527>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=319523)
>
> [https://naturallivingfamily.com/harmoni-pendant-review-personal-emf-protection/](https://naturallivingfamily.com/harmoni-pendant-review-personal-emf-protection/)
>
> The link above talks about it.

Hi Billybats

The bit from the link that jumped out at me was
_"By improving the body’s energetic communication, the Harmoni Pendant can improve communication efficiency and may clear stressors resulting from energetic blocks in your biofield. **This is the level that human intention and thought works on as well**, as observed and reported in Dr. Bruce Lipton’s book, The Biology of Belief."_

If the pendant is the representation of the human intention and thought of the pendant developers, we kinda accept that intention and thought into our biofields, but theoretically we could use the power of our own intention and thought, without a pendant.

_"when you hold the pendant 12 inches from your body and activate it, the sacred geometry inside communicates with your body to finely tune its frequency. This clears disharmonious frequencies and makes it so that your body is better able to regulate and deal with any stressors that it encounters (including EMF). This amplifies your vibrational blueprint to help reset it back to when it was perfectly tuned, **as the trauma and stressors that have impacted you throughout a lifetime are released."**_

The bit in bold particularly is one hell of a claim  and - if that's what it really does - is not without risk.

When I worked in a complementary therapy clinic, we stocked something similar, after seeing blood analysis slides showing the difference when they were used. But the tests had been using one mobile phone, not the then incoming avalanche of new technology, towers etc. The problem is that they can become lifestyle choices, people may become complacent about testing their environment, not consider the need to move, get sunlight, avoid blue light etc. There is the danger of relying on what a device developer knows, when what they don't know about someone's n=1 may be more important to that person, particularly if they were already sick. It runs the risk of not paying attention to the changes happening in the nnemf environment, or possibly buying more powerful phones etc because the pendant makes us feel "safe". When that purchase pattern started to emerge, and because the testing hadn't been done with multiple sources of exposure simultaneously, the remaining stock was binned, and we switched to info about meters etc.

### Billybats — 2023-02-10T14:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/harmoni-pendant.27454/post-319528>

Hi Sue,
I figured this would be the answer. You make sense as usual . I am currently limited on making changes. I just told hubby we are going to turn off wifi and tv connection at night. He is finally seeing how important this is since having unusual headaches and more. I am also figuring out a different type of job because working on a computer all day is definitely causing these headaches. iIt's very hard to protect ourselves from nnemf.

Thank you Sue.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2023-03-09T00:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-blogs-in-spanish.27502/post-319903>

Does anyone have access to Jack's blogs in Spanish?

We would love to get the leptin reset in Spanish to start with.

We have new friends in El Salvador who are very interested in Jack's teachings.........

### Marko Pollo — 2023-03-09T04:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-blogs-in-spanish.27502/post-319907>

**El milagro metabólico (Spanish Edition) Paperback – Illustrated, October 8, 2019**
Spanish Edition by [Dr. Carlos Jaramillo](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=dp_byline_sr_book_1?ie=UTF8&field-author=Dr.+Carlos+Jaramillo&text=Dr.+Carlos+Jaramillo&sort=relevancerank&search-alias=books) (Author)

This is obviously not jack's book but is the one my wife uses to introduce alternative ideas to her spanish speaking friends and relatives. Creo que es importante a practicar hablando en las cosas que estan interestante y importante a nosotros. Practicing speaking and writing about these topics simply and clearly is a tall order, but doable and rewarding because you learn to converse about things you are passionate about.
Here is a funny story about translation. A person told me this in spanish: Look out the window, 200 hundred years ago a group of llaneros (coyboys from the llana region) rode bareback across those mountains and out numbered and out gunned killed 600 armed spaniards with spears. He asked me did I understand? I said yes, the window. You were talking about the window. This is a two way street and even when both parties are trying to communicate clearly, misunderstanding is a real problem. This is a great impetus to simplify and use common easy words. It is hard to explain this stuff even to an intelligent, english speaker who is interested in the topic.
Buena suerte. Espero que puedas disfrutar todas las cosas diferentes en su vida nueva.

# Thread: What is healthy and unhealthy about laundry?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-healthy-and-unhealthy-about-laundry.27509/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2023-03-13T06:04:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-healthy-and-unhealthy-about-laundry.27509/post-319982>

**From feeling clean, smelling well, clothes care and sun drying to chemical soaps and dryer sheets and balls to dust and lint to EMF what is healthy and unhealthy about doing laundry?**

### JanSz — 2023-03-13T16:04:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-healthy-and-unhealthy-about-laundry.27509/post-319986>

smelling well
\===================
For me one of the best smells is smell of hard-working sweaty horse.
Not so about sweaty ox. No testosterone.
My Saturday dancing socials.
Second or third hour of non-stop dancing.
Each dance with different girl.
Happy, sweaty girls, somewhat intimate touching, beautiful.

.............

### caroline — 2023-03-13T20:27:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-healthy-and-unhealthy-about-laundry.27509/post-319989>

We live in Atami - on the coast of El Salvador.
The native Salvadorans that we see here on a daily basis ....working for us, working on building sites and working on the roads etc are always very clean and really don't have a sweaty smell.
They do very heavy manual labour - a lot done the old fashioned way.
When Tony was pouring concrete - the guys helping him would mix the cement by hand at the top of the driveway and then put it in buckets and lift it to their shoulders and carry it down our fairly long driveway. they did this for hours in the very hot sun. They didn't smell bad.

generally the Salvadorans that live and work around here live in Palmarcito ...which is kind of next door on a river feeding into the ocean.
They don't have washing machines. They go down to the river to wash their clothes and then lay them on the rocks to dry. They are always spotlessly clean.
The family that help take care of us and our home and property wash their clothes this way. There are five in this family ...used to be six - one has now moved to the US for work.
They are always very clean personally and their clothes as well.

Likely water and electricity are at a premium for them as well.

They are building an addition on their home and have invited us to come over when they are done.

We feel responsible for this family and there isn't anything they wouldn't do for us ...at any time day or nite if we need help.

We feel like we have gained a family here of lovely, kind, generous people.

They don't have a lot ...and yet ....they have everything.

### JanSz — 2023-03-13T21:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-healthy-and-unhealthy-about-laundry.27509/post-319991>

This "smelly" topic does not want to end.
I have just got e-mail from Polish doc on the topic of cleanliness.
English translation first.
\================================================
Morning Health Diary
Dr. Bartek Kulczyński Monday, March 13, 2023 ☝
List of mild and irritating ingredients in body washes.
good morning in the morning,
in the Middle Ages it was believed that washing makes one sick.
Ubiquitous dirt and – to put it mildly – unpleasant body odor was the everyday reality of every social group.
It is different now.
There is no longer a stubborn belief that good hygiene causes disease.
Much depends on age
**Excessive attention to hygiene is mainly not beneficial for children who are just developing their immune systems.**
Animal studies have shown that keeping them (animals) in a sterile environment greatly impairs their immunity.
And it's the same with people
The human body during the first years "learns" to coexist with the surrounding microbes. Contact with pathogens, bacteria, viruses causes the immune system to get to know their molecules better and better.
And thanks to this, he learns to recognize their strengths and weaknesses, so that in the next "meeting" with the pathogen, he already knows how to deal with it.
This science of "dealing" with microorganisms mainly consists in changing the immune processes from inflammatory (acute) to regulatory (mild).
Therefore, to a reasonable extent, exposure to germs when it comes to children has a positive effect on immunity in the long term.
It is not recommended to treat their skin with antiseptics.
In the case of adults, this effect is no longer observed, as the immune system is already formed; stimulating it with germs will probably not bring additional benefits in accordance with the saying "frequent washing shortens life."
For adults, it's different Experts say that soaps and "detergents" used for washing are (generally) substances that are very harmful to the skin.
Their use (the longer, the worse) causes changes in the pH of the skin and the content of fatty acids on its surface.
And the right pH and the presence of fatty acids are the main factors ensuring the skin's defensive functions.
Their action also includes reddening of the skin and loss of water from deeper layers.
In short, "detergents" weaken the natural protective barrier of the skin and expose it to the action of microbes and physical factors.
This harmful effect of soap and detergents is particularly visible in people who, due to their profession, wash their hands often, e.g. nurses.
According to statistics, up to 85% of nurses at some point in their professional life have suffered from skin problems due to frequent washing: eczema, irritation, dryness, redness, contact allergy.
It has also been proven that washing with ordinary soap can increase the spread of bacteria on the skin by as much as 17 times - a paradox, because soaps are supposed to do the opposite.
But there is a way Not every soap and detergent is so harmful.
First of all, be careful with anionic and cationic surfactants.
Less harmful are non-ionic detergents and they should be looked for in the composition of washing agents.
To maintain proper skin functions, it is best to avoid such irritating washing substances as: ☒ SLS - Sodium Lauryl Sulfate,
☒ SLES - Sodium Laureth Sulfate,
☒ ALS - Ammonium Lauryl Sulfate,
☒ MLS – Magnesium Laureth Sulfate, ☒ SMS - Sodium Myreth Sulfate.
It is better to replace them with milder washing substances:
☑ Lauryl Glucoside (lauryl glucoside), ☑ Coco-Glucoside (coconut glucoside), ☑ Decyl Glucoside (decyl glucoside),
☑ Cocamidopropyl Betaine (cocamidopropyl betaine),
☑ Sodium Cocoamphoacetate.
These are found in currently popular cosmetics described as "natural" - gentle on the skin. However, you should reject "antibacterial" agents for washing or at least not use them on a daily basis - they are usually irritating.
You already know what to wash with, and now… ...how often to do this?
Well, there is no clear answer to this and you have to be reasonable - neither washing your body too often, nor neglecting hygiene is good.
Studies indicate that the amount and type of bacteria on the skin is similar both before and after bathing.
Washing the whole body once a day is enough for us.
Hands should be washed with a mild detergent several times a day.
But there should always be a reason for washing your hands, e.g. after contact with a sick person, with an animal, with a shopping cart or after using the toilet.
I have strayed a bit from the main topic: diet.
Please let me know if it was of value to you, I read every e-mail.
On the other hand, the skin is also an organ, it should be properly taken care of.
\================================================
\================================================
**Poranny Dziennik Zdrowia**
doktora Bartka Kulczyńskiego

poniedziałek, 13 mar 2023

☝ Lista łagodnych i drażniących składników w środkach myjących do ciała.

_Dzień dobry o poranku,_

w średniowieczu wierzono, że mycie się wpędza w choroby.

Wszechobecny brud i – łagodnie określając – nieprzyjemny zapach ciała był codziennością każdej grupy społecznej.

Obecnie już jest inaczej. Nie wierzy już się uparcie w to, że dbanie o higienę powoduje choroby.

**Wiele zależy od wieku**
Przesadne dbanie o higienę głównie nie jest korzystne dla dzieci, które dopiero rozwijają swój układ odpornościowy.

Badania na zwierzętach wykazały, że trzymanie ich (zwierząt) w sterylnym środowisku mocno upośledza ich odporność.

**I podobnie jest z ludźmi**
Organizm człowieka przez pierwsze lata „uczy się” współistnieć z otaczającymi go drobnoustrojami.

Kontakt z patogenami, bakteriami, wirusami powoduje, że układ odpornościowy poznaje ich cząsteczki coraz lepiej. A przez to uczy się on rozpoznawania ich mocnych oraz słabych stron, przez co w kolejnym „spotkaniu” z patogenem już wie, jak sobie z nim radzić.

Ta nauka „radzenia sobie” z mikroorganizmami głównie polega na zamianie procesów odpornościowych z tych zapalnych (ostrych) na te regulatorowe (łagodne).

Dlatego do pewnego rozsądnego stopnia kontakt z zarazkami, jeśli chodzi o dzieci, pozytywnie przekłada się na odporność w dłuższej perspektywie. Nie zaleca się traktować ich skóry antyseptykami.

W przypadku dorosłych nie obserwuje się już raczej tego efektu, gdyż układ odpornościowy jest już ukształtowany; bodźcowanie go zarazkami zapewne nie wniesie już dodatkowych korzyści w myśl powiedzenia „częste mycie skraca życie”.

**U dorosłych co innego się liczy**
Eksperci twierdzą, że mydła oraz „detergenty” służące do mycia stanowią (uogólniając) substancje bardzo szkodliwe dla skóry.

Ich używanie (im dłużej, tym gorzej) powoduje zmiany w pH skóry oraz zawartości kwasów tłuszczowych na jej powierzchni.

A odpowiednie pH oraz obecność kwasów tłuszczowych to główne czynniki zapewniające funkcje obronne skóry. Ich działanie obejmuje także zaczerwienianie skóry i utratę wody z głębszych warstw.

Słowem, „detergenty” osłabiają naturalną barierę ochronną skóry i narażają ją na działanie drobnoustrojów i czynników fizycznych.

To szkodliwe działanie mydła i detergentów szczególnie widać po osobach, które z uwagi na zawód często myją ręce, np. pielęgniarki.

Według statystyk do 85% pielęgniarek w którymś momencie życia zawodowego miało problemy skórne przez częste mycie: egzema, podrażnienie, przesuszenie, zaczerwienienie, alergia kontaktowa.

Dowiedziono też, że mycie zwykłym mydłem może aż 17-krotnie zwiększać rozprzestrzenianie się bakterii na skórze – paradoks, bo przecież mydła mają działać wręcz odwrotnie.

**Ale jest na to sposób**
Nie każde mydło i środek myjący tak szkodzi.

Przede wszystkim, należy uważać na anionowe i kationowe środki powierzchniowo czynne. Mniej szkodliwe są niejonowe detergenty i takich właśnie należy szukać w składach środków do mycia.

Dla utrzymania prawidłowych funkcji skóry najlepiej unikać takich drażniących skórę substancji myjących jak:

☒ SLS – Sodium Lauryl Sulfate,

☒ SLES – Sodium Laureth Sulfate,

☒ ALS – Ammonium Lauryl Sulfate,

☒ MLS – Magnesium Laureth Sulfate,

☒ SMS – Sodium Myreth Sulfate.

Lepiej zamienić je na łagodniejsze substancje myjące:

☑ Lauryl Glucoside (glukozyd laurylowy),

☑ Coco-Glucoside (glukozyd kokosowy),

☑ Decyl Glucoside (glukozyd decylowy),

☑ Cocamidopropyl Betaine (betaina kokamidopropylowa),

☑ Sodium Cocoamphoacetate.

Te znajdują się w popularnych obecnie kosmetykach określanych jako „naturalne” – łagodne dla skóry.

Należy natomiast odrzucić środki „antybakteryjne” do mycia bądź przynajmniej nie używać ich na co dzień – zwykle są drażniące.

Wiedzą już Państwo, czym się myć, a teraz…

**…jak często to robić?**
Otóż nie ma na to jasnej odpowiedzi i trzeba zachować rozsądek – ani zbyt częste mycie ciała, ani niedbanie o higienę nie są dobre.

Badania wskazują, że ilość i rodzaj bakterii na skórze jest podobna zarówno przed kąpielą, jak i po.

Mycie całego ciała raz dziennie nam wystarczy. Ręce powinniśmy myć łagodnym środkiem myjącym, kilka razy dziennie. Ale zawsze mycie rąk powinno mieć swoją przyczynę, np. po kontakcie z chorą osobą, ze zwierzęciem, z wózkiem sklepowym czy po toalecie.

Trochę odbiegłem od głównego tematu: diety. Proszę o informację, czy było to dla Państwa wartościowe, czytam każdy e-list. Z drugiej strony skóra to też narząd, należy o nią właściwie dbać.

# Thread: Bottled Waters - like all water still a mystery to me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2023-03-18T16:11:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320050>

Will mention that I think cucumber water and juice, taking drops of _Activation Products_ brand ****Seabuckthorn** Seed Oil** [https://shop.activationproducts.com/products/perfect-press-sea-buckthorn-oil](https://shop.activationproducts.com/products/perfect-press-sea-buckthorn-oil) and taking GeroNova's Bio-Enhanced® R-Lipoic acid [https://geronova.com/](https://geronova.com/) on an empty stomach has helped hydration of my skin.

Mostly I drink **_RO water that has sunbathed_** for 1-3 days outside in glass gallon jugs.
Retail bottled Waters I use:

**_Icelandic Glacial_** Natural Spring Water from Iceland [https://icelandicglacial.com/](https://icelandicglacial.com/)
but I have some concern about it being too alkaline at 8.4. My former doctor suggested that it and other high ph waters cause candida if it is used exclusively. Do not like that I can only get it in plastic bottles.

**_Virginia Artesian_** Ph 7.7 (local to me in central Virginia) [https://virginiaartesian.com/our-water/](https://virginiaartesian.com/our-water/)
I don't like that it comes in a metal can.

**_Liquid Death_** Mountain Water - the plain one not flavors - However do not like Aluminum cans
[https://liquiddeath.com/](https://liquiddeath.com/)

Still **_Richards Rainwater_ [https://www.richardsrainwater.com/](https://www.richardsrainwater.com/) in glass bottles.**

**_Hydro-Health DDW_** 25 alternating with 50 Hydro-Health - too expensive but using mixing a little with my sunbathed RO or other bottled waters.

**PENTA** [https://pentawater.com/](https://pentawater.com/) Ultra pure I drank it when I had been suffering silent acid reflux and I start every day with two large glasses of PENTA OR Distilled water from my Distiller equipment. I start the distiller with my sunbathed RO water. RO system is an AuquaSana unit we put under the kitchen sink.

**Watt-Ahh by AquaNew** [https://www.aquanew.com/](https://www.aquanew.com/) is by far the water I feel best drinking.

I used to drink **Mountain Valley Spring Water** but it was pretty much all the water my daddy drank it before he succumb to early onset Alzheimer. But it was most likely the small bottles of coco-cola that he consumed that contributed more to his decline. When I studied it years ago I remember it was high in aluminum. The link I had to the analysis from back then is now broken but it was showing higher in Al than other waters and I stopped using it unless I'm out and it is my only choice among these other waters I prefer.

**I do not have a well. I have not found a source for Hard Water that I want to try.**

**What are you Drinking for Water?**

### Sue-UK — 2023-03-18T18:47:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320051>

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=320050)
>
>
> **What are you Drinking for Water?**

There's 3 of us living here at the moment, so I've had to find a reasonably priced water for all of us, so absolute minimum 6L a day excluding any used for cooking, and its got to be easy to get hold of in terms of going to get it/delivery charges etc. The economy in the UK has gone nuts, and prices of fresh food are going up and up and its a question of being realistic about what to spend on more expensive waters if that impacts the amount of seafood and decent meat and seasonal veg we eat.

The nearest supermarket stocks a spring water that has a pH of 7.8, in plastic bottles unfortunately, but I think its better to spend the budget on a reasonably priced water for all of us rather than something expensive for me and them drink something of less quality or even worse drink tap water. I also buy San Pellegrino (pH 7.7) but I'm the only one who likes sparkling water. In terms of magnesium the spring water is 14 mg per litre, compared to 49.5 mg per litre in San Pellegrino. Andrew Huberman in his video on water mentioned that waters with a pH over 7.4 got through the aquaporins quicker. I think why I often crave San Pell over the spring water despite the similar pH is because of the extra mineral content generally, particularly magnesium. The nearest supermarket doesn't stock Volvic (pH 7) which I've used in the past because of its silicon content to clear aluminium, (mother and grandmother had dementia) but when we're passing a larger supermarket that stocks it I'll get that and drink a litre a day until I run out.

### Martha Ray — 2023-03-19T02:18:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320061>

**When I drink San Pellegrino, Volvic** and sometimes with **Fiji water I get really bad leg and foot cramps.**
I do not know why this happens. It might be that cramps related to my magnesium and calcium levels or sodium, potassium balance. I take L-Threonate and magnesium glycinate magnesium supplements.
Two things that help the painful foot and leg cramps are **tonic water which is best relief** (sadly l can not cannot find a healthy tonic water) and **pickle juice** is the next best remedy. **I have no idea why those work as remedies.** One of many health puzzles.

### JanSz — 2023-03-19T03:17:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320062>

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=320061)
>
> **really bad leg and foot cramps.**

tonic water works because it contains quinine.

I and my late wife, we both were suffering from foot cramps, since at least 1975.
So, for me we are talking about 50 years.
Quinine is hard to get, but you could get it at amazon.
Recently I got so desperate that I asked doctor for help.
I got muscle relaxant and it helped with cramps.
It helped, I used it for over 2 months.
I have suspicion that those pills stared screwing my liver.
\====
By chance, I talked to my neighbor.
He advised stretching achilleas tendons.
**It helped right away.**
Then I asked chiropractor about how to do the exercises in more proper way.
He told me to do it **(slowly)** while standing with both feet on the edge of a stair and do it with **deep squatting allowing heels to go below the stair as much as possible.** While doing it hold on to the stair #4 assuming that stair you are standing on is #1.

Chiropractor told me to do those squats while keeping my feet in three different positions.

Straight
duck footed---out toed
pigeon toed---in toed

### JanSz — 2023-03-19T03:45:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320064>

### Sue-UK — 2023-03-19T12:02:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320066>

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=320061)
>
> **When I drink San Pellegrino, Volvic** and sometimes with **Fiji water I get really bad leg and foot cramps.**
> I do not know why this happens. It might be that cramps related to my magnesium and calcium levels or sodium, potassium balance. I take L-Threonate and magnesium glycinate magnesium supplements.
> Two things that help the painful foot and leg cramps are **tonic water which is best relief** (sadly l can not cannot find a healthy tonic water) and **pickle juice** is the next best remedy. **I have no idea why those work as remedies.** One of many health puzzles.

The interesting thing is that although San Pell and Fiji are alkaline waters, Volvic isn't. But when I looked at the mineral content, the bicarbonate content of San Pell, Fiji and Volvic is 243mg/L, 132.14mg/L and 74mg/L respectively. Icelandic Glacial may have a pH of 8.4 but the bicarb content is 35mg/L. (Just checked and my spring water is 166mg/L).

On the watt-ah website it says

"According to the DOE, there are a series of buffers which help the plasma resist any change in pH. Some of these include: the hemoglobin in the red blood cells, other plasma proteins, the bicarbonate buffer, and the phosphate buffer. Many physiological processes influence the pH, but one of the largest contributors is the CO2 content of the blood."

From a website giving reasons not to drink distilled water it says
"As the US Environmental Protection Agency says:
Distilled water, being essentially mineral-free, is very aggressive, in that it tends to dissolve substances with which it is in contact. Notably, **carbon dioxide** from the air is rapidly absorbed, making the water acidic and even more aggressive."

You mention 21+ wonky blood markers, and not being medically qualified I'm not suggesting anything, but one of the symptoms of metabolic alkalosis is muscle twitches or spasms. [https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/ph-of-blood](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/ph-of-blood)

The remedies you use after drinking high bicarb waters.....possibly help because tonic water and pickle juice are both acid????

### JanSz — 2023-03-19T20:44:54+0200

### Daulatwant — 2023-03-20T08:03:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320091>
