> \============
>
> [View attachment 23844](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/23844/)
> On tha above picture he is still standing up.
> I would rather see him (at the same time) squatting (very slowly) to the position as shown below:
> [View attachment 23845](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/23845/)

Leaning forward and holding onto a wall that puts more resistance(weight) on the mucle and tendon and makes the training more effective. If the resistance is not enough progress may be too slow or none at all.

The idea is that the bottom end of the movement is stretching the tendon to the max and then going to the top end of the motion the muscle is working to the max also. If it is just streching of the tendon without hitting the muscle hard enough then this may not work to relieve pain, fix lack of mobility and function.

You could just train in the seated position with a weight on the knee lifting the heel. Using a platform under the knee to lift your toes up will allow the maximum range of motion. And keep adding more weight on top of the knee.

### Sue-UK — 2023-03-20T12:29:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320092>

I've been reading the transcript underneath the video of this interview with Dr Que Collins. Some quite interesting bits. Had never heard of Kure water ....

### Sue-UK — 2023-03-21T21:00:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320130>

This is not using the Kure oxygenated water, and participants of the human trial didn't change any of their usual habits

[https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0254619](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0254619)
**Effects of water stably-enriched with oxygen as a novel method of tissue oxygenation on mitochondrial function, and as adjuvant therapy for type 2 diabetes in a randomized placebo-controlled trial
**
Makes me wonder what the results would be if they had followed the protocols for light hygiene, circadian rhythm, light water magnetism etc .....

According to the DD center on a page about a proposed covid study, Kure delivers 100 times more oxygen than regular water........ "with bioavailable oxygen at concentrations you won't find in any other water."

The spring water is from Cornwall in the UK so I don't think it is deuterium depleted. Its pH is 6-7 and its total dissolved solids is less than 110mg/L. I've been trying to think about how it works with deuterium depleting and wonder if its because oxygen isn't picky about whether it attaches to hydrogen or deuterium ...and if the aquaporins "excrete" it ...?

Bought some to try.

### Daulatwant — 2023-03-22T19:12:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bottled-waters-like-all-water-still-a-mystery-to-me.27521/post-320156>

What I gather is that rain and snow is lower in deuterium than ground water in the same location.

The Russian town is 57°N. Not super fat north but snow is quite low in deuterium.

The graphs display a Delta number. Which is a measure of deviation from a standard of 155.76 ppm. So a delta of -130 is a negative number, means the deuterium is lower.

(1000-130)/6.420135 = 135 ppm

Edit: I got confused about the location of the mineral waters. Was not sure they were in the same location. Their ppm is 141-142. So lower than rain. I checked other datasets around the world in temperate climates and summer rain has quite a bit more deuterium than snow. The lowest deuterium source in my country is also a mineral water spring. It may be lower in deuterium than rain. But most other springs will be worse than rain.

### Sue-UK — 2023-03-23T10:16:26+0200

[https://www.hindawi.com/journals/jhe/2020/1323270/](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/jhe/2020/1323270/)
**Effects of Highly Oxygenated Water in a Hyperuricemia Rat Model
**
Yesterday was day 1 of hack. Using 1 x 500ml bottle a day of the Kure water, cutting 100ml with 400ml of my usual spring water, spread out over the day. Water has a smoother taste. Cost of hack, £1 per day.

Prostatitis

His initial problems were always mainly around prostate inflammation.
So,
I would suggest concentration on locating any inflammation process within his body.
First, aggressive investigation of any inflammation process
followed up with aggressive measures to fight it.
\----------
Testosterone level, high or low has no influence on this process
but
doctors are wasting precious time and energy just on that.

,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,
homocysteine

Some of the more commonly used inflammatory markers used include the following:
1\. Blood counts (e.g., white blood cell count, red blood cell count, hemoglobin count)
2\. C-reactive protein (CRP)
3\. Erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR)
4\. Procalcitonin
5\. Calprotectin Other inflammatory markers are sometimes assessed in specific circumstances.

# Thread: Are these Healthy Light Bulbs for Daytime use?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-these-healthy-light-bulbs-for-daytime-use.27551/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2023-04-03T01:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-these-healthy-light-bulbs-for-daytime-use.27551/post-320388>

Light bulbs for garage and home during the day?
Is this light healthy to use in the home during the day?
[https://lumiram.com/products/chromalux-full-spectrum-incandescent-light-bulbs/](https://lumiram.com/products/chromalux-full-spectrum-incandescent-light-bulbs/)

No one solved my April Fools Riddle,
'How many Black Swans does it take to change a light bulb?' so I edited the title of my post

****On October 28, 2015 Dr. Jack Kruse** on Facebook said...**
So what indoor bulb is best?
[sneodpStor9cgtir9a uga518f0h60ilf616 5u6cb0t,2ieohO05ti12m54](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1042775292453491/#) ·

So what indoor bulb is best? Might it be the one our federal government just got rid of? In the first decades of electrical lighting, no one had a problem with the only disadvantage of incandescent light: its high energy consumption. Today, we have a different idea of why they need to be replaced by the high color LED. Only five percent of the power investment comes back as light in an incandescent bulb, the rest is transformed into heat, what makes this light comparable to a kind of tamed bonfire, still near to nature. The incandescent lamp is the only electrical light source which deserves the label “full spectrum” because it delivers all wavelengths without any gaps or energy peaks. When a filament bulb is operated with direct current, it produces a completely clean light, free from modulation frequencies and flicker – the best you can get. All new bulbs have flicker and flicker is associated with its own set of biologic tolls for cells. Below is the spectrum of these bulbs
[

# Thread: Portable Sleep Gear, Away from Home
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-sleep-gear-away-from-home.27559/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-04-07T16:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-sleep-gear-away-from-home.27559/post-320442>

Portable Red Lighting, Eye Mask, and Ear Plugs?

### Marko Pollo — 2023-04-08T02:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-sleep-gear-away-from-home.27559/post-320449>

Add an emf meter. When we check in, we unpower wifi, night lights, tv and fridge. black electrical tape to cover any small lights. We turn them back on before we leave. The meter will find where the wifi is hiding and save time. If the room seems higher in emf than surrounding area when checking in, ask for different room.

### coho3131 — 2023-04-08T18:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-sleep-gear-away-from-home.27559/post-320454>

Thanks. What are the healthiest ear plugs, eye mask, and portable red lighting? I have not done any research on these products yet.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-04-08T19:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-sleep-gear-away-from-home.27559/post-320455>

We use Hooga Health reading lights at home and while travelling. they clip on, are rechargeable and have a bendy arm allowing you to direct the light away from you. I tried beeswax but found foam earplugs more comfortable. My wife made a bunch of fun eye masks with fabric scraps.

### coho3131 — 2023-04-09T03:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/portable-sleep-gear-away-from-home.27559/post-320461>

That's great! Thanks!

# Thread: Sauna sweating for detox, some positive results

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 2

### Warren Canavan — 2023-04-07T18:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauna-sweating-for-detox-some-positive-results.27560/post-320447>

I had some good results today. I have had it in the past where the sauna made me feel ill and my body would not sweat. This was initially helped, with taking carrot juice/ tomato soup. I managed to sweat.

Today I tried;
I had AM sunshine exposure(might have helped?) I used vitmain c 1 gram, niacin 50mg, and teaspoon salt. The sweating stated almost immediately, and I felt robust in the heat, and did not feel fatigued.

I am starting to think it had something to do with the salt, perhaps the body resists perspiration without the extra salt or minerals to preserve organ function?

### Jacks — 2023-04-12T05:03:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauna-sweating-for-detox-some-positive-results.27560/post-320506>

Hi. Warren. Sounds like you are having some success.

Interestingly enough, I just started using some new sauna lights (red/NIR) this past weekend and have not been feeling great - I had a headache yesterday afterwards and don't feel too great today - slight headache when I did my HIIT skipping. I started with just 20 mins and changing my position every 5. I did not sweat much, like you describe in your earlier sessions, but I don't have a super small space to heat up - just the bathroom. I also started drinking silica water in the last week to try to detox Aluminum (based on the work of Dr. Exley), so I guess that could be a factor for me.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Warren Canavan — 2023-04-12T00:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerson-therapy-detoxification-and-the-circadian-sun-missing-link.27569/post-320499>

I have noticed increased appetite with vitamin C (Bile salt co-enzyme).
I also feel better with Taurine(a molecule component of bile salts)

But I have also noticed worsening appetite with been inside and no sunlight exposure.
\========================================================================
So basically my question is Bile production a circadian phenomenon? If so this means your ability to detoxify via the liver is inhibited when you have no access to natural sunlight.
No appetite >>> No bile movement >>> means no toxin expulsion.
No appetite also means, not meeting the daily requirements of nutrition(Minerals and Vitamins).
\==================================================================
The basic idea of what causes cancer by the Gerson therapy is Toxicity and lack of nutrition.

Toxins = Enzyme inhibitors
Vitamins and minerals = co-enzymes and Metal activators >>> both needed for enzymes to work.

One of the key discoveries of the therapy is that potassium is depleted in cancer, and is a key metal activator of enzymes. Also that sodium acts as an enzyme inhibitor in excess.

The Gerson cancer therapy uses high amounts of vegetable juices and potassium based vegetarian foods. The consequence of this is that toxins are mobilized into the blood stream. Early patients of the therapy died of hepatocoma, liver coma. The circulating toxins overwhelmed the liver and the cancer patients died.
The solution for this by Dr Max Gerson was Bile Stimulation with Caffeine and Potassium, take via enema rectally. "A Cancer Therapy: Results of Fifty cases" by Dr Max Gerson. Page 191. Caffeine its is hypothesized, when taken rectally, stimulates the visceral nervous system. Dr Gerson later came up with the idea of coffee enemas: because of their caffeine and potassium content, and the easy availability of coffee!
So the the net effect of this strategy is that the liver is not overwhelmed by toxins, and is got rid of through bile into the digestive tract. Also in Dr Chris Shades detoxification methods, bitter herbs are used as bile stimulants, he claims Myrrh as particularly useful. He calls his system "push catch", bitters to push bile and catch with binders like charcoal and others.
\====================================================================
However is the caffeine/potassium technique fighting against the circadian biology of the liver???

To be fair to Dr Gerson and the Gerson therapy, he encouraged going to bed early. But he didn't respect the circadian aspect of eating and sun exposure.

Here is my thinking of the circadian component of the liver. Liver functions on a circadian rhythm. So mobilizing toxins, without respecting circadian rhythm is a bad idea, leads to toxins in the blood stream and not going anywhere. The liver not bothering to make bile.
Dr Gersons thinking also was that of detoxify fast due to the urgency of cancer, so the patient can heal quicker. But perhaps there is a better way of detoxification through circadian methods, thus maximizing bile function of the liver? Matching toxin mobilization and toxin expulsion via the livers ability to make bile.

The next step after getting the bile into the digestive tract is toxin binders(Charcoal, Clay, pectin etc).

anyone have additional thoughts on using unchained capital versus a Trezor wallet?

I guess I am wondering - Is unchained Capital still a safe way to store bitcoin or are laws/regulations changing and holding your own cold wallet is the best option?

I worry about being robbed etc etc and being forced to give up your seed phrase.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-unchained-capital-multi-sig-or-ledger-trezor-cold-wallet.27643/post-321166>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-unchained-capital-multi-sig-or-ledger-trezor-cold-wallet.27643/post-321187>

I got burned by the Genesis bankruptcy. Never trusting another party with my keys again. I don't even trust self-custody hot wallets. Only do cold storage now. Ledger has been good from my experience. The upfront cost is definitely worth the peace of mind to me.

### DebraGM — 2023-05-24T05:01:01+0300

### Daniel Renaud — 2023-05-24T16:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-unchained-capital-multi-sig-or-ledger-trezor-cold-wallet.27643/post-321643>

The Ledger thing is a warning sign - and it's not just a problem for Ledger devices. They made a product to backup private keys, and botched the release. In that window we were all able to see behind the curtain a little bit. It makes me wonder if any of the cold storage devices are secure from firmware upgrade attacks. He's the thing - if there is an exploit it will be exploited. Maybe not when its $27K per coin, but what about after hyperbitcoinization? How much do you trust Ledger, or Trezor or even ColdCard when BTC is $1MM per coin?

# Thread: Pine Pollen
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pine-pollen.27644/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sunman — 2023-05-09T14:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pine-pollen.27644/post-321126>

i have used pine pollen for a few days now and it seems to improve stamina, calmness and clarity of mind; sometimes i put a bit into my smoothie, other times under my tongue and let it absorb; As a airline pilot surrounded by nnEMF and 5G, is it a good idea to be using pine pollen?

### Ryan Carter — 2023-05-09T20:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pine-pollen.27644/post-321145>

I think its pissing in the wind in the grand scheme of things.
You could make the case for spirulina too which is supported as a performance aid.

There's a jetlag or plane hack RX somewhere on the blogs. Perhaps explore that.
Where Dr Kruse breaks down a lot.
He also talked about the plane on the Huberman convo.
Surprisingly he mentioned creatine as one supplement I have never heard him discuss before.
This would support more ATP recycling because likely pseudo-hypoxic and unable to utilise fats. < context.

My thoughts would be

\- Methylene blue
\- bringing in a portable red light device onboard that's chargeable for when you "off" duty or for use in the bathroom.
\- Vie light for nasal.
\- Vitamin C
\- DHA/ iodine/selenium = SEAFOOD

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-05-10T03:25:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pine-pollen.27644/post-321167>

EDI, I have almost 20 pilots under active care. I always suggest to my patients what I would do if it were me in their situations. **Remember this is what I would do and not a recommendation.** First, I don't think it's a wise career from a health perspective. That said, I know flying can be a personal passion, and being paid to do your hobby is the best possible way to make money. Now for high-flying-protection. I would be taking 10 mg of astaxanthin, 250 mg of annatto tocotrienols, and 800mg's EGCG. There are others, but these would be my main three. The key is to focus on a strategy that can, at the same time, upregulate the expression of genes that promote longevity and healthspan, and down-regulate the genes that promote inflammation and oxidative stress. Per this forum, you should also maximize natural morning and daily sunlight, CT, sleep, exercise, seafood, and general stress reduction. My recommendation is to not focus on the minutia. It's easy to be caught up in that. The first few sentences in the bible say, "Light is Good" and then"There Was Day and There Was Night - Circadian Rhythm". If God wanted us to be bogged down in the minutia, God would have told Moses how it all works, in explicit detail. Evolution or creation, we are what we are. Eve did not know there was a thing called naked/nude until she ate from the Tree of Knowledge. At that moment she became aware and began to attach significance (personalized defined meaning) to everything. Eve was ashamed and embarrassed, and sowed fig leaves to cover herself. A life lesson: "It is what it is". If it's good, beneficial, ethical, moral, and legal, do it, often. The more significance you attach to things or concepts the more you lose the basic fundamentals of life and living. Gaining basic knowledge as to why something is a benefit to health and well-being is good. It removes doubt and allows you to move forward with that activity unencumbered. This is just my personal opinion and not advice.

### sunman — 2023-05-17T00:13:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pine-pollen.27644/post-321417>

How about DDW and QUANTLET; would these have good effects;

Why annatto tocotrienols and EGCG; i don't understand the reason; which brands;

Do you know of any portable travel size magnetic sleep pads;

what do you think of the MERCOLA SILVERSHIELD TENT;

Thanks again;

>
> They have big claims & massive following.

If your phone, computer, WiFi etc still work - you still have the same RF frequencies in your home, going through your body. If the lights, TV and toaster still work - you still have a 60Hz (50Hz) AC field coming from these appliances and the wires in the walls etc affecting melatonin etc.- science is science.

# Thread: New Home Build Wish List
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FutureVision — 2023-05-19T23:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321525>

I've been watching a lot of videos on home building and the newest thing catching on here in the states seems to be building homes to passive house standards. There seems to be a lot of benefits from constant fresh, filtered air from outside to more light (large windows to heat the home), less electric used etc. I'm curious if there are any drawbacks to this way of building. One though was that maybe the constant temperature these homes can maintain might take away a stressor that might be essential for health although I suppose you can just get outside more often if you want some type of climate change. Anything else to be concerned about?

Beyond the drawbacks what would you build into/onto the home to make it better? Obviously, there would probably be some level of NNEMF mitigation (shielded wiring) but what else? What about materials? Do steel frames or metal roofs advantageous or detrimental? What kind of floors would be best?

### Daulatwant — 2023-05-23T11:33:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321608>

The only type of ventilation I would think about. Is either designing the home to generate a passive draft with hatches and air inlets, or install a simple extraction fan. And use them only to quickly air out a home few times per day. No fussing about with any long ductwork, recuperators, automated controls or anything. Just open a couple windows or flip a switch and the home is aired out in a few minutes.

I would do the same with heating. Just one switch, dial. Just crank it up, heat the home as much as you like and turn it off

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/earthship-ventilation-global-coolingtube-jpg.24199/)

### JanSz — 2023-05-23T18:35:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321612>

If you have stream nearby.
Reroute it or part of it to be near or going thru the house.
Water stream is a source of heat or heat sink.
Helps with heating or AC.

### FutureVision — 2023-05-24T20:10:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321663>

What are your thoughts on using steel frames for the main structure of the house or those steel studs? Does that cause more problems with NNEMF?

### FutureVision — 2023-05-24T20:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321664>

> [Daulatwant said:](/goto/post?id=321608)
>
> The only type of ventilation I would think about. Is either designing the home to generate a passive draft with hatches and air inlets, or install a simple extraction fan. And use them only to quickly air out a home few times per day. No fussing about with any long ductwork, recuperators, automated controls or anything. Just open a couple windows or flip a switch and the home is aired out in a few minutes.
>
> I would do the same with heating. Just one switch, dial. Just crank it up, heat the home as much as you like and turn it off
>
> [View attachment 24199](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/24199/)

With a passive house you end up using some kind of fresh air system that pulls in a certain amount of CMS of fresh air into every room in the house on an ongoing basis. We're not really going to want to open windows during bug season, hot humid days or in the freezing cold. Any one of those options would kick on AC or heating and that's an energy cost for trying to avoid

### FutureVision — 2023-05-24T20:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321665>

> [8Phoenix said:](/goto/post?id=321619)
>
>

Haven't had time to watch the second video but the first one is interesting. My thing was that design is the Delta is so large. Granted, it's also in Zimbabwe were the climate is not near what it is here in the Northeast US. For the passive home your deltas are much much smaller. We're talking the power can go out in the dead of winter and the temp might drop 10 degrees in the house over a few days. Same deal in the summer. It might rise of a bit but nothing extreme. Regardless I'm still curious as to what are the health implications of having such a consistent temp year round. Well, maybe not completely consistent. I would cool the place to 65° to sleep but other than that it should remain pretty consistent

### FutureVision — 2023-05-24T20:20:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321666>

I imagine shielded wiring is going to be important. What other materials should be avoided or used?

### FutureVision — 2023-05-24T20:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321667>

I was also thinking there might be a neat way to ground the house to mimic walking outside. Not as an excuse not to go outside but to get the benefits while inside too. Any thoughts on this?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-24T20:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321669>

On the last Q&A I asked Jack about grounding inside where there is nnEMF (WiFi, electric fields from wires and appliances etc) and he advised no. Grounding makes you part of the circuit which outside is good but inside with nn-electric and magnetic fields not so good. Shielded wiring is good especially in bedrooms and office. Turn off power circuits, especially in and around bedrooms at night. Think about DC lighting (no flicker).

### FutureVision — 2023-05-24T20:54:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321671>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=321669)
>
> On the last Q&A I asked Jack about grounding inside where there is nnEMF (WiFi, electric fields from wires and appliances etc) and he advised no. Grounding makes you part of the circuit which outside is good but inside with nn-electric and magnetic fields not so good. Shielded wiring is good especially in bedrooms and office. Turn off power circuits, especially in and around bedrooms at night. Think about DC lighting (no flicker).

Are you referring to grounding with a grounding mat or grounding sheet that plugs into an outlet? I realize those are probably not a good idea. I was thinking along the lines of having a concrete floor that is grounded into the Earth. Essentially an extension of outside. I think that might be different than grounding on the electrical system.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-24T21:32:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321672>

Grounding is grounding it makes you part of a circuit, outside good, inside bad. All the outlet ground wires are connected in the breaker box and then to a ground rod outside. The same ground your house sits on and all your floors are resting on. They are all connected. If I am using a body voltage meter I get a different reading with my feet off the ground (not grounded) than with my feet on the ground (carpet, wooden floor, concrete etc). Not as good a connection as a ground pad but still a ground). Your home electric field is an AC field, if this AC nnEMF has a path of least resistance thru you to ground, it will take it. Your body electric field is DC you don’t want an AC field going thru you All day. Now if you had an all DC home that would be different.

### Daulatwant — 2023-05-24T21:54:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321674>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=321672)
>
> Grounding is grounding it makes you part of a circuit, outside good, inside bad. All the outlet ground wires are connected in the breaker box and then to a ground rod outside. The same ground your house sits on and all your floors are resting on. They are all connected. If I am using a body voltage meter I get a different reading with my feet off the ground (not grounded) than with my feet on the ground (carpet, wooden floor, concrete etc). Not as good a connection as a ground pad but still a ground). Your home electric field is an AC field, if this AC nnEMF has a path of least resistance thru you to ground, it will take it. Your body electric field is DC you don’t want an AC field going thru you All day. Now if you had an all DC home that would be different.

I found the same thing. The indoor ground is also connected with the AC netural in the breaker box. And I could no go to sleep grounding to it, I got odd sensations from it.

But throwing an insulated wire out the window into the dirt and using that as a ground inside is a good workaround, didn't feel anything wrong grounding this way, slept just fine.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-25T00:23:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321683>

Have you measured the nnEMF level (electric field) in your bedroom from the wires in the walls and everything plugged in?

### FutureVision — 2023-05-25T03:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321698>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=321672)
>
> Grounding is grounding it makes you part of a circuit, outside good, inside bad. All the outlet ground wires are connected in the breaker box and then to a ground rod outside. The same ground your house sits on and all your floors are resting on. They are all connected. If I am using a body voltage meter I get a different reading with my feet off the ground (not grounded) than with my feet on the ground (carpet, wooden floor, concrete etc). Not as good a connection as a ground pad but still a ground). Your home electric field is an AC field, if this AC nnEMF has a path of least resistance thru you to ground, it will take it. Your body electric field is DC you don’t want an AC field going thru you All day. Now if you had an all DC home that would be different.

Here is what I was thinking when I say grounding inside the house. In the concrete floor i run a copper rod/pipe through the floor and into the ground outside. Nothing in the outlets.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-25T04:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321704>

You are missing the point. It is not so much a matter of where you ground but whether you have 60Hz electric fields in your house that you are exposed to or not. If you have any nnEMF 60Hz fields in your home you do not want to be grounded. You don’t want any 60Hz fields going to ground thru you ALL DAY. IF you eliminate ALL 60Hz then you can be grounded. By the way, concrete is conductive and will be grounded by all the rebar, no need for copper. Minimize nnEMF inside and get outside as often as you can. You could make a sun room, big windows that open, with NO nnEMF 60Hz fields (no wires) and a good ground to the outside well away from any power lines.

### FutureVision — 2023-05-25T16:09:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321718>

Ah. I see. I have something to learn about nnEMF indoors. What can detect the 60Hz field? Or better yet how can you build in a way to prevent the 60Hz field? I have a EMRSS Cornet ED88T Trimode Meter sitting in my draw. Would that work?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-25T20:53:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-home-build-wish-list.27703/post-321721>

The best way to detect and measure 60 Hz electric fields is with a Body Voltage Meter (1,000x more accurate than a regular electric field meter) [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/store](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/store) (use code Kruse10 for 10% discount)

# Thread: No flicker bulbs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-21T21:25:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-321563>

I just found these bulbs at my local ACE Hardware: FEIT Electric LED Red Light bulb - No Ficker. You can test all the bulbs in your home (and at a store before you buy) with the “slo-mo” video camera on your “smart” phone. Just shoot a second or two of slo-mo video and then play it back. YOU WILL SEE.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-05-22T01:36:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-321566>

I think UVA lights might be superior to red lights (for night lighting, if you must)

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-22T20:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-321587>

I leave a visible red light on all day to help balance light coming in thru windows and unavoidable screen time. I have been using a 850nm IR light morning and evening also. I am researching UVA lights, anyone got suggestions - reptile vs black light vs other?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-05-22T20:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-321590>

Normal black lights will only have UVA, reptile typically has UVA and UVB. Some reptile bulbs have UV and IR together.

I think UVA is the only one that is okay for night. All 3 okay during the day. Obviously, sun is best, but if you can't get sun, I would think having the bulbs would be better than no UV or IR (during day).

I see a lot of recommendations for Mega-Ray and Reptisun bulbs. I can't verify myself if these are actually a good idea or not, but I see them referenced a lot.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-05-22T20:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-321591>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=321587)
>
> I leave a visible red light on all day to help balance light coming in thru windows

I do this as well when I am trapped indoors, even with windows open. I imagine the benefit from this is negligible, but at least I feel like I am making an attempt to further balance the lighting inside.

### Donald Shrump Jr. — 2023-06-13T17:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-322361>

I have black light UVA with IR and red light on during time when the sun goes down as light source, counterbalance TV with family, and if up before sunrise (winter in NE). Seems to help. My Vit D is up faster then last year and solar callous is coming up faster. Feel and sleep better too.

### ShaneR — 2023-06-20T23:23:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-322601>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=321563)
>
> I just found these bulbs at my local ACE Hardware: FEIT Electric LED Red Light bulb - No Ficker. You can test all the bulbs in your home (and at a store before you buy) with the “slo-mo” video camera on your “smart” phone. Just shoot a second or two of slo-mo video and then play it back. YOU WILL SEE.

Hey Anthony- Be careful with relying solely on slow motion videos for testing flicker. There are various aspects of "flicker" to be aware of, and depending upon the frame rate of the camera, it can look zero- or low-flicker when you play it back, but measure poorly w a spectroscope. I found this to be a useful article for better understanding around relevant topics for investigating flicker: [https://www.alexfergus.com/blog/light-flicker-health](https://www.alexfergus.com/blog/light-flicker-health)

### Anthony Smith — 2023-06-21T01:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-322607>

> [ShaneR said:](/goto/post?id=322601)
>
> Hey Anthony- Be careful with relying solely on slow motion videos for testing flicker. There are various aspects of "flicker" to be aware of, and depending upon the frame rate of the camera, it can look zero- or low-flicker when you play it back, but measure poorly w a spectroscope. I found this to be a useful article for better understanding around relevant topics for investigating flicker: [https://www.alexfergus.com/blog/light-flicker-health](https://www.alexfergus.com/blog/light-flicker-health)

Thank you Shane. I believe my sloMo camera is 4x. I know it is not scientific but at least helps people become aware of the problem and get rid of the really bad bulbs. A step in the right direction.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-03-08T22:59:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-flicker-bulbs.27708/post-348154>

Would you bring these if I can't find the guy ?

# Thread: What is your favourite Jack Kruse article/blog?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4

### Bastian — 2023-05-24T07:08:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-favourite-jack-kruse-article-blog.27724/post-321627>

Hi guys,
I would love to know which of Jacks's articles/blogs was most influential for you and why.
Mine is Cold Thermogenesis 6, probably because it was my first exposure to Jacks's work.

We want to put together about 20 articles to add to our core library for the users of our app (Circadian: Your Natural Rhythm).
I would love to make sure we share the best blogs with our audience.

Thanks!
Bastian

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-05-24T17:55:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-favourite-jack-kruse-article-blog.27724/post-321658>

The relatively new Hacking the Periodic Table blogs are fascinating to the chem teacher in me. Right up my alley. Very cool to see the biology, chemistry, and physics intermingling here. These are definitely my favorite blogs thus far. For reference, I have only read through about 50...I have lots of catching up to do. Very interested in seeing what others post

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-25T00:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-favourite-jack-kruse-article-blog.27724/post-321689>

Although I have listened to Jack for many years I’m new here on the forum. The last few weeks I have taken a deep dive down the Kruse rabbit hole and I must say my favorite article/blog is the one I am reading (at any given time).

### Awakenotwoke — 2023-06-14T05:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-favourite-jack-kruse-article-blog.27724/post-322388>

So far, I like this one. Max asks just the right questions and keeps Jack on track (he loves showing off his AMAZING brain, haha I don't blame him!). Jack can get lost in crazy explanations many of us don't fully follow and really, concern mainly academia and braniacs (which I used to think of myself as such... until I found this group!). This is a great one to try and introduce newbies into the work.. I have shared the Jack/Huberman talk and most ppl won't follow, even my doctor friends. To peak ppl's interest and get them to understand in one short hour, this one is it!

# Thread: Allergic Rhinitis, Sunrise, Sunset, Red, IR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-rhinitis-sunrise-sunset-red-ir.27755/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-05-31T15:15:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-rhinitis-sunrise-sunset-red-ir.27755/post-321912>

More red and infrared light is present at sunrise and sunset, right? My hypothesis is that allergic rhinitis might improve with more time spent outside during sunrise and sunset.

### [Effects of Combined Visible and Infrared Light Rhinophototherapy in Patients With Allergic Rhinitis - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36266929/)

Four-week course of intranasal phototherapy with visible and infrared light leads to clinical improvement in allergic rhinitis patients.

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### [Phototherapy as a Treatment for Dermatological Diseases, Cancer, Aesthetic Dermatologic Conditions and Allergenic Rhinitis in Adult and Paediatric Medicine - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36676145/)

The development of light-emitting diodes (LEDs) has led to an increase in the use of lighting regimes within medicine particularly as a treatment for dermatological conditions. New devices have demonstrated significant results for the treatment of medical conditions, including mild-to-moderate...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### [Treatment Effect of Phototherapy with Low-Level Energy in Patients with Allergic Rhinitis: A Single-Arm Observational Study - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36837427/)

Allergic rhinitis is one of the most common health challenges and has a chronic and repetitive course that requires symptomatic treatment. We aimed to investigate the effect of phototherapy on allergic rhinitis and how long it takes to demonstrate treatment effect. Twenty-one patients who were...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

# Thread: Red screen on iphone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-screen-on-iphone.27758/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2023-05-31T19:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-screen-on-iphone.27758/post-321927>

Hi have recently change my iphones screen to red using colour tint in the settings - does anyone know how effective this setting is?

Settings >accessibility > display and text size > colour filters > colour tint > intensity scroll it to the right.

Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2023-05-31T20:34:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-screen-on-iphone.27758/post-321929>

I use “Night Shift” all the time with Dark mode. Adding the red filter would take it to the next level, especially if you have to use the phone in the evening. I do a lot with photos and video so I need to see some color but this is great for reading text etc. Remember you still have WiFi and Bluetooth if you are not hard wired (Ethernet) and 50/60Hz AC electric fields if the device is plugged in (charging) to consider but this definitely will help reduce blue light exposure.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-screen-on-iphone.27758/post-322072>

it is my understanding on iPhones you can automatically block apps from access to the microphone and camera and/or having cross application access. Each App has to have your expressed permission for them to have access. However on Android phones all apps automatically have access. I don’t have an Android so don’t know if you can go in and block them or not. Can Apple or some 3 letter agency get access, in theory no but in reality . . . ? Unless you take out the battery (not possible with iPhone) or use a faraday bag you can be located/tracked, even if the phone is off.

# Thread: new generation being on average taller than the previous

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11

### JanSz — 2023-06-04T02:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-generation-being-on-average-taller-than-the-previous.27772/post-322055>

My son was 6'2" tall
I am 5'8"
My father was shorter than me.
My grandfather was shorter than my father.
I would estimate that none of us were ever grossly overweight.
\----
What is going on is likely unhealthy? Each new generation being on average taller than the previous generation.
\======================
I posted this question to Jack here;

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-qe-34.27533/post-320196](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-qe-34.27533/post-320196)

Jack replied that:
I've already discussed it.
But you do not seem to read my work.

I am glad to hear that, but that answer it is not helpful to me.

I think that growing very tall is a health problem.
My oldest grandson is already about 6'5" and still growing.
The other three of my grandkids are younger and still short. I would like to help them.

Someone who figured out how Jack proposes to deal with excessive height please help me and post his advice on the following post.
Please cut out everything else, **I need only advice on how to prevent child from growing taller than their parents.** Advice in the form that real dummy would understand.

I thank you ahead.
.

///

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-06-04T03:12:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-generation-being-on-average-taller-than-the-previous.27772/post-322056>

This explains why it is bad:

### [TIME #2: HOW IS TIME BUILT?](https://jackkruse.com/time-2-how-is-time-built/)

READERS SUMMARY: 1. WHERE DOES TIME COME FROM? 2. WHY DOES BIOLOGY HAVE ONLY A FORWARD DIRECTION FOR TIME? 3. WHAT IS A QUANTUM DOT? 4. DOES THE SUN, WATER, AND DHA DETERMINE TIME? 5. WHAT IS GRAVITATIONAL LENSING? Since the 19th century, light was known to be a wave of electric and...

 jackkruse.com

Haven't found the prevention, but if I had to take a wild guess I would say it probably has something to do with the rest of the body losing sync with the SCN, resulting in uncontrolled growth. This is likely caused by too much nnEMF or blue light or lack of UV. I imagine a lot could be due to circadian mismatches in the diet as well...

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-06-04T03:52:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-generation-being-on-average-taller-than-the-previous.27772/post-322059>

I see you have been looking for this answer for a while.

I wouldn't stress too much over height. For you and your son, just do what you can with the body type you have. You can't make yourself or your son shorter, but there are many things you CAN do to offset the detrimental effects of height to varying degrees. Soak up plenty of red/IR/UV from the sun, chow down on the DHA rich foods, and limit the blue/nnEMF as best as you can.

For your grandkids, the prevention seems to be the same as the mitigation. Teach them to do the same.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-06-04T04:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-generation-being-on-average-taller-than-the-previous.27772/post-322060>

My Grandfather (watched the Titanic set sail as a boy) was 6’2” ish, my Dad raised on mostly organic local food and lived outdoors 6’, I was raised on mostly whole food in the UK and outside a lot - 6’, my son raised on American non-organic beef and chicken and a “gamer“ is 6’3”. MY answer to the question - Not enough natural light, too much blue light and nnEMF . . . And just maybe too much growth hormone in food and other products (plastics etc.) . . . prevention/solution - having been down this rabbit hole for a while - have kids get more natural light, reduce blue light and nnEMF and eat organic LOCAL food (without growth hormones). But that is just me.

I am just looking for someone to find it. Most likely also to dumm it dumm enough so I could understand it and be able to transfer that answer to my grandkids.

I am also looking for his answer myself, so it is not as I want somebody to do the heavy lifting with me just waiting for the results.

it takes a very creative mind to spot wrong questions.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-06-05T05:36:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-generation-being-on-average-taller-than-the-previous.27772/post-322089>

I guess imagine you are trying to transport stones from point A to point B. The  **longer** the **distance** between A and B the  **more**  **time** and  **energy** will be required to get the job done.

In the body there are all sorts of signals and secretions that have to travel differing distances. If you are taller, this means  **more**  **distance** things have to travel inside of you. **More**  **time** and  **energy** is required for your body to get things done, as opposed to someone with a shorter frame. Molecules are extremely tiny. A distance of just a few centimeters makes an unfathomable difference. Think about a tiny sugar ant crossing a driveway. Then imagine the driveway was a few feet wider. Just a change of a few feet would add considerable  **time** to the ant's journey. But, for a larger human walking this same distance, a few feet would only add mere seconds to the relatively short journey. Distances that are conceivable to us have huge impacts to things that are infinitesimally small, like the various substances and signals travelling inside of your body.

The bigger effect comes with the fact that your eyes (eye clock) is further from the rest of your body. The eye clock is the master clock controlling everything in your body. Because this controller, is further from everything else in your taller body, it makes it more difficult for things to stay in sync.

Does this help?

Just a change of a few feet would add considerable  **time** to the ant's journey. But, for a larger human walking this same distance, a few feet would only add mere seconds to the relatively short journey. Because this controller, is further from everything else in your taller body, it makes it more difficult for things to stay in sync.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-deciphering-deuterium-water-analysis.27799/post-322662>

I don't know how to identify your well on the graph but on the table number 31 shows -162 for delta H. The Vienna standard has R of 155.76 ppm so you can calculate the deuterium ppm of your well.
It's 130.53 ppm for your well.
This seems to be pretty good.

# Thread: Material on the different wavelengths from the sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/material-on-the-different-wavelengths-from-the-sun.27807/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack b 54321 — 2023-06-12T12:44:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/material-on-the-different-wavelengths-from-the-sun.27807/post-322324>

Is anyone aware of any material that shows the different wavelengths provided by the sun at different times during the day?

Of course this will vary depending on where you are and what time of the year it is, however it would be good to see some examples of this?

Or perhaps it is available as a percentage of the amount of sunlight available on one day.

I think this would be a helpful resource to try and construct ones life around to combat modern light that we battle against.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-06-13T15:46:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/material-on-the-different-wavelengths-from-the-sun.27807/post-322358>

Idk how you will find accurate percentages without directly using a wide range spectrometer at your location, but...

Visible/IR anytime sun is out
UVA ~1hr after sunrise to ~1hr before sunset
UVB can be thought of as gradually increasing as the sun approaches the point directly above your head (called a zenith I think). The closer the sun is to this zenith, the higher the %UVB (or technically all wavelengths for that matter)

Another way to think about it - If you are standing, the shorter your shadow is, the higher the apparent brightness (relative intensity) of all wavelengths from the sun at your location (assuming no cloud cover)

### Anthony Smith — 2023-06-13T19:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/material-on-the-different-wavelengths-from-the-sun.27807/post-322366>

Lots of variables: latitude, altitude, time of day, time of year, weather etc. You could Google the info for your location. This is one option BUT the UV Index is accompanied by (their) recommendations for sun protection etc . . . [https://www.epa.gov/sunsafety/uv-index-1](https://www.epa.gov/sunsafety/uv-index-1)

### Jack b 54321 — 2023-06-13T22:49:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/material-on-the-different-wavelengths-from-the-sun.27807/post-322371>

Thanks Joseph, Anthony. Both super helpful

### Edith Szwarc — 2023-06-20T19:56:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/material-on-the-different-wavelengths-from-the-sun.27807/post-322586>

I use this app called Dminder. It’s to roughly show/hell calculate vitamin D made while in the sun. You set your location and tells you the UVI for location and time of year. Kind of spiffy

# Thread: Tin foil hat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tin-foil-hat.27819/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeremiasbtome — 2023-06-15T16:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tin-foil-hat.27819/post-322418>

If I wear a tin foil hat, does 5g and other emf jump conduct onto it rendering it useless?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-06-15T21:28:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tin-foil-hat.27819/post-322434>

Tin foil (aluminum) can be used to block (reduce) RF BUT being a conductive metal will attract electric fields. When used to block RF from cell towers, “smart” meters etc in the home it needs to be grounded to give the electric field a path of least resistance to ground or else by fixing one problem you create another one. 5G is a technology not a frequency. The 5G technology allows for the use of higher millimeter wave frequencies, pulse signaling and focused beam transmission (4G cannot do that). When a tower detects a 5G device it then beams that signal to the device. These higher frequencies (20 gig+) are (at this time) only being used in very limited places (stadiums, a few down town areas etc). Many 4G signals are a higher frequency than the 5G signals in general use (TMoble uses 600 megaHertz). The problem with the higher frequencies, for the phone companies is they travel less distance and have less penetration into buildings, glass can stop them. If you are indoors and do not have a 5G device there is little chance of a (high frequency) 5G signal reaching you.

**WiFi and Bluetooth (and microwave ovens) use 2.4 GHz
4G LTE frequencies - USA: 600MHz - 2500 MHz (2.5 GHz)**

## 5G Spectrum Usage by Carrier - USA​

Not all service providers use the same frequency band for 5G. Like we mentioned above, there are advantages and disadvantages to using any part of the 5G spectrum.

-   **T-Mobile**: Uses [use low-band spectrum (600 MHz)](https://www.lifewire.com/t-mobile-5g-4178962) as well as 2.5 GHz spectrum. Sprint has been [merged with T-Mobile](https://www.lifewire.com/t-mobile-sprint-merger-4165155) and claimed to have more spectrum than any other carrier in the US, with three spectrum bands: 800 MHz, 1.9 GHz and 2.5 GHz.
-   **Verizon**: Their [5G Ultra Wideband](https://www.verizon.com/about/news/theres-5g-then-theres-verizon-5g-ultra-wideband) network uses millimeter waves, specifically [28 GHz and 39 GHz](https://www.verizon.com/about/news/verizon-qualcomm-and-novatel-wireless-collaborate-expedite-trials-and-commercial-deployment-5g). Check for coverage.
-   **AT&T**: [Uses millimeter wave spectrum](https://about.att.com/story/2018/5g_cities_2018_2019.html) C-Band 4 GHz for dense areas and mid and low-spectrum for rural and suburban locations.

### Saichi — 2023-06-16T04:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tin-foil-hat.27819/post-322460>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=322434)
>
> The 5G technology allows for the use of higher millimeter wave frequencies, pulse signaling and focused beam transmission (4G cannot do that).

Pulsing and beam forming is well within 4G capability. Only thing new with 5G is millimeter spectrum.

### JanSz — 2023-06-23T13:32:57+0300

### [Tenzing Norgay, the Sherpa who climbed to the top of the world - archive, 10 May 1986](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2019/may/10/tribute-to-late-tenzing-norgay-everest-1986)

10 May 1986 Lord Hunt, leader of the 1953 expedition that made the first successful ascent of Everest, pays tribute to Tenzing Norgay

 www.theguardian.com

Tenzing Norgay better known as **Sherpa Tenzing**
Tenzing was the 11th of 13 children, several of whom died young.
Born: May 1913, Nepal/Tibet
Died: May 9, 1986 ------so he died being 73 years old.
So, very far from being old.
**How has he died?**

I’ve been listening to many of jacks podcast. In one he made the comment he saw two options (I’m paraphrasing) move north to optimize water and magnetism (I’m assuming this is the reason) or move south towards the equator (to maximize sunlight). We are stationed in Alaska with a son that has been diagnosed with uc. Since finding Jack and learning about emf, I purchased a emf meter. Needless to say I was disappointed with my findings . My kids room was on top of Wi-Fi router, it also had the highest body volt readings in the house at 6 volts. Most concerning was their trampoline located under the high voltage transmission lines in our backyard. That exceeded even the fcc safe thresholds and 82 volts/meter. If we were to move to a cleaner emf environment and practice cold thermogenesis ( which he’s doing after jack’s recommendation) and eat per our latitude( already carnivore and in remission) embrace a cleaner light indoor environment can we still safely live here?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alaska-can-it-be-done.27878/post-323610>

### pfedorow — 2023-06-28T20:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jobs-and-career-changes.27885/post-323021>

What are usually the career changes that people have done to turn around their health?

I'm feeling interested in volunteering to expand my skills or developing a non-profit to help communities as that is what my heart is saying to do, but I am curious about what other people have done on their healing journey.

I see the usual suggestions for working online but I feel I just dont feel great working on a computer for over an hour or so, which possibly might mean I need to change my setup. Any suggestions on EMF computer setups would be appreciated also.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-06-29T02:03:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jobs-and-career-changes.27885/post-323044>

I think choosing a job that puts you outside in the sun and away from emf, bad light, computers and such is more important than choosing for income. I chose ranching because I am outside every day of the year, away from population density and I think this is about as close to being a hunter/gatherer as you can get in the modern world while still kind of controlling your own destiny. I also feel working to put food on your own table is an important part of life for everyone. Paying people to do the stuff I wanted done didn't make me happy. I think you can have a positive impact on people's lives no matter what you do. Helping loads of people sounds awesome, but if I can connect with one person, I'm happy.

### jackmck — 2023-07-04T23:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jobs-and-career-changes.27885/post-323242>

Earlier this year I left my job working on the railway in Melbourne, Australia. I had no idea it was such a high-risk job... never mind the risk of getting hit by a train lol... there was way too much EMF! Yes, I worked outside on the tracks in the sun, but the EMF was killer - constantly ungrounded due to PPE, always using cell phones and radios to communicate and most 5G towers in Melbourne are close to or at railway stations. There was a lot of night shift work. So, I left and now I'm travelling. I'm planning to create my own online business, so I can do an hour at the computer and then do other things. Helping out on a farm currently. We castrated 3 pigs yesterday. Seems like the time of year for it, hey Marko Pollo!

### JanSz — 2023-07-05T00:47:10+0300

> Earlier this year I left my job working on the railway in Melbourne, Australia.

My life was highly and positively influenced by railways.
My aunt, my mom's sister was married to railway worker.
He was painting the letters on railway cars.
Other than payment for his work the company has given him a house with large garden for the life.
They took me and carried me thru high school.
My parents were farmers in the most remote sticks, very far from any schools.
My uncle, my mom's brother was a blacksmith at the railroad.
He had restaurant on the side, eventually he got very large building across the street of the railroad station.
There was a Post Office and restaurant in that building other than apartments.
My uncle helped me with the idea to immigrate from Poland to USA.
My wife convinced me that she was the right person for me.
It was hard to figure such a luck. She was beautiful, ballerina, extremely hard worker.
Why would she want me?
Her grandpa was a director of railroad in the very large city in Poland.
Germans kill him in Auschwitz for hiding Jews.
Her grandma was hosting high class clergy in her home, among it St Pope John Paul II.
Her aunt was a nun and profesor at university, Pope John Paul call her to work for him.
\---
Sorry to hear about your negative experience about Australian railroad.

So I am looking for a place to live on the short to mid term to be able to make up my mind for the near future. Do you know of lowEMF communities in EU?  Would not that be great? And HOW do you survive winter at this latitude? I discovered Dr. Kruse in January 24. Winter was not so great. How do we ground and redox in rainy/windy weather? Not to mention: how do we find an electrician who knows of these things?  Luckily lots of sun now. Great to find people nearby on this bizarre and difficult but also mindblowing journey!  Have a wonderful day!

There are no really optimal locations here in Europe with low EMFs and strong sun, to be honest with you (haha, I'm starting to sound like Jack!)
So what I did is I went to El Slavador for two months and I didn't regret it. In fact, it changed my life! Wish you all the best!

-   Tabasco, to taste (optional)

# Thread: Carnivore - well done meat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-06-29T01:46:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323043>

hi everyone,

My doctor who is treating my CIRS has recommended a carnivore diet (seafood included). The first week is beef/bison/lamb salt and water, and I’m having trouble tolerating the meat. The smell makes me so nauseous, and the thought of eating it repels me. I’m on day 3 now, and it has gotten better, but mostly because I’m slicing the beef really thin, air frying is with iodized sea salt, and eating it dark and crispy. I’m wondering if there really is danger with eating well done meat, or if the studies are innacurate/false info.

Any info is helpful! Thanks

### SunHunter ☀️ — 2023-06-29T08:37:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323051>

There are many carnivores who never eat raw meat and are doing pretty well. While eating raw meat will give you an extra boost, you can stick with crispy slices for now.

### Daulatwant — 2023-06-29T13:56:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323055>

> [Taylor Freeman said:](/goto/post?id=323043)
>
> hi everyone,
>
> My doctor who is treating my CIRS has recommended a carnivore diet (seafood included). The first week is beef/bison/lamb salt and water, and I’m having trouble tolerating the meat. The smell makes me so nauseous, and the thought of eating it repels me. I’m on day 3 now, and it has gotten better, but mostly because I’m slicing the beef really thin, air frying is with iodized sea salt, and eating it dark and crispy. I’m wondering if there really is danger with eating well done meat, or if the studies are innacurate/false info.
>
> Any info is helpful! Thanks

Slow transition to carnivore over 6-8 weeks is recommended. Full adaptation can take 20 weeks plus or minus 6.

So long as you experience issues with appetite, digestion, energy, sleep. Drinking a lot of water may help with these issues while you go through adaptation, so try keeping a habit of drinking plenty even if you don't necessarily feel like drinking.

Also on carnivore your need for salt may change. Overtime try including some meals without salt, just to switch things up. Your old habits of salt intake may not work anymore on carnivore, so giving your body a choice between salted and unsalted food will let you to form a new habit that is better suited.

You can put some beef in a pot with a lid. Cook it in the oven at 145-170F overnight. After it's done you can put a quick sear on it. Try meat cooked this way for a change.

There is no inherent problem with eating crispy salty meat. But maybe when something tastes too good, you may end up eating it when your body is not ready for it, you may overconsume on salt which can mess up your appetite, satiety and thirst even further.

Simply swapping out some of your meals for something a lot milder, can help you avoid falling into a vicious cycle.

### 5G Canary — 2023-06-30T16:10:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323080>

> eating it repels me

Have you considered Alpha-gal? I think [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) brought it up awhile back.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-06-30T20:10:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323089>

Anyone tried “Sous Vide” cooking? Put the meat in a good freezer bag, drop it in water, heat to 135F, leave for several hours even 24 (slow cooker, check water temp with thermometer), throw on a super hot pan for 1 min. Best meat ever, a chuck roast was like filet mignon.

### JanSz — 2023-06-30T21:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323094>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=323089)
>
> Anyone tried “Sous Vide” cooking? Put the meat in a good freezer bag, drop it in water, heat to 135F, leave for several hours even 24 (slow cooker, check water temp with thermometer), throw on a super hot pan for 1 min. Best meat ever, a chuck roast was like filet mignon.

Note that any talk about organic beef is really just a talk.
Cows have two stomachs, enough to take care of seed oils that they are fed with.
Infant formula is probably more dangerous from that pov.

/

### Daulatwant — 2023-06-30T21:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323099>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=323089)
>
> Anyone tried “Sous Vide” cooking? Put the meat in a good freezer bag, drop it in water, heat to 135F, leave for several hours even 24 (slow cooker, check water temp with thermometer), throw on a super hot pan for 1 min. Best meat ever, a chuck roast was like filet mignon.

Same can be done in the oven. Simply tightly pack meat into a pot with a lid. As it warms up the meat juices will come out and the meat will cook just like sous vide. It just takes a long time to get to temperature. I've done round roasts this way and it took 16 hour s to get the middle to 131. The lowest I will set the oven is 144.

I've pondered about freezing the meat juices, later submerging a new cut of meat in those juices when cooking this way.

### Niti The Farmacist — 2023-06-30T22:54:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carnivore-well-done-meat.27890/post-323105>

> [Taylor Freeman said:](/goto/post?id=323043)
>
> hi everyone,
>
> My doctor who is treating my CIRS has recommended a carnivore diet (seafood included). The first week is beef/bison/lamb salt and water, and I’m having trouble tolerating the meat. The smell makes me so nauseous, and the thought of eating it repels me. I’m on day 3 now, and it has gotten better, but mostly because I’m slicing the beef really thin, air frying is with iodized sea salt, and eating it dark and crispy. I’m wondering if there really is danger with eating well done meat, or if the studies are innacurate/false info.
>
> Any info is helpful! Thanks

There is nothing wrong with eating charred meat - charcoal is a supplement. You might have heard of ACTIVATED CHARCOAL.
Charred meat is delicious and will help gut balance. HOWEVER - the more well done, the less essential fatty acids you have access to because you are cooking it all out so be cognizant of that. Overcooked meat is also harder to digest. You may just need to start with leaner meat. NEW carnivores haven't fully fat adapted and so leaner meats like chicken with skin on are more palatable. then work your way to fattier meats like pork, beef, lamb etc. Regenerative meats are optimal and less nauseating for many reasons. so there is also that point. Stay the course. Blessings on your journey!

My other thought is that maybe this is low stomach acid/enzymes and that is why the meat is so unappetizing.

Hemoglobin normal range:

Male (ages 15+): 13.0 - 17.0 g/dL
Female (ages 15+): 11.5 - 15.5 g/dL
Hematocrit normal range:

Male: 40 - 55%
Female: 36 - 48%

# Thread: Sunlight and Skin Cancer
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-and-skin-cancer.27891/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### grillosun — 2023-06-29T10:44:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-and-skin-cancer.27891/post-323052>

Hi All, I'm new to all this from Spain. I got a Basal cell carcinoma taken out a couple of years back. Since then my dermatologists (3 different ones and supposedly the best ones) put me in a high-risk profile with more than 50 melanocyte-type moles and I have to go for photo monitoring every 4-6 months. Of course, they scared me as hell and told me I should absolutely not expose to sunlight that much. And I have taken that very seriously. No sun for me at all! Now Im scared I've made things worse. Where could I find more info on a more balanced treatment? Thank you all in advance, I have a gut feeling this is the right path. Love. Andres.

### coho3131 — 2023-06-30T02:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-and-skin-cancer.27891/post-323073>

Welcome!

You just pitched us a "fast ball right down the middle".

I would suggest reading more about non-native EMF, oxidative stress, melanin, and the anti-tumor effects of vitamin D synthesis in the skin to get you closer to where you would like to be. The articles below might be helpful to get you started.

### [The Vitamin D Receptor as Tumor Suppressor in Skin - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32918224/)

Cutaneous malignancies including melanomas and keratinocyte carcinomas (KC) are the most common types of cancer, occurring at a rate of over one million per year in the United States. KC, which include both basal cell carcinomas and squamous cell carcinomas, are substantially more common than...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### [The Role of Classical and Novel Forms of Vitamin D in the Pathogenesis and Progression of Nonmelanoma Skin Cancers - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32918223/)

Nonmelanoma skin cancers including basal and squamous cell carcinomas (SCC and BCC) represent a significant clinical problem due to their relatively high incidence, imposing an economic burden to healthcare systems around the world. It is accepted that ultraviolet radiation (UVR: λ = 290-400 nm)...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### [Protective actions of vitamin D in UVB induced skin cancer - PMC](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3501582/)

Non-melanoma skin cancers (NMSC) are the most common type of cancer, occurring at a rate of over 1 million per year in the United States. Although their metastatic potential is generally low, they can and do metastasize, especially in the immune ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Saichi — 2023-06-30T19:32:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-and-skin-cancer.27891/post-323087>

You need to give up wifi for good and mitigate nnemf to building biology standards as much as possible then SG preferably starting with sunrises.

# Thread: Optic nerve pigmentation on fundus exam
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optic-nerve-pigmentation-on-fundus-exam.27892/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dockeiser — 2023-06-29T14:33:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optic-nerve-pigmentation-on-fundus-exam.27892/post-323056>

I apologize if there is a better place for this to land… I’m a member on Patreon as well, but thought this might be the better venue.

I am new to this group, but when I saw Jack’s insistence on using OCTs and a retinal examination it piqued my interest.

I have been using OCT’s and full-field funduscopic pictures for a while now to supplement my bedside exam in the setting of evaluating patients with autonomic syndromes.

I have been noticing pigmentation in the optic nerve in cases on fundoscopy and have been frustrated that I haven’t been able to locate much in the peer reviewed literature to suggest why this is (other than that it’s a natural finding of skin pigmentation).

Hearing Jack talk about migration of melanin, really resonated with what I was seeing and I'm hoping some of you out there might have more insight on this topic than I do.

I am sharing a couple of fundus images here. If you want, take a look and see if we are on the same page here. One image has a speckling of black dots in the optic nerve, the other has a dark ring deposition around the edge of the nerve (more common finding). It would be great to put some context to what I'm seeing.

Again if there is a better place to ask this question, let me know.

Thanks


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-testosterone-associated-with-increased-mortality.27896/post-323114>

testosterone below 250 ng/dL is associated with almost doubled mortality rate.

Men with the lowest testosterone, below 241 ng/dl, were **40% more likely to die**.

Reduction in Mortality with Testosterone Replacement

Testosterone Therapy Reduces Cardiovascular Risk

Unfounded Fears of Testosterone Replacement

Risks Associated with Testosterone Replacement
Elevated Hematocrit from Testosterone
Few peoples have this risk, most often of Celtic descent. ( [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) ???) polycythemia
polycythemia (high hematocrit) while on testosterone should be treated with either reduction in dosage, or therapeutic phlebotomy.

When dealing with high hematocrit, watch out for excessive liver consumption.

Personally, I use testosterone since about 2000.
Found that daily Androgel 1.62% works best for me.
At first Androgel was very expensive, but it fell in price now.

Other than doctor required testing I use DUTCHtest.com to figure out my ways.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/needless-fear-of-estrogen-replacement-for-menopause-or-before-it.27897/post-323116>

reduced mortality in the estrogen users (50-59 yrs) after hysterectomy.
27% reduction in mortality with estrogen use.

women should avoid Progestins and other synthetic hormones
use natural, bioidentical estrogen and progesterone in the proper balance is the preferred choice.

Women using hormone replacement therapy were:

1) Thirty percent less likely to die than those not on hormone therapy.
2) Twenty percent more likely to have a coronary calcium score of zero (the lowest possible score, indicating a low likelihood of heart attack)
3) Thirty-six percent less likely to have a coronary calcium score that indicates extensive atherosclerosis and a 10-fold increase in heart attack risk.

Use DUTCHtest.com when monitoring hormone replacement.
Consider monitoring and adjusting all hormones that are tested there.
Note, above includes testosterone.
Use DUTCHtest extensive educational webinars.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-why-we-should-eat-local-definition-of-local-food-is-codified-somehow-by-the-photoelectric-effect.27907/post-323195>

\>eNOS directly inactivates the function of hypothalamic NPY! NPY is stimulated by carbohydrates in the hypothalamus and it drives carbohydrates cravings and food seeking behaviors. Why would mammals and humans have this hardwired into their DNA and in their brains? Evolution says it is biologically impossible to find carbohydrates in chronic freezing cold conditions because these foods require serious amounts of photons and electrons from sunlight. When it is chronically cold this is not possible in nature, unless an extinction event is ongoing. That is the short answer. **This implies that our food is codified somehow by the photoelectric effect.** There are massive benefits to cell membrane signaling when carbs are excluded in a cold environment. Cell membranes have to exclude carbohydrates in cold to function well. This is one part of the reason why diabetics get neuropathy and many other illnesses from glycation.

\>food is codified somehow by the photoelectric effect

How is it codified? How does the body know? How can we test this? How many miles from my home should I consider local and why? same latitude but food from california in florida? does that make sense? Where can learn more about this

### coho3131 — 2023-07-04T19:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-why-we-should-eat-local-definition-of-local-food-is-codified-somehow-by-the-photoelectric-effect.27907/post-323231>

I think the goal should be as close to your exact latitude, longitude, and elevation as possible, and only in-season food. Of course, none of this will matter that much if you are indoors most of the day. Also, because of all the artificial light we are exposed to, sources of DHA might be important to include even if you can't find them locally. My theory is that if we lived outside all of the time with no nnEMF, then we might not need to supplement with non-local DHA. Unfortunately, if there are a lot of people around, there will be a lot of nnEMF. The nnEMF also creates a situation where lower carbohydrate intake can have benefits, and even in-season fruit consumption can have consequences.

I think this topic is related to Xenohormesis.

### [Xenohormesis: health benefits from an eon of plant stress response evolution - PMC](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3024065/)

Xenohormesis is a biological principle that explains how environmentally stressed plants produce bioactive compounds that can confer stress resistance and survival benefits to animals that consume them. Animals can piggyback off products of plants' ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

The attached articles support in-season eating.

### fredrick347 — 2023-07-04T21:05:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-why-we-should-eat-local-definition-of-local-food-is-codified-somehow-by-the-photoelectric-effect.27907/post-323239>
