### SunHunter ☀️ — 2023-07-05T08:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-why-we-should-eat-local-definition-of-local-food-is-codified-somehow-by-the-photoelectric-effect.27907/post-323252>

Hi Fredrick, great questions! Here is what I understood so far:
Food is codified by photosynthesis in plants. The code is still intact in meat. The code used is H+ vs. Deuterium. Yes, it's a binary code and it sits within molecules. The information in the code is the exact location, time & season. Our mitochondria decode it and inform the many clocks, so every process gets synced. This synchronization enables quantum entanglement.

The Kruse for Dummies Workshop was all about that topic.

However I don't know how to measure it except with an MRI.

I'm trying to get everything local within 100km. There is no local seafood here in southern Germany, but seafood is ok if it comes from a higher latitude than your position. Some food items are hard to get or substitute: coffee, parmesan, olive oil, lemon, ginger, chili.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-05T16:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-why-we-should-eat-local-definition-of-local-food-is-codified-somehow-by-the-photoelectric-effect.27907/post-323262>

> [fredrick347 said:](/goto/post?id=323239)
>
> wonder if we change the "light signature" of our food by eating outside

More like - we improve our body's ability to decode the light signature of the food if we eat outside (further decreasing mismatches)

The light signature of the food itself is only determined by the frequencies/intensities of light energy it received before it was harvested/slain.

On top of the above.
Be aware of "Organic" foods.
The name sounds delicious but

Organic animals are still fed with seed oils, so they could grow fast.
Organic vegetation uses "natural" fertilizers.
Those fertilizers are feces and urine.
They are taken from where it is available, which is Intensive animal farming. (And that is legal by the law.)
That is a lot of feces, urine and antibiotics and other extras.

So "organic" meat or vegetation is really a lipstick on a pig.

# Thread: Does anyone know of a monitor w/ internal controls to remove blue light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Katie P — 2023-07-04T04:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/post-323215>

I currently use f.lux and Iris on my personal computers but I have to use a work computer and I'm not allowed to install software on it which isn't approved. Since I can replace the monitors I'd like to find the best solution to this problem. Yet I haven't found a monitor which offers screen controls to do something similar to what f.lux or Iris does. Those hokey blue light filters which can be turned on don't do crap. My last resort will be wearing my red filter glasses which nearly block out all blue light which isn't the worst solution. So if anyone has any recommendations I'd really appreciate it!

Ideally, I'd not work for this organization but I'm legally bound till my contract is out and then I'm out.

### Mal Pal — 2023-07-04T17:32:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/post-323226>

Could try blue light filter screen covers.

### annelies_1 — 2023-07-04T18:08:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/post-323228>

Following. I was also wondering if there's any difference between old monitors (the big ones) and flat screens.

### Katie P — 2023-07-05T20:53:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/post-323288>

> [Mal Pal said:](/goto/post?id=323226)
>
> Could try blue light filter screen covers.

Thanks, great recommendation. I looked at those but they all seem to appear clear. Do you know of any that have a yellow, orange, or red tint to them? My clear blue light-blocking glasses are also clear and don't do a thorough job vs. my red blue light-blocking EMR-TEK glasses definitely filter all light except red. I would imagine a good-quality screen cover would have a visible tint to it.

### Rocky — 2023-07-13T16:20:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/post-323655>

You can go to 'Display Settings' and adjust the screen warmth or temperature there. Go to desktop, right click and you will see display settings, click on that to adjust.

### Mal Pal — 2023-07-26T00:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/post-324313>

[https://www.amazon.com/dp/B071G2XTD9](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B071G2XTD9?psc=1&ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_product_details) This works and screen is readable, tested personally. Theres others like it so if you want all red.

### Katie P — 2023-08-18T16:15:41+0300

I like this idea for short-term situations like traveling or working in different office locations.

### Katie P — 2023-08-18T16:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-of-a-monitor-w-internal-controls-to-remove-blue-light.27909/post-325284>

Thank you for all your help. I was able to find a monitor with internal controls which allow me to drop the blue and green from the screen without requiring the computer software to do so. I'm sharing this for those in similar situations such as government workers. Benq makes a $250 monitor model GW2785TC which is 27" and fits most applications but has really good internal monitor controls. Since I'm limited to spending $700 for work monitors per policy I can get two of these and they work perfectly to save my eyes from blue light in the office.

I found the monitor by researching BenQ's options and bought it off Amazon here: [https://amzn.to/3QPNYKR](https://amzn.to/3QPNYKR).

# Thread: How can we measure dirty electricity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-we-measure-dirty-electricity.27911/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SunHunter ☀️ — 2023-07-04T20:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-we-measure-dirty-electricity.27911/post-323238>

Never found anything helpful on the internet.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-04T22:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-we-measure-dirty-electricity.27911/post-323240>

Dirty Electricity (“DE”) can be detected with a medium wave radio. It can be measured with an EMI meter (Greenwave or Graham/Stetzer) the best and most accurate way is with an oscilloscope. “DE” is a VERY small % of the AC electric field it rides on and the AC 60Hz (50Hz in Europe) field is far worse then the “DE”. Kill the circuit and you kill the electric field with any attached “DE”. [https://liveemfsafe.com/de](https://liveemfsafe.com/de)

### SunHunter ☀️ — 2023-07-05T17:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-we-measure-dirty-electricity.27911/post-323265>

Thanks Anthony! Great presentation btw. I learned a LOT. Measured the EF as you suggested as body voltage with the multimeter. Turning off the circuit decreases body voltage on my bed from 280 mV to 60 mV. Now I need a kill switch here in Germany.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-equipment.27913/post-323271>

As far as I understand, it’s good to have the following measuring tools to start biohacking my living place:
\- spectrophotometer
\- RF meter
\- emf meter
\- Gauss meter

Am I missing something?

Also, any good recommendations for what to buy? (Europe)

### JanSz — 2023-07-05T17:41:06+0300

.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-05T19:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-equipment.27913/post-323283>

Respectfully disagree Jansz. We have helped many people in apartments significantly reduce their nnEMF. Readings in apartments differ greatly. Measuring lets you know what is there so you can make an informed decision and do something about it. 70% - 80% reduction in any situation is a big improvement. Even if the decision ends up being to move, you need measuring equipment to make sure the new place is better. I would add a body voltage meter to your list 1000x more accurate than a regular electric meter. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### annelies_1 — 2023-07-05T20:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-equipment.27913/post-323284>

Ye I would never make the decision to move before having quantified my current situation. But I don’t live in an apartment.

Thanks Anthony!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/measuring-equipment.27913/post-323302>

Get a meter and see how much emf comes from your own tech gear. Don't worry about towers till you clean up your own life. Then test and move.

### Lindman112 — 2023-07-21T15:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-developing-knees-and-back-and-ankles-and-hips-as-20-28-29-30-year-old-players.27916/post-324077>

Before the internet I think most of us, especially young were in great shape.

The only problem today is, unlike myself my nieces and nephews are spending 4 hours a day on the ipad and another 6 hours in the computer.

Bring the kids outside and help them enjoy activities and all knee & back problems go away, ofcourse it's not as easy as it seems because
even if you're a great parent guiding your child on the right track then the other parents might not do that, and that will lead to your child feeling isolated.

It's a very tricky situation, but all we can do is try and spread as much awareness as possible and do the best for our own kids.

Befriend your neighbors and encourage sports and outdoor activities.

# Thread: Quantum biology vs randomised controlled trials
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biology-vs-randomised-controlled-trials.27919/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### annelies_1 — 2023-07-06T13:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biology-vs-randomised-controlled-trials.27919/post-323312>

Clinical studies use statistics to proof hypotheses. But when you statistically proof something, you rely upon the assumption that the universe behaves randomly.

That assumption is no longer true in the area of quantum mechanics. Doesn’t the reliance upon randomised controlled trials in medicine then pose a paradox for when ppl try to understand quantum biology?

I don’t want to downgrade clinical trials or the use of statistics. I think it’s the best we can do when we’re trying to formulate strategies that need to be applicable to large amounts of people. but am I right in thinking there’s a paradox here?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-07T18:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biology-vs-randomised-controlled-trials.27919/post-323381>

If studies were done in the wrong environment (a majority of them - under artificial light), with the wrong species (diurnal vs nocturnal), in the wrong time of year (season), the results can skew what is reality for humans.

The resulting statistics also don't matter if the experiment didn't begin with a good premise or good questions in mind. For example, if a study is seeking to find ways to lower cholesterol, we should step back and ask why are we even trying to lower cholesterol. Is this even the correct goal we should strive for? (Absolutely NO) The statistical results of such a study should be thrown in the dumpster bc it has a faulty premise. Lots of studies fall into this category.

This is why most RCTs should be downgraded. They are not truly the holy grail they are heralded to be.

### JanSz — 2023-07-07T21:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biology-vs-randomised-controlled-trials.27919/post-323396>

### [An Increase in the Omega-6/Omega-3 Fatty Acid Ratio Increases the Risk for Obesity](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4808858/)

In the past three decades, total fat and saturated fat intake as a percentage of total calories has continuously decreased in Western diets, while the intake of omega-6 fatty acid increased and the omega-3 fatty acid decreased, resulting in a large increase ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

An Increase in the Omega-6/Omega-3 Fatty Acid Ratio Increases the Risk for Obesity

**In the past three decades,** total fat and saturated fat intake as a percentage of total calories has continuously decreased in Western diets, while the intake of omega-6 fatty acid increased and the omega-3 fatty acid decreased, resulting in a large increase in the omega-6/omega-3 ratio **from 1:1 during evolution to 20:1 today or even higher.** This change in the composition of fatty acids parallels a significant increase in the prevalence of overweight and obesity.

### JanSz — 2023-07-07T21:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biology-vs-randomised-controlled-trials.27919/post-323398>

There are steep, hidden costs to CAFO chicken, including environmental costs, human
health costs and ethical considerations

Current-day CAFO chickens contain more fat than protein and have lower amounts of
omega-3 fats and higher omega-6 than they used to. **Previous research has linked diets
high in omega-6 to a rise in obesity**

Another significant hazard linked to CAFO chicken is the spread of antibiotic-resistant
disease, **specifically urinary tract infections (UTIs), including drug-resistant UTIs, which
are on the rise**

Studies have conclusively demonstrated that a majority of UTIs are caused by exposure
to contaminated chicken; American, Canadian and European studies have all confirmed
close genetic matches between drug-resistant E. coli collected from human patients and
those found on poultry

### annelies_1 — 2023-07-18T19:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biology-vs-randomised-controlled-trials.27919/post-323895>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=323381)
>
> If studies were done in the wrong environment (a majority of them - under artificial light), with the wrong species (diurnal vs nocturnal), in the wrong time of year (season), the results can skew what is reality for humans.
>
> The resulting statistics also don't matter if the experiment didn't begin with a good premise or good questions in mind. For example, if a study is seeking to find ways to lower cholesterol, we should step back and ask why are we even trying to lower cholesterol. Is this even the correct goal we should strive for? (Absolutely NO) The statistical results of such a study should be thrown in the dumpster bc it has a faulty premise. Lots of studies fall into this category.
>
> This is why most RCTs should be downgraded. They are not truly the holy grail they are heralded to be.

Ye absolutely doing studies on nocturnal animals under blue light doesn’t make any sense at all. I actually can’t even wrap my brain around the fact that REALLY everything people rely on are those studies. How can nobody have made that remark earlier? It’s just mind boggling. Anyway

Your comment about the premise is interesting. When I was in my eye surgeon’s office challenging his statement that IOLs couldn’t potentially have a negative impact on neurochemistry, he said: “there is no evidence that these IOLs could have an impact on your psychology. There have been done studies on thousands of people about this and nothing of that kind could be concluded.” But that’s when I was thinking about what Jack said in the Huberman podcast. What’s his measuring stick if that’s the question being investigated? Nature definitely isn’t. If that’s the question being investigated, the measuring stick is more something like the average poor mental health of that sample of people. And I don’t compare myself to these people. My measure stick is more like the hypothetical best version of myself when I’m placed in an environment that’s optimal, which you could call nature?

# Thread: Dutch test for troubleshooting low metabolized cortisol (cortisol clearance)- thyroid?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-for-troubleshooting-low-metabolized-cortisol-cortisol-clearance-thyroid.27923/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 44
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stel — 2023-07-06T20:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-for-troubleshooting-low-metabolized-cortisol-cortisol-clearance-thyroid.27923/post-323338>

Hi everyone!
When I did my annual blood work last year, my T and Free T were on the lower end of the range for a 40yr old (414 ng/dL)
however, I was almost 30% body fat and I thought I'd increase my T by losing fat and building muscle (with a small calorie deficit). However after losing several pounds of fat and building muscle over the last year (now ~ 15%BF at 175lb), my T seems to have dropped even lower (without any other life changes.. normal sleep, etc). This led me to start researching potential reasons for low testosterone, and I came across the dutch test. I just got my results back and they validate that my testosterone is low. my current research is pointing me into two areas:

1) vericocele - after visiting the urologist through a physical exam he confirmed I have grade 1 varicocele (grade 1 can only be diagnosed when straining (valsalva maneuver). does anyone have thoughts on addressing this?
2) liver function - if my liver is not working properly this may be affecting my thyroid (free t3/t4 seems within range, but tsh is high at around 4.5), and as a result cholesterol seems high?

attaching the results from blood work (30% vs 15%bf) as well as the dutch test results in case anyone is curious what the dutch test is showing. of course if anyone has ideas about interpreting some of these results I'd definitely appreciate pointers as well.

thank you

### JanSz — 2023-07-07T00:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-for-troubleshooting-low-metabolized-cortisol-cortisol-clearance-thyroid.27923/post-323351>

40yr old male

High homocysteine
15mg twice weekly L-Methylfolate 15 MG

Low DHEAs
100 mg DHEA once daily
High LH but testicles not able to produce more Testosterone.
Consider replacing all your testosterone supply with
2 pumps of Androgel 1.62% daily
That will
make you mostly sterile, so think of that.
That within about a year will cause your testicles and scrotum to disappear.
To prevent that use
250 iu HCG subcutaneous injections twice weekly.

If you would implement above
within about six months redo all your tests as you shown them on your post above.

Faraday pouch blocks the signal getting in or out, why not just turn it off? If your phone is on and can’t find a connection it ramps up its signal to max. Same with a cover, if the blocking material is on the back of the phone away from you (while texting etc) it can increase your exposure. When talking you always want the block between you and the phone with the back open so the phone can connect at the lowest power possible. You can DM me or all my contact info is on the bottom of our website.

### stel — 2023-07-11T20:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-for-troubleshooting-low-metabolized-cortisol-cortisol-clearance-thyroid.27923/post-323540>

a quick update - I noticed in the test notes the below comment about the urine tests' cortisol results:
"The patient shows significantly higher free cortisol compared to metabolized cortisol. It may be advisable to check thyroid hormones if you have not. Overall free cortisol levels are within range, but metabolized cortisol (the best marker for overall cortisol production) is low. This implies that overall HPA-Axis is low. Cortisol clearance may be a bit sluggish, which keeps free cortisol levels within range in spite of low overall production. Hypothyroidism and other conditions may lead to slow cortisol metabolism. If treating the patient for potential thyroid issues be sure to take into account the interplay between the thyroid and adrenals."
based on this I am looking at my thyroid in more detail, as I am getting regular free t3/free t4 but elevated tsh, which could be for many different reasons

_from their_ [_faq_](https://dutchtest.com/video/cortisol-tutorial/)_
What increases or decreases cortisol metabolism/clearance?
Cortisol and cortisone are metabolized by 5a-reductase and 5b-reductase (and then by another enzyme) to tetrahydrocortisol and tetrahydrocortisone for excretion.
This metabolism process is particularly increased (sped up) in obesity, high insulin and hyperthyroid. It may also be that long-term exposure to cortisol (long-term stress, supplementation) or other glucocorticoids speed this process up.
This metabolism process is slowed in cases of hypothyroidism, anorexia or impaired liver function.
There is also a process of metabolism where by cortisol is deactivated to cortisone (and also reactivated back to cortisol from cortisone). For the purposes of clarity, we will consider this process “deactivation” and “activation” and not “metabolism.”_

**Eat whole milk, heavy cream, lots of butter.**

### Published: 18 May 2020​

###

### [Efficacy of dietary odd-chain saturated fatty acid pentadecanoic acid parallels broad associated health benefits in humans: could it be essential? - Scientific Reports](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-64960-y#:~:text=Dietary%20odd-chain%20saturated%20fatty%20acids%20%28OCFAs%29%20are%20present,intake%20of%20OCFAs%20is%20associated%20with%20lower%20mortality.)

Dietary odd-chain saturated fatty acids (OCFAs) are present in trace levels in dairy fat and some fish and plants. Higher circulating concentrations of OCFAs, pentadecanoic acid (C15:0) and heptadecanoic acid (C17:0), are associated with lower risks of cardiometabolic diseases, and higher...

 www.nature.com

​

### Efficacy of dietary odd-chain saturated fatty acid pentadecanoic acid parallels broad associated health benefits in humans: could it be essential?​

Dietary odd-chain saturated fatty acids (OCFAs) are present in trace levels in dairy fat and some fish and plants.
Higher circulating concentrations of OCFAs, **pentadecanoic acid (C15:0**) and **heptadecanoic acid (C17:0)**, are associated with lower risks of cardiometabolic diseases, and higher dietary intake of OCFAs is associated with lower mortality.

C15:0 as an active dietary fatty acid that attenuates inflammation, anemia, dyslipidemia, and fibrosis in vivo, potentially by binding to key metabolic regulators and repairing mitochondrial function.

In the U.S., during the 20-year period following this initial recommendation, the average individual’s daily intake of whole fat milk was reduced more than 4-fold, **from 283 to 65 grams per day,** in an effort to lower dietary saturated fats2,3. Despite this drop in whole fat milk intake, the global prevalence of obesity, type 2 diabetes, metabolic syndrome, and nonalcoholic fatty liver disease has increased4,5,6.

### The.Naked.Doctor — 2023-08-23T07:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dutch-test-for-troubleshooting-low-metabolized-cortisol-cortisol-clearance-thyroid.27923/post-325450>

> [stel said:](/goto/post?id=323338)
>
> Hi everyone!
> When I did my annual blood work last year, my T and Free T were on the lower end of the range for a 40yr old (414 ng/dL)
> however, I was almost 30% body fat and I thought I'd increase my T by losing fat and building muscle (with a small calorie deficit). However after losing several pounds of fat and building muscle over the last year (now ~ 15%BF at 175lb), my T seems to have dropped even lower (without any other life changes.. normal sleep, etc). This led me to start researching potential reasons for low testosterone, and I came across the dutch test. I just got my results back and they validate that my testosterone is low. my current research is pointing me into two areas:
>
> 1) vericocele - after visiting the urologist through a physical exam he confirmed I have grade 1 varicocele (grade 1 can only be diagnosed when straining (valsalva maneuver). does anyone have thoughts on addressing this?
> 2) liver function - if my liver is not working properly this may be affecting my thyroid (free t3/t4 seems within range, but tsh is high at around 4.5), and as a result cholesterol seems high?
>
> attaching the results from blood work (30% vs 15%bf) as well as the dutch test results in case anyone is curious what the dutch test is showing. of course if anyone has ideas about interpreting some of these results I'd definitely appreciate pointers as well.
>
> thank you

Hi Stel ,
A couple of questions..., are you a mouth breather (a huge factor in low T), snore or have sleep apnea? the waking cortisol, on DUTCH, usually is representative of overnight production so I suspect something overnight is stressing your physiology out.

The metabolized cortisol is representative of total production so that, along with lowish DHEAs- VS DHEA to me suggests issues with inflammation (CRP is only a marker of IL6-driven inflammation so its not really fair to say no inflammation if someone has a low hscrp).

Where do you live in the Bahamas? I know we had MASSIVE issues with air quality with the dump being constantly on fire in Nassau when I was still living there. This spewing toxic fumes over the island when I was still in practice caused lots of issues in my CIRS and chronically low redox patients.

Your dopa/NA metabolites are also low, as well as your morning-free cortisol also suggests that you (potentially) are not getting good immune regulation via your body's natural steroid. (you need appropriate cortisol to help remove autoimmune cells)

Have you been checked for thyroid Antibodies?

Are you getting AM sun? That's the best thing for dopa and T as well as thyroid...you look pretty well melanated but get some sun on the boys in the morning ;P

The water in the Bahamas SUCKS, tap water is super chlorinated and the Aquapure etc all in plastic bottles will constantly be dosing you with xenoestrogens...

The high LDL/lowish D3 and overall lowish sex steroid hormones all suggest you're not making pregnenolone from your cholesterol and hormones and the entire steroidogenic pathway is blocked.

I'd be most concerned with the apoB being high vs total chol.. omega 3 is a great tool for lowering this as well as improving your ability to harness the energy of the sun (id want to see vitD at least 80)...

Sun will lower your LDL and promote steroidogenesis/ sex hormones.
Regarding,. weight loss, we used to say in environmental medicine, the body's solution to pollution is dilution- meaning fat-soluble toxicants are stored in adipose, so weight loss can burden organs of elimination..why do you say your liver isn't working? Are you itchy? slimy/smelly poos?

However after losing several pounds of fat and building muscle over the last year (now ~ 15%BF at 175lb), my T seems to have dropped even lower (without any other life changes.. normal sleep, etc). I have been reviewing the material at the Dutch test website and the part that jumps out is my cortisol clearance/metabolism:

You will notice the free cortisol and total cortisol and dhea are within range, however the metabolized cortisol is below range:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

in more detail the measurements are here:

I am wondering if this could be caused by subclinical hypothyroidism. Even though my thyroid blood work is:
TSH 4.37 (my average TSH has been ~4 over the past 10 years, however free t3 and free t4 well within range)
Total T4: 6.7 mcg/dL
Free T4: 1.2 ng/dL
Total T3: 89 ng/dL
Free T3: 3.9 pg/mL
Reverse T3: 18.6 ng/dL

Here's the plot twist- I have the homozygous Thr92Ala-DIO2 genotype which appears to have clinical significance as it affects the deiodinase 2 activity:

DIO2 (deiodinase 2) is also involved in converting T4 to T3, mainly in the skeletal muscles, central nervous system, pituitary, thyroid, heart, and brown fat. [_"carriers of the Thr92Ala-DIO2 polymorphism exhibit lower D2 catalytic activity and localized/systemic hypothyroidism_](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37150515/)_. This could explain why certain groups of levothyroxine-treated hypothyroid patients have improved quality of life when also treated with liothyronine (LT3). Furthermore, Ala92-D2 was abnormally found in the Golgi apparatus, what could constitute a disease mechanism independent of T3 signaling."_
as well as _"T[ype 2 Deiodinase Thr92Ala Polymorphism and Aging Are Associated with a Decreased Pituitary Sensitivity to Thyroid Hormone](https://www.liebertpub.com/doi/abs/10.1089/thy.2022.0472)"_

My question is, would subclinical hypothyroidism potentially explain the big difference in cortisol metabolism/clearance, or could it be some enzymatic block in cyp enzymes - or something completely different?

# Thread: Need grounding advice! Beginner.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-grounding-advice-beginner.27925/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lindman112 — 2023-07-07T09:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-grounding-advice-beginner.27925/post-323367>

Does anyone have good grounding advice? I've found that grounding is probably one of the strongest anti-stress activities availble in the world.

I have a few places i've tried out that works and others that doesn't.

Sitting near water or a lake with bare feet on the ground for a good ten minutes or more (I live far from the ocean) works best. I can literally feel the electrons rushing up my legs and removing all stress caused by probably EMF and spending time indoors.

I've also tried areas that are more rocky near cliffs and so forth and I feel barely nothing, also dry areas.

Grassy areas with dirt that is especially damp and wet always works.

I have not yet tried grounding on the beach near water as Dr.Jack recommends, little to no beaches where I live sadly.

Any other advice? Thanks!

### Sue-UK — 2023-07-07T14:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-grounding-advice-beginner.27925/post-323369>

There are more sweat glands per inch of skin on the feet than anywhere else on the body, so the way I look at it is if the environment I'm in doesn't promote sweating, the terrain I'm grounding on needs to be damp, or at least not bone dry. I live by the sea now, but even if I'm not at the beach just sitting outside our home I can sprinkle water on the dry ground underneath my feet, or just wet my feet. We get a fair bit of rain, so only need to do it during a prolonged dry spell. Being so close to the sea we have very few trees but when I lived in the countryside, I would also ground through a tree, which also emits IR, DH was getting grounding through the hands through gardening. The best grounding is done outside in nature, but I also use an under sheet grounding mat at night, but the cable goes out through a window and the earthing rod is in soil. From memory the grounding from the mat is supposed to kick in after about half an hour, when the skin's respiration slightly dampens the sheet.

### Lindman112 — 2023-07-07T20:14:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-grounding-advice-beginner.27925/post-323391>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=323369)
>
> There are more sweat glands per inch of skin on the feet than anywhere else on the body, so the way I look at it is if the environment I'm in doesn't promote sweating, the terrain I'm grounding on needs to be damp, or at least not bone dry. I live by the sea now, but even if I'm not at the beach just sitting outside our home I can sprinkle water on the dry ground underneath my feet, or just wet my feet. We get a fair bit of rain, so only need to do it during a prolonged dry spell. Being so close to the sea we have very few trees but when I lived in the countryside, I would also ground through a tree, which also emits IR, DH was getting grounding through the hands through gardening. The best grounding is done outside in nature, but I also use an under sheet grounding mat at night, but the cable goes out through a window and the earthing rod is in soil. From memory the grounding from the mat is supposed to kick in after about half an hour, when the skin's respiration slightly dampens the sheet.

I see that is very interesting, I haven't done enough research but I had a hunch that is the way it works. Probably something to do with magnetism and electricity currents that are more easily transferred via water.

I thought the only benefit of grounding near the sea is that you can actually see the sunrise, I can't sadly as the sun rises around 2 AM in northern scandinavia and im surrounded by forests so I can't see the sun clearly until around 9 AM.

Thanks for the advice! I've been hesitant to try out a grounding sleeping mat for the night as i've heard that some people get disrupted sleep but that could be other causes, have you only had good experiences with the grounding during the night?

### caroline — 2023-07-07T20:31:51+0300

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-07T23:17:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-grounding-advice-beginner.27925/post-323402>

> [Lindman112 said:](/goto/post?id=323367)

>

> Grassy areas with dirt that is especially damp and wet always works.
>
> I have not yet tried grounding on the beach near water as Dr.Jack recommends, little to no beaches where I live sadly.
>
> Any other advice? Thanks!

We are developing equipment to measure ground voltage for safe grounding/earthing. Testing close to buildings/homes (small town, little industry close by) we are finding AC voltage in the ground. That said the further away from the building the AC drops. Ground wires and water lines can carry AC so even away from a building there can still be AC in the ground. Our bodies and nature are DC, nnEMF electric fields in buildings are AC. Grounding/earthing makes you part of the circuit - DC good, AC not so good, which is why grounding inside in an AC field is counter productive. I just came back from testing the Oregon coast (beach) and the Redwoods - no AC in the ground. Get outside as far from buildings a possible, I’m going to be doing some river tests locally, will keep you posted.

### Sue-UK — 2023-07-09T16:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-grounding-advice-beginner.27925/post-323480>

> [Lindman112 said:](/goto/post?id=323391)
>

>
> Thanks for the advice! I've been hesitant to try out a grounding sleeping mat for the night as i've heard that some people get disrupted sleep but that could be other causes, have you only had good experiences with the grounding during the night?

I've had good experiences so far. I've lived in 5 apartments (all low population density and no 5G) since I first started grounding overnight, always using an outside grounding rod, placed in areas I wouldn't avoid grounding on during the day, backed up with finding out where the wiring come into the properties. Being fearful of what I'm doing will affect any result, if I'm not afraid I can judge the results better.

What I've found is that it is most noticeable when recovering from more acute exposure to nnemf like a weekend in a city, or a flight, so for me I think its generally beneficial. But if I've had a long period of chronic unremitting stress, I don't feel the benefits as much, although its possibly mitigating it a bit. I think of it as what is the context of my life at the time. A quick half hour of grounding outside to top up with electrons can quickly be depleted if I go back indoors and allow myself to be exposed to nnemf, blue light toxicity etc . Even overnight grounding is less effective generally if something in my life is chronically raising cortisol, covid restrictions for months on end and the effect on family relationships being an example, I couldn't out ground that, which led to another move when the opportunity arose.

The nnEMF many people forget about is the 60Hz AC field (50Hz UK) from the wires in the walls, so IF you are grounding at night, even with the ground wire going outside, your results will be better if you turn off the circuits to your bedroom.

Just to clarify, from what I've gathered above, as far grounding sheets, pillow and pads go through an electric socket's grounding hole (or ground pole outside for that matter), you'd be better off not grounding at all if there's 'dirty' AC voltage in the earth around your house? Meaning if you are grounding in even a relatively weak AC electric field it is deleterious and entirely counterproductive and you may as well not be grounding at all? Or is there there some small benefit to be had through grounding even there's some AC voltage present. I was all gung-ho to get an earthing pad for sleep, but I'm now deeming this to be counter-productive seeing as I live in densely populated South Florida marinated in AC fields. I guess as the logic would continue to follow, even sitting outside grounding barefoot in my backyard here in South Florida could ostensibly be deleterious if AC fields were present?

I am looking at bigger units but I a quick google search showed small ones that might work for a house or part of house. # Thread: Cycloset: Successes and possible long-term D2 receptor desensitization
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycloset-successes-and-possible-long-term-d2-receptor-desensitization.27933/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ekarge — 2023-07-09T00:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycloset-successes-and-possible-long-term-d2-receptor-desensitization.27933/post-323454>

I wanted to try and get some constructive feedback from people on any long-term successes and/or possible missteps with the use of Cycloset in restoring depleted dopamine levels to assist in the process of regaining leptin/insulin sensitivity and ultimately yoking circadian biology to natural light cycles.

Most of the Cycloset threads I’ve seen consist of older posts from 2012-2013 (very possible I just have to do more sifting), which means by now there’s likely a decade’s worth of meaningful trials/experiences/anecdotes with bromocriptine that could help guide people like myself who are considering using it as a tool for circadian biology entrainment.

In the Reddit world, specifically in the nootropics community, dopamine agonists (DAs), especially the ergot class in which bromocriptine resides, notoriously get a bad rap for tolerance issues due to long-term receptor downregulation/desensitization and subsequent DAWS (Dopamine Agonist Withdrawal Syndrome) in some cases. The basic principle is that any ligand (bromocriptine molecule in this case) that acts on a receptor longer and more potently than it normally would, will ultimately cause the receptor to desensitize/downregulate eventually over time. I’m not necessarily saying this is the case with bromocriptine as many have reported no long-term use issues but receptor downregulation is certainly something to be aware of/consider at the very least.

Overall, it can be difficult to draw conclusions and weigh the possible long-term risks/benefit of DAs from the Reddit community alone as there is not really many people on that platform using it for hacking circadian biology (most anecdotes are people using DAs for prolactinomas, anhedonia, PSSD, RLS etc.).

Look forward to possibly hearing the good, the bad and ugly in regards to Cycloset from others in this community. Would be great to hear from some of the OGs ('Original Gangsters' for you older folks) on this forum a decade later on their experiences/ostensible successes. And as the usual disclaimer/adage goes, Cycloset isn’t for everyone so do your due diligence by reading up/researching and conferring with a doc to decide if bromocriptine might make sense for your n=1 and journey towards optimal. Nothing but gratitude in advance for anyone who cares to impart any words of wisdom, their opinion, or perspective.

### JanSz — 2023-07-09T17:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycloset-successes-and-possible-long-term-d2-receptor-desensitization.27933/post-323482>

When using Cycloset always have good number of candies (sugar) ready with you (just in case).
Cycloset reduces blood sugar (significantly).
Probably better way of taking it would be right after or during meal.

Unless you measured and know that you have higher prolactin
I would stay away from it
and
Bromocriptine.
..
Not sure what to think about pineal tumors.
Is really the life after surgery any better?

///

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preparing-seafood-best-omega-3-preservation.27934/post-323474>

So maybe a lot of people asking themselves in which way the seafood should be prepared to get the most out of the DHA content, too.
Does some people actually have some data, how DHA react to different preparing methods?

Methods that come in mind are freezing, cooking, smoking, raw and of course canned.

### JanSz — 2023-07-09T14:50:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preparing-seafood-best-omega-3-preservation.27934/post-323479>

I do test my fatty acids for the last 20 or more years.
At first at Genova Diagnostics latter at LabCorp.
I have found that generally there is no need to worry about DHA and EPA.
They are always at excellent levels (but no guarantee, there are always outliers).
Different story with AA. Arachidonic Acid.
Always on weaker side.

Recently I have learned the reason why Omega-3 is in vogue.
A whole human population is fed with extremely high amounts of seed oils whose major component is Linoleic Acid.

We need major (and honest) (and open) discussion about how to deal with this subject.

(and honest) (and open) not involving someones income
It is not going to be easy

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-in-summer.27936/post-323497>

In summer for long days, when the sunrise & sunset are less than 8 hours apart, is it ok to not get 8 hour of sleep? Like in summer is it ok if we only get 6 hours of sleep in order to see sunrise & set?

### coho3131 — 2023-07-11T02:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-in-summer.27936/post-323517>

Last light to first light should still get you close to 8 hours in Dublin, right?

### Lindman112 — 2023-07-11T12:25:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-in-summer.27936/post-323523>

I live in northern scandinavia and the sun never sets, it's rough in the summer but you should still be able to get 8 hours of sleep.

Actually last night I only slept about 4 hours but if that occurs then I just stay awake until the next night and sleep like a baby.

Avoid sleeping during the day as it will make you stay awake during the night, and that's when you resolve to watching TV or iphones, which is really horrible for your body and your mind.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-11T18:48:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/less-sleep-in-summer.27936/post-323539>

# Thread: Is Starlink leaking LF radiation?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-starlink-leaking-lf-radiation.27937/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### grillosun — 2023-07-10T15:43:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-starlink-leaking-lf-radiation.27937/post-323498>

Have anyone researched this?

[https://www.sciencealert.com/spacex...il&utm\_term=0\_fe5632fb09-b5c4703652-366125804](https://www.sciencealert.com/spacexs-starlink-satellites-are-leaking-radiation-scientists-confirm?utm_source=ScienceAlert+-+Daily+Email+Updates&utm_campaign=b5c4703652-RSS_EMAIL_CAMPAIGN&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_fe5632fb09-b5c4703652-366125804)

### Jeremy Fox — 2023-07-10T22:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-starlink-leaking-lf-radiation.27937/post-323513>

Hmm, the leakage is at a lower frequency than cell phones, seemingly between some television frequencies. If the signal was strong, I would worry about it. But the positioning of this as "leakage" suggests the signal is not strong.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-11T00:20:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-starlink-leaking-lf-radiation.27937/post-323514>

Dr Jack tells us to move away from populated areas because of the cell phone mm wave RF. If your cell phone can communicate with a cell tower 1 mile away so does everyone else’s. When anyone within 1 mile of you makes a call, the signal is going out 360º thru walls - thru you. In my local town there are 85,000 people. Let’s say 80% have cell phones, 68,000 cell phones pinging the tower every few seconds plus all the calls going on at any given moment. PLUS _324 cell towers and 493 radio antennas within a 3.0 mile radius of downtown._ PLUS 33,000 households (33,000 “smart meters”?). PLUS the radar from the local airport. How big is your town/city? PLUS the wires in the wall 12” from your head putting out an 60Hz AC field all night. I live 15 miles from town with 5 towers and 15 antennas within 3miles. Not saying we should not be concerned BUT a little low frequency from a satellite is the least of our problems! Don’t over look the obvious in front of you, remember switch off what you can and distance yourself from what you can’t.

# Thread: Fish: raw vs cooked vs canned
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 55
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### annelies_1 — 2023-07-11T14:19:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-323525>

I'm trying to eat as much raw fish as I can. Some seafood I prepare in the oven, but I'm wondering: is it worth it to spend the money on quality wild fish to then destroy the DHA in the cooking process? That brought me to thinking: is there actually any point in eating canned fish at all?

### coho3131 — 2023-07-11T18:03:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-323535>

If you can't catch sardines, mackerel, fatty fish, or crustaceans yourself, or buy them within hours from someone at the source who did, I think canned from a good company is the next best choice. I think the "fresh" raw fish at a nice store even has various chemicals applied for "freshness" purposes, not to mention the radiation it might have been exposed to in air travel. I also think low temperature preparations are best for cooking, and surrounding the food with liquid with a poaching method is helpful.

This article is about thermal stability of farmed salmon oil, but it is still relevant. For example, even at 302 degrees F, 37% of DHA remains. If this was in whole flesh form, a wild-caught fish, and much lower temperatures were used, I think the amount of DHA remaining would be higher. I cook our fish in a Dutch oven pot covered in water in a 190 degree F oven, or I poach it with barely a simmer covered in water on the stove.

### [Thermal and oxidative stability of Atlantic salmon oil (Salmo salar L.) and complexation with β-cyclodextrin - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26977177/)

The thermal and oxidative stability of Atlantic salmon oil (Salmo salar L.) as well as its β-cyclodextrin (β-CD) complexation ability has been verified for the first time. The main omega-3 fatty acids, EPA and DHA, were significantly degraded, even at 50 °C. Their relative concentrations...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-12T20:45:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-323601>

[Planning and engineering data 2. Fish canning - 4. Processing](https://www.fao.org/3/R6918E/R6918E05.HTM)

According to this, canning temps get up to 130 degrees C (266 deg F).

There is also significant pressurization that occurs. This can affect nutrient stability and retention as well.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-13T03:10:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-323628>

Salting and pickling work great for fish. In the past every salmon coming out of Alaska was salted. We used to salt fillets for 6 months or more and then smoke em. You could buy fresh salmon now and salt them in 5 gallon buckets. Sockeye is the highest in DHA I think and now is when the fresh Bristol Bay reds are hitting the market.

### Lindman112 — 2023-07-13T14:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-323645>

I only have access to and eat smoked mackerel, it's perfect as it hasn't been boiled or grilled which can remove alot of the nutrients.

My local grocery market has alot of salmon but it's all farmed in norway which I avoid like the plague. They feed "color pills" to the fish because they are so uncomfortable in the "pens" that they lose their color (Yum yum right?).

If you don't have access to smoked fish then try buying a smoker on your own and buy fresh seafood and smoke it.

It's quite easy and it smells terrific.

### JanSz — 2023-07-19T16:34:09+0300

There are at least 9 ANIMALS WITH MULTIPLE STOMACHS

Monogastric Animals
Ruminant Animals

Animals, birds, fish that humans eat are raised in factory farms

### [Intensive animal farming - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intensive_animal_farming)

 en.wikipedia.org

To make them grow as fast as possible they are fed with seed oils, whose large part of content is Linoleic Acid (Omega-6)
That LA becomes large content of what humans eat.
Chicken, pigs, fish, consumed increases our LA possibly just as much as eating Crisco oil, margarine or other seed oils.
Chicken from the egg ready to the table within 5 weeks
Pork 5 months
Costo will have to open their own chicken farms, because they need 6 pounds chickens and farms would rather deliver them 8 pounders.

Human sons are higher than their fathers, wonder if that is just because they are fed LA?

For whatever reason ruminants (cows) (from intensive farms) have minimal amount of LA in their fat. (Maybe the second stomach is helping?)
1-- it would be nice to know why
2--spending money on grass fed beef seems questionable

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-19T21:07:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-323983>

If you know the farmer, how it was raised, and when it was slaughtered, grass fed beef is indeed worth the cost and effort of finding it. Loopholes in the organic and grass fed labels make simply buying grocery store beef hit and miss. The reality is, 99.9% (just guessing) of all food produced to be sold is done in a way that maximizes yield. By focusing on maximum nutritional value, we can produce better foodstuffs. Probably need to do it yourself.

You brought a wiki article to a gun fight? You can believe what you want, but if you read "What your food ate" by Montgomery/Bikle you might understand what I tried to explain to you before. 57 pages of citations backing up the text are available at [www.dig2grow.com](http://www.dig2grow.com). Animals harvested after eating living, green forage will have a different fatty acid profile than the same animal after eating the same forage (albeit now not living and green but dry and bailed). That is why one should select grass fed beef harvested after grazing for a few months on lush pasture versus after months of eating hay, silage or grain. Fill your freezer in the fall with meat from a producer you respect.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-20T19:44:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-324043>

> [annelies\_1 said:](/goto/post?id=324016)
>
> I won't eat meat anymore if I don't know here it comes from. But when I used to consume a lot more meat I would buy grass fed. I just find it so sad that there are so many animals that don't even get to be outside in a field. that's why i would buy grass fed

Annalies, I respect and share your sentiment and that's part of why I raise my own meat and veggies. I wouldn't buy pork or poultry if I don't know the farmer and am convinced it is to my standards. I raise my own. However grass fed beef never sees the inside of a barn or building. It is born and raised on pasture and or range land. Calves playing and running around every day. Live that way with its amigos till the end. My animals are harvested on site so they live every day of their lives in the best environment I can provide. I have never seen a cowboy/farmer/rancher abuse a cow. In fact, they love their animals. I'm not say abuse doesn't happen, but just like in elder care facilities, there are stories of abuse. The overwhelming majority of caregivers are compassionate, caring people. I think PETA and some vegans will say anything to further their agenda. It is worthwhile to find a producer you respect.

I didnt read your former post. Many people likely had to eat mostly vegetarian, because not everybody can hunt down an animal of significant size.
That doesn't mean that eating animals wasn't a good thing or a thing we were meant to eat. Meat was the food of better off/stronger people. The meat distribution among tribes reflects that. You could look into it and see wether it was given to children and pregnant women.
I know that there is the narrative that people only ate meat in winter, because there were no plants alive. Well, whatever your believes, why don't you try out what works best for you today?

For gastric issues? Baking soda? Hm, ph regulation comes to mind. If that worked for you: bitters of all sorts are aiding your stomach to produce the accid it needs. That would be the opposite of taking tums. Food for thought.

A whole avocado has **975** mg potassium—that’s double the amount in a banana!
So, only 211 avocados one has to eat (every day) to get that much potassium.
After that is there any hunger left for a best grass fed steak?

/////////////////////////////
18 Foods That Pack More Potassium Than a Banana
**none of them are meats.**

### [Potassium Rich Foods: 18 Foods High in Potassium](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/foods-loaded-with-potassium)

Bananas are often the first food you think of when you want potassium, but plenty of other foods are rich in this mineral. Here are 18 foods that pack more potassium than a banana.

 www.healthline.com

### Daulatwant — 2023-07-21T21:36:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-324093>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=323958)
>
> Chicken from the egg ready to the table within 5 weeks
> Pork 5 months
> Costo will have to open their own chicken farms, because they need 6 pounds chickens and farms would rather deliver them 8 pounds
>
> For whatever reason ruminants (cows) (from intensive farms) have minimal amount of LA in their fat. (Maybe the second stomach is helping?)
> 1-- it would be nice to know why
> 2--spending money on grass fed beef seems questionable

When I eat only pork or chicken for a while, then I finally eat some beef the same day I get the feeling that something was missing before on just chicken or pork.

I don't think it all about the fatty acids in beef that make the difference.

I feel it is something in the muscle meat of ruminants that makes it stand out.

I can eat factory pork or chicken anytime I like and it's just fine for me. I will eat whichever I have access to. But I will stock up on beef and eat only that it over a few weeks. Pork and chicken is convenience food.

The fatty acid profile of tallow vs lard is just not that different. To me both lard and tallow are equally abundant for very cheap, so I eat whichever I feel like. Lard works much better for frying than tallow.

My cat is very old and when I first gave him some pork fat, he went mad for it and ate a lot. And he will just eat straight up lard from time to time. While it is very rare he will eat any fatty beef.

Kervan observed workers in the Sahara who ate an excess of salt excreted more potassium than they took in, a cooling mechanism.
I think there's more than one transmutation route to potassium but one is thought to be
Sodium and oxygen to make potassium Na23 + O16 + specific enzyme :=: K39

More complicated than that, but Jack talks about transmutation in a couple of posts on this thread.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coast-to-coast-mention.18845/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coast-to-coast-mention.18845/)

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-21T22:32:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-324098>

I think it is important to realize the food we eat and the amount is very different from what was available in the distant and recent past. There has never been carbs or fat like there is now. Our farm animals are fat because of selection and we have grown an abundance of grain to feed them. Not so much fat on wild animals. Try deer, elk, geese, ducks, anything. They are lean. Any wild plant food source was also fought over by insects, birds, and animals. I think ancient man ate more mice, bugs etc. I think the thing we are really missing is hunger, which is a lot more prevalent in nature.

### annelies_1 — 2023-07-22T18:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-raw-vs-cooked-vs-canned.27942/post-324128>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=324043)
>
> Annalies, I respect and share your sentiment and that's part of why I raise my own meat and veggies. I wouldn't buy pork or poultry if I don't know the farmer and am convinced it is to my standards. I raise my own. However grass fed beef never sees the inside of a barn or building. It is born and raised on pasture and or range land. Calves playing and running around every day. Live that way with its amigos till the end. My animals are harvested on site so they live every day of their lives in the best environment I can provide. I have never seen a cowboy/farmer/rancher abuse a cow. In fact, they love their animals. I'm not say abuse doesn't happen, but just like in elder care facilities, there are stories of abuse. The overwhelming majority of caregivers are compassionate, caring people. I think PETA and some vegans will say anything to further their agenda. It is worthwhile to find a producer you respect.

So cool that you raise your own animals. Which animals do you raise?
I absolutely agree that PETA and vegans have an agenda. I also agree that farmers want the best for their animals. Yet, my finding has been that farmers in conventional agriculture often cannot exercise on that care in a way that's good enough for me because of the laws here. People can buy whatever meat they want, I'm not here to change the world . But I just really don't enjoy eating meat from an animal that didn't have a decent life. That's even irrespective of whether it has the same nutritional value, which I don't believe it has, but that put aside. I just like to be super conscious and mindful of what I put in my mouth.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-16T21:17:03+0300

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=323554)

> Yes ...but a red lite comes in handy some times.....

Sunlight is best . . . That said my wife was in great pain and used a NIR 850nm light directly on her feet for 15 mins and the pain left. I had a BAD tooth ache and used a NIR 850nm 10 watt flash light (used for hunting at night) directly on my cheek for 10 - 15 mins and the pain left. You are correct, Red lights DO come in handy - even in “paradise” . . .

# Thread: Histamine & Fish Oil reactions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/histamine-fish-oil-reactions.27949/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MartynChilvers — 2023-07-12T14:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/histamine-fish-oil-reactions.27949/post-323572>

New here. Thank you for any help. Does anyone know what Jack says about histamine reactions to shellfish (primarily mussels and prawns)?
Additionally, does anyone know why I experience head pressure when taking fish oils (the fish oils are from metabolics.com in UK which assess every batch for oxidation and heavy metals).

### JanSz — 2023-07-12T17:51:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/histamine-fish-oil-reactions.27949/post-323584>

> [MartynChilvers said:](/goto/post?id=323572)
>
> New here. Thank you for any help. Does anyone know what Jack says about histamine reactions to shellfish (primarily mussels and prawns)?
> Additionally, does anyone know why I experience head pressure when taking fish oils (the fish oils are from metabolics.com in UK which assess every batch for oxidation and heavy metals).

Not sure, possibly there is a connection to high uric acid.
High uric acid=gout

# Thread: Sunlight --> Water --> DDW ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-07-12T17:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/post-323581>

If you put a clear glass of water in sunlight, does it deplete the deuterium in the water?
What wavelengths of the sunlight are doing this?
Does it work through the clear glass, or only on the portion of water not blocked by glass?
How many minutes does this process take?
What can be used to test the water before and after?
Thank you.

### JanSz — 2023-07-12T17:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/post-323583>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=323581)
>
> If you put a clear glass of water in sunlight, does it deplete the deuterium in the water?
> What wavelengths of the sunlight are doing this?
> Does it work through the clear glass, or only on the portion of water not blocked by glass?
> How many minutes does this process take?
> What can be used to test the water before and after?
> Thank you.

D is heavy. Only light water evaporates.
Do not boil DDW.
Do not let it stay long in open bottle.

There are at least two laboratories that test for deuterium content.

### coho3131 — 2023-07-12T18:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/post-323586>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=323583)
>
> D is heavy. Only light water evaporates.
> Do not boil DDW.
> Do not let it stay long in open bottle.
>
> There are at least two laboratories that test for deuterium content.

I'm asking about a process where sunlight splits the neutron from the proton.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-12T19:17:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/post-323592>

I vortex my well water and put it in Miron glass in the sun for several hours. Miron violet glass only lets thru UVA, violet and IRA light. Don’t know if this affects the deuterium level or not but since those frequencies are the best for our body and it is the water in our body that absorb them I figure pre exposing the water I drink to them can’t hurt.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-12T21:09:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/post-323606>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=323583)
>
> D is heavy. Only light water evaporates

When the D is bound up in a water molecule, the mass difference is not that much.

D2O is 20 g/mol
H2O is 18 g/mol

The mass only differs by a factor of 1.11. If only one hydrogen in the molecule is deuterated, then it's 19 g vs 20 g. Even smaller diff.

Now the mass diff between D and H becomes MUCH more significant when hydrogen is stripped from molecules and is alone, like in proton tunneling. The mass differs by a factor of 2 in this case. (2 g vs 1 g)

### Branden G — 2023-07-16T01:28:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/post-323794>

I’ve heard Icelandic water is deuterium depleted. Is that correct?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-17T00:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-water-ddw.27950/post-323823>

Yes, but you should save your money and **focus more on the DDW you can make**

# Thread: How to setup an ethernet system in your home
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ShaneR — 2023-07-13T01:40:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-323625>

I get a ton of questions about ethernet, a hard-wired connection to the internet, so I created this article to educate and help you pull off the transition away from WiFi to this safer and faster connection: [https://www.optimaldwellingspaces.com/post/is-a-wired-internet-connection-safer-than-wifi](https://www.optimaldwellingspaces.com/post/is-a-wired-internet-connection-safer-than-wifi)

### doctorwu85 — 2024-03-15T16:56:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332561>

I'm planning on hard-wiring my house and trying to figure out how much of an undertaking it will be. Anyone have a useful guide on how to do this that isn't just a marketing link to a paid post? I'm not opposed to paying for information if I know it's going to be useful, but not really interested in sponsor links by inactive members just trying to make money.

We use a router shield (reduces about 90% of the RF based on my tests) and turn off when not in use, but planning to go fully wired for optimal nnEMF management. There are phone jacks in most rooms but no ethernet ports. Current wifi router receives its signal from the one active phone jack. I have confirmed I can turn off the wifi function and use as an ethernet router, but need to figure out how to run ethernet jacks to other rooms. At minimum, I believe I can switch the active connection outside the house to the jack in my office, and then plug into the Wifi-disabled router for my necessary internet use. But I would prefer to have connections available in other rooms as well.

### coho3131 — 2024-03-15T17:12:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332563>

It's challenging but you need to fish wires to all of the rooms where you want a port.
Sometimes there are small attics for access, otherwise you might need to cut out small pieces of drywall and replace them later.
Some electricians, home theater installers, or alarm system installers are good at fishing wires if you want help.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-15T18:26:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332564>

Use shielded (groundable) Cat6A not Cat 5
until then (the meter in the photo is 2 ft from the router, distance is your friend)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### JanSz — 2024-03-15T18:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332568>

> [doctorwu85 said:](/goto/post?id=332561)
>
> I'm planning on hard-wiring my house and trying to figure out how much of an undertaking it will be.

Depending on the type of the house you have.
I have a two stories wooden frame home, with attic on top.
Indoor surfaces are made of sheetrock.
To run wires from one place to another you must remove sheetrock's that are in the way.
To get from the first floor to the second, in my case it was easier to go to attic first. Otherwise, would have to remove some wooden floors on the second level.

After all wires are in place,
pray that you installed all that you will need two years later.

Then, reinstall sheetrock's, paint the whole indoors.
Done.
Maybe.

\----------------
Initially there were metal wires.
Now, part of the installation is via optical cables.

You may be stuck to the type of provider.
If you change providers, or existing provider changes his delivery system, you may need to start from scratch.

.......

### jules3791 — 2024-03-15T19:18:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332572>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=332563)
>
> It's challenging but you need to fish wires to all of the rooms where you want a port.
> Sometimes there are small attics for access, otherwise you might need to cut out small pieces of drywall and replace them later.
> Some electricians, home theater installers, or alarm system installers are good at fishing wires if you want help.

I agree with [@coho3131](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24779/) that you could hire an outside company (Audio/Video/Home Automation) to run the ethernet wire and as [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) says... "Use shielded (groundable) Cat6A not Cat 5" is a must. I am in the AV industry (working on exit plan from this business) and our techs do it daily for what I consider "cheaper" labor rates compared to when we program systems. Drywall will be affected if you cannot find attic or crawl space areas to fish through.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332577>

Thanks guys, appreciate the tips. I'll have to check more into the construction of my house. I would not have known to use Cat6A and not Cat5, great feedback. And [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) , I had a very similar magnitude of reduction when testing RF with my Safe and Sound 2 with/without the cage we use.

Has anyone personally verified the claims about significantly reduced RF from the "low EMF" wifi routers that are out there? The home run scenario for me would be wired connections in all the rooms for 98% of our internet use, and then an optional low-RF Wifi router we could turn on if we wanted to have short periods of internet access outside the house, for guests, etc.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-16T02:21:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332586>

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-16T06:12:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332591>

### doctorwu85 — 2024-03-16T20:49:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-332598>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=332586)
>
> Before you hard wire- Just use the router you have. Do all your email, text etc off line then turn on router for 30secs every hour. 120x reduction in exposure

That would be a great option normally, but while I'm working from home with video calls, database work, etc. I need to have a consistent connection. We use your method on weekends or days off. Most likely my first step will be activating the phone line in my office, moving the router there, and plugging my computer directly into the router with Wifi off. Won't have the internet in the rest of the house for most of the day, but not a big deal. Then I'll research the labor necessary to run some cat6a cables to other parts of the house.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-04-17T05:34:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-333671>

Wanted to post an update on this in case it would help anyone. I was able to hard-wire internet to my office (on a different floor than my modem/router) using the coaxial cable connections that already existed in both rooms.

After a lot of research I discovered that there is a technology called MoCA (Multimedia over Coaxial Alliance) that allows you to convert an ethernet signal to run through coax cable, and then convert back to ethernet on the other end. It allowed me to hard-wire internet via existing coaxial cables in different rooms, with a little bit of labor in between. Here is the adapter set that I used [(link)](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B08ML1TSXC).

I noticed before that several rooms in my house had these coaxial ports, but after trying adapters I determined that they were not connected to anything, or to each other. All of the cables from these ports terminated in raw wires that were in a bundle outside the house, near my internet fiber receiver box. I had to strip the ends of these cables, add connectors to the ends, and use a tester to determine which cable corresponded with which port in the house. I then used a waterproof connector to join the two outside coax cables from my modem room to my downstairs office. I connected the adapter on both ends, and was able to get hard-wired internet on a different floor of my house without running any new cable.

To summarize, here's the order of operations for the adapters:

Modem (a wifi router with wifi turned off) > Ethernet cable > MoCA adapter > Coax cable into wall port near my modem > Coax cable out of wall in my office > MoCA adapter > Ethernet cable into my computer.

### Ozzy1 — 2024-10-05T21:37:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-setup-an-ethernet-system-in-your-home.27952/post-340466>

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-28T23:48:02+0300

> [enyaw said:](/goto/post?id=325667)
>
> Circulation for healing? I'm not concerned with it spreading. It's been like this for three years now and is currently a bit better than the pics. I was thinking of cold water dunks for my feet though. I thought maybe CT would help. I'll try the hot/cold combo.

I asked because some skin conditions can be because of poor circulation. Hot feels good and increases circulation but also increases inflammation, cold reduces inflammation but also reduces circulation. Hot/cold done this way increases circulation while reducing inflammation. It was successfully used in clinics in Europe (and America) over a hundred years ago until the Cartel took over. Not medical advice just what I would do. I used to work with Dr. who made a machine that automatically did this and he saved several people with neuropathy.

### JenniferM — 2025-04-15T21:30:51+0300

Enyaw, As for the pic's it looks like some sort of dermatitis. If any raw red places then an antifungal would be good then cover it and keep check on it. Bleach water soak for a few minutes is also good. For the thick skin the Eurea Cream would be beneficial to soften up the hard waxy like skin build up. JMO.

### TaylorF — 2023-07-14T00:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/never-ending-sugar-cravings.27959/post-323705>

Hi everyone,

I’m hoping for some advice. I’ve spent the last 4 years reading Jacks website and learning all of his protocols. I’ve changed where I‘ve lived a few times, tried Epi-Paleo Rx for a long time, tried a variety of lifestyle changes (light, water, magnetism), and now I’m trying carnivore. Even through all of his, I still have cravings for sugar and it seems never ending. I have no desires for meat, but I still eat that and fish. Is there a nutrient I’m missing, something wrong I’m doing with my diet, etc.? Any ideas for how to curb this once a for all?

Caveat: I do currently have allergies to milk and severe ones to ALL shellfish (both mollusks and crustaceans).
Dx: mast cell activation (MCAS), postural orthostatic tachychardia syndrome (POTS), and CIRS (chronic inflammatory response syndrome) from mold.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-14T02:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/never-ending-sugar-cravings.27959/post-323713>

# Thread: Andrei Sommer, Thomas Sanderson:  750nm and 950nm light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrei-sommer-thomas-sanderson-750nm-and-950nm-light.27960/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RisleyDC — 2023-07-14T03:03:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrei-sommer-thomas-sanderson-750nm-and-950nm-light.27960/post-323714>

Just getting started on reading papers on PBM....Read most of Tiina Karu's work and led me to Sommer and Sanderson. Fascinating study on two pronounced solar terrestrial minima wavelengths: 750nm and 950nm due to blocking via atmospheric O2 and H20. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7154450/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7154450/)

Also, Sanderson et al and ischemia/reperfusion brain injuries and use of 750nm and 950nm light to reduce ROS in the brain via reduced tunneling between Cytochrome and cytochrome c oxidase . (Photo-FET or photo field-effect transistor) [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5823933/pdf/41598\_2018\_Article\_21869.pdf](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5823933/pdf/41598_2018_Article_21869.pdf)

Are 750nm and 950nm used in your surgical practice to reduce ROS?
