# Thread: The Ideal Day.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ideal-day.27961/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MSH — 2023-07-14T05:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ideal-day.27961/post-323720>

What would the ideal day look like as far following the Kruse protocols? I’d like to know what an optimal schedule of activities looks like for the day-to-day. How much sunlight and at what times? Diet and best times to eat? When is the best time to exercise? When is the best time to take an ice bath? How much sleep and best time to go to bed? Etc…. A rough template for this lifestyle would be great! Thanks!

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-14T16:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ideal-day.27961/post-323731>

Maximize time outdoors. Ideally you would be outside the entire day. Not necessarily in direct sun whole day. Shade is fine. Just listen to your body. You will know when your skin has had too much direct rays.

Diet consisting of mostly marine life. Times will depend on the person. Most recommended is lots of protein/fat upon waking and after getting some dawn light. Then, eat again around 5-6 pm. Lots of protein/fat again. If you are gonna eat carbs, do that in the first meal. Make sure the carbs are local and in season.

Exercise in the afternoon (1-5 pm). Ideally weight lifting or a short HIIT workout. Morning workouts are not ideal as your cortisol is already naturally high from the waking process. Workout at night not good either, bc you don't want to spike cortisol before bed.

Ice bath I think is best in early morning or late evening.

Sleep duration should ideally be 7.5-8 hrs. This actually varies based on the person and time of year. Most fall in this ideal range though. Aim to go to bed about 2-3 hrs after sunset. Rise about 30 min before sunrise. Notice how the ideal sleep times will change based on the season with changing solar cycles.

After the sun has set, ideally NO light of any kind. If you must use light, candles would be ideal. Next best would be low flicker red lights. Most normal bulbs ppl use in their homes destroy melatonin production when used after sunset. "When you know better, you do better." (Kruse quote).

This is what I understand to be an ideal day. Maybe others have other pointers I am forgetting.

Also, very important - **the location you are living out this "ideal day" is of utmost importance!

For example, if you are carrying out this ideal day in NYC, you will be moving quickly down the road to disease.**

Hope this helps!

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-14T16:15:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ideal-day.27961/post-323733>

Forgot to mention, exercise should ideally be done outside!

### MSH — 2023-07-14T23:33:02+0300

### jules3791 — 2023-07-15T17:22:29+0300

Not sure of the science, but I've recently changed (a week in) from fasting until 9am, to what has been recommended, eating within 30 minutes of waking. What I have noticed is my glucose is now stable in the morning ranging from 70-78, whereas before it was in the upper 90's. Also, I do not feel the need to snack in the mornings and can hold 5 hours before lunch. Dinner (most days) I feel like I don't need. Trust me, it is a totally odd concept (waking and eating) that took a minute to wrap my mind around, but so far, I like the results.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-16T23:37:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ideal-day.27961/post-323818>

> [MSH said:](/goto/post?id=323769)
>
> Is there an advantage to eating breakfast shortly after waking over waiting until later in the day?

The whole purpose of eating right after waking is to get your hormones back in sync. Eating lots of protein early in the morning is a powerful way to do just that. If your hormones are already in a healthy state, you can get away with eating other times of the day. Not very many ppl these days have optimal hormone panels though.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-16T23:50:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ideal-day.27961/post-323820>

> [MSH said:](/goto/post?id=323769)
>
> I’ve read/heard that this is good for autophagy to fast for this period of time

Autophagy happens anytime you fast long periods. I don't believe it has to be a morning fast though.

Also, autophagy will happen naturally every night when you sleep if you don't expose yourself to artificial light before bed.

If you flip on a light switch at 9:30 right before going to bed say, at 10, you won't get a normal surge of prolactin around midnight, your endogenous melatonin levels will not rise to the appropriate levels, and the regenerative autophagy that is supposed to occur during your sleep cycle will be severely stunted.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-17T17:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ideal-day.27961/post-323840>

> [MSH said:](/goto/post?id=323828)
>
> Does eating early help with circadian rhythm?

Yes. Diet is a useful tool for anchoring your circadian rhythm, especially the **timing of your eating**.

Even more important than diet though, is your exposure to sunlight early and your avoidance of artificial light at night. Make sure to focus on light first and foremost.

> [MSH said:](/goto/post?id=323828)
>
> Could it possibly help me to be able to go to sleep earlier in the evening?

I think the best way to be able to go to sleep earlier is to get up and see the sun earlier. Since you are in Georgia, sunrise looks to be about 5:45 AM currently. So ideally, you want to be naturally waking sans an alarm clock around 5:15ish this time of the year.

> [MSH said:](/goto/post?id=323828)
>
> Will this help with dopamine as well?

It should. I think the best thing for dopamine is maximizing time outdoors. Getting plenty of UVA into your eyes and on your skin.

Sitting inside on a phone/laptop or inside watching TV is the fast lane to wrecked dopamine levels.

# Thread: nnEMF phone case personally tested
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-phone-case-personally-tested.27964/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-07-15T19:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-phone-case-personally-tested.27964/post-323784>

Any recommendations for personally tested nnEMF phone cases? I keep my phone off and in airplane mode at almost all times. If it's on and I need to use it, the screen is fully red. However, if I need to make a phone call outside of the house, I want the best protection possible. Does anyone have any recommendations of iphone cases that you've tested block nnEMF? I've only tried a few myself, but they still show some RF.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-15T21:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-phone-case-personally-tested.27964/post-323786>

If you are talking on the phone there will always be some nnEMF - Radio Frequency (RF), the phone has to “talk” to the tower and the tower has to “talk” to the phone. You want to block/reduce the RF (nnEMF) coming in your direction from the phone and leave the other side open. Defender Shield are the best I know of (tested by EHS friends), there may be others.

### Awakenotwoke — 2023-07-16T00:35:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-phone-case-personally-tested.27964/post-323793>

> [Taylor Freeman said:](/goto/post?id=323784)
>
> Any recommendations for personally tested nnEMF phone cases? I keep my phone off and in airplane mode at almost all times. If it's on and I need to use it, the screen is fully red. However, if I need to make a phone call outside of the house, I want the best protection possible. Does anyone have any recommendations of iphone cases that you've tested block nnEMF? I've only tried a few myself, but they still show some RF.

Emf Fortress cases are tested and work really well. You can see the actual tests here [https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCa7b5qhiA29pZXz1p3zaYWQ](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCa7b5qhiA29pZXz1p3zaYWQ)
The Freedom case is the best for actually making phone calls with just the antenna of the phone peaking out of the case as you see the girl on the website doing. [www.emffortress.com](http://www.emffortress.com)
Use code JK30 for 30% off.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-16T02:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-phone-case-personally-tested.27964/post-323796>

> [Awakenotwoke said:](/goto/post?id=323793)
>
> Emf Fortress cases are tested and work really well. You can see the actual tests here [https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCa7b5qhiA29pZXz1p3zaYWQ](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCa7b5qhiA29pZXz1p3zaYWQ)
> The Freedom case is the best for actually making phone calls with just the antenna of the phone peaking out of the case as you see the girl on the website doing. [www.emffortress.com](http://www.emffortress.com)
> Use code JK30 for 30% off.

The antenna locations are different on different phones models and which antenna are you covering to shield from or exposing get a good signal? Each phone may have ALL of the following: 5G, GPS, GSM, PCS, WiFi antennas etc. each in a different location. The 5G/mm wave antenna on the iPhone 12, 13 & 14 is on the side below the power on/off button. For minimum exposure when using any phone to talk on - shield the whole side toward you and expose the far side.

### Awakenotwoke — 2023-07-16T20:09:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-phone-case-personally-tested.27964/post-323805>

> I've tried an I phone 13 and a Samsung Galaxy in the Freedom case just like in the picture above and was able to carry on a conversation great.

the conventional advice is to just eat "light" food for some days. Generally this means starches and not much fat, all that is light digestable.
My personal experience is, that when eating normal epi paleo with protein and fat, it triggers diarrhea again, when you start to soon.
When the gut is upset it cant handle big protein and fat loads so quick again.
Since oatmeal, pudding, toast etc. one should not eat:

What should one eat, that is Epi-Paleo and doesnt trigger the runs again?

# Thread: Boston Area EMF Inspection Tour August 10-14, 2023
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-area-emf-inspection-tour-august-10-14-2023.27976/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ShaneR — 2023-07-17T23:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-area-emf-inspection-tour-august-10-14-2023.27976/post-323857>

Hey Tribe- I'm a Building Biologist specializing in EMF inspection and remediation.
I'm testing the waters for a tour of the Boston area for consultations/inspections between August 10-14, 2023. Depending upon the response level, this trip may or may not happen.

Here are some services I can provide:
\*full home inspection
\*pre-purchase inspection
\*hourly consult for certain areas of the home
\*learning how to use meters
\*vehicle inspection
\*office/workplace inspection

If you have interest in this, please send me an email: [**help@optimaldwellingspaces.com**](mailto:help@optimaldwellingspaces.com) with the following info:
\-Service(s) you are interested in

\*\*If interested in a home service, please answer the following\*\*
\-What type of home do you have? I.E. home, condo, apartment, town home
\-approximate square footage of your home
\-home address
\-# of electrical panels
\-do you have solar, battery wall, home automation, smart security, a well, a pool/hot tub, EV charger, backup generator
\-How many of the home's bedrooms would you like tested?

Based on the info provided, I'll send you a quote for services and available time slots.

For more info on my home inspection process, check out this article: [https://www.optimaldwellingspaces.com/post/what-to-expect-during-a-home-emf-assessment](https://www.optimaldwellingspaces.com/post/what-to-expect-during-a-home-emf-assessment)

# Thread: Addison's D, sunlight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addisons-d-sunlight.27977/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-07-18T02:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addisons-d-sunlight.27977/post-323864>

Can someone point me in the right direction for learning more about sunlight exposure in the context of Addison's Disease (primary adrenocortical insufficiency)?

In this condition, low plasma cortisol results in increased adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) due to the lack of feedback inhibition. The precursor to ACTH, pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC), also contains melanocyte-stimulating hormone (MSH), which is released with the production of ACTH. The increased MSH is responsible for the well-recognized hyperpigmentation of Addison’s patients.

I'm guessing sunlight exposure is still important, but I am not sure how the increased MSH affects what might be optimal.

Thank you.

### Clydesdale — 2023-07-18T23:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meniscus-tear.27982/post-323919>

Greetings,

I joined this forum like 10 years ago, but lost track of my account over the years. Good to be back and see that it's still humming nicely.

I know part of Jack's story started with a meniscus issue, but I can't remember if he ended up having surgery?

Either way, I finally had an MRI after my knee was bothering me for months and was diagnosed with a horizontal tear of my medial meniscus. They of course want to operate and remove a portion. It wasn't an acute injury, but seems to have been more of a wear and tear issue. I'm determined to see if I can bring some healing in other ways before going under the knife.

Does anyone have success stories with similar issues?

I have a lot of flexibility with my life currently and can commit to anything that might help. Looking to purchase a quality red light device soon, but am very much open to any advice you all might have. Supplements? CT? Heavy seafood?

Many thanks and hope everyone is soaking up the sun while we can.

### Tmmicheletti — 2023-07-19T00:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meniscus-tear.27982/post-323929>

Ive torn multiple ligaments in both of my knees. Ive spent many years trying exercises and supplements to relieve pain and the best thing i ever tried was using BPC-157. I took it orally 500mcg twice a day for a couple months after a partial tear and the pain as well as inflammation were almost completely gone. If you inject into specific spot it may work faster but i have no experience, just something ive read. A popular combo is BPC-157 and TB-500. something to look into! good luck

> Either way, I finally had an MRI after my knee was bothering me for months and was diagnosed with a horizontal tear of my medial meniscus. They of course want to operate and remove a portion. It wasn't an acute injury, but seems to have been more of a wear and tear issue. I'm determined to see if I can bring some healing in other ways before going under the knife.
>
> Does anyone have success stories with similar issues?
>
> I have a lot of flexibility with my life currently and can commit to anything that might help. Looking to purchase a quality red light device soon, but am very much open to any advice you all might have. Supplements? CT? Heavy seafood?
>
> Many thanks and hope everyone is soaking up the sun while we can.

Same situation about 5 years ago moving a refrigerator by myself..right medial meniscus tear.....I read a study on outcomes doing nothing versus sham surgery and real surgery. Best outcomes if it is just a tear long term were doing nothing. Mine still gets soar but i've hiked the grand canyon and still do yoga regularly if that helps. Good Luck!

### AcuHealth — 2023-07-19T22:08:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meniscus-tear.27982/post-323991>

Meaning they made a slit in the knee (the patient believing they had surgery) but did not have any changes to the meniscus, the other group had the surgery (worst outcome long term) other group did nothing and had the best long term outcomes. N=1 however could make this irrelevant given the different conditions of all the patients. Anyhoo I did nothing and have only occasional soreness...Good Luck!

### Tmmicheletti — 2023-07-22T05:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meniscus-tear.27982/post-324108>

> [Clydesdale said:](/goto/post?id=324056)
>
> Thanks so much. Tried BPC 157 a few months ago with limited success. But didn't know it was a tear at that point, May try another round with higher dose.

No problem. It helped me alot. I understand some peoples hesitation, especially on here where anything unrelated to light, water, and magnetism is looked at with skepticism lol. Those things have obviously had a giant impact on my healing as well, but i cannot deny the hump BPC-157 helped me get over. I love being active and i dont think i had it in me to stay inactive long enough to fully heal on my own. I like these peptides because they are naturally occurring in our gut and can greatly jump start the healing process in a short amount of time. Im with jack and his belief that if you make it you shouldn't take it but context matters. I dont believe these peptides are something you should regularly take. I just think its something worth trying for someone with a nagging injury who has already made or is making changes to optimize their light environment. Kneesovertoesguy and his ATG workouts have also been amazing for me. Ive been doing his exercises for over a year now and i have complete knee bend and almost no pain in my left knee which has had 3 ACL tears along with PCL tear, meniscus damage, and partial retears. Same results for my right knee which has had MCL, PCL tear and meniscus damage as well. Took the exercises very slowly making sure i was never working through pain while maximizing blood flow in order to really saturate that area since tendons and ligaments have poor blood supply. Those things plus the implementation of all the stuff ive learned from jack has my knees and body feeling better than ever. I believe my biannual knee reconstruction thats been going on for nearly a decade is over! No doubt in my mind you can heal without surgery.

For the knee, elbow, hand or any other small targeted area I would recommend these three red light products.

A gembared vector panel for lower intensity longer treatments over a larger but still targeted area directly on the skin. Use 10 minutes. [https://gembared.com/collections/al...bared-vector-mini-red-and-nir-led-light-panel](https://gembared.com/collections/all-products/products/gembared-vector-mini-red-and-nir-led-light-panel)

Then this Chroma 850nm flashlight. It’s a pretty intense flashlight and I do like that it’s only infrared light. It can be a pain to use because you have to use rechargeable batteries and the flashlight uses up the batteries quickly. Use this directly on a spot for 3-5 minutes. Jack kruse recommended this product on twitter last week. [https://getchroma.co/product/chroma-near-ir-flashlight/](https://getchroma.co/product/chroma-near-ir-flashlight/)

I would then use this cheap 660nm flashlight from aliexpress for $16. Make sure to select 660nm [https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005001800891205.html](https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005001800891205.html)
It’s a very powerful flashlight the lower setting of brightness that you want to use it on is 190 mW/cm^2. The highest brightness that you probably want to avoid using directly on this skin is 500 mW/cm^2. How I use this flashlight is targeting very small areas. I like to put it on the different acupuncture meridians. I would be interested to see this intense flashlight used directly on the injured knee or hand.

Buy a Samsung INR18650-25R 3.7volt rechargeable battery to use in that flashlight.

An EMR tek panel will do next to nothing, they are absolutely terrible. You need to use red light directly on the skin

Im not very familiar with Ben Greenfield. Ive seen some clips of things and from what i understand he just trys everything under the sun and if he likes it promotes it. I do see some utility in it! Ive liked experimenting with things in the past. My environment has been shit for most of my life. It has only been this past year since ive discovered Jacks work and it has already made a huge impact. Currently not taking any supplements and my body is pretty much pain free. I think 30-60 days of exogenous naturally occurring peptides to super charge healing in a ligament or tendon is a better option than surgery, prescriptions, and a time table of recovery that can be many months longer. That is at least my experience. Could be misguided. Lifestyle and environment is always number one factor.

### Sue-UK — 2023-07-22T13:57:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-emitting-fabric.27984/post-324114>

For men, wide mesh fabrics for a top is more doable than for women. If I have to be out in public, some of the larger mesh alternatives would be too revealing, and some of the less revealing might have less holes per square inch than Kiniki. My go to test for checking alternatives that wouldn't get me arrested would be can I read through it. If I pull a piece of kiniki fabric over my face, close to my skin, I can see through it and read the words on the screen, or the text of a printed out blog. I'm working on the principle that what I see is what my skin sees. Not as good as nude, but at a higher latitude, when good sunlight is available every extra photon is welcome.

### annelies_1 — 2023-07-19T18:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/street-lights.27985/post-323967>

I'm wondering how disruptive streetlights during sunrise and sunset are. Here the lights stay on in the morning until it's fairly light. They kind or stress me out and that stress sometimes causes me to look straight into them. But when looking at the sky, how disruptive to the circadian signal is a lightbulb when the majority of what you see is sky light?

### JanSz — 2023-07-19T19:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/street-lights.27985/post-323971>

### ChristineH — 2023-07-19T20:04:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/street-lights.27985/post-323976>

> [annelies\_1 said:](/goto/post?id=323967)
>
> I'm wondering how disruptive streetlights during sunrise and sunset are. Here the lights stay on in the morning until it's fairly light. They kind or stress me out and that stress sometimes causes me to look straight into them. But when looking at the sky, how disruptive to the circadian signal is a lightbulb when the majority of what you see is sky light?

Very disruptive for me. I avoid looking at them. Blue blockers might help.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-19T21:41:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/street-lights.27985/post-323989>

I think of myself as a sponge that adsorbs light. It is dry when I wake up and I make sure to only allow it to adsorb sunlight. Once it is saturated with that sweet sunshine, I worry less. Likewise, in the evening, I avoid all light.

### annelies_1 — 2023-07-20T14:07:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/street-lights.27985/post-324017>

Ye that makes sense of course it would have an effect. What also has an effect is how freaking angry I get at people in general when that happens  Sure enough that also raises my cortisol levels.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-21T22:52:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/street-lights.27985/post-324099>

I was living at my sweet mother in law's house during covid lock down. She is almost blind and it seemed there was no way she would turn off lights in her house, so after spending the day out in the sun, I would come in and retire to our light free room to watch the sunset. I would light or leave out a candle for my wife for when she came to bed. 12 hours of awesome darkness and sleep followed by 12 hours of sweet sunshine. Trying to get others to live like I do is folly, so I focus on me and don't worry about the rest of the world. Much lower cortisol. Also, when we have guests or go out, I just don't worry about it.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-23T01:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/street-lights.27985/post-324146>

> [annelies\_1 said:](/goto/post?id=323967)
>
> stress sometimes causes me to look straight into them

I understand this for sure. I have a bright street lamp very close to my backyard that blasts me every morning. It is weird how eyes are drawn to the brightness. I wouldn't worry too much about it though if the suns light is visible. Just try your best to not stare directly the street lamp lol. I wouldn't block your eyes though. You need that sunrise light into your naked eyes every morning.

# Thread: Shellfish allergy/restarting epi-paleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-07-20T00:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324002>

I've done the epi-paleo restart a few times, but this time I have a serious shellfish allergy and MCAS (mast cell activation syndrome). This means I can only eat low histamine meats and fish.

Does anyone have any recommendations for the best meat and fish recommendations if you can't have shellfish? Small-owned/sustainable companies you buy from?

### pfedorow — 2023-07-20T03:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324011>

Hey Taylor.

Would suggest searching your issues you listed with jack Kruse name. I got a lot of articles that have people with similar questions

### TaylorF — 2023-07-20T23:07:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324052>

Thanks! That's helped. I'm on so many supplements for histamine and I still can't get mine down without following a strict low histamine diet. So frustrating!

### pfedorow — 2023-07-20T23:31:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324054>

How is your light environment? Where do you live? Time spent outdoors vs indoors? Avoidance of non native emf?

### TaylorF — 2023-07-21T03:21:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324067>

Good question. I spend my time half and half, but when I’m inside, it’s during the day and I don’t have any lights (besides red for night time). I wish I could spend more time outside, but the heat and my POTS don’t agree always.

### pfedorow — 2023-07-21T19:19:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324086>

Keep working on it, Morning sun is key to building tolerance up. Can always hit the shade when you cant tolerate key is to always be outside. Also i find grounding in water is especially helpful for cooling and duration.
Have you networked with fish mongers locally for trout or salmon? and farmers for non aged beef , lamb?

### Marko Pollo — 2023-07-21T23:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324101>

Check out Five Mary Farms or Alder Springs Ranch. Check out their sites. You will feel good about their animals and meat. There are many others but these are great for grass fed meat.
On the seafood side, I have had some questionable seafood from small shops. Smaller shops cannot afford to throw away stuff that doesn't sell, so I prefer Costco. Some places will dip older fish in bleach water to make it smell less old. Costco sells salmon, crab, halibut, shrimp all caught by small fishing boats and quality is great. Bags of frozen sockeye fillets are a winner. When buying salmon, the belly and gill collar have the most fat, tail the least. Saw fresh sockeye fillets at local grocery yesterday for $9 lb. Good price in the modern world.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-23T01:45:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shellfish-allergy-restarting-epi-paleo.27986/post-324147>

Costco near me has about 95% farm raised seafood :/

# Thread: Coming off SSRI's
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-off-ssris.27989/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### timtalbot96 — 2023-07-21T04:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-off-ssris.27989/post-324069>

Hi all, has anyone had any experience managing withdrawals from SSRI's/stop taking them? Recently, I stopped taking them after being on the lowest therapeutic dose for a few years and within a couple months I was becoming overwhelmed by the slightest of stresses and highly emotional with no regulation of my nervous system. So having gone back on them, I am looking for some answers. As I understand it the withdrawals are hell for anyone with a lowered redox, which I currently fall under.

Information about me: I am a 27 year old male living on the East Coast of Australia. I work as a mathematics teacher in highschool. Used to be highly active and fit (in my perception, not metabolically) however recently having a lot of fatigue, loss of muscle mass, low testosterone, system feels like its slowed down (low gut motility, lower core body temp etc).

Current practices:
\- Watching every sunrise and sunset barefoot, getting as much midday sun as possible.
\- Blue Blocking Glasses after sun down, using red lights.
\- Barefoot most of the time I am not at work
\- CT with ocean swims (currently Winter in Oz), cold showers, breathwork, spending time next to waterfalls in the mountains
\- Drink DDW, consuming lots of seafood
\- Minimising NNEMF

Is changing the light environment enough to regain redox to then navigate coming off SSRI's? I understand Australia is not ideal generally due to high uv from donut hole + lowered magnetic flux from geoid low + poor hydrology cycle...so maybe my concern with coming off SSRI's means little in the scheme of things without drastic environmental change, but would love to hear if anyone has had any experiences with this

Hope everyone has a great day, I am grateful for all the knowledge shared on here from everyone.

# Thread: Question about Playa del Carmen

### Evee — 2023-07-21T18:25:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324081>

Is the population density of Playa del Carmen too high to make it a good choice to stay in Quintana Roo or do the natural magnetic fields and light still make it a decent choice?

I’m planning to head to Mexico for fall but I need to work remotely and I’ll be traveling alone so PDC seems like an appealing choice for those reasons, but just wondering if it’s a poor choice for pop density?

### OptimalSkr — 2023-07-22T16:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324123>

Great question! Maybe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) can comment

Do you need to be at the beach daily to get the full effects of PDC's (light and magnetism) or can you get these just from living in the city
and going outside in a park or yard?

>
> I’m planning to head to Mexico for fall but I need to work remotely and I’ll be traveling alone so PDC seems like an appealing choice for those reasons, but just wondering if it’s a poor choice for pop density?

I will go to PDC for a while, just to be able to choose where I want to rent .

### OptimalSkr — 2023-07-22T19:39:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324130>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=324125)
>
> You just go on the beach in PDC

Yes - But if you don't live close to the beach and cant always go is PDC still worth it? - can you still get the benefits?

Also even if you live close the seaweed (sargasso) is becoming a problem - people say that a lot of times it is so disgusting there and not a good experience.

### Anne V — 2023-07-22T20:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324133>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=324130)
>
> Yes - But if you don't live close to the beach and cant always go is PDC still worth it? - can you still get the benefits?
>
> Also even if you live close the seaweed (sargasso) is becoming a problem - people say that a lot of times it is so disgusting there and not a good experience.

I do not know. I live in the UK, I have been to puerto Aventuras for a month 1n March2019.

I went back in March 2020 after the open heart op. there was no seaweed then.I think it gets clean early morning

at least in places.

### Ronald Waters — 2023-07-23T00:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-playa-del-carmen.27992/post-324136>

I think the seeweed problem is somewhat seasonal but yes in many places they are out cleaning it up and hauling it out. I've Heard it's a problem coming up from Brazil so the weather could shift next year and the problem disappear. It's anybody's guess.
In my opinion, take along some meters and test a place before you make a long-term decision. nnEMF can vary from house to house..

### ateale — 2023-07-21T20:02:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-or-ir-light-lamp-or-panel-for-home-use.27994/post-324089>

Hi everyone I am looking to purchase red or IR light bulbs or a panel that I can setup in my bathroom or bedroom.

Any recommendations? Any particular brands / models?

Are there bulbs or panels that emit both?

I hear that the red bulbs / heat lamps can get quite hot so a ceramic socket is required, any thoughts on that?

Thanks!
Adam

### ateale — 2023-07-21T20:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-or-ir-light-lamp-or-panel-for-home-use.27994/post-324091>

This looks like a heat lamp which is Far-InfraRed 2000nm - 3000nm +/- (Obvious some red and NIR)
Near-InfraRed is about 850nm +/-
Red light (visible) is 660nm +/-
What are you trying to achiev?

### ateale — 2023-07-22T15:53:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-or-ir-light-lamp-or-panel-for-home-use.27994/post-324119>

[@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) thanks for replying to my post, I'd appreciate any comments that you might have.
To be honest I'm not completely sure about what i am trying to achieve apart from getting as healthy as I can be and hopefully set a good example for my kids. I spend most of the day inside working at a desk+computer so I am trying to compensate for that I guess.

I understand that exposure to red light in the early hours of the day preconditions our skin for UV - obviously the idea is to get out to see the sunrise and get grounded but some days of the week I'm unable to do that so I figured it wouldn't hurt to have something that I could setup in the bathroom or bedroom to get some exposure.
I'm considering to also replace the white lightbulbs i have in my bathroom with red ones or similar to reduce that exposure to the blue spectrum in the morning

### TaylorF — 2023-07-22T16:55:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324124>

Is it harmful to wear grounding shoes in a city like NYC due to the high nnEMF levels?

OR is it always helpful to wear grounding shoes on something like concrete no matter where you are?

### TaylorF — 2023-07-23T11:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324168>

I go one day every few months to check in on the school where I teach online. I don’t understand why you’re saying still? Do you know anything about me?

### TaylorF — 2023-07-23T11:51:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324169>

### Sue-UK — 2023-07-23T12:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324172>

> [Taylor Freeman said:](/goto/post?id=324124)
>
> Is it harmful to wear grounding shoes in a city like NYC due to the high nnEMF levels?
>
> OR is it always helpful to wear grounding shoes on something like concrete no matter where you are?

Jack has posted a video about dirty electricity such as

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-time-line-of-tech.23126/#post-323817](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-time-line-of-tech.23126/#post-323817)

### ShaneR — 2023-07-24T04:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324224>

grounding to the soil in a population dense area is not recommended. Somewhere around 60% of the return current in North America's electrical grid goes through the soil. It is like a huge circuit. When you ground in any big city, even in a city park or green space, you have significant potential to become part of this circuit. That's no bueno.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-24T09:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324234>

> [Taylor Freeman said:](/goto/post?id=324124)
>
> Is it harmful to wear grounding shoes in a city like NYC due to the high nnEMF levels?
>
> OR is it always helpful to wear grounding shoes on something like concrete no matter where you are?

Our bodies run on DC voltage as does nature. Grounding makes you part of the circuit and in nature this is good. In any man made nnEMF AC field the AC field is induced thru your body. If you are in any city close to buildings and power lines grounding is probably not healthy. Get out in nature and ground.

### TaylorF — 2023-08-09T03:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324872>

I wonder if this is safer in small towns where electric power lines are above ground vs under. I would think it is safer for the lines to be above ground.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-09T22:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes-nyc.27996/post-324892>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=324872)
>
> I wonder if this is safer in small towns where electric power lines are above ground vs under. I would think it is safer for the lines to be above ground.

When power lines are above the ground it is easier to avoid them and find a “clean” place to ground/Earth

### JanSz — 2023-07-23T19:32:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-3-labs-can-i-run-now.27998/post-324193>

Do you have idea when this first appeared?
This:

### [Bio-Hacking 101 – Kruse Longevity Center](https://kruseatdestin.com/biohacking-101/)

 kruseatdestin.com

Please inform me when you get more information about it.

I have not seen this before. Thank you for posting it.
If anything, it is a proof that our doctor suggests laboratory testing.
I say that because:
you may see posts that claim that we should not do testing.

Indeed, it will be nice to see which tests are more valuable than others.

For the last over 20 years I am hooked up on fatty acids.
Mostly
how to look at them
how to manipulate them.
What is the right level
But all the time my DHA is within recommended range.

Asking questions, not getting answers.

There is a rather forceful misinformation going on about fatty acids.
At first, long time ago, around 20-25 or even 30 years ago,
what to test, how to test, how to make conclusions, how to apply them when attempting corrections.

There is a lot of misinformation's that are often made with premeditations.
But the initial, years ago, were by learning in process of doing research.
\-----------
One of the problems that are rarely discussed are the recommended laboratory ranges.
How they are developed?
Basically, laboratories are doing many of tests.
Then they use statistics to figure out those ranges.
But
who is coming to laboratory for testing?
Sick people are coming to have the tests done.
So, **laboratory ranges are guesses based on sick people.**
But this is the bases that we are forced to deal with.
\----
Laboratory that does those tests are around no more than 100-150 years.
We are being fed with Crisco oils, seed oils, margarine, Kentucky fried chickens for about that long.
We have our fatty acids screwed with long time, many generations; it will be hard to figure what levels are desirable.

These are the difficulties and more.
But without testing
we are open to unnecessary bs from number of interests.
None of those interest has our wellbeing as their major goal,
no matter how much you are paying for it.

//////////////////

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-07-24T16:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-3-labs-can-i-run-now.27998/post-324238>

For educational purposes only. This is the base for my new patient's blood work. Sometimes, it's all I'll run. If needed I will add additional makers based on what they're coming in for. Other possible tests are Dutch Complete, OAT, GI-MAP, Ca-Artery Score, and Lipoprotein Particle Assessment with Insulin Resistance Markers and Graph.

Components:
C-Reactive Protein (CRP), High Sensitivity
Complete Blood Count (CBC) with Differential & Platelet Count
Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP14)
Ferritin
Fibrinogen Activity
Free Thyroxine Index
GGT
Hemoglobin A1c
Homocysteine
Iron/TIBC (Total Iron Binding Capacity)
LDH
Lipid Panel with Total Cholesterol/HDL Ratio
Magnesium, RBC
Phosphorus, Serum
Reverse T3
T3 Uptake
Thyroglobulin (TGA) Abs
Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO) Abs
Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone (TSH)
Thyroxine (T4), Total
Thyroxine, Free (T4, Free), Direct, Serum
Tri-iodothyronine, Free (T3, Free)
Triiodothyronine (T3), Total
UIBC
Uric Acid, Serum
Urinalysis, Routine
Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy

### JanSz — 2023-07-24T16:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-3-labs-can-i-run-now.27998/post-324240>

**Genova Diagnostics
MetaMetrics**

### [Metametrix Laboratory Testing](http://www.weblac.com/Metametrix.html)

Acupuncturist Steve Sterling, San Diego, California

www.weblac.com

### [Metametrix Laboratory Testing](http://www.weblac.com/Metametrix.html#Fatty)

Acupuncturist Steve Sterling, San Diego, California

www.weblac.com

### [Billing Information | Genova Diagnostics](https://www.gdx.net/billing-information)

 www.gdx.net

[https://www.gdx.net/core/sample-reports/nutreval-fmv-3000-sr.pdf](https://www.gdx.net/core/sample-reports/nutreval-fmv-3000-sr.pdf)

NutrEval
is a nice test that I did 3 or 4 times years and years ago.
I noted that the Fatty Acid part of this test does not have both (min & max) ranges for some of them.
Another words you may never have too much of them, they are always on the green. I do not think that is right. Besides it must screw up the results of otherwise good idea behind NutrEval test.

Not having better choice, I still used this test. Until I was able to get Fatty acids from LabCorp.

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2023-07-24T18:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-3-labs-can-i-run-now.27998/post-324253>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=324241)
>
> [@DrEttinger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22307/)
> Thank you for this list.
> Why not include in your list:
>
> Potassium RBC
> Fasting insulin
> Fasting glucose
>
> (I think that potassium, serum from (CMP14) should be removed from that list, is there just to make Big Pharma rich and people sick)
> ??

Glucose, Fasting is included in the CMP14
Insulin, Fasting - Is added if needed, about 30% of the time.
Potassium, RBC - Is rarely added as I recommend potassium to just about everyone. The need is huge.

Jan, as an FYI, I just got back from 7 days in at an all-inclusive in Mexico (2 hours in the ocean daily catching waves, 90 degrees, high humidity, cocktails/wine/beer, and salty food). By the end of the trip, I was taking 2.5 tsp (about 3 grams of actual potassium). My normal daily is 1 tsp. It's the most I've ever taken, but it was 100% needed. I am so glad I brought it.

### JanSz — 2023-07-24T19:02:08+0300

### kalikaye — 2023-11-16T17:40:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-3-labs-can-i-run-now.27998/post-328342>

Not sure if anyone is still looking for this, but I just signed up for the biohacking 101 course and received the tests in the second email. The body of the email text is pasted below with the tests in **bold**:

As Dr. Kruse explained, not getting your labs done is going to the car shop and asking a mechanic to fix your car — when it’s at your house instead of the mechanic’s shop. Labs act as a rosetta stone to help us understand what is, or isn’t, going on in your body so you can begin to develop a treatment plan.

If you’re just getting started with labs and are concerned about the cost of getting your labwork done, Dr. Kruse suggests starting with the cheap Dirty Paleo Hack, which includes:

-   [**Highly-sensitive CRP**](https://jackkruse.ontraport.net/c/s/s6Q/a9yw/6/NA/rEg/6IvLAV/vf6xzxeX70/P/P/xm) — This test allows you to see how much inflammation, the foundational condition of all disease, is in your body. The optimal value is < 0.5.
-   [**Vitamin D**](https://jackkruse.ontraport.net/c/s/s6Q/a9yw/s/N4/rEg/6IvLAV/nW7EwixS8/P/P/xm) — Vitamin D supports the immune system, so this test reveals how well your body is able to maintain your immune system and extinguish inflammation. The optimal range is 70-120.
-   [**DHEA**](https://jackkruse.ontraport.net/c/s/s6Q/a9yw/v/NK/rEg/6IvLAV/sJtIIpYN6P/P/P/xm) **— DHEA** reveals your metabolic efficiency and allows doctors to see how well you’re sleeping and how well your gut is functioning (in other words, it tests how well your “engine” is running). Optimal DHEA is top 25% of reference range (and up to 10% over reference range).

This “toe-dip” into biohacking will give you a starting place to begin assessing your own n=1. If your lab values are not optimal, you have some work to do and this e-course will help.

If you experience resistance from your primary care physician in ordering labs or helping you understand them, you have a couple of options:

1.  Find a local doctor who will order labs for you (more on this in an upcoming email).
2.  Order labs yourself. You don’t need a doctor to order labs for you to get started (though we definitely recommend finding a doctor to work with locally — more on that in an upcoming email). Dr. Kruse is a fan of Life Extension, and you can [find a local draw station](https://jackkruse.ontraport.net/c/s/s6Q/a9yw/z/Nb/rEg/6IvLAV/vh5syYsBGH/P/P/xm) who will draw your blood for you and send it to Life Extension with your paperwork.

Want to get started with minimal investment?
Stay tuned for the next Biohacking 101 email to learn how the Dirty Paleo Hack helps to reveal where you are on the spectrum to optimal.

Later in this series, we’re also going to show you how to go deeper than the Dirty Paleo Hack and how to get your insurance to pay for your labs to boot.

To your optimal health,

Team Kruse

the first SOB (but director of really good place in Manhattan NYC) tells me that I should get ready to die. That was almost 30 years ago.

The next, more willing doctors do not know shit, but want to experiment on me.

It was in the middle of 90's, not much knowledge. Most from bodybuilders, and Nazis from their experimentations on peoples.

if I experiment on myself, at least I will eventually learn something.

# Thread: Raise fat percentage with eating more fat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raise-fat-percentage-with-eating-more-fat.28000/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7

### jelle — 2023-07-23T15:19:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raise-fat-percentage-with-eating-more-fat.28000/post-324175>

Hi guys,
I'm 192 cm, about 82 kg and have a fat percentage of about 8 percent. I'm on a carnivore diet because that works best for my sleep and energy-levels. But it looks like my body only takes the nutrients it needs. When I eat more I don't gain weight, but I get more digestion. I do grounding, red-light therapy, wear blue blockers in the evening and take cold showers. I had some digestion issues in the past (and still working on some candida), but that doesn't seem to be a problem. I can digest more food when I'm more active, or during holiday in the Alps (on high altitude it is even possible to gain weight, I don't know why). When I do a lot of grounding or cold thermogenisis, it need less food. Does anybody have an idea why I can't store extra meat as fat? It would help me a lot.
Thanks in advance.
Kind regards

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-23T17:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raise-fat-percentage-with-eating-more-fat.28000/post-324183>

I don't remember the physiology behind it, but eating fat doesn't make you gain fat. Only things that raise blood glucose make you gain fat. So carbs and blue light can make you gain fat.

### JanSz — 2023-07-23T17:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raise-fat-percentage-with-eating-more-fat.28000/post-324184>

Chicken from the egg to a six or eight pounds grow within 5 weeks.
Over their life span they eat less food by weight than they weight themselves.
Overall, they have decent amount of fat.
Crisco oil or any other vegetable oil is doing it, mostly soybean oil.

So, to gain fat, other than carbs, vegetable oil maybe the way to go.

..

# Thread: Sensation of the back of my hand came back within 6 weeks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-the-back-of-my-hand-came-back-within-6-weeks.28003/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2023-07-24T01:41:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-the-back-of-my-hand-came-back-within-6-weeks.28003/post-324211>

My serious success was, that after doing the sun rise morning sun, and really overall sun thing that Dr. Jack suggests, the sensation on the back of my left hand came back.

I had no sensation on my thumb, index finger and middle finger. And while it doesn’t hurt, it’s just super annoying, because you feel it all the time not being right.

It presumable comes from moderate stenosis I have, I had received a cortisol pack for it, that didn’t do anything. The orthopedic surgeon said that my only other alternative would be getting an epidural and I didn’t want that.
He said I would then have to live with it, which I’m ok with.

This is much better though and I’m very, very happy it healed. I have maybe a half a percent left of it. I can still feel it, but really it’s almost like normal.

Thank you, thank you, thank you, Dr.Jack.

P.s.: there is of course a slight chance, that it was a coincidence and has nothing to do with the sun protocol, but this was what I changed, so I find it more likely that it has to do with the changes I made.

### NikkiYork — 2023-07-31T03:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-the-back-of-my-hand-came-back-within-6-weeks.28003/post-324501>

I think it’s safe to say that my hand feels like nothing ever happened. Woohoo. Let’s keep it that way. Thank you, Dr. Kruse!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-the-back-of-my-hand-came-back-within-6-weeks.28003/post-324508>

# Thread: Freezing and Headaches

### Awakenotwoke — 2023-07-24T04:18:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-and-headaches.28004/post-324220>

I started this 3 months ago and although I've seen my immune system improve (always got colds too easily) I am still freezing when the AC is at 75 F. I sleep with it at 79 with long pant pajamas and covered. I'm 5'10'' and 132 lbs (and small boned so I'm not too skinny but before I found Jack I had a hard time keeping weight on which started 2 years prior, although always thin. I'm not vxd and took tons of Ivermectin to stay well in 2021. I had to close my store on 1/22 due to my health and up until I found the sun I had to take Fenbendazole 5x week after Iverm stopped working, to not get colds). I wake up with light headaches half the time and soon after I put my feet in the ocean I'm better. Full body in ocean and all better. They spray the sky a lot here and I feel it in my sinuses a little. I'm healthy outside of colds which I don't get now but always fight. I like trudging and lunging with 100% of my legs underwater. No need for gyms! But I don't feel great half of the time yet my blood work looks normal! I have a small 5g tower a block away but I mitigate well (I have a Trifield meter so I tested). I've been super tan for 3 months now but still suffering, feel like I'm constantly fighting a cold and light headaches so often. Looking to live winters in ES or Yucatan because I need to earth in the ocean year round... Help!

Hi,
I had previously talked to our pediatrician, who is also our good friend, about running some lab tests on my son (almost 10), and he said, that it's no longer done before the kid is 16 or if he has symptoms of any kind.

It would be great to at least know my son's vitamin D. I'm sure I can suggest it to him at our next well visit. Unless it doesn't make sense.

What is the black swan mitochandriac view on children and lab testing?

### Branden G — 2023-07-25T04:19:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-lab-tests-for-kids-recommended-among-mitochondriacs.28010/post-324274>

Finally I managed to convince his even whiter than white Dad, that he burns because he only goes out with SPF 100… oh well, a little exaggerated, but bad enough to be a constant year long debate.

> [lsu9546 said:](/goto/post?id=324276)
>
> I would check vitamin D, just cause your in the sun doesn't mean your body synthesizes sulfated D3. Lots of factors involved. You don't need a doctor to get a D test, there pretty cheap to find. You could check is hs-crp to see inflammation markers are high.

Other than seeing as much sunlight as possible I also take supplemental vitamin D.

I check my vitamin D two different ways.
One is the common LabCorp test
Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxycalciferol
the other
Spectracell.com
that is supposedly the functional level of what they are testing.

Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxycalciferol--- is almost twice of the maximum in the lab range
Spectracell.com ---- shows vitamin D being barely adequate

........................

# Thread: Sun makes me tired.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-makes-me-tired.28016/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cadoia — 2023-07-25T19:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-makes-me-tired.28016/post-324299>

So I live in Ecuador andes mountains as I relocated from my scandinavian country of origin.
Im at 1400 meter altitude right at the equator.

Now I have a question: When I get in the sun at noon here completely naked I get VERY tired every single time. Even just 20 min will do this to me.
I know the UV radiation here is off the charts. I have done sunbathing and watching sunrise for years on and off here. It has always been a problem for me I get so fried from the sun I feel exhausted every time.
On cloudy days I notice much more energy in the body.

Why is this? any ideas?
And if the answer is its because the sun is too strong, then what is it about the sun that makes one tired?
Just trying to understand.
Its frustrating as I really do wish to be in the sun all day but thats impossible as it is now.

My diet is clean organic only natural. Low carb. Drinking a lot of super clean mountain water.
Watching sunrise each morning 15 min. Doesnt make me tired.
Doing sauna and ice right after.
Its the noon sunbathing that makes me tired and fried.
Any feedback would be appreciated.
thank you.

### JanSz — 2023-07-25T20:02:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-makes-me-tired.28016/post-324302>

### Linz — 2023-07-29T12:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-makes-me-tired.28016/post-324442>

## ****From facebook****​

## [**Dr. Jack Kruse**](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse?__cft__[0]=AZWDe9GujSKnFkLAB1H4c3TkLFfeWKgCdgnWz2rQk4U7EzK6j_rRmSds029waRXwNR4F8VNMmi29IwfywXNt9sxTwvW3N3SZLrC3MfchW4f3yw69uq8KB1kDNVnodtCZSVuFnqlL3sTtSuPSOlvL5Rut&__tn__=-UC%2CP-R)​

[23 August 2015](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/965815156816172?__cft__[0]=AZWDe9GujSKnFkLAB1H4c3TkLFfeWKgCdgnWz2rQk4U7EzK6j_rRmSds029waRXwNR4F8VNMmi29IwfywXNt9sxTwvW3N3SZLrC3MfchW4f3yw69uq8KB1kDNVnodtCZSVuFnqlL3sTtSuPSOlvL5Rut&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R) ·

Getting tired from the sun is a reaction to what the sun can do to a trashed hormone panel of a lifetime of mismatches cause by calcium efflux. What are the human photosynthetic chemicals that sun light makes? Vit D3, histamine, and sulfhydryl groups of cholesterol in the skin I mentioned in Tensegrity 7 blog post on my website.
What does it destroy naturally? UV light lowers adrenalin the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system. When it drops in someone who is chronically stressed sleep can be induced. This is a sign of how bad your environment really is. UV and IR AM light also lowers aberrant steroids (CT 7) and nucleic acids without any liberation of heat on the skin's surface. This helps preventing aberrant optical scattering of cell signaling below (non linear optics). In this way it serves as a supreme example of how the surface photochemistry controls the biochemistry of your cells below in fat cells and in your organs. Biochemistry is not deterministic as most believe (think about the weather today while you lived, does it affect you? Its a surface effect too on your biology!).......it is determined by what happens to the surfaces in our eye, skin, and gut. This is why circadian biology are incredibly important. Natural harmony requires diurnal and seasonal destruction and renewal. Without renewal we cannot sustain. We have to come apart so we don't fall apart.
Nature presents us with a series of great opportunities brilliantly disguised as impossible situations. We are forced to take our atoms and collide them against the light we face in our daily lives. If that light i man made you get a facade of a life.
I told you all long ago the KT event was key to understanding Jack Kruse's perspective on things. This answer has that shot of truth in it........WHY?
This is why I get so pissed with providers who deal with genetic/SNP's data from 23andme.......they think, and make those with SNP's believe, they can change the inside of their genome with supplements.....and they can't. This is akin to the story of the KT event and why geologist did not believe the asteroid impact story for so long. When Alvarez postulated the sequence of events that occurred 65 million years ago do you know why geologist thought he was crazy? Because prior to 1990, no one had ever thought that an asteroid from space (outside Earth's realm) could change Earth fundamentally. They just could not see how an outside force from space could change the energy flows to reverse photosynthesis in a short period of time. That is what happened. ........they just thought climate weathering was the key to surface erosion. Modern humans are making the same error in judgment. None of them can fathom ho blue light or non EMF can change their cells biochemistry deep in their heads and organs.
Alvarez's ideas made them realize they had to look further outside. I am doing the same thing. I am telling people that genes and SNP's are not tied to endogenous breakdown in your nucleic acids or the way you work. It is tied to the environment you allow and via ubiquitination rates they become expressed.........that is when they are a problem. Genomic/SNP's therapies requires an environmental clean up first and foremost and not a pill or surgery. The Rx should not be prescribing to do things the other way around trying to solve the SNP presence. It is very similar to the KT event perspective of Alvarez. Do you see it yet? It is an environmental epigenetic issue tied to an altered environment increasing ubiquitin rates. SNP's just slow down epigenetic changes and this is why in environments with higher exogenous energy flows they get sicker faster than other people.
A sphere within a sphere can create electrical tension. Schumann taught us this in 1951, and yet we still don't realize he was teaching us about how energy can or can't flow from outside in or inside out. The Earth is a sphere surrounded by the ionosphere. That tension becomes a pulse wave for thing that live within both spheres. When the tension changes in the environment the pattern of signaling changes between things in a cell. Blue light and nn EMF change the tension in that sphere to increase ubiquitination rates on all things that live upon its tectonic plates.
Awareness, alone doesn't enlist change. You all have known this........you have to understand why you must change your environment. Then wellness will visit you.
Learn about the sun........and your skin..........you have a lot yet to learn about the photoelectric effect and biology. Good thing I have time left to teach you. Become a member at [jackkruse.com](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fjackkruse.com%2F%3Ffbclid%3DIwAR3d93KuCyI_Og4BtGQl_JoI843iEI4-hZIdk7HSHXJ1V3NJwuI_D5myaBI&h=AT1sMNRVjnVlt5gcsu3nvyUwTc2FJ3y79vD5kMhY8t_hs65H8TKdK6ZbvKm4HngHvjjdB34nXZ3_RAM6tlN6bAuk4BBczP1yE8EFripyI7YoUMluHc_9M_C6cv3EQtOnn6uDDbJL5A&__tn__=-UK-R&c[0]=AT1jApfF41nfO_hlKzviqnzLsc3gjO-gJf_qs72iFd1uLhHv1lmKQm9INHYBFleH2Jd2gxGTz3hmVeta6NUBdq6mhTv4pRMK64jMi7s9R8cKN5g1fHrAVZLd_P6GtLy4mbpAG6W3C0NUFq7-FxK1NCx9nGneLu4lg0cQDwKXiTOmMgrr) and change your life.

# Thread: Grounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 44
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trippg — 2023-07-26T21:36:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324348>

Thoughts on grounding while indoors?

I understand that you cant do better than being outside with your bare feet on the ground (nature always wins), but what about grounded sleeping sheets, or grounding pads while in doors?

I currently have a copper plate I stand on (bare foot) wired to a grounding rod outside of a window.

Any good links/resources on this? Thanks!

### JanSz — 2023-07-27T00:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324354>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324348)

>

>
> I currently have a copper plate I stand on (bare foot) wired to a grounding rod outside of a window.
>
> Any good links/resources on this? Thanks!

grounding rod outside
may be working or not.

One way to figure out if your grounding is acceptable is to hook it up to GFCI.
See if it trips the GFCI.

/

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-27T03:10:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324361>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324348)
>
> Thoughts on grounding while indoors?
>
> I understand that you cant do better than being outside with your bare feet on the ground (nature always wins), but what about grounded sleeping sheets, or grounding pads while in doors?
>
> I currently have a copper plate I stand on (bare foot) wired to a grounding rod outside of a window.
>
> Any good links/resources on this? Thanks!

Don’t need a link, think it thru. So many people in the Earthing community have this wrong, they know grounding is good outside so they thing if they ground to the outside - that’s good even while inside. Grounding make you part of the circuit. If you are inside, in a 60Hz AC electric field and you ground, that field is now following the path of least resistance to ground THRU you. Your body runs on DC and grounding outside in nature connects you to the natural DC of Mother Earth. Grounding while in an AC field is exacerbating the problem not solving it.

Your body runs on DC and grounding outside in nature connects you to the natural DC of Mother Earth. Grounding while in an AC field is exacerbating the problem not solving it.

Interesting, I though that might be the case. Thanks.

Seems there are very few workarounds that solve the problem of being indoors.

### ekarge — 2023-08-01T06:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324560>

Yea very discouraging to hear here that grounding in the modern indoor world is actually detrimental, and equally hard to swallow that sitting in the backyard with my shoes off is according to the info laid out here, actually more deleterious than avoiding contact with the ground due to the dirty AC fields ..smh.

I've come to the conclusion the only thing I can do EMF wise in my house is sleep with an EMF blocking blanket (ideally EMF blocking bed canopy if you have the money) to at least block some nn EMF. I guess grounding indoors/at night just isn't a possibility if you live in a normal on the grid home surrounded by AC fields..

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T08:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324562>

> [ekarge said:](/goto/post?id=324560)
>
> Yea very discouraging to hear here that grounding in the modern indoor world is actually detrimental, and equally hard to swallow that sitting in the backyard with my shoes off is according to the info laid out here, actually more deleterious than avoiding contact with the ground due to the dirty AC fields ..smh.
>
> I've come to the conclusion the only thing I can do EMF wise in my house is sleep with an EMF blocking blanket (ideally EMF blocking bed canopy if you have the money) to at least block some nn EMF. I guess grounding indoors/at night just isn't a possibility if you live in a normal on the grid home surrounded by AC fields..

Hi ekarge,

#1 - Grounding/Earthing is a reconnecting with nature thing that needs to take place - in Nature. Your house ground in part of the modern electrical system which is part of the problem. If you are in a 60Hz AC electric field (inside your home) connecting to ground makes you part of the modern electrical system and is not a good idea.

#2 - Sitting in the back yard may well be perfectly ok if your back yard is not in the middle of a City or next to an industrial area. Grounding/Earthing in a high AC polluted area is NOT nature.

EMF is not a single thing. EMF is the electric field, the magnetic field and the radio frequency (RF). Each requires different equipment to measure and a different approach to mitigate.

#3 - an EMF blocking blanket/canopy can block RF from getting to you BUT being metallic could attract and increase the electric field in the room that you are exposed to. Controlling the electric field in your home is EASY . . . TURN OFF THE CIRCUITS and KILL the 60Hz (50Hz) electric fields, especially at night. Reconnect, ground/earth with Nature - in Nature - it is that simple.

### Trippg — 2023-08-01T20:13:41+0300

Controlling the electric field in your home is EASY . . . TURN OFF THE CIRCUITS and KILL the 60Hz (50Hz) electric fields, especially at night. Reconnect, ground/earth with Nature - in Nature - it is that simple.

Anthony,

I tested this, when I'm ungrounded indoors the electrical field around me is ~5V/m as soon as i touch the copper plate attached to a grounding rod, the electrical field jumps to 60-70 V/m. One thing that is interesting is that my body voltage tested with a multimeter does drop significantly when I touch the grounding plate.

ungrounded indoors = high body voltage (delta between body and ground), low electrical filed (V/m)
grounded indoors = low body voltage (delta between body and ground), high electrical filed (V/m)

Is this because I'm becoming an antenna for the ELF into the ground? Thanks.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T21:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324585>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324579)
>
> Anthony,
>
> I tested this, when I'm ungrounded indoors the electrical field around me is ~5V/m as soon as i touch the copper plate attached to a grounding rod, the electrical field jumps to 60-70 V/m. One thing that is interesting is that my body voltage tested with a multimeter does drop significantly when I touch the grounding plate.
>
> ungrounded indoors = high body voltage (delta between body and ground), low electrical filed (V/m)
> grounded indoors = low body voltage (delta between body and ground), high electrical filed (V/m)
>
> Is this because I'm becoming an antenna for the ELF into the ground? Thanks.

Would need to know your set up. Are these Electric meter readings or body voltage readings? An electric meter gives a very different reading when being touched than when not touched (due to body electric field induction). This is why we prefer body voltage because it tells you what is going on with your body and offers consistency of readings. Being double grounded does not reduce the electric field you are in it just goes thru the other ground and not the meter.

Whatever your BV reading is inside, when you touch a second ground, the reading should go to zero because the field is no longer going thru the BV meter but follows the path of least resistance to ground (the ground pad or grounded appliance - touch a fridge). The reading goes down but the electric field you are in is exactly the same. Put a second meter on the ground pad and you will see what is happening.

To get rid of an electric field ground the appliance or unplug it, or a wire with MC cable. When you ground yourself you make yourself part of the circuit.

5V/m (5,000mV) is a high BV reading, 60,000mV is _#$@&%\*!_

Hey Anthony. Thanks for the response and feedback, greatly appreciate it.

1\. Couldn't agree more nature is king and the ultimate solution.
2\. I live in densely populated South Florida adjacent to a plaza/restaurant/hotel and many neighboring houses. So I just assumed my backyard is marinating in dirty AC current but hard to know for sure of course unless measured. How might one go about measuring this?
Even the beach 5 minutes down the road is inundated with neighboring high rises/restaurants within a few hundred feet, making nature/clean grounding feel inaccessible. Would be nice to have a better understanding of the safe distances from dirty AC current sources that would be safe for grounding (I'm sure there's a multitude of factors but any resources/rough ball parks would be great to know).
3\. I wish it were as easy as just turning off the circuits at night but as I mentioned I'm down here in sweltering South Florida where central air is a necessity and I also use a CPAP to sleep at night, so I’m just trying to mitigate what I can with EMF blocking blanket and wrapping what electronics I can in excessive faraday material. Maybe grounding the blanket would help dispel the electric field a bit? Or would this be just grounding/exposing the blanket to more dirty AC fields making it more of a fishing net for dirty electric fields? How would I be able to tell if its helpful or just increasing the electric field and ultimately harmful?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-02T08:06:10+0300

> 3\. I wish it were as easy as just turning off the circuits at night but as I mentioned I'm down here in sweltering South Florida where central air is a necessity and I also use a CPAP to sleep at night, so I’m just trying to mitigate what I can with EMF blocking blanket and wrapping what electronics I can in excessive faraday material. Maybe grounding the blanket would help dispel the electric field a bit? Or would this be just grounding/exposing the blanket to more dirty AC fields making it more of a fishing net for dirty electric fields? How would I be able to tell if its helpful or just increasing the electric field and ultimately harmful?

Several things here but simply if you measure you know. A body voltage meter measures the electric field you are in that is being induced into your body. You can measure where you are and if the reading is too high you can start turning things off and see the results. Shielding from RF fields with blankets is good BUT you must be sure not to increase the electric field at the same time. By doing what you are doing you may actually be making the total nnEMF situation worse.

At night you only need to turn off the circuits that affect your bedroom. It is ok to flip the breakers for a few weeks. Leave the Air on. Run an extension cord in from a part of the house that is on for the CPAP machine.

Earthing/grounding outside in a built up area (as Dr Jack has pointed out) can be a problem. AC currents in the ground are a very real Issue. If you go to the beach down by the water I expect you will be fine.

You ask in regard to measuring “dirty” AC currents in the ground outside

> How might one go about measuring this?

Give me a couple of weeks and I may have an answer for you.

### ekarge — 2023-08-03T00:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324633>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=324606)

Thanks, Anthony, great suggestions.

Though still a bit unsure exactly how I would ascertain if the EMF blanket is doing more harm than good? Body voltmeter? Compare EMF readings under blanket vs. no blanket?

And will stay tuned for the rest. Appreciate your time/shared knowledge.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-03T02:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324634>

> [ekarge said:](/goto/post?id=324633)
>
> Thanks, Anthony, great suggestions.
>
> Though still a bit unsure exactly how I would ascertain if the EMF blanket is doing more harm than good? Body voltmeter? Compare EMF readings under blanket vs. no blanket?
>
> And will stay tuned for the rest. Appreciate your time/shared knowledge.

The EMF blanket is to reduce RF. It does this by being metallic and reflecting the RF away. If you are using it in an area that has a 60Hz AC electric field, being metallic, it will attract this electric field. An RF meter, I hope would show a reduced RF. A body voltage meter (BV), I expect, will show an increased electric field. Measure on the bed without the blanket then with. If you then ground the blanket your BV will show close to zero (because you would be double grounded) BUT the field would be exactly the same, except now you are part of the circuit and this 60Hz AC field is flowing thru you all night.

You are obviously trying a number of things to hopefully reduce your nnEMF exposure but you have no idea if what you are doing is helping or hindering unless you measure.

### ekarge — 2023-08-03T05:36:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-324641>

Got it. So sounds like generally speaking, EMF blankets in many cases are futile and not really beneficial when they are being used in area with AC electric fields bc while it may be reflecting RF, it's still attracting the surrounding AC fields and making you more a part of that 'dirty' electrical field. The only seemingly positive roll it could play is if you can drastically/measurably eliminate this AC field by turning off circuits, in which case the blanket could serve its intended beneficial purpose of reflecting RF, without being a fishing net for AC electric fields.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-03T05:59:22+0300

Yup, you got it. Although, if high RF is the primary concern, (ie close proximity to a cell tower) then that is the priority and compromises may need to be made in other areas (while making plans to move). By the way, is this RF blanket under you as well as over you? If not it could be making the RF worse also. Bed canopies are a better solution where there is no physical contact with the material but even then without turning off the electric field, problems can be created. People selling products tend to focus on the problem they are offering a solution to without consideration of the problems they may be creating.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-17T21:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-325233>

> [Elizabeth Leonard said:](/goto/post?id=325212)

>
> We have been shutting our breaker off at night surrounding our bedroom, in this case, is it ok to use grounding pad on bed, or still no?

You are turning off the circuits at night, what is your reading now? How many 60Hz AC mV do you still have?

The benefit of Grounding/Earthing is reconnecting with Nature and making yourself part of the natural DC energy circuit of the Earth. Being outside, away from man made nnEMF with your bare feet on the ground.

ALL the neutrals in your house are connected together in the breaker box. ALL the ground wires are connected together in the breaker box AND ALL the neutrals are connected to all the ground wires. All these wires also act as antennas picking up RF. If you ground to the house ground you are making yourself part of the AC circuit. If you use an outside ground you are still inside. You are not on the ground you are connected to a long copper wire (antenna).
