### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T20:06:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-325334>

> [Elizabeth Leonard said:](/goto/post?id=325322)
>
> Is this measuring that we could do ourselves at a reasonable price(getting device), or do we need to hire expert?

Measuring for oneself is simple and does not cost that much (relatively speaking). Some people just don’t want to do it themselves so they higher in an expert. Others may have a more complicated situation and need the special equipment and expertise of a professional to resolve their issue. If you have a Building Biologist or EMF Consultant close by this need not be too expensive either but if they are several hours away the price goes up. A virtual consult can be a good compromise but you will still have to do the measuring yourself.

There are different schools of thought on this but you asked me. For in home 60Hz AC electric fields, which for most people is the largest single source of daily nnEMF exposure, we recommend a body voltage meter. This will tell you how strong the electric fields are throughout your home and help locate the source (hidden wires) so you can mitigate. The Building Biology Institute say a BV reading at night below 10 milli-Volts (mV) is of “no concern”, between 10mV and 100mV of “slight concern”, above 1,000mV “extreme concern”. I have seen readings in bedrooms of over 7,000mV before mitigation and most are above 2,000mV! Touching my iPad while charging 40,000+mV. We have a body voltage kit (BVK) on our web site, less than $150 with Kruse10 discount code.

For Radio Frequency (RF) - WiFi, cell phones, Bluetooth, “smart meters”, microwave ovens etc you need a RF meter. The goal is to first identify any problems, locate the source and then mitigate. The important number is the end number, the difference between the first reading and the mitigated reading, did the mitigation work? RF meters can get very expensive but a consumer grade meter many people use is the TriField TF2. This meter measures RF up to 6 GigaHertz (GHz) - covers microwave, 4G and lower frequency T-Mobile, AT&T 5G. By the way, microwave ovens, WiFi routers and Bluetooth devices ALL use 2.4GHz. The TF2 also measures magnetic fields, available on Amazon for $180+/-.

When you switch off ALL the circuits at night that are within 8 feet of your bed, you remove 100% of the electric field, magnetic field, “DE” and RF on those circuits. This creates an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary and because we are more sensitive at night to the affects of EMF you could be reducing the negative affects of daily nnEMF exposure by as much as 70%. Many EHS people have told us this significantly improved their condition and they became less affected by the outside EMF When they went out.

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-22T10:28:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-325407>

Native Americans would dig a shallow pit and put babies in it to ground them (they had no 60hz). In Europe they put babies outside (away from 60Hz) in the sun every day. Modern science is just trying to catch up.

I've always felt better using a grounding mat at night connected to the outside, knowing where there is no pipes or wiring out there, and with no 5G. Pre the 2020 global meltdown after a long weekend in a 5G city and/or a flight, a night of grounded sleep made a huge difference to recovery and mitigation. (Finally able to go to the sun this coming winter for a UV top up. )

In Becker's book Cross Currents there is a discussion on the earth's rotation through the magnetosphere, and that any given spot on the earth's surface is in a constantly changing magnetic field. There's a diagram of the daily rise and fall in the strength of the magnetic field at one spot on the earth during a quiet period of solar activity. On the pineal, after acknowledging the retinal pathway, he says

_"It appears that, over 2 billion years of evolution, living things have taken advantage of the two portions of the electromagnetic spectrum that could be depended upon to always be present: the geomagnetic field and visible light. In this view, it is no more surprising that life developed specific organs to sense the geomagnetic field and to derive timing information from it than that it developed specific organs to sense and derive information from light."

"More recently, it has been shown that the pineal is also sensitive to the daily cyclic pattern in the Earth's magnetic field. **Melatonin secretion in human subjects may be changed at will by exposure to steady magnetic fields of the same strength as the geomagnetic field.** Apparently, nature determined that biological cycle activity was too important to be left to one environmental signal alone. "

"Nature intended the pineal to simultaneously receive the same signals from the daily pattern of day-night and the same rise and fall in strength of the geomagnetic field. Obviously, when one or both signals are abnormal, the pineal does not respond in the normal fashion, and the body's biological cycles become disturbed - with important clinical consequences."_

Figure 2 of the paper is interesting. If grounding has this vagal tone effect on vulnerable babies under hi tech conditions without removing the 60Hz exposure, then theoretically turning off the breakers to a bedroom at night, and using a grounding mat with an outside rod in a safe outside space (context as usual) could gain signalling benefits from the geomagnetic field when light is absent, as well as electrons. Doubt and fear changes the results of any experiment I do. Even after reading research on grounding, I've always preferred not using the sockets, but its more of a left brain/right brain thing. Intuitively the outside rod is the better option for my n=1, and by checking where its put in the outside environment, my logical brain gets out of the way.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-22T20:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.28023/post-325424>

Most of us go by our feelings when it comes to grounding, EMF mitigation etc. I am reminded of a lab experiment that was done with rodents. Two groups exposed to the same EMF, one group had a so called EMF “neutralizing“ devise in with them, the other didn’t. The group with the “neutralizer” ran around all day ”feeling good”. The second group stayed as far away from the source of the EMF as they could (feeling not so good?). The group that “felt good” and ran around ignoring the EMF (because the short term affects were covered?) lived half as long as the group that avoided the EMF. The affects of nnEMF on the body are the affects of nnEMF no matter how we feel. What nnEMF is doing at a cellular level in the background may take years to manifest despite an apparent short term benefit. Covering up nnEMF is never as good as removing it or being removed from it. Reduce nnEMF exposure and spend as much time in Nature away from it as possible. More research needs to be done on the short term and long term physical affects of Earthing/Grounding while in a 60Hz AC electric field beyond how people feel.

The second group stayed as far away from the source of the EMF as they could (feeling not so good?). Nocturnal animals in the wild would be reliant on the geomagnetic field. If the neutraliser in the rodent study wasn’t grounding them to the geomagnetic field (in darkness, not running around under any light frequency they would normally sleep through) the study isn’t comparable to humans. If the (diurnal) human baby study had measurable effects - sure they would be better off if they were well without all the tech, but finding something to support their progress without risking the removal of lifesaving monitoring or oxygen delivering equipment etc- I'm curious as to how it did what it did.

Although nnEMF is bad, the question I have is, were the effects seen in the baby study because whatever nnEMF was present, grounding at the same time was better than nnEMF without grounding, (although avoiding nnEMF and being grounded would obviously be better?)

There's a lot about why grounding is good or bad depending on the viewpoint. But I haven't seen any studies looking at long term blocking of the native electromagnetic field at night on vagal tone, UV signalling, repair and regeneration, or sleep cycles including REM, with or without nnEMF, so what Becker said may be important.

_"Nature intended the pineal to simultaneously receive the same signals from the daily pattern of day-night and the same rise and fall in strength of the geomagnetic field. Obviously, **when one or both signals are abnormal,** the pineal does not respond in the normal fashion, and the body's biological cycles become disturbed - with important clinical consequences."_

Although what nnEMF is doing at a cellular level in the background may take years to manifest despite any short term benefits "felt" by grounding, by the same token it may take years to manifest what _not_ experiencing the _natural_ EMF is doing to us, particularly during sleep. Blocking nnEMF tied to an _absent_ geomagnetic field signal at night when its supposed to be the dominant signal to the biological cycles (12 hours at the equator, swinging between 8 and 16 at my latitude) doesn't seem anywhere close to optimal. Theoretically I could sleep like a log for 8 hours with no nnEMF but not regenerate, repair or enter the sleep cycles properly, and wouldn't know it until disease hits me.

Easier said than done for many I know, but if my environment was so bad I was considering faraday options or too frightened to put a grounding rod in an outside space and switch off the breaker at night I would move at the first opportunity, however "clean" from nnEMF I got my sleeping environment.

rye and wheat were milled to get white flower

we had oil from rapeseed

[@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/)

i learned that yesterday. Maybe i live under a rock, but when did this come about?
Anyways, I listened to Dr. Alexander Wunsch, who was recommended by Dr. Jack as a German doctor. Apparently in the EU you can't get any incandescent lighting for years. And while no artificial light is better than any, it is acording to Dr. Wunsch a lot better than LED.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-30T20:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/august-first-incandescent-light-is-banned-from-being-sold-in-the-usa.28025/post-324491>

On Monday night I think we should all light a candle in remembrance of the incandescent bulb.

Much has been written about bulbs and light. Incandescent are inefficient from a lighting point of view because 90% +/- of their energy consumption is heat (not light). That heat though is IR “light”, VERY beneficial in our nnEMF lit inside world.

I’m leaning toward #1 bed at sunset. #2 candles. #3 red lights. #4 In the winter when I need to see, 12V DC bulbs where I can (little to no flicker) or no flicker, full spectrum LED‘s (grow, aquarium, reptile lights) with a red and/or NIR/UV light in the same room.

Of course this ban is for energy saving reasons because electric stoves, heat, A/C and car chargers are all so efficient . . .

### Tyler Hanson — 2023-07-28T06:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/car-windows-tinted-or-clear.28033/post-324410>

So I've been pondering this and not sure what to think.

Clear car windows are still blocking UV, and a lot of the red, and tinted windows are going to be blocking even more red, and creating a more blue rich environment, but with considerably less bright light.

What's better.... a brighter light with a better spectrum, or a darker more blue spectrum.

# Thread: Raised age spots
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raised-age-spots.28034/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-07-29T23:05:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raised-age-spots.28034/post-324453>

My dad has a ton of raised age spots on his back that bother him. The dermo obviously told him it’s from the sun, which is why I turn to the proper crowd to help me with him why he should avoid wearing sunscreen and SPF/zinc.

Any studies or information on how the sun is not the main cause of raised age spots? Any info is helpful!

### JanSz — 2023-07-29T23:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raised-age-spots.28034/post-324456>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=324453)
>
> My dad has a ton of raised age spots on his back that bother him. The dermo obviously told him it’s from the sun, which is why I turn to the proper crowd to help me with him why he should avoid wearing sunscreen and SPF/zinc.
>
> Any studies or information on how the sun is not the main cause of raised age spots? Any info is helpful!

You are using the word **"raised"**.
Usually, they are called Age spots, and are small, **flat** dark areas on the skin.
Or liver spots, or sunspots.
They vary in size and usually appear on areas exposed to the sun.
\-----------
Sun should not be avoided.
Sunscreen should be avoided.
Likely it may help if you would post (good picture) and describe why this bothers your dad?
Is that mental or physical, say itch that he must scratch.
\---------------
When intentionally seeking the sunlight,
still
there are times for sun.
and
times for shade.

Just observe animals.
..

# Thread: Burning man, it what it has to do with Dr. Kruse's teachings

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/burning-man-it-what-it-has-to-do-with-dr-kruses-teachings.28035/post-324482>

Hi all, the man burns in 34 days, I am going in 20 days, completely off grid.

If anybody who doesn't know (much) about Burning Man wants to get an inside scoop, I shall gladly provide. Ask me anything, I have been going since 2010, my husband since 2004, and it's our son's 5th burn.

No, not everybody is naked and yes, many people don't do drugs
In my opinion it is an amazing playground and practical experimentation station for all sorts of living, decentralized living being a huge one of it.

The amounts of human energy in their most intricate form and shape over there is stunning and overwhelming, and the sunrises are the most unique on the planet, since the Black Rock Desert part named "playa" consists of white alcaline dust from a dried out lake, that reflects the light in colors unlike anywhere else. Photos can't capture.

\>> waiting for the sunrise

# Thread: Windows "nightmode" bluelight block
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lindman112 — 2023-07-30T19:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324488>

I'm not subscribed to any of the apps that are downloadable, im just using the natural windows function.

Does the normal "nightmode" function on screens actually help? I always have it turned to max. My screen is bright red and I can hardly see but it feels very relaxing on the eyes.

Any expert on windows computers can chime in? I feel like it helps but it might just be the placebo effect.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-30T21:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324494>

Does your screen have a backlight? If so, no software filter will eliminate all the blue. Try viewing the screen with/without blue blockers as an easy test. If the screen filter is doing its job, you should not be able to tell a diff with/without the glasses.

I prefer to use F.lux

It's simple to download and set up and works really good.

### coho3131 — 2023-07-31T03:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324503>

I tried F.lux. I like Iris even better. PWM Flicker is a factor also; I am not sure if F.lux helps with this now, but when I tried it years ago it did not.

### coho3131 — 2023-07-31T03:55:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324504>

Brightness is also harmful to our circadian rhythm.

### Lindman112 — 2023-07-31T17:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324532>

Thanks guys, I have blue light blocker glasses so i'll try that out.

Yeah, im having trouble sleeping right now in scandinavia and staring at the dark ceiling just makes my head race around so I thought i'd try staying up one night with blue light blockers + nightmode on my screen and get some work done and recover tomorrow.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-02T15:19:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324614>

> [Lindman112 said:](/goto/post?id=324532)
>
> thought i'd try staying up one night with blue light blockers + nightmode on my screen and get some work done and recover tomorrow.

I wouldn't. Embrace darkness after sunset as much as possible.

Im curious, what is the length of your day in Scandinavia these days?

### Lindman112 — 2023-08-08T23:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324857>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=324614)
>
> I wouldn't. Embrace darkness after sunset as much as possible.
>
> Im curious, what is the length of your day in Scandinavia these days?

Sorry for late response, yes the scandinavian day length is getting better now, from may to july it's been like 3 AM to 9 PM, then the sun "sets" but it doesn't really go dark, it's just semi-dark from 9 PM to 3 AM and it starts over again, now it rises around 4 AM and we actually have some darkness around 11 PM - 05 AM in august.

I've got my windows covered with pitch black sheets to block out most of the lights (Not only from the sun but also all the annoying street lights outside my apartment).

I try to sleep around 10 PM while it's still fairly bright but it's a "bit" dark around 2 AM before the sun rises and then back at it again.

I used to think the long winters was hard but my new-found obsession with darkness and sunlight has taken it to a whole new level.

In a month or two it will be the opposite, just darkness all day around.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-11T00:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-nightmode-bluelight-block.28036/post-324941>

I honestly wouldn't even know what protocol to follow in that environment. That sounds tricky. My inclination would be to listen to your body for the proper bedtime and wake time, assuming as you are getting adequate time outdoors. I would also be CTing like crazy if I lived that far north.

# Thread: Bringing Kruseology to Friends/Family

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-07-30T22:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bringing-kruseology-to-friends-family.28038/post-324496>

I am really struggling with getting friends/family to care about light/water/magnetism. Been trying to bring these concepts to ppl for the last couple months and have had zero success.

I've focused only on the light pillar. Haven't even attempted bringing water and mag to them yet. I like to think I do a pretty good job explaining the concepts of light to them, but they just don't believe it, or don't care.

I started out just trying to explain everything. Now I have backed off a bit and am trying more indirect "lead by example" approach. Problem is I've never had major health probs, so they aren't going to physically see a big change in me through this approach.

I know I ultimately cannot make others understand or care about these topics, but I feel like I need to continue trying. It pains me to continue watching the ppl closest to me go on living like they do and continue falling apart healthwise. I am wondering if anyone else shares in this struggle and if there are any tips on different approaches/mindsets to this conundrum.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-07-31T04:23:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bringing-kruseology-to-friends-family.28038/post-324505>

Joseph, there is a good chance the people you know, especially family will not listen. Not personal, just a fact of life and as other have said here we each come to this in our own way and time. They will hear it better from someone else. The lucky amongst us didn’t have to “hit the wall” before paying attention but many did. Share what you know then shut up, be an example and wait for questions. Copy and share this if you want (or not).

My name is Anthony Smith, I am a professional EMF consultant and I talk to people all day about non-native ElectroMagnetic Fields (nnEMF) and the affects they are having on their health and how to mitigate them in the home. Some already get it but even they have family that don’t and it helps for their healing journey if they do. Here is what I share with them to help get their family on board.

Our bodies are electrical (think EEG, EKG) and they run on DC voltage, nature runs on DC voltage. Our modern world largely runs on AC voltage. We are surrounded by 60Hz AC electric fields (a Body Voltage Kit will physically show these) and 60Hz electric lights (vibrating 60x per second) as well as an assortment of nnEMF radio frequencies (RF), TV, mobile phone, micro waves etc. all going thru us, all the time.

During the day our brains mostly operate between 16Hz to 100Hz +/- (Beta & Gamma). At night our brains need to go thru a reset, to relax and drop down to 8Hz to 15Hz (Alpha) and then to sleep, deep sleep, heal & detox drop even further to 1Hz to 7Hz (Delta & Theta). In the evening the signal from nature to shut down is the light changing (sunset) and the natural EMF (from the sun) going away. So if in the evening we sit in 60Hz AC electric fields and look at artificial lights (the color spectrum of midday sun light) and then lay in 60Hz AC electric fields (from the wires in our walls) all night, our brains cannot relax and reset and our immune system cannot operate optimally because all the necessary body systems THAT ARE CHARGED BY BEING IN SUN LIGHT DURING THE DAY are not properly activated. All the signals are wrong and melatonin is suppressed.

Natures signal to get us going again in the morning is the light frequencies of sunrise. These also charge the batteries in our cells (mitochondria) to give us energy. The more full spectrum natural sun light we get during the day, to charge up all our systems and the less artificial light and nnEMF we get during the evening/night the more efficient our body/brain/system will work for us. Simple biology/physics/science.

I think I was at your point a few years ago. It is very frustrating.

I've had some success by just mentioning that "I feel better when I go outdoors a lot." or "I try not to use any artificial light after dark."

Except for my close family members, I don't put too much time into trying to help people unless they ask for help first.

Sometimes if you have planted a "seed" of information in a person in the past, you get to see it grow in the future, if they think they discovered the information for themselves.

It was many years ago that I had told people about the toxicity of sunscreens. Recently some of those same people are telling me that they have "heard" that sunscreens are toxic, and that they stopped using them. Maybe they were finally convinced because of the seed I planted years ago.

It's admirable that you keep trying to help.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324502>

I am almost done reading John Ott's book - Health and Light.
Its now obvious to me that I need to install some black light lamps indoors for daytime exposure as I am not outside enough hours in the day.
got to get this UVA and UVB in the retinas.
But with this several questions arise:
1) what lamps do you recommend for this purpose? Just regular florescent black light light bulbs? How many watts? how many? how close to as a example my work area?
2) I wear glasses. This blocks it. Is there any glasses that allows UV light in?
3) I read somewhere contact lenses doesnt block UV. would this be a better option to use? I prefer not as it reduce oxygen uptake on the eyes by covering them over with this artificial material.
Any suggestions?
Thank you

Frequency of pink light
The **color pink**, is not a part of **spectrum**, it does not have a particular frequency.
It's an extra-spectral color, and it has to be mixed to generate it

### Cadoia — 2023-08-01T18:05:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324575>

So found this online:
"Without any specific technology or coatings designed to block UV light, standard regular soft contact lenses typically offer limited UV protection. The percentage of UV light transmission through these lenses can vary depending on the lens material and its specific characteristics.

On average, it is estimated that regular soft contact lenses may block or absorb around 10% to 30% of UVB rays and 3% to 15% of UVA rays. This means that a significant portion of UV radiation may still reach the eyes when wearing standard soft contact lenses without additional UV-blocking features."

It makes me consider maybe using contact lenses instead of glasses as the block a lot more UV.
I just dont like the idea of putting this over my retina as it lowers oxygen flow to the tissues I would assume.
But I dislike the idea of having "window glass" covering my eyes all day just so I can see, even more.

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=324577)
>
> Wonder if you came across similar evaluation for my lenses.
> They’ve replaced my cataracts.

I am assuming you mean you had an operation and they put lenses inside your eye correct?
Its made of what?

The amount of UV light blocked by intraocular lens (IOL) materials can vary depending on the specific type of material used. Generally, IOLs made from acrylic or silicone materials are designed to block a significant portion of harmful ultraviolet (UV) radiation to protect the eye's structures.
The UV-blocking capability of IOLs is typically measured by their UV transmittance percentage. For example:

1.  Acrylic IOLs: These IOLs often have UV transmittance values of around 10% or less, meaning they block at least 90% of harmful UV rays.
2.  Silicone IOLs: Silicone IOLs usually have UV transmittance values of about 5% or less, blocking at least 95% of harmful UV rays.

Considering in John Ott's own case he went into the sun all day every day in Florida with little to no effect on his arthritis and only by accident broke his glasses only to discover having the eyes exposed to sunlight directly healed his issue right away, I would say having UV blocking lenses operated into ones eyes is a bit of an issue.
This is why I started this thread because I wear glasses 98% of my time as I have very bad vision. Was born with it. So my story is: how to get glasses that transmit UV. Or buy "cheap" unprotected low tech contact lenses that will let the UV in.
But if you got one of these above mentioned materials firmly operated in your eyes its a whole different situation. Im no expert. Maybe what you got is different?

Have found some plastic with no uv blocking ,might be hope for us after all

**UV Acrylic: Acrylic Sheet | Plastic Stockist**

Plastic Stockist offers **Sunbed grade acrylic sheet** in 2mm, 3mm and 5mm material thickness. Sunbed acrylic panels are for applications requiring transparency to UV rays and these acrylic sheets are produced without UV filters.

**Common uses for Sunbed Grade UV Acrylic Sheet

Sunbed grade UV acrylic sheet** is often used in sunbeds and acrylic tanks for reptiles that requires UV rays. These UV sheets are for internal applications.

taken from

[https://www.plasticstockist.com/Sunbed-Grade-Acrylic-Sheet.aspx](https://www.plasticstockist.com/Sunbed-Grade-Acrylic-Sheet.aspx)

### Cadoia — 2023-08-03T18:19:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324658>

According to the web search results, contact lenses without UV filter will let in most of the UV radiation that reaches the eye, depending on their thickness and material. One study measured the UV and visible light transmittance of different soft contact lenses with and without UV filter, and found that contact lenses without UV filter had an average UV-A transmittance of 87.7% and an average UV-B transmittance of 88.5%.
Another study found similar results, with an average UV-A transmittance of 86.6% and an average UV-B transmittance of 87.8% for contact lenses without UV filter.
These studies also showed that contact lenses with a positive back vertex power (BVP) (+1.00 to +6.00 D) had slightly lower UV transmittance than contact lenses with a negative BVP (−1.00 to −12.00 D), because they were thicker in the center.

So it looks to me if one has reduced eye sight and need glasses or contact lenses the contact lenses is the better option.

The data for the UV transmittance of contact lenses at the 380 to 400 nm range is not very abundant, but I found some information from the web search results that might be helpful. One study that measured the UV and visible transmittance of soft contact lenses with and without UV filter reported the average UV transmittance for different brands and powers in the range of 280 to 380 nm, but not beyond that.
However, another source from ZEISS Vision Care stated that the gap between 380 and 400 nm accounts for 40% of total UV exposure at sea level, and that the most common lens material sold globally typically only fully blocks UV below 360 nm.
This implies that contact lenses without UV filter would let in a significant amount of UV radiation in the 380 to 400 nm range, unless they are made of a special material that blocks more UV.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-04T21:52:04+0300

### coho3131 — 2023-08-05T03:05:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324712>

#1 Sunlight --> Contact Lens Filtering and Refraction --> nnEMF --> Retina ( No thank you )

#2 Corneal Hypoxia is a huge issue.

#3 Eyeglasses, especially small ones, allow natural sunlight to enter the eye in the regions not directly blocked by the lens.

#4 You can easily remove your eyeglasses outdoors when sitting or walking in a safe area.

#5 I would use "data and information" that is supplied by manufacturers, distributors, and merchants with caution.

\[ There might be some safety and performance advantages to wearing contact lenses for sports or other activities, but I would consider the risks versus the specific benefits in those situations. Competitive swimming gets mentioned as one of those activities, but there are now plenty of very affordable options for swim goggles for near-sighted or far-sighted people. Reggie Jackson, Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Eric Dickerson, Bob Griese, and Billie Jean King wore eyeglasses while destroying their competition. \]

### Cadoia — 2023-08-06T04:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-324754>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=324712)
>
> #2 Corneal Hypoxia is a huge issue.

According to the sources I found, the amount of oxygen that reaches the cornea depends on several factors, such as the type, material, fit, and wear time of the contact lenses. Some types of contact lenses are more breathable than others, meaning they allow more oxygen to pass through them. For example, gas-permeable hard contact lenses are more breathable than soft contact lenses, and silicone hydrogel contact lenses are more breathable than hydrogel contact lenses. The fit of the contact lenses also affects the oxygen supply to the cornea, as a tight-fitting lens can reduce the tear flow and create a stagnant layer of fluid under the lens. The wear time of the contact lenses is another important factor, as wearing them for too long or overnight can deprive the cornea of oxygen and increase the risk of hypoxia

One study measured the oxygen transmissibility (Dk/t) of different types of contact lenses and found that gas-permeable hard contact lenses had a Dk/t of 100 or higher, silicone hydrogel contact lenses had a Dk/t of 80 to 140, and hydrogel contact lenses had a Dk/t of 10 to 30. The Dk/t is a measure of how much oxygen can pass through a lens of a certain thickness. The higher the Dk/t, the more breathable the lens is. The study also estimated that the minimum Dk/t required to avoid corneal hypoxia is 24 for daily wear and 87 for extended wear. Therefore, gas-permeable hard contact lenses and silicone hydrogel contact lenses are more suitable for preventing corneal hypoxia than hydrogel contact lenses.

Now the question remains which kind of lenses blocks the least UV and are they the same that allow oxygen flow to the cornea?

### doctorwu85 — 2024-09-24T20:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-glasses-sold-in-the-world-that-lets-in-uv-light.28039/post-339969>

I've used prescription VivaRays for about a year and have been very happy with them. The basic eyeglasses let in about 60% of UV as others have pointed out, and they include three magnetic attachments with increasing levels of blue light blocking. If I'm doing sun and grounding I typically take them off, but if you're doing something outside that requires perfect vision they're the best I've found.

For the magnetic clip-ons, I wear the light amber ones at work/in front of the computer, the full amber ones after sundown, and the red ones about an hour before sleep (or if exposed to extreme bright LED light at night). I've noticed that my eyes have become much more sensitive to blue light, to the point where the fluorescent lights in the main office actually hurt my eyes if I don't wear one of the sets of lenses. I assume being more sensitized to blue light is probably a beneficial thing.

# Thread: The effect of nnEMF on brain waves
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T04:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/post-324556>

I am so not qualified to discuss this in detail (there is a Neurosurgeon in the room), all I know is what I have read, the brain wants to (need to) reset each night which means down regulating first to Rest/Alpha wave (8Hz to 12Hz) then to Sleep/Theta (4Hz to 7Hz) and then to Deep Dive/Delta (.5Hz - 4Hz.) The Earths natural background frequency is 7.83Hz - Schumann Resonance (this can and does jump around).

The in home AC electric field in USA & Canada resonates at 60Hz (50Hz in other countries). In many of our bedrooms, before mitigation, this field is 1500 - 3000 Milli-Volts). It stands to reason that it is difficult for our brains to find the whisper or Schumann (~300 microVolts) in this 60Hz ”Rock Concert”. There are 1,000 micro Volts in 1 milli-Volt - 3,000 milli-Volts is 10,000x the “volume” of 300 micro-Volts.

I say turn off, or at least turn down, the 60Hz and give peace (Schumann) a chance.

Add to this the very presents of nnEMF makes the brain (Pineal Glad) think it is still day time. Back in the day when the “Big Ball” went bye bye there was no more EMF which is one of the signals to release the melatonin. Any nnEMF interferes with the natural process. Add to that the 60Hz (50Hz) frequency and you have a double whammy.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-01T05:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/post-324558>

Thank you so much, Anthony.

I think some people live awake in alpha a good amount of their day, calmly. And sleep? it would be interesting to refine it, or define it. Theta is usually unconscious, but it doesn’t have to be.

It never occurred to me that an electromagnetic field of the type bedroom electricity could have an impact on my brain waves. Hm. I need to reassess.

On a cellular, respectively mitochondrial level that is different, as I can see the sensitivity of those little guys. And that would obviously also count for the brain tissue mitochondria. But are those in charge of forming what we call brain waves? I don’t know.

I do know that when I started reading about the brain and its uses and functions, I was 8 and I tricked my brain into learning stuff over night, BUT, mostly all that was written about how our brain works that I could find in the times before we had an internet was bs. I read so much, and it was useless information.

It still is probably a ton of bs, because scientists are not as great as most of them believe that they are and unfortunately they clog up the pathways for people like Dr. Jack or anybody who wants to speed past them.

That said, we are on our own. And experimenting on humans has its healthy limitations. Training Gama is a thing.

I talked to the people from Dave Asprey who have brain wave modulation pods or whatever they call it. Unpleasant.

It’s unbelievable, that they charge like 20k for training and adjusting brainwave patterns according to a mishmash of what they figured out successful people are displaying.

I’m not saying it cant work. And I believe in biofeedback, of any sort… like a good tan, that’s good biofeedback

So ok, Schuhman, that’s grounding, and the frequency is 7hz -8hz. So wait, would we sync up with 7hz brainwaves sleeping? No.
Sleep is lower, much lower.

I don’t understand the Milli Volt part, you are talking about.
I get that the electro noise is unfortunate and omni-present.

Do I need a certain voltage in my mitochondria? I am not getting this together.

Thanks again, I find this so interesting.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-01T08:47:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-nnemf-on-brain-waves.28040/post-324563>

There is awake, conscious (to various degrees), then there is relaxed, prayerful, meditative, then there is sleep (dream state), then there is deep dive sleep (dreamless, healing). Obviously there are overlaps and areas that a disciplined mind can access and take advantage of.

The Earths natural frequency is what we call Schumann 7.83Hz +/- and the brains natural reset is also 7.83Hz. There are various states of consciousness all of which have a purpose. As Nicola Tesla said: “If you want to find the secret of the Universe, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration.”

I’ve done biofeedback and found it interesting and useful. I’ve also done prayer and meditation and would lean in that direction. Taping into the natural process seams . . . more natural and I get that is where Jack is coming from also.

Milli-Volts vs Micro-Volts is just a way to measure the strength of the signal/field (power). People rant and rave about “something” that is measured in micro-Volts (not that it is not important) BUT ignore something else (saying it doesn’t matter) that is 1,000x or 10,000x stronger. Context and perspective are vital.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-something-spiritual-to-light-exposure-or-just-neuro-chemicals.28043/post-324601>

I believe there is a chemical correspondence to a spiritual state.

I once read a dissertation online, probably 20 years ago, in which the student compared the PET scans of schizophrenia with that of spiritual enlightenment. And they looked pretty much the same. I wish I could find that paper again.

So that would prove that the chemistry alone doesn’t do the trick and you also need context.

The whole subject regarding hallucinations is very dubious and not quite researched. Group hallucinations of all sorts exist, with and without drugs.

When I grew up, the thesis was considered true that the brain basically does not develop after 20. I always said that’s wrong, but I also was too lazy to walk the walk and prove it. Others have done this work now and at least we are at 50 I believe. Like they can prove that we are still forming all kinds of things at 50 in our brains, new synapses,why thank you.

Why would it stop?
I’ve been following the psychedelic and you know, mostly recreational drug use if people would be more honest, for expanding human potential. It’s problematic, because the people who believe in drugs like to go overboard, and they also need, you guess it: money.

This ruins pretty much everything, because you need to be a strategist more than a researcher. Dr. Shulgin, (phical) who I truly believe did it for research and exploring the human condition found his lab seized constantly, then always a foot in jail. And that’s just a drug guy.

There is so much that is being researched by people who prefer to stay off the grid or under the radar, because the sanctions are super high. It’s not a good thing, because all those idiots, who obviously are also out there, can claim that that’s what they do: research. But they don’t, they are just the guru type with ego issues. most people in the esoteric and spiritual scene are unfortunately like that. Very hard to find an honest person.

I’ll look up Marzinski, thank you.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-04T22:05:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-something-spiritual-to-light-exposure-or-just-neuro-chemicals.28043/post-324700>

I think this all depends on perspective and your thoughts about light. Light certainly has a plethora of biophysical/biochemical effects on the body, but let's say you aren't aware of these direct effects. You might be inclined to believe there is some mystical healing powers to light. That simple thought alone can alter your neurochemical state. Or let's consider the opposite, say you believe that sunlight is truly toxic. That thought alone might change your brain state in a different way if your are in the light. Might cause you to experience a stress response when in sunlight.

At the end of the day, all of these outcomes are biophysical/biochemical in nature, but I think different ppl can experience different outcomes based on what they think about light.

It’s the one thing all those studies have in common that the studies try to exclude for. I’ll give Dr. Joe Dispenza all the credit from my advantage point for bringing the topic to my attention. And even with him, I still had the thought caged to every once and while versus a continuous part of the experience.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-05T19:48:49+0300

You can always take parts from people and if he inspired you to something, that’s great.
I’ve seen the first couple of episodes and thought ok, and then he turned wrong for me. I forgot about what it was though, because I already decided that it was a waste of my time.

Of course thoughts influence your reality and your whole being. That’s not really particularly…particular.

Here is how you find the truth: you ask yourself and you listen to yourself. Nobody can do that trick for you. If you would like me to be more specific: you listen to your heart. Some say gut, but I would say that as great as the gut is, it is a more practical organ in that sense and the heart (that is still a metaphor) is the reliable resource.

All other pathways can be corrupted. They don’t have to be, but they can be, as by their nature. The connection to your heart is as direct as can be.

If you can’t hear your heart, well that is something that can be practiced. And the best thing to ask is “who am I ?”

I cannot correct you, if you are wrong. I mean, I can, but ideally you do that yourself

### Awakenotwoke — 2023-08-06T22:44:49+0300

Your intention is vital in your ability to receive. process and bear fruit. Your decision to align yourself with your true dharma, to detach yourself and shed the ills of the world as you drink the orange manna is vital in truly receiving all it has to offer. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-something-spiritual-to-light-exposure-or-just-neuro-chemicals.28043/post-332287>

So religion has lifted humanity to incredible heights, producing amazing art and culture, and it does tell you it's about light, but the religious still wear sunglasses, headcoverings, and you never get told to see the sunrise, and when they do their praying and theologizing or icon painting or what have you, they're doing it indoors in isolated blue light or under artificial light at night.

And so they'll talk lofty things about metaphysical light, but it becomes hard to take any of it serious once you see they'd have women covered up at the beach, and all the intellectuals out there wanting to debate it are super pale looking.

# Thread: Magnetism and geographic location
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetism-and-geographic-location.28044/>

### Evee — 2023-08-02T04:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetism-and-geographic-location.28044/post-324600>

Can someone help me out - I was researching areas in the Yucatán to stay and came across an article that says the magnetic field is different on the east and west side of the peninsula (lower in parts of the east and higher in parts of the west)
It goes on to say that NATURAL low magnetic field have been shown to be beneficial against cancer, while NATURAL high ones are better for things like neuro degenerative diseases. (Not talking about NNemf!)
I’m new as a member and working my way through the blogs (and don’t feel like I fully understand the magnetism piece as it relates to geography yet) so does anyone know if this article tracks with what Jack says about magnetism and where we should be looking to stay? If I’m in my 40s and concerned about hereditary cancer risk, is a place like Merida (should be lower natural magnetic field according to article) a smarter choice than Isla de las Mujeres (higher)?

Thanks!

### [Deuterium, Secret of Yucatán](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/deuterium-secret-yucatán-doris-loh)

When discussing the role of deuterium in the origin of life, it is impossible to exclude the mass extinction event that extinguished over 75% of species on earth. The Chicxulub impact crater in Yucatán, Mexico is the only known terrestrial impact structure that has remained intact.

 www.linkedin.com

I cannot speak to the magnetic geography part of your question but in regard to your statement:

> If I’m in my 40s and concerned about hereditary cancer risk,

It is my understanding that “hereditary” is a TINY part of cancer risk. Over 90% of cancer “RISK” has to do totally with environment. Your parents cancer problems are not your cancer concerns UNLESS you are in exactly the same environment.

# Thread: Water Softener, Potassium Chloride or Sodium Chloride?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-softener-potassium-chloride-or-sodium-chloride.28045/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gabriel L Rosenfeld — 2023-08-02T18:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-softener-potassium-chloride-or-sodium-chloride.28045/post-324622>

Hello,

I hope everyone is doing well. A couple of clarifications on water. Jack says to not add salt to water.

I have a water softener for hard well water. Is the small amount of sodium chloride added fine? Or should I switch to potassium chloride?

Also, I saw somewhere that Jack said to not add halides to water. I add iodine to my water bottle everyday, is that alright or not recommended?

Thank you so much for your help!

### coho3131 — 2023-08-16T03:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-softener-potassium-chloride-or-sodium-chloride.28045/post-325123>

Welcome Gabriel!

You are not required to use a water softener for well water.

If you do comprehensive testing (unfortunately it is not cheap) of the well water, and the water has undetectable or low enough levels of any toxic substances that you are concerned about, I think this water is much better for drinking and bathing than city water that has been "treated" and recycled. If your property is near, or downstream of, current or previous farmland, I would add Glyphosate to your testing.

Web 3 ideas like NFTs are only part of building the next generation of the internet, argues the host of the “Hello Metaverse” podcast.

 www.coindesk.com

"Web 3, on the other hand, is a far more specific paradigm that provides clear solutions to specific shortcomings of the Web 2 internet. It is a reaction to the walled-garden ecosystems that platforms like Facebook and YouTube created, which caused people to have their data extracted, privacy breached and ability to control the content they create oppressed. Web 3 subverts that model because it directly addresses the issues of ownership and control."

**We have been looking at homes to move ... may need to build a home but whether buying or building wondering about window orientation...perspective from this article brings up factors but we want ideas relation to optimal health....any comments or additional ideas?

Of course want a home that pulls inhabitants to spend more time outdoors. I always look at and consider outside factors first.**

### Plural — 2023-08-08T15:57:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/window-orientation-for-a-home.28062/post-324830>

Passive solar design tip that I've heard: "During the heating season, south-facing windows should not be shaded so your home can collect solar energy and store heat"

But as far as letting light in while they're open, having some east and west facing ones also seems like a +.

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-08T16:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/window-orientation-for-a-home.28062/post-324832>

I’ll follow this, because I am interested in the same subject: what would the optimal house look like?

I had the idea that any type of retractable roof could be amazing. Also the Hawaiian lanais could inspire.

I once stayed in an old house in Hawai that had no glass at all, I forgot the name of that type of building though, it had something to do with cowboys. It was a building of about 1000 square feet and it was screened in against bugs, no panes.

The overall idea would likely be, to let full spectrum light in as much as possible during the day, while not loosing the shelter qualities needed.

If you are asking about directions, basically you would move with the light. Morning place, midday place, evening place, sleep place. That’s how I would think of it.

I don’t see a home as having a particular face  but I’m weird like that. We put our home “the wrong way” everybody says. It’s perfect. It “faces” backwards towards green and East and not to the street.

# Thread: 5G - Practical Actions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trippg — 2023-08-08T19:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324835>

I realize the best thing we can do with 5g is to move as far away as possible. But besides that, are there any mitigation techniques that move the needle?

Since 5g is a millimeter wave and a poor penetrator of objects (why they need so many towers), can you shield yourself by placing walls, buildings, trees between you and the tower even if the tower is a few miles away?

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-08T21:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324846>

[@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/)

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-08T22:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324849>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324835)
>
> I realize the best thing we can do with 5g is to move as far away as possible. But besides that, are there any mitigation techniques that move the needle?
>
> Since 5g is a millimeter wave and a poor penetrator of objects (why they need so many towers), can you shield yourself by placing walls, buildings, trees between you and the tower even if the tower is a few miles away?

It is my understanding that a 5G tower (of the high mmWave 20Gz+ type) more than a few hundred feet away is of no concern, it can’t reach you or get thru the walls. 5th Generation technology (5G) using 600Mz (T-Mobile) or 3 - 4 Gz (ATT) can get thru just like 4G LTE signals do. I am not concerned about a tower more that 1/2 mile away talking to my phone. I AM concerned about the phone in my hand talking to the tower 1/2 mile away.

All nnEMF is cumulative so turn off, remove, reduce what you can and keep your total daily load as low as possible. That way the stuff you cannot control in town, down the road will affect you far less. 70%+ of most peoples daily nnEMF exposure is from inside their home. #1 source is the 60Hz AC electric field from the wires in your walls and everything plugged in. Turn of the power to your bedroom at night (and all the other circuits running thru the walls and floor) and get 8 hour a day of little to no nnEMF exposure. During the day unplug EVERYTHING you are not using and stay as far away from the thing you need on. Measure, be informed and make good choices. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Trippg — 2023-08-08T22:47:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324850>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=324849)
>
> It is my understanding that a 5G tower (of the high mmWave 20Gz+ type) more than a few hundred feet away is of no concern, it can’t reach you or get thru the walls. 5th Generation technology (5G) using 600Mz (T-Mobile) or 3 - 4 Gz (ATT) can get thru just like 4G LTE signals do. I am not concerned about a tower more that 1/2 mile away talking to my phone. I AM concerned about the phone in my hand talking to the tower 1/2 mile away.
>
> All nnEMF is cumulative so turn off, remove, reduce what you can and keep your total daily load as low as possible. That way the stuff you cannot control in town, down the road will affect you far less. 70%+ of most peoples daily nnEMF exposure is from inside their home. #1 source is the 60Hz AC electric field from the wires in your walls and everything plugged in. Turn of the power to your bedroom at night (and all the other circuits running thru the walls and floor) and get 8 hour a day of little to no nnEMF exposure. During the day unplug EVERYTHING you are not using and stay as far away from the thing you need on. Measure, be informed and make good choices. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

Thanks Anthony, sound advice.

### 5G Canary — 2023-08-08T23:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324859>

Low population density, ridiculous amount of trees and having neighbors that are willing to remove smart meters. In my opinion... with 5G it is the ground that becomes the bigger issue. I used to walk 4 miles every morning on the same path for 17 years. When they put the fiber optics in the ground for 5G I could hardly walk and all my joints started swelling. I moved to the middle of the street because it naturally felt better. I had no understanding of what 5G was at the time. It was driving my husband crazy that I was walking in the street. I challenged him to test the sidewalk I had been walking and the numbers were off the charts. He was completely shocked with the readings he was getting. We moved pretty quickly after that... with all this new technology coming be prepared to move quickly if needed.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-11T06:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324950>

> [Trippg said:](/goto/post?id=324860)
>
> Intresting, whats the deal with smart meters? Do they create dirty power, in addition to communicating via RF? Why are they worse than any other IoT device?

From a purely nnEMF point of view - There are different types of “Smart” meters some “talk” to the Power company thru out the day, others (they claim) only once a day (at night). The main grouping of meters are to all intense and purposes (from an EMF point of view) just a Cell phone attached to the side of your house. TWACS meters use a different system. NO RF, the power company send a VERY low frequency (60Hz signal) down the power line to get the info. they want. There is NO WAY to block/filter out this signal, it is part of the electricity entering your home. There are 8 million+/- TWACS meters mostly in rural areas. ”Smart” meters also have a switch mode power supply in them which create dirty electricity (”DE”). Your TV, lab top, cell phone charger etc. all use switch mode power supplies but they are all different. ALL the “smart” meters in your town are the same and all have the same “DE” signature which also becomes part of the electricity entering your home. If you have 5,000 home in your town/city close to you, you can opt out but there are still 4,999+/- other meters around you. Add to these “smart” meters 5,000 - 10,000+ cell phones and WiFi routers. Could be why Uncle Jack is encouraging us all to move away from crowded areas and go south.

WHAT TO DO? (Before moving south)

Turn off and control the nnEMF you do have control of, the nnEMF originating from inside your home. ALL nnEMF is cumulative, electric fields, magnetic fields as well as the RF. For most people 70%+/- of their daily (negative/harmful) nnEMF exposure is from the 60Hz AC electric fields, from the wires and plugged in appliances in their homes. Create an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary. You don’t need to spend 10’s of thousands of dollars covering it up . . . JUST TURN IT OFF. Reduce your daily load and be more resilient. The less total daily exposure you have, the less the exposure you do have will affect you . . . Measure your in home EMF (BVK), be knowledgeable, be equipped, be empowered, make informed decisions. [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### 5G Canary — 2023-08-13T17:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-324998>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=324995)
>
> Your husband measured high electric or magnetic fields or both?

[@NikkiYork](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28233/)

He said both seemed to increase significantly. He said he measured both- just learning the meters at the time- use both a Trifield and an Accoustimeter based on recollection spiking in multiple places. Part of the issue was our neighborhood was getting both Google fiber and experimental HS ATT system. And installation crews were hitting several buried gas lines and power cables at least twice requiring emergency response... lots of chaos. Seemed like a lot of gas line issues that entire year. The big thing he noted at the time was the change- done readings over the year of installation chaos went from negligible to pegging readings. Trucks and crews were running cables everywhere- all under and adjacent to the 4 miles of mirroring sidewalks throughout the development.

Not sure what is going on here because fiber optic cable works on light, (even carrying “5G” data) no electric or magnetic field and no RF until the data it is converted. As Jeromy points out in this article if the convention is done in or close to your home, that can be an issue. There must be something else in the trench because I do not see how it can be the fiber optic cable.

Any help here from a technical person or an engineer?
From Jeromy Johnson: [https://www.emfanalysis.com/fiber-optics-increasing-electrical-sensitivity/](https://www.emfanalysis.com/fiber-optics-increasing-electrical-sensitivity/)

My husband is an electrical engineer and if it was a single fiber that is correct. But it’s the intermittent boosters, tracing wires and other infrastructure wiring that they seem to install at the same time. AT&T also put in high speed internet cables at the exact same time and they also used this as a reason to install several neighborhood antennas. Google fiber alone is not the problem... it’s what came with it but they could not answer questions about.

silly question maybe?
I don't have a smart tv. we still use older ones with the Roku for streaming.
Is my older TV emitting blue light or is this a different type of light?
LCD maybe?
Thank you.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-18T23:01:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325294>

> [TANNER said:](/goto/post?id=325289)
>
> silly question maybe?
> I don't have a smart tv. we still use older ones with the Roku for streaming.
> Is my older TV emitting blue light or is this a different type of light?
> LCD maybe?
> Thank you.

Some of the light will be blue. You can use a spectroscope to measure how much is blue. I'm not an expert. I'm pretty sure someone else will give you a better answer. You can adjust the TV picture to emit less blue. You could also make the jump to watching a fireplace or outdoor fire pit, solving cardboard puzzles, and reading paper books when you are ready.

### TANNER — 2023-08-18T23:05:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325295>

thank you. but a "smart TV" I assume is worse?

### coho3131 — 2023-08-18T23:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-practical-actions.28064/post-325296>

> [TANNER said:](/goto/post?id=325295)
>
> thank you. but a "smart TV" I assume is worse?

From a blue light-emitting perspective, I would bet that they are pretty much the same.

The newer TVs are probably even better because you can lower the brightness, contrast, backlight, and color temperature, and still see the picture clearly. Older TVs are not as good at those things.

You can always turn off the WiFi and Bluetooth on the TV and just use it as a monitor.

Again, I bet some of the the more knowledgeable people here will be giving us a more comprehensive answer.

# Thread: Are our thought patterns apart of or separate from light, water, and magnetism?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2023-08-08T22:53:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324853>

I have been dealing with a chronic illness for about 8 years now and my life goes through a vicious cycle:
1) When I am teaching, for years in NYC now online in a remote location, I am happiest and feel best mentally, even though I have more nnEMF in my life and I don't necessarily eat well, get enough sun, or drink enough water (I do make it a point to get morning light, and midday sun everyday),
2) When I am drinking mountain valley water and taking care of my diet, and getting plenty of sun my health is good, but my mental health struggle with loneliness and agoraphobia over the summer (out of work) becomes terrible.

I seem to go through this in and out of the school year. When I have light, water, magnetism over the summers, everything feels good except my mental state. When I am teaching, I gain weight, there's more stress, less sunlight, more nnEMF but I am happier. What gives?

I just started CBT to work through the anxiety and agoraphobia, so maybe there is more to the picture than just sunlight, good clean water low in deuterium, and avoidance of nnEMF. Maybe past traumas don't allow us to heal, even in the best possible environment?

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-08T23:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324854>

While I agree water light magnetism is important, so is having a challenging life every day. I require some physical activities every day so that I am tired and ready for bed. We are meant to be struggling to survive, just like every wild animal in history. Could you try teaching outdoors, working as a camp counselor or something of the sort?

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-08T23:37:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324856>

> 2) When I am drinking mountain valley water and taking care of my diet, and getting plenty of sun my health is good, but my mental health struggle with loneliness and agoraphobia over the summer (out of work) becomes terrible.
>

>
> I just started CBT to work through the anxiety and agoraphobia, so maybe there is more to the picture than just sunlight, good clean water low in deuterium, and avoidance of nnEMF. Maybe past traumas don't allow us to heal, even in the best possible environment?

Is it possible that teaching provides a distraction to the mind and that is why you "feel happier"? And in the summer you don't have that distraction, instead sort of feels like you're reinforcing the illness because you're doing all of these things to help you heal?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324866>

I have been wrestling with this same thinking and am curious to hear what others have to say. How much are our thoughts dictated by our light environment or vice versa? Is there a place for gratitude, mindfulness, faith or other modalities to support our Rx?

### TaylorF — 2023-08-09T03:48:12+0300

### NikkiYork — 2023-08-11T05:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-324948>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=324943)
>
> Becoming a homeschool teacher would be my dream. I wouldn't even know where to begin to make that a reality though. It would need to somehow pay the bills.

You can do it!

Homeschooling your own child? That’s really not hard, I did it for 1.5 years during Covid and up until probably 7th grade it shouldn’t not be too hard. After that depends on you, hahaha.

Homeschooling others? That is a job. You could look into it. I’ve home schooled with Blueridge academy and they have teachers there that accompany home schooling. Somebody who collects the tests and such.

From my experience home schooling takes up way less time than regular school and if you can work with your child around, you can still work.

I worked and homeschooled, but my husband works from home. My son still says I am his favorite teacher. It depends on your child of course. Some children might not be as easy.

I also put him in a forrest school 2 days a week that was paid for from unschooling. For social contacts and to be outside in nature. You get money for things like that from your state, because you don’t occupy a spot in public school.

I don’t know how many children you have. More than one certainly is more difficult, but usually the home schooling/unschooling community offers get-togethers a or groups your kid can join.

I started meeting up with other parents to understand how this works. There are Facebook groups and many helpful Moms and Dads.

> “Filtered“ meaning what exactly? Theory is theory, facts are facts. A 60Hz fielded is a 60Hz field. Measuring equipment (meters) would show what is actually going on. My partner worked in a Faraday Room and most of the people in there got sick.

What sort of work and electronics were in your partner's faraday? Was the circuit correctly wired as in no hot and neutrals reversed? And was the faraday shielding itself grounded? Everyone has 60Hz AC including Jack except real off grid people. I filter with these units: [https://saticshield.com/super-duty-power-perfect---emi-meter/](https://saticshield.com/super-duty-power-perfect---emi-meter/)

I measured a 90% reduction in DE using Alpha Labs EMI meter (over 95% with Satic Shield's EMI meter but that's surely because it has a smaller frequency range) and 70% reduction in electric and magnetic fields with Alpha Labs 3-axis gauss meter and Gigahertz Solutions ME 3951A with the above installed.

Do you not shield your living spaces? If I remember correctly you posted you live in a small town and in my experience without personal wireless devices and no 'close' cell masts average background RF is around 40 to 100 µw/m2 which both ime and building biology knowledge isn't sufficient for health much less optimal health.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-14T23:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-325071>

> What sort of work and electronics were in your partner's faraday?

My partners Faraday experience was in a military facility. That said we are aware of many people (including one well known bio hacker) who professionally put in a Faraday Room only to get more sick and end up taking it out.

> Everyone has 60Hz AC including Jack except real off grid people.

You are right most people do have 60Hz AC fields, even “off grid” people unless they chose to go all DC - OR - people who have MC cable and grounded everything - or - people who turn off the circuits especially at night. Kill the circuit, No 60Hz AC therefore no “DE” or magnetic or RF (on those circuits). Sure, “Everyone” has 60Hz AC but some have MUCH more than others.

The whole point of a Faraday Room, from an EMF mitigation point of view, is to eliminate ALL nnEMF including 60Hz AC. If you introduce 60Hz AC into a Faraday Room you will be exacerbating the electric field problem and exposure.

> I measured a 90% reduction in DE using Alpha Labs EMI meter (over 95% with Satic Shield's EMI meter but that's surely because it has a smaller frequency range)

Filtering the “DE” only takes care of a VERY small part of the problem. ”DE” is nothing more than an artifact/harmonic of the 60Hz AC. Some people are particularly sensitive to these harmonic frequencies and a “DE” filter can reduce them BUT all these filters leave the 60Hz exactly as it was and it is the 60Hz AC (50Hz Europe etc) that does much of the harm. The Alpha Labs, Satic Shield, Greenwave and Stetzer EMI meters all measure a narrow frequency range. The best, most scientific way is with an oscilloscope. Using an oscilloscope our whole house “DE” PQFilter got better results in real world, independent tests than the Satic. Link to results here: [https://liveemfsafe.com/measure-1](https://liveemfsafe.com/measure-1)

> and 70% reduction in electric and magnetic fields with Alpha Labs 3-axis gauss meter and Gigahertz Solutions ME 3951A with the above installed.

It is physically impossible for ANY “DE” filter (by itself) to reduce electric and/or magnetic fields by 70%. Physics is physics. If you got a 70% reduction in the electric and/or magnetic fields it is for a different reason. Something else was done/changed.

> Do you not shield your living spaces? If I remember correctly you posted you live in a small town and in my experience without personal wireless devices and no 'close' cell masts average background RF is around 40 to 100 µw/m2 which both ime and building biology knowledge isn't sufficient for health much less optimal health.

I have never published the RF reading I have at my place so I do not know where you got those numbers. The Building Biology guide lines for “no concern” RF is based on data gathered and seen health responses to which many other factors contributed. This does not mean everyone will have a health response or even be affected above this level, it certainly does not mean a reading below this level is required for health. Recommended maybe but not necessarily required. You are sensitive to things I am not . . . but I may be sensitive to things you are not.

> I can't use my phone for more than a minute without feeling symptoms/ill, same would apply to wifi/bluetooth which I never use.

You are obviously very sensitive, I know this can make life difficult, we have helped hundreds of EHS people over the years. That said every person’s symptoms and sensitivities are different and require unique solutions. What you need, have found works for you, may not work or be required by others. My set up works for me and my family and I KNOW what my readings are.

What I do know, from years of experience working with hundreds of EHS clients is that people who turn off the 60hz AC at night and create a nnEMF mitigated sleep sanctuary, become less sensitive to, and less affected by ALL nnEMF during the day. They get their lives back.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-15T01:50:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-325076>

I still know less about EMF compared to most people here, but it always made sense to me that a Faraday room could have unplanned toxicity, because the Faraday room does not let any EMF (nnEMF or natural EMF) escape. Our human bodies make EMF, so in a Faraday room, I was thinking we might actually be able to "microwave" ourselves, especially when we are close to the walls.

Is this accurate, or would the dose be too low to cause toxicity? If someone has EHS, might it be even more concerning?

Also, is the EMF-blocking clothing probably toxic in the same way, because it blocks our natural EMF from leaving our body, and also deflects it back through our bodies? I'm thinking that those EMF-blocking wallets in someone's pants pocket or purse might be a risk factor for genital and pelvic cancers.

\[ Unfortunately, I think many "treatments and cures" end up having more unintended side effects than benefits. \]

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-15T02:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-our-thought-patterns-apart-of-or-separate-from-light-water-and-magnetism.28068/post-325078>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=325076)
>
> I still know less about EMF compared to most people here, but it always made sense to me that a Faraday room could have unplanned toxicity, because the Faraday room does not let any EMF (nnEMF or natural EMF) escape. Our human bodies make EMF, so in a Faraday room, I was thinking we might actually be able to "microwave" ourselves, especially when we are close to the walls.
>
> Is this accurate, or would the dose be too low to cause toxicity? If someone has EHS, might it be even more concerning?
>
> Also, is the EMF-blocking clothing probably toxic in the same way, because it blocks our natural EMF from leaving our body, and also deflects it back through our bodies? I'm thinking that those EMF-blocking wallets in someone's pants pocket or purse might be a risk factor for genital and pelvic cancers.
>
> \[ Unfortunately, I think many "treatments and cures" end up having more unintended side effects than benefits. \]

Hi Mike, I really can’t speak to the effect of a Faraday room might have on natural (in body) produced EMF, my gut is that would not be an issue. The whole point of a Faraday room is to either keep all nnEMF out or in and a well designed one will do both. This is why you can’t have anything inside that produces any EMF because it is reflected back and bounces around. Think of turning on a light in a mirrored room. Bring a cell phone into a Faraday room and it will switch up to maximum power looking for a connection and that RF is just mirrored back. Also, anything that blocks RF attracts electric fields and the electric fields need to be dealt with at the same time. Many people forget this part and is where grounding comes in. A Faraday room could also cause the rooms next to it to have higher RF.

I DO think a well designed Faraday room can work for a very sensitive person IF they have the understanding and discipline to use it correctly.

An RF blocking hat is also an electric field antenna, unless grounded. If the RF signal comes from the direction of the opening (looking at a cell phone) the hat could reflect the signal back into the head making things worse. A cell phone case should block the signal from getting to you while leaving the back open to keep the signal as low as possible. If you are looking at the screen with the blocking material behind the phone you are reflecting the signal towards you making exposure worse.

> \[ Unfortunately, I think many "treatments and cures" end up having more unintended side effects than benefits. \]

Agreed.

Why would anyone want to get back what was slowly killing them? Especially when they have a choice to dance with nature or, die slowly by man as a dancing monkey.

The young girl in this Instagram is just a dancing monkey...

This poor girls “thoughts” and actions are clearly being controlled by the 5G airport. This behavior seems to suddenly be increasing too- as Hack predicted in the 5G world. Hence the thread title... “Are our thought patterns apart of or separate from light, water, and magnetism?” Definitely not separate. For the observant it appears the security checkin is across from her and she is barefoot which tells me she probably just went through the security scanning machine. She also has beautiful long thick dark hair which is an electromagnetic antenna. Instead of helping her... they video tape her and laugh while calling her crazy. Everyone assumes it must be drugs or it could never happen to them because the environment couldn’t possibly affect you that much. Imagine how different her life along with the masses would be if homeschooled in a decentralized system out in nature living authentically. Collective consciousness would have never allowed for us/the masses to be controlled like this by technology. Saichi put it nicely... “The failure of consciousness coupled with technology that has put us all in such an unconscionably toxic environment.”

Einstein said..”Everyone is a genius. But if you judge a fish by its ability to climb a tree, it will live its whole life believing that it is stupid.” It is exactly what they have done with the masses. They have conditioned the masses to only understand a centralized system. We have been taught that we are apart from Nature instead of understanding we are a part of Nature.
