> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=324853)
>
> I have been dealing with a chronic illness for about 8 years now and my life goes through a vicious cycle:
> 1) When I am teaching, for years in NYC now online in a remote location, I am happiest and feel best mentally, even though I have more nnEMF in my life and I don't necessarily eat well, get enough sun, or drink enough water (I do make it a point to get morning light, and midday sun everyday),
> 2) When I am drinking mountain valley water and taking care of my diet, and getting plenty of sun my health is good, but my mental health struggle with loneliness and agoraphobia over the summer (out of work) becomes terrible.
>
> I seem to go through this in and out of the school year. When I have light, water, magnetism over the summers, everything feels good except my mental state. When I am teaching, I gain weight, there's more stress, less sunlight, more nnEMF but I am happier. What gives?
>
> I just started CBT to work through the anxiety and agoraphobia, so maybe there is more to the picture than just sunlight, good clean water low in deuterium, and avoidance of nnEMF. Maybe past traumas don't allow us to heal, even in the best possible environment?

Hey fellow Taylor! You nailed it. Light, water, and magnetism are clearly foundational to our well-being, and so is mindset. I say the 5 most important things for health are light, consumption (food/water/personal care products/information), magnetism, movement, and mindset. The mindset piece is underrated in my opinion. How we think/speak to ourselves determines how we feel, how we feel dictates how we act and show up for ourselves, how we act determines our quality of life. Something I teach and practice is that if you want your change how you act/feel/think, it starts by changing the words you're using and the stories you're telling yourself. I have many examples of this. Let me know if you'd like to hear more

Turn one on and do a slow motion video with your phone camera. If there is flicker you will see it. This is not scientific because there can still be flicker you don’t see with this method but it weeds out the really bad ones. Have you tried candles and a mirror?

### Wouterbekkers — 2023-08-11T23:13:50+0300

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-12T00:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indoor-light.28069/post-324971>

Most (older) LED lights flicker VERY badly. Newer ones not so much but do need to be tested. When I have to be on the computer I use color correction on my screen and a red light behind it to help counteract any blue, especially in the evening. You can get 660nm LED grow lights which is the frequency used in many Red Light Therapy Lights. There are different schools of thought on this (as there is on everything) but I figure a little counter balance is probably better than non. For reading after sunset (and avoiding legos on the floor) candles are the recommendation. Candles give off NIR & IR with the visible light.

In regards to your chart, remember sunlight changes with time of day, time of year, latitude, altitude and weather and there has NEVER been sunlight after sunset.

# Thread: Collection of good places for grounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-good-places-for-grounding.28073/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2023-08-12T18:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-good-places-for-grounding.28073/post-324983>

I’m trying g to figure out where to ground best and I thought, if we collect this together, it could help everybody.

1) on the beach, best in wet sand, if it’s ok with hands and feet touching the ground

2) in New York in Central Park on one of the bedrock stones that reaches all the way into the ground - not sure, if there was a specific one

3) on earth/ground soil that seems to be far away from the grid.

And I have a question: I have a steel and aluminum deck that is free standing. It is basically an industrial mezzanine. Four steel poles anchored into the ground with concrete. Not attached to the house. The deck flooring is an aluminum pan that was filled with concrete.

When I water the plants and I’m standing barefoot in a puddle on our deck, it feels pretty good. It should be grounding well, am I correct?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-12T22:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collection-of-good-places-for-grounding.28073/post-324991>

Measure and you will know. This is one thing a body voltage meter is also good for. Since you are grounded when using a BV meter if you touch something that is grounded your BV drops (to zero sometimes) because you are double grounded. The field you are in is exactly the same but the induced E-field goes to the second ground not the meter. If your deck is a good ground this will happen. IF the deck is not grounded but picking up electric fields, the BV reading will go up as you get closer and jump up when you touch it. Most refrigerators, dish washing machines, tumble dryers are grounded and your BV reading drops when you touch them. If you stand on the deck WITH SHOES ON whatever BV reading you get is a good indication of the 60Hz AC field that the deck is picking up all the time. Even if the deck is grounded, being metal and close to the house there is a good chance it is carrying a 60Hz AC field. IF SO, grounding on the deck would like grounding to the house ground. Grounding/Earthing in Nature, by definition, is being away from “not-nature” (any source of nnEMF electric fields).

# Thread: Better back to school laptop option
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-back-to-school-laptop-option.28075/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2023-08-13T17:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-back-to-school-laptop-option.28075/post-324999>

I like the direction this is going new safer certifications.

Lenovo Xiaoxin 12.7-inch learning tablet is here. Unlike the scores of Android tablets dotting the marketplace, the new Lenovo Xiaoxin is special in that it features a 12.7-inch ‘paper-like’ LCD display that causes the least eye fatigue. Lenovo said this has been achieved thanks to the incorporation of the nano-etching AG process and diffuse reflection technology that has imparted a paper-like feel to the display while still being in color.

As [GizmoChina](https://www.gizmochina.com/2023/08/10/lenono-xiaoxin-learning-tablet-for-2499-yuan/) reported, the hardware-level low blue light feature which can effectively reduce blue light emission to the minimum goes a long way in making it safe and easy for the eyes. Lenovo is claiming this has helped the tablet achieve the first RÜV LCD paper display certification. Apart from this, the tablet has also received the Rheinland global eye protection 3.0 certification, Rheinland flicker-free certification, Rheinland growth care certification, and National Eye Engineering Center eye protection certification five-stage authoritative eye protection certification.

Apart from this, the Lenovo Xiaoxin also lets parents exercise appropriate control over the tablet’s Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity options. All of this makes the tablet ideal for use by the student community who can focus on consuming content pertaining to their education even for long hours without getting worried about the eyes getting fatigued in the process. With the right parental controls applied, the students will have the minimum distraction, letting them focus more on their studies instead.

### Lenovo Xiaoxin learning tablet with 12.7-inch ‘paper-like’ display now on pre-order​

August 11, 2023 By [Sovan Mandal](https://goodereader.com/blog/author/sovy) [4 Comments](https://goodereader.com/blog/tablet-slates/lenovo-xiaoxin-learnign-tablet-with-12-7-inch-paper-like-display-now-on-pre-order#disqus_thread)

[](https://assets.goodereader.com/blog/uploads/images/2023/08/11104942/lenovo-xiaoxin-learning-tablet-with-paper-like-display.jpg)
Lenovo Xiaoxin 12.7-inch learning tablet is here. Unlike the scores of Android tablets dotting the marketplace, the new Lenovo Xiaoxin is special in that it features a 12.7-inch ‘paper-like’ LCD display that causes the least eye fatigue. Lenovo said this has been achieved thanks to the incorporation of the nano-etching AG process and diffuse reflection technology that has imparted a paper-like feel to the display while still being in color.
As [GizmoChina](https://www.gizmochina.com/2023/08/10/lenono-xiaoxin-learning-tablet-for-2499-yuan/) reported, the hardware-level low blue light feature which can effectively reduce blue light emission to the minimum goes a long way in making it safe and easy for the eyes. Lenovo is claiming this has helped the tablet achieve the first RÜV LCD paper display certification. Apart from this, the tablet has also received the Rheinland global eye protection 3.0 certification, Rheinland flicker-free certification, Rheinland growth care certification, and National Eye Engineering Center eye protection certification five-stage authoritative eye protection certification.
Apart from this, the Lenovo Xiaoxin also lets parents exercise appropriate control over the tablet’s Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity options. All of this makes the tablet ideal for use by the student community who can focus on consuming content pertaining to their education even for long hours without getting worried about the eyes getting fatigued in the process. With the right parental controls applied, the students will have the minimum distraction, letting them focus more on their studies instead.
Coming to the specs, the Lenovo Xiaoxin comes with a 2.6GHz octa-core processor that is coupled to 8 GB of memory and 128 GB of storage. You can further add another 1 TB of storage via microSD cards as per your needs. A large 10200mAh promises ample run time, with Lenovo claiming around 8 hours of video playback time with a single full charge of the battery.
The 12.7-inch LCD display has a 16:10 aspect ratio and 2944 x 1840 pixels resolution. It comes with a USB Type-C port that is also used for charging the device. There is an audio port available too. On-board sensors include GPS navigation along with a compass, gyroscope, and a light sensor.
The tablet is currently on pre-order in China where it is priced 2499 Yuan, which comes to around $346. Lenovo said early buyers will be provided with 50,000 Yuan worth of learning resources along with a protective case, a tablet stand, and AI learning tools.

### lsu9546 — 2023-08-13T22:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d.28076/post-325023>

Got a question and would like some input. Have a friend who is tan, spends lots of time in sun(sunrise, day, and sunset), works a job that they are outside a lot. Has a good sollar callous and doesn't need sunscreen and never uses it. Doesn't wear sunglasses at all. Eat animal based diet high in fat and eats seasonally for veggies and fruits. Lives in Louisiana so not a terrible latitude and lives in a small town with about 2000 people. Checked house already and emf is not bad. Gets about at least 1.5 hours per day with just shorts on in sunlight during UVB. On weekends has a pool and spends sometimes 6 hours in midday sun. They are physically active and not obese. Limits ALAN and wears blue blockers after sunset. Had labs done and still have low vitamin D of 17ng/ml. Could there be some genetic defect in producing vitamin D or something else could be causing this?

### JanSz — 2023-08-13T23:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-vitamin-d.28076/post-325024>

> Got a question and would like some input. Have a friend who is tan, spends lots of time in sun(sunrise, day, and sunset), works a job that they are outside a lot. Has a good sollar callous and doesn't need sunscreen and never uses it. Doesn't wear sunglasses at all. Eat animal based diet high in fat and eats seasonally for veggies and fruits. Lives in Louisiana so not a terrible latitude and lives in a small town with about 2000 people. Checked house already and emf is not bad. Gets about at least 1.5 hours per day with just shorts on in sunlight during UVB. On weekends has a pool and spends sometimes 6 hours in midday sun. They are physically active and not obese. Limits ALAN and wears blue blockers after sunset. Had labs done and still have low vitamin D of 17ng/ml. Could there be some genetic defect in producing vitamin D or something else could be causing this?

I would consider that -
1) this may be a lab measurement error
2) 1,25(OH)2D3 was ordered instead of 25-OH 25(OH)D3

[https://asbmr.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1359/jbmr.070716](https://asbmr.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1359/jbmr.070716)

### [Vitamin D: Metabolism](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2879391/)

The biologically active metabolite of vitamin D, 1,25(OH)\[2\] D\[3\] , affects mineral homeostasis and has numerous other diverse physiological functions including effects on growth of cancer cells and protection against certain immune disorders. This chapter ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### [Don’t routinely measure 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D unless the patient has hypercalcemia or decreased kidney function.](https://www.aafp.org/pubs/afp/collections/choosing-wisely/140.html)

Many practitioners become confused when ordering a vitamin D test. Because 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D is the active form of vitamin D, many practitioners think that measuring 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D is an accurate means to estimate vitamin D stores and test for vitamin D deficiency, which is...

 www.aafp.org

# Thread: Questions coming up daily upon waking..today was Environment, EMF Filters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-coming-up-daily-upon-waking-today-was-environment-emf-filters.28089/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2023-08-17T05:10:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-coming-up-daily-upon-waking-today-was-environment-emf-filters.28089/post-325167>

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

I wake up almost every morning with questions tons of them so many I cannot write them all down.
I have my families spiritual and physical health on my mind day and night and I do not have a local face to face support system to brainstorm with. My husband who is 85 does not buy into a lot of what I've come to believe however he does read blogs and articles I print out for him and he does try to accommodate me with a number of things RO water, cold showers, lighting etc. I have not found a good functional integrative forward thinking practitioner to work with who can take our medicare + insurance.

**Two things today.

1)** **If,** (as in a 1st class condition) **we never get well in the environment we get sick in,** **does that always mean moving south?
We live in Central Virginia at 38° N / 78° W.**

We both show English as the highest percent in our _23 and Me_ ancestry report.

Husband maternal Haplogroup is **T2b** and he grew up in a rural town in the _Shenandoah Valley of_ Virginia '38-'79, then in Houston to '79 to 1998 and '**98 to present 2023 in Central Virginia.**
My Maternal Haplogroup **H1a3** and I grew up inside the loop of Houston, Texas from '52 to 1998, then'98-present 2023 in Central Virginia.
Since living here in central Virginia in the same home 25 years we both became mildly hypothyroid and I developed cancer in 2009 and my husband developed Afib in 2013. One of the prior owners, a husband & wife, of our current property female had Alzheimer's and husband had cancer . I know there are many, many factors but my husband wants to cut our living expenses and I want a healthier environment so we have been looking to move slightly west and slightly south to less densely populated towns.
The Towers and Antenna's _**62** towers and **428** antennas within a 3.0 mile radius_ concern me and many of them are in clear site of our home.
Our home has been evaluated by an EMF expert and EMF is high. The worse being from dirty electricity measured high both inside and around our property.

I believe it is true that messing with the eyes is one fast way to mess with one's health. In my husbands case he had **cataract surgery** of his right eye done on 12/3/2013. (He had use of **steroid antibiotic eye drops** in relation to that surgery.). Afib began 7 days after that a 2nd surgery was done and the left eye was operated on on 01 14 2014, and we were both stressed by the loss of our little dog on Jan 28, 2014 and we bought a used 2010 hybrid Ford Fusion on New Years Eve 2013.

I suspected causal factors with his Afib: **Surgical stress,** those **antibiotic eye drops -UV/blue light blocking lens filters** resulting retinol signaling malfunction of bodily circadian rhythms And I have been wondering, what are the long time effects of loss of Vitamin D, melanopsin, melatonin from the **100% of blue filtered in both of his cataract lens** ever since then. I have encouraged my husband to get as much sunlight on his skin as possible. Meds and procedures (3 ablations and 9 cardio versions) had not been the cure we had hoped for.
Challenged by our abilities and our means at 70 and 85 years of age, plus what we want and what we can afford to move. My husband has stated before that a move may kill him and I always tell him, that, that is not an option! But I contemplate all the research and logistical factors. We may need to build a home but new building materials come with other sets of problems. Plus I have trouble even walking and am somewhat handicap with fragile skin, weak muscles, two bad knees and one bad shoulder. I've never fully recovered from 2009 cancer treatments. Might be best to remediate the best we can where we are...I love our home and after 2 years of searching have not yet found anything I liked better. We made 3 offers on properties but I was just as happy when 2 of our offers were not accepted, happy to not have to move. Decisions have to be made in 1-2 days or the homes sell. I can barely get a viewing lined up before then. The last 3rd offer we made by the Grace of God our Realtor got us an inspector to do a spot check for quick unofficial standard home inspection and crawl space had extremly fungus (mold). We were happy to retract our bid the evening of the same day we made it. I hate to be pressured plus hate mold and hate crawl space foundations----what were we thinking? The home was beautiful, not too old or too new, had a good price, great yard and a great community and location. Painful and Thankful at the same time!
**2)** **EMF Wise** I want to remediate our home NOW but hard to invest in that if we are trying to move. I want either / or a **full house filter ($2,600.)** or **Kill Switch ($800. w/two** [**Stetzer**](https://www.stetzerelectric.com/) **or** [**GreenWave**](https://greenwavefilters.com/) **filters** -one for each of our 2 panels- about $30. dollars a piece).
Do any of your know about [**The STETZERiZER® Home Kit**](https://www.stetzerelectric.com/product/stetzerizer-home-kit/) **? It is also $800.00.

EMF around us ... might also be related to the earth's grid where we are and I have no idea how to check that.

And I have wanted a** [**_Magnetico Sleep Pad_**](https://magneticosleep.com/) _for a long time_...but not sure if our under ground electric sources, inside wiring, or if being on a 'concrete' slab that does not get a ground if _**Magnetico Sleep Pad**_ will help or hurt us. First of all we can not afford it but if I can finance it I still need to choose between the 3 strengths, hire some body builders to install it and figure out if I need a King size or a Queen size. Can't decide to get King size or buy a whole new Queen size bed since we may move and room dimensions for a King Size bed is 13 X 13 minimum. Older homes within our budget do not always come with that much space. The weight of the Magnetico the cost of moving it to a new home (if we ever find one) and the cost of new pillows and linens would be a lot for us....however all could work out financially if we get what we hope for when we sell our home. We have a buyer for our home but what we will want for it depends on what we can find to buy.
It might be healthier if we were but we do not want the chance to be homeless ... by selling our home first.
With God all things are possible, if it is His will he will provide the help we need.

takes your insurance
He should give you what you are asking for as far as tests, prescriptions

you figure out what you want
\========================================
Do not sell your home, minimally fix it.
Mold. Get a single craftsman and lots of Clorox.
Do not use big companies or specially any mold specialists.
To fight mold long term, dehumidifiers (more than just one), lots of air and sunlight.

Get very large party tent and porta potty.
Spend most time under the tent or just outside.

**Health Goals:** Improve vision, get 21+ wonky blood markers optimal, get off thyroid medication and supplements, move more, **be rid intermittent full body pain, improve skin fragility on arms, heal body rashes and rashes behind ears, improve memory, focus, mental clarity, sexual and overall strength and energy levels.**

It looks to me that you are looking for something that I just found and want to share with you.

For your skin problems you want this cream, buy it from amazon:

For the rest you want this lozenges. Take one put on one in the morning.
Put it on the back of your tongue, let it sit there until it dissolves, 5-15 minutes.
Do that again in the evening.
Make sure that you **stop cleaning your mouth**, especially with mouthwashes but also fluoride toothpaste.
If you really want, take a toothbrush and rub the back part of your tongue.

To learn why above should help you read book or listen to videos.

# Thread: Black light good or bad?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Elizabeth Leonard — 2023-08-17T16:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-325186>

Hello, still new to all this, so maybe there is a basic knowledge that I don't have that would help answer the question.

I got a free black light from my sister last night, and aside from the fact that it is fun to use, I am wondering what the effects of it are... good? bad? (assuming the position that any light is sub par to real light from sun)

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-08-17T22:46:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-325240>

Any artificial light is sub par to sunlight, yes. Also, artificial UV is better than no UV.

But, if you are getting plenty of sun in the middle portion of the day, then a black light is not necessary.

### Elizabeth Leonard — 2023-08-18T15:44:16+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329182>

Does anyone know what the caveats are? I recently got a fairly inexpensive 385–400 nm LED strip and it’s neat, but I do wonder whether it’s safe to use it indoors all day long and stare directly at the diodes at times.
Thank you!

### JanSz — 2023-12-13T18:08:16+0200

> Thank you!

the major reason was that it contained 380 nm

### Dragonfly — 2023-12-13T19:40:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329194>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=329183)
>
> When initially Jack was talking about this light,
> the major reason was that it contained 380 nm
>
>

I see, in terms of benefit or mitigation of harm? Conceivably, it could either be the case that artificial UV-A light can be quite dangerous since it’s not within the full spectrum or, as is probably the case with IR, it’s alright at low enough intensities since you’re made to be out in the sun all day and that’s some heavy radiation. That’s what I’m trying to assess.

### Dragonfly — 2023-12-24T12:13:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329449>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=329183)

> When initially Jack was talking about this light,
> the major reason was that it contained 380 nm
>
>

Also, did he mention how it was positioned? I’m concerned about the direct vs. indirect eye exposure aspect—does it matter?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-24T18:23:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329455>

The goal is to get as much direct sun as possible, no glass/window and no sun glasses. 42% of the sun is NIR and 5%+/- UV (depends on location and time of day etc) so IF using artificial light duplicate the sun, never use UV without NIR. (NIR by itself will not harm).

### calebthomas — 2023-12-24T20:58:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329458>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329455)
>
> The goal is to get as much direct sun as possible, no glass/window and no sun glasses. 42% of the sun is NIR and 5%+/- UV (depends on location and time of day etc) so IF using artificial light duplicate the sun, never use UV without NIR. (NIR by itself will not harm).

Im sure this is a silly question, i'm a new member, but if NIR makes up 42% and UV 5%, what makes up the rest?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-24T21:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-good-or-bad.28090/post-329459>

> [calebthomas said:](/goto/post?id=329458)
>
> Im sure this is a silly question, i'm a new member, but if NIR makes up 42% and UV 5%, what makes up the rest?

Visible light 400nm - 700nm

Hey everyone...
I would like to have some feedback from some blood tests I made today. I start my Leptin Reset 2 weeks ago...
I will share the numbers that I'm worried about...

Total Cholesterol . 327mg/dl
Cholesterol not HDL 228 mmg/dL
Triglicerydes - 64mg/dL
HDL - 99 mm/dL
us CRP - 0,030 mg/dl
Vit D 47ug/L
TSH - 0,680mUI/L

I know that's a lot of things missing in this picture, but in your opinion what should be next steps? I'm not doing sports for now (I will wait until the end of my leptin reset) and I'm seeing all the sunrises and exposed to sun every morning and afternoon.

Thank you

# Thread: Laptop emf mitigation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2023-08-19T03:56:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325303>

So I am heading to Mexico in the fall but I have to work while I’m there, so I’m buying a laptop and I just went to the Mac store and the electrical fields coming off of MacBooks are HORRIBLE (tested with a Trifield)

My thought is to buy a really long usb wired mouse and keyboard and sit like 5 ￼ feet back from it.

Also, I’m planning on working with the laptop unplugged as I’m assuming that the laptop being plugged in at the store is was part of why the electrical fields were so high, but does anyone have a MacBook and know HOW much unplugging it removes the EMF?

But aside from working on it unplugged and using USB keyboard and mouse so I can sit far back - Is there anything else that I am missing?

I normally work on an iMac w a grounded plug which tests pretty well as long my face isn’t right up in it

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T07:58:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325313>

I would guess a MacBook would be the same as an iPad. Picture says a thousand words. The 463mV was not from the iPad but another power cord close by. Unplugged, running on battery there is no 60Hz AC field so just don’t use it while it is charging. You still have RF/WiFi/Bluetooth to consider. Hard wire your internet connection, turn off WiFi and Bluetooth.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Evee — 2023-08-19T23:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325346>

Thank you!!!!! I got my hands on an old laptop today and watched the v/m go from 304 to zero on a Trifield! Crazy! I do turn off bluetooth and wifi and I'm going to use a wired usb mouse and keyboard because I noticed the laptop keyboard has too much magnetic field even when unplugged. Crazy and kinda scary what I didn't know just a month ago.

Thank you for your answer!

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T23:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325347>

> [Evee said:](/goto/post?id=325346)
>
> Thank you!!!!! I got my hands on an old laptop today and watched the v/m go from 304 to zero on a Trifield! Crazy! I do turn off bluetooth and wifi and I'm going to use a wired usb mouse and keyboard because I noticed the laptop keyboard has too much magnetic field even when unplugged. Crazy and kinda scary what I didn't know just a month ago.
>
> Thank you for your answer!

The TriField measures the ambient electric field not what is being induced thru your body which in what the body voltage meter is doing. You will notice you get a very different reading on the TriField when you hold it VS putting in down. 304V/m on a TriField is horrendous! That said zero is always a good reading, but I am alway suspicious when I see zero. Was the lap top plugged in/charging or running on battery?

### Evee — 2023-08-19T23:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laptop-emf-mitigation.28094/post-325349>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325347)
>
> The TriField measures the ambient electric field not what is being induced thru your body which in what the body voltage meter is doing. You will notice you get a very different reading on the TriField when you hold it VS putting in down. 304V/m on a TriField is horrendous! That said zero is always a good reading, but I am alway suspicious when I see zero. Was the lap top plugged in/charging or running on battery?

Thank you, good to know. 304 was plugged in. zero unplugged. Magnetic fields stayed around 3 on the keyboard even unplugged though, but dropped to ambient levels (.2) on a wired usb keyboard 2 feet away. So seems that even unplugged, better to wire in a keyboard and mouse and sit back from a laptop. (essentially use it for it's portability, but sit in relation to it like a desktop computer)

 jackkruse.com

# Thread: Clarification needed on morning light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-needed-on-morning-light.28107/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TMellody — 2023-08-23T16:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-needed-on-morning-light.28107/post-325468>

Hi there, I am hoping a veteran mitochondriac can help clarify something for me.

I live in Florida 500 feet from a river. The bugs are merciless. So I can’t stay out at sunrise for too long or I will get eaten alive. So what I’m currently doing is watching sunrise totally clothed while grounded for about 15 minutes until I can’t take it anymore. Then I get up and go for a 5 mile walk (bugs are better when I walk) from about 715-9am with a sports bra/short shorts in full Sun (have an area around a lake with no trees). I am in the Sun when UVa shows up around 815. Then I generally go into work around 10am for a few hours. I’m back at home around 2-3pm and lay out nude for about an hour. Often more. I try to get a minimum of 12,000 IUs of D a day. Just depends on the day but always at least an hour unless it’s storming.

My question: is the afternoon light good enough for my naked body or am I missing out by not being naked at dawn with balanced IRa/UVa light? And if so, does anyone have a solution for bugs for someone who lives in FL near a river? Or a better way to do my morning routine?

Thanks in advance, yall. ☺️ Cheers!!!!

### JanSz — 2023-08-23T17:05:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-needed-on-morning-light.28107/post-325470>

What counts more are results.
Use sunlight to achieve these two (testable) results.
If you have to, fight bugs with fragrance oils. Put them on you clothing not on your skin.

Cortisol chart
Vitamin D levels.

### [DUTCH Plus® - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/product/dutch-plus/)

The DUTCH Plus® takes hormone testing to a new level. In addition to sex hormones and their metabolites, the DUTCH Complete™ looks at the overall diurnal pattern of free cortisol, and the total and distribution of cortisol metabolites. The DUTCH Plus® adds the Cortisol Awakening Response (CAR)...

 dutchtest.com

### [DUTCH Plus® (Female) - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/resource/dutch-plus-female-sample-report/)

 dutchtest.com

You want your cortisol to be somewhere between max and min.
Note that this test is different (more inquisitive) than say ZRT tests (that are also good at least for a first approximation).

> Use sunlight to achieve these two (testable) results.
> If you have to, fight bugs with fragrance oils. Put them on you clothing not on your skin.
>
> Cortisol chart
> Vitamin D levels.
>
>
> ### [DUTCH Plus® - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/product/dutch-plus/)
>
> The DUTCH Plus® takes hormone testing to a new level. In addition to sex hormones and their metabolites, the DUTCH Complete™ looks at the overall diurnal pattern of free cortisol, and the total and distribution of cortisol metabolites. The DUTCH Plus® adds the Cortisol Awakening Response (CAR)...
>
>  dutchtest.com
>
> ### [DUTCH Plus® (Female) - DUTCH Test](https://dutchtest.com/resource/dutch-plus-female-sample-report/)
>
>  dutchtest.com
>
>
> You want your cortisol to be somewhere between max and min.
> Note that this test is different (more inquisitive) than say ZRT tests (that are also good at least for a first approximation).
>
> [View attachment 24925](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/24925/)

Ok thank you so much. I will get this test done asap. I started with sunrises about 10 weeks ago and I’m making great strides. So that’s good news. But I will test to verify. Thank you so much for taking the time to respond so thoughtfully. Cheers!

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-08-08T23:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-needed-on-morning-light.28107/post-338210>

> [TMellody said:](/goto/post?id=325468)
>
> Hi there, I am hoping a veteran mitochondriac can help clarify something for me.
>
> I live in Florida 500 feet from a river. The bugs are merciless. So I can’t stay out at sunrise for too long or I will get eaten alive. So what I’m currently doing is watching sunrise totally clothed while grounded for about 15 minutes until I can’t take it anymore. Then I get up and go for a 5 mile walk (bugs are better when I walk) from about 715-9am with a sports bra/short shorts in full Sun (have an area around a lake with no trees). I am in the Sun when UVa shows up around 815. Then I generally go into work around 10am for a few hours. I’m back at home around 2-3pm and lay out nude for about an hour. Often more. I try to get a minimum of 12,000 IUs of D a day. Just depends on the day but always at least an hour unless it’s storming.
>
> My question: is the afternoon light good enough for my naked body or am I missing out by not being naked at dawn with balanced IRa/UVa light? And if so, does anyone have a solution for bugs for someone who lives in FL near a river? Or a better way to do my morning routine?
>
> Thanks in advance, yall. ☺️ Cheers!!!!

What I have bought in advance of going to El Salvador are these battery operated UV kill lamps attuned to bugs favourite band of UV light I am planning to use these to see if it does something. and that it redirects the insects away from my internal uv light to the kill lamp.

The other way is to buy yourself a mosquito net tent and zip yourself in They retail on Amazon They compress down into something that fits into a suitcase. I was almost inclined to dump mine but I just have to angle it correctly in the suitcase. I also need to open it and fix the rip on it with some duct tape. but by and large I felt very safe in it from insects because I left the windows open in the first place I used it and the mesh on the windows was not perfect. Even I would get attacked by insects whilst watching the sunrise. I didnt think about moving it though.

The blurb says this. I actually had mine with me in Panama and in El Salvador but it was a bit of a tricky customer to repack and I decided to get bitten rather than utilise it at every move How ever If youre in one location you can just hang it up after use and then not have to move it . I saw another one when I was in El Salvador in Atami in a house used by our tribe.

"this pop-up mosquito net is not only suitable for use in bed, but also can be used as a tent in the yard in which you can enjoy the sun during the day, and enjoy the stars and moon at night. Or use it as an outdoor tent when camping in spring and autumn." The mesh is all over it.

Im not sure if this will help anyone but if you dont want a fan it'll do.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acupuncture-made-my-chronic-fatigue-100x-worse.28111/post-325496>

I have and have had severe chronic fatigue for several years. When I was 16 and been ill for one year I got acupuncture. I went for up to 15 sessions despite it making me feel worse. It caused extreme tingling and pain all over my body, I could barely stand up or walk, it destroyed ever part of me. I went to a healing)reiki style acupuncturist shortly after and that immediately undid some of the damage by the previous acupuncture. It's several years on and I'm still severely ill due to many other things happening since then. But what did the acupuncture do to make me feel so much worse?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cardiac-problems-after-covid-19-exposure.28117/post-325592>

So i've been noticing more and more recently about people having cardiac problems such as heart palpatations and dizzyness and myocarditis.

I thought it was only for vaccinated people but as it turns out it can affect ANYONE who has been exposed to the covid-19 virus.

They have been hiding it well but there's tons of videos of athletes, news anchormen and celebrities randomly dying from heart failure.

People who were seemingly in great shape and anywhere between 15 and 50 years old, which to me is a problem, sure the odd case always existed but heart attack's has been on the rise since covid and vaccination and they are doing everything they can to hide it.

I've been in great shape for years since going on my Kruse journey and im regularly fasting and getting my daily sun and eating clean.

I'm 30 years old and i've recently started getting heart palpatations after a few weeks of what I thought was a cold. My heart flutters and hurts randomly.

I can just be relaxing in bed and my heart starts racing and feels weak, im starting to get a bit worried.

Has there been any talk about this on the forums since it's quite a new topic im not quite sure, anything that could help?

I'm thinking of doing a pro-longed fast like days or weeks just to let the body rest and repair it's DNA structure maybe that would heal my heart but perhaps there
is some supplement or medicin I should take?

I dont trust normal doctors anymore that's why im turning to you gang, shoot.

### JanSz — 2023-08-26T18:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cardiac-problems-after-covid-19-exposure.28117/post-325599>

The fasting insulin level you want to strive for is about 2 or 3 microU per mL (μU/mL). If it’s 5 or above, you clearly need to lower your insulin level to reduce your risk of high blood pressure and other cardiovascular health hazards. Keep in mind that the so-called "normal" fasting insulin level is anywhere from 5 to 25 μU/mL. Please do not make the mistake of thinking that this "normal" insulin range equates to optimal.

# Thread: Has anyone tested Ocushield to determine if their cell phone and laptop screen protectors really block out blue-light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-tested-ocushield-to-determine-if-their-cell-phone-and-laptop-screen-protectors-really-block-out-blue-light.28118/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ana D — 2023-08-27T16:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-tested-ocushield-to-determine-if-their-cell-phone-and-laptop-screen-protectors-really-block-out-blue-light.28118/post-325614>

Hello I'm new and am wondering if someone has already done the homework to determine how to best reduce blue-light from cell phones and laptops. I already installed IrisTech on my personal home computer but am not permitted to install anything on my work laptop so am looking for an external liner/protector for the laptop. Thanks.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-27T19:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-tested-ocushield-to-determine-if-their-cell-phone-and-laptop-screen-protectors-really-block-out-blue-light.28118/post-325622>

I can’t speak to Ocushield but what I do is - having adjusted the color balance in setting to be as warm “not blue” as possible, also have as much natural light around me as I can (may not be an option for you at work) AND have a small (rechargeable) battery powered RED LED light next to the screen to add back in the red light. Not perfect but a step in the right direction when screen time is unavoidable.

### JanSz — 2023-08-28T21:05:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-about-dehumidifier-water-as-ddw.28119/post-325653>

> [EDI said:](/goto/post?id=325621)
>
> I've thought about using the water from my dehumidifier and running it through a RO system to make it drinkable; i suppose this may end up being lower deuterium content water as well, maybe; your thoughts are appreciated; thanks.

Mechanical problems with that idea.
Water from dehumidifier is at atmospheric pressure.
You would need pump to get it to something like 50-80 psi.
Then you would lose about half of that water to get the RO water.

I have AC and dehumidifier in basement.
Same kind of water comes also from AC.

I just buy RO water at Costco.
I tested that RO water
and
water from my kitchen sink. See below the results.
Each line with my tests there cost me $250.

Actually, if you want just right deuterium level in your drinking water ...
I am 5'8" and 150#, drink daily 2.5 to 3 liters of water
4 or 5 half liter bottles of RO water from Costco
and one half liter of DDW-25
this if the least expensive that I know of:
If you live closer to them you can get 5 gallons container even cheaper.

# Thread: Thyroid removed, how to work around it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rod MidlifeMetabolicHealth — 2023-08-29T11:27:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/post-325686>

Hi all, I'm fairly new at quantum biology, but from my numerous leaps down the rabbit hole, I've come to understand how important the thyroid is in all this. However, what happens if your thyroid has been removed? Case: Man, aged 45, had thyroid cancer in his 20s resulting in his thyroid being removed and since then on thyroid replacement therapy.

How does missing the thyroid gland impact how all the quantum biology works? And, more importantly, how can he mitigate his lack of a thyroid?

### JanSz — 2023-08-29T14:47:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/post-325689>

Probably it would be better if Jack addressed the issue.
From what come to my mind:

thyroid replacement is better done with NTH, Natural Thyroid Hormone.
dose someplace between 3 and 5 grains
To figure out the dose:
check body temperature in the morning, when waken up, still in the bed
aim at 36.6C=97.88F
check pulse
FreeT3 in the mid laboratory range

Thyroid hormones when made in the body need iodine and selenium.
Those items are used in the body not only to make thyroid hormones but have other functions.
So even without thyroid gland it is good idea to watch them over.

Do not recall now, but it is somewhere in the posts, try to search for it.
Jack mentioned importance of
sonic hedgehog

### JanSz — 2023-08-29T15:19:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-removed-how-to-work-around-it.28124/post-325691>

iodine and selenium
I supplement selenium.
I supplement iodine using Lugo's
Lugol's contain iodine and potassium iodide.
There are reasons why KI (potassium iodide) is need.
That is, supplementing with just iodine is insufficient.

To check for iodine status, it is best to do 24 hr urine test.
One should pee out as much iodine as he/she took.
If it is less meaning body is missing on iodine.

Selenium and other I check using
spectracell.com
see my latest known below. I am 83.25 yo

# Thread: Eggs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2023-08-29T13:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325687>

We have a friend who has noticed when they eat farm raised eggs it messes up their sleep and when they don’t eat eggs things return to normal again- any reason why this could be? Thank you

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T13:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325688>

Can you provide more context? When are they eating the eggs? Are they eating egg whites only, or the whole egg? Are the chickens subsisting on their natural diet, or are they supplemented feed? If supplemented, what does that feed consist of? Can you elaborate on "messes up their sleep"?

### healthy2015 — 2023-08-29T15:30:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325692>

They buy supermarket eggs so not overly sure what the diet is but most likely grains. Regarding sleep nothing else has changed for them apart from eating 3 eggs a day which results in waking up 2/3/4 in the morning, they mitigate emf, they blue block and also see sunrise and sunset

Thanks

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T16:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325700>

Industrial Egg Layer Feed components include many by-products of various food industries like Wheat Middlings, Corn Distillers Dried Grains with Solubles, Soybean Meal, Processed Grain By-Products, Grain Products, Plant Protein Products, Fish Meal, Canola Meal, Dehydrated Alfalfa Meal, and others. There would probably also be various other components like sources of minerals, amino acids, and preservatives.

[@Marko Pollo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21456/) probably knows more details.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T16:49:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325701>

Small family farms would be more likely to feed their egg laying hens things like locally grown oats, corn, and wheat, and table scraps, and the hens would also have their run of the property for the pasture, grasses, clover, insects, worms, and small animals.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T17:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325703>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=325692)
>
> They buy supermarket eggs so not overly sure what the diet is but most likely grains. Regarding sleep nothing else has changed for them apart from eating 3 eggs a day which results in waking up 2/3/4 in the morning, they mitigate emf, they blue block and also see sunrise and sunset
>
> Thanks

You mention "they". So is this happening to more than one member of that household? Is it only when "they" eat 3 eggs? What about if they eat 1? Does it change? Where are "they" located? Is it natural for chickens in their area to be laying eggs right now? Who knows where the eggs are coming from when purchased from the supermarket. Perhaps there is a circadian mismatch that could be contributing to this early morning wakefulness.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-29T17:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325705>

I've got no idea why they can't sleep. In my experience, even with free range birds on a small farm, a fair percentage of nutritional need comes from a feed bag. I do everything I can to feed all my animals from our local environment like scraps, bugs, grasses and fruit, but I still need to give them feed. I am planting more fruit trees for this. We are getting 50 more meat birds chicks tomorrow to harvest in late fall.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T17:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325707>

I do everything I can to feed all my animals from our local environment like scraps, bugs, grasses and fruit, but I still need to give them feed. I am planting more fruit trees for this. We are getting 50 more meat birds chicks tomorrow to harvest in late fall.

Yup, agree. We supplemented ours with no soy, no corn, non-GMO feed. Pricier, but worth it in our opinion.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T18:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325711>

While of course local grain would be optimal, how much more money is it for no-soy feed that is certified organic versus non-GMO? (In my opinion, non-GMO is probably more important than less pesticide residue, but both would be nice.)

### coho3131 — 2023-08-29T18:46:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325712>

I do everything I can to feed all my animals from our local environment like scraps, bugs, grasses and fruit, but I still need to give them feed. I am planting more fruit trees for this. Could you plant a section of pasture with clover (and other legumes) to help with giving them less feed?

Some of our local family farms are thinking of raising worms for this also.

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T19:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325715>

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-08-29T19:40:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325716>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=325712)
>
> Could you plant a section of pasture with clover (and other legumes) to help with giving them less feed?
>
> Some of our local family farms are thinking of raising worms for this also.

Not sure where [@Marko Pollo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21456/) is, but when we had our chickens last year here in Texas, it was during a devastating drought (which is happening again right now). We couldn't grow anything if we wanted to where we were rotating them. In fact, we would have to move them twice as far some days just to avoid the gaping cracks in the ground. We fermented their feed to help stretch it further and give them more water.

### Daulatwant — 2023-08-29T21:30:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325721>

Fried or even boiled eggs were always weird tasting to me.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-08-30T00:06:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325731>

I have heard that you can't starve a profit out of a cow. Probably the same with chickens. I supplement with crumble, but back off if there are double yolkers. The other day I was going to weedwhack around the corral but for some reason just started pulling the weeds by hand. About an hour later I uncovered a nest with a hen sitting perfectly still on her eggs. Hopefully we get some chicks. Ducks are better foragers than chickens
and delicious also. GMO is not organic, so if it is labeled organic its non GMO. If you are going to get some chicks, you may want to get them now, so they'll be ready for winter and mature enough to start laying in the spring. I recommend getting extras as you will probably lose some and its nice to have an abundance of eggs.

In the last month I am preoccupied with Nitric Oxide (NO).
Two things that stuck in my mind.
Vegetables these days are **poor** in delivering N3O.
That goes **double for organic** vegetables.
Above was tested and verified.

Looks like humanity is screwing itself.
Too much LA
Not enough NO
Not even talking about sunlight.

It looks like all neolithic diseases can be derived from the two above.
Starting with high blood pressure ending with Alzheimer's and multitude of other in between.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-31T00:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs.28125/post-325784>

This is one farm's experience. I'm not sure if it is accurate though.

### [Egg-spectations: are Eggs Seasonal?](https://www.oldtime.farm/blogs/blog-1/are-eggs-seasonal)

Today, we expect eggs to be available on grocery store shelves year round and expect hens to be egg laying machines who produce almost an egg a day throughout the year. But ... Is that "normal" for hens? Is that what really happens on local farms? Are our expectations in line with "normal"...

 www.oldtime.farm

# Thread: The Power of Grounding and Expedited Healing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-grounding-and-expedited-healing.28129/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### IanKites — 2023-08-30T06:50:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-grounding-and-expedited-healing.28129/post-325740>

Hello everyone, thanks to Dr JK, At 53 years of age I'm a massive advocate of grounding. January 1st 2022 I was flying through the air kitesurfing at approximately 30 ft up off the water and to this day, I'm still not sure what went wrong but it all came unstuck and i fell out of the air backwards hitting the water and breaking a rib. The following 4 days was spent in a zerogravity recliner attempting to breath and sleep. MY phone rang on the 5th day and a courier announced a package had arrived. It was a grounding kit from the USA I had actually forgotten that I had ordered. It plugged into the ground of the powerpoint and had 2 leads to clip onto the ECG style sticky patches they stick onto patients for checking heart rate.. I quickly read some of the book provided and stuck a patch onto the base of either foot just next to the left and right balls off my feet and connected the grounding wires from my wall power socket ground. My wife returned home 2 hours later and asked how I was and I explained there had been no change still bad pain when I moved, at which point I stood up to go to the restroom and as I held my breath waiting for the intensity of pain to hit, the pain came in but at least 50% less. THAT'S IN JUST 2 HOURS of GROUNDING! So I slept for the 1st time in almost a week since the accident that night for the whole night with the patches on either foot. 3 Weeks later the rib had healed! Everything I was reading was saying I will be out for a minimum of 6 weeks and some further time after that I can resume activity. I WAS BACK ON THE WATER KITESURFING IN 3 WEEKS.
2 days after getting back on the water my harness broke while I was in the air again at a similar height and fell into the water on my back and broke the rib above the 1st healed rib. So this time I stayed at the resort and had the owners of the resort hit a 5 ft piece of steel reinforcing into the ground by my room window as they only had a 2 pin wall socket (Vietnam) and used the same patches connected to the steel in the ground. The same amazing results with a massive reduction in pain.
Grounding is phenomenal. We sleep grounded now with a sheet on our bed underneath the main sheet. Our Sleep has been transformed especially after the circadian disruption of the Covid shots and we dream good dreams every night. Incredible.

### JanSz — 2023-08-30T16:13:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-grounding-and-expedited-healing.28129/post-325749>

Make sure that you have a plenty of Nitric Oxide.

Nitric Oxide (NO) is dissolved in our blood (if you have it).
When any part of our body is under stress, cardiovascular system there causes release of NO.
NO is a gas. When as gas it last there a small fraction of a second.
Walls of our arteries or veins feeling NO, expand, under heavy stress up to a 10% of volume.
That causes higher blood delivery to affected area.
\----
Lack or insufficient NO causes that cardiovascular system have a constant volume.
That is the reason for many diseases, but immediately for insufficient energy in places that needs that.
...

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=325743)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### coho3131 — 2023-08-30T19:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookware.28130/post-325761>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=325743)
>
> Is Stainless steel or Porcelain okay to use in the over?

Acidic substances will cause stainless steel to leach nickel and chromium.
Many porcelains, ceramics, and similar materials could contain lead, arsenic, or other heavy metals, and might be coated in glazes that might contain lead, cadmium, or other heavy metals.
We use enamel-coated cast iron pots and pans from France for cooking, which are not perfectly free of toxins, but might be the closest you can get. You could also use lead-free tempered glass baking dishes in the oven.
You can buy testing materials for lead and cadmium, but I do not know if they are accurate or reliable.

### coho3131 — 2023-08-30T19:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookware.28130/post-325762>

### [Stainless Steel Leaches Nickel and Chromium into Foods During Cooking](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4284091/)

Toxicological studies show that oral doses of nickel and chromium can cause cutaneous adverse reactions such as dermatitis. Additional dietary sources, such as leaching from stainless steel cookware during food preparation, are not well characterized. ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

# Thread: Which Musicians dont die earlier? Which music doesnt kill you?

### Butters — 2023-09-02T13:32:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-musicians-dont-die-earlier-which-music-doesnt-kill-you.28137/post-325862>

So I found I want to make more music and get better at guitar. But when you research you quickly find
that most if not all popular musicians die earlier A LOT like 25 years earlier than normal population.

The question to make the right decision is: Is it the choice of the instrument (like electric guitar) and the EMF of the Instrument environment or is it
the choice of the light environment, where and when you play? Or is it both equally?

What if you were playing in a band, but you would do concerts only in daylight, practise in daylight hours etc. but you would play still electric guitar?
What if you were playing an acoustic instrument, but you would play in evening and nighttime hrs?

You have no real control because todays electric musicians all play in shitty light and at shitty times. But I found older Bluegrass guitarists seem to lived longer, like Doc Watson. You have Roger Waters and David Gilmore who get to 80 and same for the Stones.
I found this list and there are so few guitar players:

[https://www.ihopeidiebeforeigetold.com/oldest-living-rock-stars.php](https://www.ihopeidiebeforeigetold.com/oldest-living-rock-stars.php)

### Dennis Clark — 2023-09-07T21:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-musicians-dont-die-earlier-which-music-doesnt-kill-you.28137/post-326105>

What happened to Jimmy Buffet? The question occurred to me when I saw that he died of a 'rare form of skin cancer'. His website obit said it was Merkel Cell Skin Cancer (a carcinoma). It is, indeed, exceedingly rare ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Merkel-cell\_carcinoma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Merkel-cell_carcinoma)). Although Jimmy's reputation of being a beach bum in Margaritaville was widespread, he was still a musician who must have played 'in shitty light and at shitty times' a lot. Maybe he didn't really get out in the sun enough AND he compounded it with artificial (high blue?) lighting and nnEMFs due to his profession. Just a thought.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-musicians-dont-die-earlier-which-music-doesnt-kill-you.28137/post-329317>

So if we make the thought experiment of an electric guitar player who is not playing at night times and mostly not under shitty light conditions (with blue blockers and long clothes), does his sun exposure. How striking would be the fact, that he would be around nnEMF through his playing conditions at some time. AMPs and stuff can emit a lot EMF, but when you are aware and you choose to have distance from that, there would be only the question how the cabel connection to the guitar transmit that nnEMF. Would it be even better to use bluetooth connection, to not have the direct connection to the power grid and only the bluetooth?

It is like almost choice kills you a bit, but the question is which choice has more impact?

# Thread: WiFi Router Guard Cover
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-09-03T18:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325896>

We prefer wired connections to our computers, but we use our WiFi Router for defined periods for certain things.

Do the guard covers work to reduce our nnEMF exposure? If they are affective, do you have any product suggestions?

Thank you.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-03T21:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325900>

If you think of your WiFi router as a light, you just need something metallic between you and it, the back side can be open, think lamp shade. You want to be in the shadow. Your WiFi router is putting out 2.4 GHz (same as your microwave oven and all Bluetooth devices) a metallic screen that looks like the door on your microwave oven will help block most (not all) of the WiFi. A piece of metal mosquito screen, a metal mesh paper tray (the smaller the holes the better). Put a box over it lined in aluminum foil. You still want to be as far away from it as possible.

### coho3131 — 2023-09-04T01:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325904>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325900)
>
> If you think of your WiFi router as a light, you just need something metallic between you and it, the back side can be open, think lamp shade. You want to be in the shadow. Your WiFi router is putting out 2.4 GHz (same as your microwave oven and all Bluetooth devices) a metallic screen that looks like the door on your microwave oven will help block most (not all) of the WiFi. A piece of metal mosquito screen, a metal mesh paper tray (the smaller the holes the better). Put a box over it lined in aluminum foil. You still want to be as far away from it as possible.

Thank you!
So metal mesh really works?
If I put a metal shield on one side, will it bounce off and create twice the waves directed in the other direction?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-04T02:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325905>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=325904)
>
> Thank you!
> So metal mesh really works?
> If I put a metal shield on one side, will it bounce off and create twice the waves directed in the other direction?

It is my understanding that is so. It acts as a mirror. The screen on the front of a microwave oven is called a Tesla screen, it blocks the microwaves from coming out (most of them).

### coho3131 — 2023-09-04T03:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-router-guard-cover.28139/post-325906>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=325905)
>
> It is my understanding that is so. It acts as a mirror. The screen on the front of a microwave oven is called a Tesla screen, it blocks the microwaves from coming out (most of them).

Thank you!

# Thread: Mystery of the milksick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2023-09-04T18:02:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/post-325918>

My girl, my Guardian Angel, since she arrived makes my life bearable after my Dear Wife died in 2017, six years ago.
She has insanely green thumb. She grows numerous plants and flowers on our small piece of properties.
Most of the stuff she is buying but some she digs out from places.
Now we are studying next of her digs.
This is going to be a Southern Catalpa.
The problem is that it currently grows near White snakeroot (Ageratina altissima).
White snakeroot is a hell of the story.

### [Ageratina altissima - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ageratina_altissima)

 en.wikipedia.org

Cow, sheep eats it, person drinks milk or eat cheese and he she is done.

### [Milk sickness - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milk_sickness)

 en.wikipedia.org

White snakeroot contains the toxin tremetol; when the plants are consumed by cattle, the meat and milk become contaminated with the toxin. When milk or meat containing the toxin is consumed, the poison is passed on to humans.
If consumed in large enough quantities, it can cause tremetol poisoning in humans.

Milk sickness was suspected as a disease in the early 19th century as migrants moved into the Midwest; they first settled in areas bordering the [Ohio River](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ohio_River) and its tributaries, which were their main transportation routes. They often grazed their cattle in frontier areas where white snakeroot grows; it is a member of the daisy family.

Nancy Hanks Lincoln, who died in 1818, was the mother of Abraham Lincoln.

\=============================

### coho3131 — 2023-09-05T03:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/post-325954>

We have one of those in our garden also. It is said to be pretty invasive, but ours has been kept in check by just trimming. I keep it because it is one of the last wildflowers to bloom during the fall in our area, and it provides food to beneficial insects during that time of year.

### coho3131 — 2023-09-05T03:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-of-the-milksick.28141/post-325955>

We don't have a cow, sheep, or goats to milk. I'm not even sure milk is that great for you in a nnEMF environment, unless you don't have access to other food for enough calories.

# Thread: "Smart" Meter Removal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 35
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### coho3131 — 2023-09-06T02:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326019>

I wrote an email to the gas company customer service to have the smart meter removed and they said it can not be removed. What is the next thing I can try? Is there a certain type of letter I can use? Thanks.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-06T03:42:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326025>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=326019)
>
> I wrote an email to the gas company customer service to have the smart meter removed and they said it can not be removed. What is the next thing I can try? Is there a certain type of letter I can use? Thanks.

While it is there put a metallic shield NOT in front of it but between it and your house. If you can’t get between it and the house (most electric “smart” meters) put the shield on the inside wall of your home where the meter is on the outside. Meter shield covers on the outside protect your Neighbor and push the EMF into you home!

### coho3131 — 2023-09-06T03:49:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326026>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326025)
>
> While it is there put a metallic shield NOT in front of it but between it and your house. If you can’t get between it and the house (most electric “smart” meters) put the shield on the inside wall of your home where the meter is on the outside. Meter shield covers on the outside protect your Neighbor and push the EMF into you home!

Thank you! A piece of sheet metal would be fine, right?

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-06T04:00:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326030>

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=326026)
>
> Thank you! A piece of sheet metal would be fine, right?

Yup. Measure before and after if you can so you will know.
In this photo there are 2 “Smart” meters on the outside wall. I had a reading of 2.004 mW/m2 before and 0.0012 mW/m2 after putting up aluminum foil (which I grounded).

Would you recommend that meter or a different one? I only have a cheap one right now that I do not find useful.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-07T01:02:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326066>

You can spend a lot of money on meters. I started with a TriField TF2 and the Cornet in the photo. Both do a reasonable job for a non professional and measure E, M & RF. I think a Body Voltage Meter (BVK) is the best and most accurate way to measure electric fields [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse). Just for magnetic fields the Alpha Labs UHS2 is a good one and for RF the TF2 and Cornet are reasonable but I recently read some good about the GQ EMF-390 which is also a “tri-field” meter and measures up to 8 GHz . Have fun.

### coho3131 — 2023-09-07T01:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-326070>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326066)
>
> You can spend a lot of money on meters. I started with a TriField TF2 and the Cornet in the photo. Both do a reasonable job for a non professional and measure E, M & RF. I think a Body Voltage Meter (BVK) is the best and most accurate way to measure electric fields [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse). Just for magnetic fields the Alpha Labs UHS2 is a good one and for RF the TF2 and Cornet are reasonable but I recently read some good about the GQ EMF-390 which is also a “tri-field” meter and measures up to 8 GHz . Have fun.

Thank you.

### michellejorge — 2025-01-21T21:24:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345528>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326025)
