If you can’t get between it and the house (most electric “smart” meters) put the shield on the inside wall of your home where the meter is on the outside. Meter shield covers on the outside protect your Neighbor and push the EMF into you home!

Wow, no use in the cover then! So the smart meter is on the outside of a wall of my bedroom. Can I put something inside to help? There are random surges. Also, I borrowed a cornet meter and there’s a huge difference between their acceptable ranges and building biologists per a YouTube I saw. Can you comment on this? With the building biologists adjusted Cornet scale in the settings, is yellow ok or just green? Thanks! Your input is very helpful!

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-21T21:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345531>

To shield from a “smart” meter and/or a WiFi router - the shield should be between it and you.
For “smart” meters figure out exactly where it is and put the shield on the inside wall.
A shields/cover over the meter is great for your neighbors but may cause the meter to increase its strength and therefore your exposure on the inside.

As far as the reading you get on ANY RF meter . . . The lower the better . . . Green is better than yellow etc.

### MikeR — 2025-01-22T00:15:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345542>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=345531)
>
> To shield from a “smart” meter and/or a WiFi router - the shield should be between it and you.
> For “smart” meters figure out exactly where it is and put the shield on the inside wall.
> A shields/cover over the meter is great for your neighbors but may cause the meter to increase its strength and therefore your exposure on the inside.
>
> As far as the reading you get on ANY RF meter . . . The lower the better . . . Green is better than yellow etc.

How far above/below and to the right/left of the meter should the shield extend?

### Mat.I — 2025-01-22T00:23:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345547>

Not that this helps you, however may help others in Australia - I think it was from 2017 that new houses required smart meters and the energy authorities rolled out upgrades to existing houses (in a very sneaky way). There is a rule that has been passed to allow consumers to request that there energy provider disable the communication link and revert to a non-communicating smart meter. This will address the RF issues.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-22T00:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345549>

> [MikeR said:](/goto/post?id=345542)
>
> How far above/below and to the right/left of the meter should the shield extend?

I tell people a 3 - 4 ft square. Get a big poster or piece of art And put aluminum foil or metal mosquito window mesh behind it.

Measure and you will know.

### Tripvan — 2025-01-22T07:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345565>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=345547)
>
> Not that this helps you, however may help others in Australia - I think it was from 2017 that new houses required smart meters and the energy authorities rolled out upgrades to existing houses (in a very sneaky way). There is a rule that has been passed to allow consumers to request that there energy provider disable the communication link and revert to a non-communicating smart meter. This will address the RF issues.

In Queensland, last week I was able to opt out again, but received the following email:

> **We are not upgrading your electricity meter
> **
> You have opted out of upgrading your electricity meter
>
> We are writing to let you know that we have actioned your request to not take part in our smart meter upgrade service.
>
> At Alinta Energy, we strive to better our services and provide additional benefits and improved functionality to our customers. However, we understand that everyone has different preferences, and we respect your choice to decline the upgrade at this time.
>
> **However the rules are changing**
>
> The Australian Energy Market Commission (AEMC) is introducing new rules for energy retailers to fast track the roll out of smart meters to their customers, so that every household and business has a smart meter. Once these rules come into effect, we need to let you know the option to opt out will be removed which means you?ll eventually be put back in the pipeline to receive an upgrade.
>
> In addition to the above, if at any time your current meter is deemed faulty by your network or you choose to have work performed to your meter (for example the installation of solar); a smart meter will need to be installed in line with energy regulations, to which an opt-out is unavailable.

It appears AEMC has announced a smart meter rollout acceleration period from 1 December 2025 to 31 May 2031

[https://www.aemc.gov.au/rule-changes/accelerating-smart-meter-deployment](https://www.aemc.gov.au/rule-changes/accelerating-smart-meter-deployment)

[https://www.aemc.gov.au/sites/default/files/2024-11/Final%20rule%C2%A0determination%C2%A0%20271124%20%28For%20publication%29.pdf](https://www.aemc.gov.au/sites/default/files/2024-11/Final%20rule%C2%A0determination%C2%A0%20271124%20%28For%20publication%29.pdf)

The somewhat better news is they're still going to offer Type 4A meters, which are the "smart meter" without communications, where a meter reader will still come out and read them:

> The final retail rule removes this provision in the NERR, which means customers on standard retail
> contracts will not be able to opt out of a new meter deployment.
> Consistent with current arrangements, meters with remote access capabilities disabled (type 4A)
> are available to customers who refuse a smart meter where these capabilities are enabled (type 4).

### Tripvan — 2025-01-22T07:16:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345566>

Some further reading for AUS:

### [NSW, QLD, SA, Tas and ACT electricity customers allowed to have communications on ALREADY-INSTALLED smart meters TURNED OFF](https://stopsmartmeters.com.au/2019/04/05/nsw-qld-sa-tas-and-act-electricity-customers-allowed-to-have-communications-on-already-installed-smart-meters-turned-off/)

The Australian Energy Market Commission (AEMC) has made a final rule to allow metering coordinators to deactivate the communications on already-installed smart meters. The AEMC’s Competition in Met…

 stopsmartmeters.com.au

### [Privacy, Smart Meters and Electricity Usage Data – Australian Privacy Foundation](https://privacy.org.au/privacy-smart-meters-and-electricity-usage-data/)

 privacy.org.au

(more info about Type 4A)

### Mat.I — 2025-01-22T11:04:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-345574>

> [Tripvan said:](/goto/post?id=345566)
>
> Some further reading for AUS:
>
>
> ### [NSW, QLD, SA, Tas and ACT electricity customers allowed to have communications on ALREADY-INSTALLED smart meters TURNED OFF](https://stopsmartmeters.com.au/2019/04/05/nsw-qld-sa-tas-and-act-electricity-customers-allowed-to-have-communications-on-already-installed-smart-meters-turned-off/)
>
> The Australian Energy Market Commission (AEMC) has made a final rule to allow metering coordinators to deactivate the communications on already-installed smart meters. The AEMC’s Competition in Met…
>
>  stopsmartmeters.com.au
>
>
>
> ### [Privacy, Smart Meters and Electricity Usage Data – Australian Privacy Foundation](https://privacy.org.au/privacy-smart-meters-and-electricity-usage-data/)
>
>  privacy.org.au
>
> (more info about Type 4A)

I'm in NSW however the AEMC rules apply here also.. When I checked in last (off the top of my head)
\- new installations have the ability to select a 4A (no remote connection) meter in lieu of a 4 meter (with a remote connection
\- existing that already have Type 4's and you wish to deactivate - you can request this deactivation via the energy provider (likely around $250) , however make sure the actual comms module gets removed (which powers the cellular modem). FYI - Some energy providers had the option of upgrading the modems only to existing 2G/3G meters due to the shut down of the 2G/3G networks to accept 4G/LTE etc..

Obviously the underlying issue is the use of the internal antenna within the steel meter box enclosures creates very low signal strength therefore power density increases (it increases your exposure based on the sum of power density x time) . A absolute fall back to get around this (in the case of not being able to disable) would be to run a remote external antenna (which there is already factory provision for on the modem PCB). This will result in no RF being directed through the house (only if correctly setup) and will result in an overall decrease in power density. Noting (I think) the AEMC stipulate 5min intervals for data dumps...

I will check in with someone that I helped through the process a few months ago to see if they lodged the request with their energy provider and what the outcome was..

N.B. any unauthorised alterations or tampers to smart meters are monitored and raise notification alarms at the monitoring service.. Even the 4A have a log that can be downloaded onsite only (not remote)..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-04T08:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smart-meter-removal.28146/post-350058>

The only thing that makes a “smart” meter “smart” is a built in cell phone.

I talk to people all day calling me on a cell phone, inches from their head, worried about their “smart” meter, 50+ feet away. What can they do about it? Put a piece of in foil on the inside wall where the “smart” meter is, to block the signal coming into the house, especially sleep areas, get off your cell phone and be outside in the sun and live your life.

Now if you are worried about the invasion of privacy and the electric company selling your electrical usage information to the highest bidder, that’s another story.

PS - if you put a “Faraday” cages over the meter outside and not the back of it, it just pushes the signal INTO your home. It also has to power up to make it’s connection.

# Thread: grounding swimming in fresh water vs. 1 or 2 parts of body touching earth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Stett — 2023-09-06T18:22:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/post-326048>

I heard in a Q&A with a QB expert that we absorb no more electrons from swimming in fresh water than if we were to just ground with bare feet on grass or with one finger on a tree. This strikes me as illogical, as the contact surface area is of course so much greater. Thanks for clarifying.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-06T18:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/post-326049>

> [Martha Stett said:](/goto/post?id=326048)
>
> I heard in a Q&A with a QB expert that we absorb no more electrons from swimming in fresh water than if we were to just ground with bare feet on grass or with one finger on a tree. This strikes me as illogical, as the contact surface area is of course so much greater. Thanks for clarifying.

When using a body voltage meter, which measures the amount of electric field being induced into the body going to ground thru a ground wire, any contact to ground gets the same result, the whole hand or one finger. Grounding pads/sheets work the same way, one tiny wire makes the ground connection.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-09-07T00:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-swimming-in-fresh-water-vs-1-or-2-parts-of-body-touching-earth.28147/post-326063>

I think we are supposed to be constantly adsorbing energy from the sun and them sending electrons to ground through the soles of our feet or any other part of our bodies, however we also make contact with and ground through wet grasses and other plants. And as we are getting rid of electrons, the plants, and microbes in the soil accept our charge and convey it to ground, probably to their benefit. We are only talking about millivolts, so we don't need a large connection, just one that is recurring often. I usually am working in wet grass with wet boots and pant legs and feel I am always grounded.

Nick lane - power sex and suicide

Robert o becker - the body electric and others

Robert becker’s friend, andrew marino - going somewhere

F. Hollwich - the influence of ocular light perception on metabolism in man and in animal

Gilbert Ling - In search of the physical basis of life

Samuel Milhan - dirty electricity

Lyn McLean - the force

Becker & Marino - electromagnetism and life

Becker - cross currents

John Ott - Health and Light

# Thread: Plantar fasciitis from grounding?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2023-09-10T19:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326195>

2 weeks ago I got a pea sized bump inside my foot under the sole where the arch part is. Couldn’t walk for three days, but it was burning man, so I toughened it out and couldn’t check the internet for diagnose.

Thought it might be a splinter, but no point of entry. Maybe a planter’s wart? Anyway, it went away. A friend said gout?

Anyways, it went away pretty much by itself.

Today I am waking up and the upper part right under the ball of my foot is swollen and hurts a lot, same type of pain, same foot, the left, different area, no bump, more dispersed, but hard-ish to the touch.

So it’s not a wart, not a splinter, since the first nodule went away, it’s not plantar fibroma. I am self diagnosing: plantar fasciitis.

I had a bout of that after carrying my kid on the left about 7 years ago and I have been doing stretching excercise since - every morning, because that stuff is really annoying to have.

What has changed? I have been actively grounding, and wearing my moccasins more. I love moccasins since I had my first pair as a child, so against all advices, I always come back to them.  you know, when you love something

I recently walked a lot on sand/beach, barefoot, and, like I said, without real sole, just leather under my feet (I’m taking out the moccasin insole).

People told me, “you are not a Native American, stop wearing these shoes, get an orthopedic insole”. I tried that too, but it doesn’t feel right to me. I have leather flip flops by vionic that are really arched. I don’t know, if that’s the right thing to do?

Anyways, the pain is really annoying. Like you want to walk on your knees.

Any suggestions or opinions on the matter? Thank you.

P.s.: I am in the sun as much as my life allows, something like 6-8 hours a day. I can practically not up that exposure.

I go to bed at 9 and sleep 8 hours.

We unplug all bedroom electricity over night. (And no, the bed situation is not ideal yet, I sleep better on a crappy air mat in a tent than on our mattress).

### JanSz — 2023-09-10T19:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326196>

After you lay for couple hours in the bed, say sleeping,
then you plan to get up,
turn, still sitting on the bed,
**but feet not touching the floor.**
Now tell us what happens when you put your body weight on your feet.
If you got sharp pain that mostly went away some seconds later, and now it is dull itch,
you must have broken something that was broken before you went to bed
and healed somewhat overnight.
That was superficial healing that you interrupted when you put weight on your feet.
If you have
what I described
you have broken or greatly interrupted, some tendon.

...

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-10T19:51:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326197>

Hi Nikki, with stuff like this (weird body things) my wife and I find our 850nm NIR red light takes care of it most the time. If it were me I would rest my foot on the light 2x day for 10-15 mins.

That’s great info, Jan. Getting out of bed most certainly is the toughest time of the day for me, but I’ve been doing better and better with it, so I don’t think it’s micro injury in this case. I’ll look out for it on other occasions though.

### NikkiYork — 2023-09-10T21:33:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326207>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326197)
>
> Hi Nikki, with stuff like this (weird body things) my wife and I find our 850nm NIR red light takes care of it most the time. If it were me I would rest my foot on the light 2x day for 10-15 mins.

What a great idea, thanks Anthony.
I have a portable truelight that can do

Yellow (580) + red (660 and 630) + NIR (850), optional pulsing

Or no yellow added. Would you do with or without yellow? With or without pulsing?

I’ll try that immediately. Thank you.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-10T23:47:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326209>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=326207)
>
> What a great idea, thanks Anthony.
> I have a portable truelight that can do
>
> Yellow (580) + red (660 and 630) + NIR (850), optional pulsing
>
> Or no yellow added. Would you do with or without yellow? With or without pulsing?
>
> I’ll try that immediately. Thank you.

It is my understanding red 660 & 630 are for surface skin issues very little penetration (1-3mm) but 850nm goes the deepest (2-3 inches). I have not used yellow or pulsing so I can’t say. Your issue seems to be deep so I would start with 850 THEN 850 pulse and see which gets the best results. Then add 660 and 630 try again, then yellow, they may add something. Again my understanding optimal results 2x/day for 10-15 mins each. Let me know.

### JanSz — 2023-09-11T01:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plantar-fasciitis-from-grounding.28162/post-326212>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=326205)
>
> That’s great info, Jan. Getting out of bed most certainly is the toughest time of the day for me, but I’ve been doing better and better with it, so I don’t think it’s micro injury in this case. I’ll look out for it on other occasions though.

I was not really talking about how tough it is getting of the bed.
I was trying to figure out if you feel breaking something in the morning that somewhat healed over night.

If that is actually happening to you
there is an idea of preventing that from happening.
You would have to position your foot for overnight sleeping.
Position your foot in stretched position, such as if you were standing.
So, in the morning you would not have to go thru this breaking event.
That would give your foot chance to heal.
**You would have to sleep that way for 2-3 weeks.**
After that 2-3 weeks would come time to face the exam.
Sllep without that brace, step on the foot, see if it hurts.
If it does, start again but do it longer.

There are all kind of braces you can get to position your foot for the night time in bed.

# Thread: Electrical cord shielding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gene Kalmens — 2023-09-11T05:15:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326230>

I am looking for ways to wrap plugged in electrical cords in some shielding material, to lower the EMF they produce. I haven't found an existing thread about it. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) and [@ShaneR](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26002/) , do you have any recommendations? Thank you!

### ND Hauf — 2023-09-11T15:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326233>

I make all my own power cords out of armored and shielded MC cable.

### JanSz — 2023-09-11T19:36:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326248>

> [ND Hauf said:](/goto/post?id=326233)
>
> I make all my own power cords out of armored and shielded MC cable.

### [MC Cable | WireAndCableYourWay.com](https://www.wireandcableyourway.com/mc-cable)

We stock MC Cable in a variety of gauge sizes for your electrical needs. Available in spools or cut by the foot. Order today for fast shipping!

 www.wireandcableyourway.com

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-11T22:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326256>

> [gene@kalmens.org said:](/goto/post?id=326230)
>
> I am looking for ways to wrap plugged in electrical cords in some shielding material, to lower the EMF they produce. I haven't found an existing thread about it. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) and [@ShaneR](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26002/) , do you have any recommendations? Thank you!

You can buy shielded electrical cords or make your own, as people here have stated. You can buy sleeves to put over regular cords. Metal Clad (MC) cable works be being grounded. The electrical field follows the path of least resistance to ground, that’s how MC cable works. Measure to know that whatever you do/try has worked. You can also put up a grounded metallic shield behind your desk with all the cords behind it and have the same results. You can also turn off power in your bedroom at night and not worry about it. If you don’t have a way to measure get a BVK here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### ShaneR — 2023-09-11T23:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326259>

Good stuff so far. I'll add the following:
1\. Always verify before and after with a meter capable of measuring AC electric fields (my preferred way), or body voltage. It is very easy to create a ground loop and make the problem worse.
2\. If it is a device power cord like a monitor or computer, with the standardized female end, you can swap [**one of these**](https://youtu.be/qrJpi60bIes?si=vyuNtnAFDAKAyrKt) in and reduce the E-field.
3\. [**This**](https://www.electrahealth.com/safe-wrap--electric-field-emf-guard-for-cords-and-cables-by-electrahealth-research-institute.html?aff=16) is newer on the market but is less bulky than MC cable, fitting over the existing cable.
4\. Don't forget to verify with a meter!!!

### Gene Kalmens — 2023-09-12T03:46:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326268>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326256)
>
> You can buy shielded electrical cords or make your own, as people here have stated. You can buy sleeves to put over regular cords. Metal Clad (MC) cable works be being grounded. The electrical field follows the path of least resistance to ground, that’s how MC cable works. Measure to know that whatever you do/try has worked. You can also put up a grounded metallic shield behind your desk with all the cords behind it and have the same results. You can also turn off power in your bedroom at night and not worry about it. If you don’t have a way to measure get a BVK here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

Thank you, Anthony! I am already your customer, so I have a BVK. Is there a manual for the included meter, so I can try its modes other than AC?

### Gene Kalmens — 2023-09-12T03:50:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326269>

> [ShaneR said:](/goto/post?id=326259)
>
> Good stuff so far. I'll add the following:
> 1\. Always verify before and after with a meter capable of measuring AC electric fields (my preferred way), or body voltage. It is very easy to create a ground loop and make the problem worse.
> 2\. If it is a device power cord like a monitor or computer, with the standardized female end, you can swap [**one of these**](https://youtu.be/qrJpi60bIes?si=vyuNtnAFDAKAyrKt) in and reduce the E-field.
> 3\. [**This**](https://www.electrahealth.com/safe-wrap--electric-field-emf-guard-for-cords-and-cables-by-electrahealth-research-institute.html?aff=16) is newer on the market but is less bulky than MC cable, fitting over the existing cable.
> 4\. Don't forget to verify with a meter!!!

Thank you, Shane! I will give SafeWrap a try!

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-12T04:00:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-cord-shielding.28165/post-326270>

> [gene@kalmens.org said:](/goto/post?id=326268)
>
> Thank you, Anthony! I am already your customer, so I have a BVK. Is there a manual for the included meter, so I can try its modes other than AC?

Works just like any other Multi-Meter. We do not include the instruction and the probe wires because they were confusing people. We have to keep it simple for people not familiar with electronics.

# Thread: Intracutaneous Methylene Blue for Severe Pain in Shingles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intracutaneous-methylene-blue-for-severe-pain-in-shingles.28169/>

### coho3131 — 2023-09-13T16:07:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intracutaneous-methylene-blue-for-severe-pain-in-shingles.28169/post-326318>

Is this worth a try for severe shingles pain (post herpetic neuralgia) that is resistant to gabapentin and tramadol?

### [\[Efficacy of intracutaneous methylene blue injection for moderate to severe acute thoracic herpes zoster pain and prevention of postherpetic neuralgia in elderly patients\] - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27777201/)

Intradermal injection of methylene blue can effectively shorten the disease course, reduce the pain intensity and prevent the development of postherpetic neuralgia in elderly patients with herpes zoster.

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

# Thread: Invisalign vs metal braces?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### airplaneman — 2023-09-15T23:30:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/post-326454>

What are the thoughts on these options for orthodontics? Other threads have mentioned metal braces come the side effects of jump conducting nnemfs and other stuff in QE48, this sounds especially bad in a major city. Is there anything that can be done to mitigate this? Alternatively, invisalign might have some plastics toxicity or something else I am not aware of? I'm also interested in alternatives to the alternatives if anyone has info or experience. Thanks for your inputs!

### coho3131 — 2023-09-15T23:48:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/post-326456>

I would consider avoiding braces unless the benefits outweigh the risks for you as an individual. Having perfectly straight teeth is not required for good health. Orthodontists make a lot of money on braces, so they are interested in everyone having them. You might be able to consider just having a retainer made instead of full braces. With just a retainer, you could wear it whenever you wanted, and in locations where nnEMF is lower. Finding an orthodontist that would only do a retainer might be challenging; I am guessing that many of the younger doctors might not want you as a patient, but an old school orthodontist who has more wisdom might be perfect for you. Good luck!

### JanSz — 2023-09-16T00:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/post-326459>

Speaking of mouth.
There are bateria in the mouth that dentist insists you get rid off.
Do not listen to that "advice".
It is good for dentist not for you.

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=326456)
>
> I would consider avoiding braces unless the benefits outweigh the risks for you as an individual. Having perfectly straight teeth is not required for good health. Orthodontists make a lot of money on braces, so they are interested in everyone having them. You might be able to consider just having a retainer made instead of full braces. With just a retainer, you could wear it whenever you wanted, and in locations where nnEMF is lower. Finding an orthodontist that would only do a retainer might be challenging; I am guessing that many of the younger doctors might not want you as a patient, but an old school orthodontist who has more wisdom might be perfect for you. Good luck!

Such a retainer would be used to move the teeth or keep the teeth as they are?

### coho3131 — 2023-09-30T01:46:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invisalign-vs-metal-braces.28179/post-326921>

I think a retainer could move teeth, but I think the process would take more time, and the distance moved would not be as far.

### JanSz — 2023-10-20T18:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/post-327447>

Some of the most damaging components in our modern diet are processed vegetable oils, as they contain excessive amounts of oxidized omega-6 linoleic acid, a polyunsaturated fat (PUFA).
The biological damage they cause is even worse than that caused by refined sugar and high fructose corn syrup.

The additional problem is that the farmed animals, birds and fish are fed with insane amounts of seed oils.

It would be of service to our friends to spread that news wildly.

**Linoleic Acid is screwing so many things in our life.**
At this time, it should be safe to assume that 50 to 75% of our problems are due to it.
The other major is lack of **Nitric Oxide**, creating another horrifying thing to our cardiovascular system.
Even worst, LA's half-life is two years.
It takes dedication and very long time to get rid of it, especially when, most of us has an 50x excess or more.
Even if you are persistent and lucky, on the end of first year expect to still have 25x excess, 12.5 on the end of second year.
So, do not even think of not having enough of it. Especially that whatever you eat, contains LA.

While LA is considered to be an essential fatty acid and support health when consumed in modest amounts,
an excessive intake of LA leads to the formation of oxidized linoleic acid metabolites (OXLAMs).
They impair mitochondrial functioning.

\==============================
Prodigal son returning home was greeted by his (rich) father.
Father arranged celebration and killed one little lamb to feed attendees.

I do not see to many proteins or animal fats in diet of celebration participants.
Probably even less in their other days.

intake likely accelerates the biological clock,

it is extremely difficult to correct this imbalance simply by taking more omega-3.

### JanSz — 2023-10-21T04:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/post-327455>

Eating a high-fat diet, when the fats are primarily LA from processed vegetable and seed oils, is far worse than eating a chronic high-carb diet. The type of fat is of crucial importance, as it impacts your mitochondrial, cellular and metabolic functioning.

Fats like LA can persist in your cell membranes for months to years, continuing to wreak havoc with your metabolism the entire time, while the sugar is quickly metabolized.

Aside from that, this also helps explain why processed foods are so fattening. It's not just that they're loaded with added sugars. They're also loaded with LA that breaks your metabolic machinery and promotes fat accumulation and inflammation.

# Thread: Any Dog Health Experts Here??

### Neurotic Farmer — 2023-09-21T20:24:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-dog-health-experts-here.28194/post-326662>

Hypocholesterolemia in my puppy-daughter. Not sure how old she is as we adopted her 5 years ago. We assume about 10 now. She's a shih-tzu.
Historically has eaten a raw beef/organ diet; however, recently she started uncontrollably itching. Read that the fat from animals can be bothersome for some dogs (not sure why it would just show up now), so we switched her about a month ago to a predominantly fish diet with white fish, salmon, green mussels, and homemade fish stock. Itching is probably 85% better.
She has lost over 1lb in the process. She's just under 9lbs now which feels really small. Did some bloodwork because we weren't expecting that and it, overall, looks good; however, her cholesterol came back "a little low".
That seems worrisome to me, for a number of reasons, but especially because she's been eating plenty of cholesterol since we adopted her. Could this be a poor light environment? nnEMF exposure?
Any thoughts would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,
Courtney

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-21T22:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-dog-health-experts-here.28194/post-326664>

I have a little experience with animals having had horses, donkeys, goats, chickens, rabbits, cats, and lots of dogs, also my wife was a groomer for a while. We found lamb was best with the dogs with issues (didn’t try fish). As far as nnEMF being involved, test where the dog hangs out/sleeps and see what readings you get. I have cleaned up a few homes and the pets responded as well as the people. Dogs sleep area should be as clean as yours, 10mV - 100mV if possible with a body voltage meter.

I do not know about Old-Goats.
I had terrible back pains, since 2-3 years before I came to USA in 1971.
So. I have always been looking for this type of solutions.
Was almost ready for spine surgery when I learned about chiropractors.
No more back pains (crossed fingers), knock on wood.
Later, I had to move and find another chiropractor.
Different story.
To make it short, use only chiropractors who use hands only (no machines of any kind).
Years latter
my wife is dying in great pains.
Fentanyl
Morphine
Lidocaine

Tested by my dying wife:
After Fentanyl, Morphine and something else
she is still in pain.

Doctors as usual always are trying to help.
So, I got prescription for **5%** Lidocaine.
In Walgreens, Pharmacist (my neighbor) tells me:
That will cost you $350 and waiting two weeks.
But there is 4% Lidocaine and almost free.
So you can get it in pharmacy, no need for script.
Or Amazon.

Make sure to use rubber gloves applying it.
Do not use any patches that contain lidocaine, they are falling off.

# Thread: EMF proofing PC
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2023-09-22T21:03:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/post-326695>

Hi
I am looking for some advice please.

My son is only allowed to use his iPad outside and on aeroplane mode. However I want to EMF proof as much as I can the pc for on the odd occasions he uses it.

We currently have iris and red rubylith covering the screen but I am worried about the EMF’s it’s giving off, it’s a desktop PC hard wired with an Ethernet cable and plugged into the mains.

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-22T21:32:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/post-326698>

Do you have meters to measure the EMF (electric field, magnetic field & RF)?

### healthy2015 — 2023-09-23T19:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/post-326736>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326698)
>
> Do you have meters to measure the EMF (electric field, magnetic field & RF)?

Hi Anthony
Thanks for the reply, I did have a cornet meterwe used to test but I have broken it, what other tests can I perform?
What would be the best metre to get, we are based in the Canary islands
Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-23T20:05:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/post-326739>

For electric fields my preference is a body voltage meter because it shows the electric field in (going thru) YOUR body. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse) (use Kruse10 for 10% discount). I have a Cornet and TriField TF2 which are both inexpensive ways to find if you have either a magnetic field or RF problem to begin with and then if your mitigation efforts worked or not. There are other inexpensive ones and the professional ones while more accurate are very much more expensive.

The desk top and monitor may or may not be putting out an electric field, I expect they have a ground pin on the plug but it depends how they are wired inside. The ethernet helps reduce RF as long as WiFi and Bluetooth are turned off BUT can increase electric field if not grounded, these are the reasons to measure. No need to fix a problem that is not there. Looks like you have blue light nnEMF from the screen reduced, I add a red light in the background as well to help neutralize any blue light, both inside during the day and definitely after sunset.

### healthy2015 — 2023-09-27T16:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/post-326845>

T

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326739)
>
> For electric fields my preference is a body voltage meter because it shows the electric field in (going thru) YOUR body. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse) (use Kruse10 for 10% discount). I have a Cornet and TriField TF2 which are both inexpensive ways to find if you have either a magnetic field or RF problem to begin with and then if your mitigation efforts worked or not. There are other inexpensive ones and the professional ones while more accurate are very much more expensive.
>
> The desk top and monitor may or may not be putting out an electric field, I expect they have a ground pin on the plug but it depends how they are wired inside. The ethernet helps reduce RF as long as WiFi and Bluetooth are turned off BUT can increase electric field if not grounded, these are the reasons to measure. No need to fix a problem that is not there. Looks like you have blue light nnEMF from the screen reduced, I add a red light in the background as well to help neutralize any blue light, both inside during the day and definitely after sunset.

Thank you for the reply
Can I ask what you would recommend just the pen or the kit?
Do you know if they would ship to Spain (Canary Islands)
Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-27T23:00:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/post-326855>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=326845)
>
> Thank you for the reply
> Can I ask what you would recommend just the pen or the kit?
> Do you know if they would ship to Spain (Canary Islands)
> Thanks

The pen is great for locating electric fields and finding/location wires in wall etc. The meter tells you how strong the field is and then when you take action (mitigate) you can see the reading go down and know if you have done enough. I recommend the kit.

We ship all over.

### healthy2015 — 2023-10-01T20:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-proofing-pc.28197/post-326951>

Thank you Anthony
I have placed my order and look forward to receiving the kit

# Thread: Light wavelength reducing clothing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelength-reducing-clothing.28201/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2023-09-23T08:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelength-reducing-clothing.28201/post-326725>

I'm curious about developing clothing that would be largely transparent and act as a medium to lower the frequencies of light coming to the user's body.

The idea is to reduce the light's energy vs blocking any particular light frequencies.

Particularly transforming visible frequencies into IR and higher energy frequencies (blue) into less harmful visible light when indoors.

Anyone have enough knowledge of this area to understand if this is possible?

To be used when forced to be indoors with blue light, at the airport, etc.

I don't know if this would be a beneficial effect but really looking to solve the problem of lack of infrared light on the skin when traveling for long periods of time in planes/airport where windows cant be opened.

### Sue-UK — 2023-09-23T14:33:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelength-reducing-clothing.28201/post-326726>

Hypothetically recycling our own light emissions might be an easier option, particularly as stressed cells emit more light. A polar bear recycles by having a melanin rich skin under crystalline fur. Apart from the endogenous light hitting the melanin from underneath, the crystalline structure of the hair reflects back IR (and presumably other frequencies emitted endogenously) that do escape which can be reabsorbed by the melanin skin layer. I think the trick might be to have a melanin containing fabric. Its whether someone could partly mimic the polar bear adaptation with a layer that did the same thing, and how effective this would be at transmuting nnemf from onboard wifi etc.

### JanSz — 2023-09-23T16:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelength-reducing-clothing.28201/post-326729>

I think there is a problem worth of more researching.
We are assuming UV radiation pattern that says that UV in high latitudes is low or not existing.
Darker skin is more successful in producing vit D.

> We are assuming UV radiation pattern that says that UV in high latitudes is low or not existing.
> Darker skin is more successful in producing vit D.
>
> [View attachment 25426](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/25426/)
>
> [View attachment 25427](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/25427/)

For the Inuit, as there's long periods of darkness and low temperatures, it may be less about UV protection and more about protection from cosmic radiation which can pass through clothing, given the proximity to the poles in relation to the magnetosphere. High altitude in mountainous regions could also have a bearing.

_"While airplanes passengers are exposed to elevated levels of cosmic radiation, especially at **higher altitudes and latitudes,** the radiation they receive in one flight is quite insignificant. Aircrew and frequent flyers are exposed to higher levels of radiation doses from space, because of how often they fly. The crew on flights typically operating at low altitude, such as most propellant aircraft, will hardly exceed a dose of one millisievert per year. Crew serving on long-haul polar routes, though, might be exposed to an annual effective dose of up to six millisieverts. "
([https://www.iaea.org/newscenter/news/cosmic-radiation-why-we-should-not-be-worried](https://www.iaea.org/newscenter/news/cosmic-radiation-why-we-should-not-be-worried))_

### Sue-UK — 2023-09-24T10:11:17+0300

On a plane or in a high nnemf area, I wouldn't wear anything with aluminum in it. I'd go so far to say I'm actively getting aluminum out of my body in preparation for a flight in December. Cosmic radiation and a wifi'd aircraft and aluminum in the body is something I'm keen to avoid.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-24T19:48:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelength-reducing-clothing.28201/post-326778>

> [Redox said:](/goto/post?id=326746)
>
> Very interesting connection.
>
> Space blankets work to reflect IR light and contain it within the blanket. Maybe this is a decent solution for recycling IR in low IR environments

Anything shiny will reflect IR, doesn’t have to be metallic, shiny plastic works. (Most) anything metallic (aluminum, silver, zinc etc) will block/reflect RF (radio frequencies, WiFi etc) BUT being metallic will attract electric fields, so in the process of blocking RF many people make their overall nnEMF exposure worse by wrapping themselves in a blanket of 60Hz AC. If you wrap yourself in a metallic blanket (for any reason) outside probably good BUT inside be aware of the electric fields you may be in.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-24T21:26:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelength-reducing-clothing.28201/post-326779>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=326756)
>
> On a plane or in a high nnemf area, I wouldn't wear anything with aluminum in it. I'd go so far to say I'm actively getting aluminum out of my body in preparation for a flight in December. Cosmic radiation and a wifi'd aircraft and aluminum in the body is something I'm keen to avoid.

I was a flight attendant with British Airways for 5 years, flew over 1 million miles and did the polar route many times (London to Anchorage). Fortunately all this was before WiFi enabled planes and every seat having it’s own screen built in.

It is my understanding that aircraft run on 400Hz electric system, smaller aircraft tend to be DC but the larger ones are AC. The power in the seat (to charge your devices) is provided by inverters. So inside this aluminum tube (faraday cage) you have WiFi, Bluetooth, 400Hz electricity, 120V electricity and magnetic fields from the engines all bouncing around with nowhere to go except thru you. NOT GOOD.

Sometimes you have to pick your battles, on a plane I would choose RF protection. Travel in a EMF hoodie and sweat pants.

### Sue-UK — 2023-09-25T11:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-wavelength-reducing-clothing.28201/post-326793>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=326779)
>
> I was a flight attendant with British Airways for 5 years, flew over 1 million miles and did the polar route many times (London to Anchorage). Fortunately all this was before WiFi enabled planes and every seat having it’s own screen built in.
>
> It is my understanding that aircraft run on 400Hz electric system, smaller aircraft tend to be DC but the larger ones are AC. The power in the seat (to charge your devices) is provided by inverters. So inside this aluminum tube (faraday cage) you have WiFi, Bluetooth, 400Hz electricity, 120V electricity and magnetic fields from the engines all bouncing around with nowhere to go except thru you. NOT GOOD.
>
> Sometimes you have to pick your battles, on a plane I would choose RF protection. Travel in a EMF hoodie and sweat pants.

I'm picking both because of the risks of my n=1. An EMF hoodie and sweat pants isn't enough. The picture I get in my head is of whatever negative fields that get through hitting aluminum in the tissues and reflected back out, hitting the metal in the protective clothing and being reflected back in, essentially recycling it. There is also the question of aluminum potentially being held in melanin surrounding the optic nerve and other eye structures which wouldn't be protected by a hoodie and sweat pants.

I think the EMF fabrics of the future could be melanin infused or coated.

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1385894721052621](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1385894721052621)

Melanin is being researched for potential use in space missions

### [Melanin protects Cryptococcus neoformans from spaceflight effects - PMC](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9326845/)

As human activity in space continues to increase, understanding how biological assets respond to spaceflight conditions is becoming more important. Spaceflight conditions include exposure to ionizing radiation, microgravity, spacecraft vibrations ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

_"We also note that the actual radiation experience by the yeast cells may have been lower considering the radiation shielding properties of silicon polymers"_ is interesting because of silicon's relationship with aluminum.

This is the other pre flight rabbit hole - in this paper the melanin is from cuttlefish ink, but chaga could be another source.

[https://apjcn.nhri.org.tw/server/APJCN/16%20Suppl%201/239.pdf](https://apjcn.nhri.org.tw/server/APJCN/16%20Suppl%201/239.pdf)

### GFam — 2023-09-25T04:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-326787>

Need help from the community. I just learned yesterday the local high school is looking to install a cell tower on its property. We live in a very small town (2500 people), the closet cell phone tower currently is 3 miles away. Cell phone coverage sucks, which is a big bonus of living here. There are safety concerns that kids can't call their parents and vice versa when at the high school because coverage is so poor. So the school trying to address safety concerns, is proposing to construct a tower. This is not about revenue for the school, its strictly to address the safety issue (although it causes a more deadly one). Its such a small town with the high school in the middle. the property is within a half mile of every resident in town.

I learned this information yesterday in talking with with one of the local county supervisors whom is a good friend. He said he has been fielding many calls from local residents opposing the construction especially from folks in the closest proximity to the proposed location. He advised me to contact the high school principal whom he said is a good guy that will listen. I am sure that the folks that have been vocal don't have a fraction of the education that Jack has taught us. I want to come at this from a education perspective, the physics of organisms perspective as Jack has taught us but not sure the best way to condense all Jack's material into an easily digestible format. I am not sure providing links to websites and resources will do the trick.

Has anyone already put together a brochure, pamphlet, something that they have shared with local school administrators with any success. When my kids were in elementary school, I tried to get the school to understand the dangers of WIFI. They ignored me, thus I pulled my kids out and they have been homeschooled ever since. Have a senior and 8th grader and they as so much better off not having been subjected to the public school system.

The objective of this work was to perform a complete review of the existing scientific literature to update the knowledge on the effects of base station antennas on humans. Studies performed in real urban conditions, with mobile phone base stations situated close to apartments, were selected...

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-27T04:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-326836>

If you Google the adverse affects of Cell Towers most of the results say there is no evidence. Who paid for the research? You have to look hard and go deep, scroll down, there are many videos from industry insiders saying cell towers, cell phone, WiFi etc cause harm. Steve Jobs would not let his kids have or use a cell phone or have an iPad. In some countries in Europe it is illegal to put a cell tower within a certain distance of a school and for children under the age of 10 (I think) to have a cell phone. The incidence of cancer for people who live within 1200 feet (from memory) of a cell tower is 50% higher than the average, this was back in 3G days. From memory the incidence of cancer for people living within 2 miles of the Sutro Tower in San Francisco is 5x normal, within 2-5 miles 2x normal.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-10-01T22:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-326953>

Each Telephone company has its own 5G frequency range it uses. The higher the frequency the faster the data but the less distance it will travel and penetrate thru walls etc. This is why they are expanding the tower network. You may have AT&T which for 5G uses “C-band” (3-4 GHz) but the towers around you also carry T-Mobile, Sprint, Verizon etc. AND all there towers also carry 4G which goes thru “everything”. This means your 4G signal is no longer coming from 1/2 or 1 mile away but from a few hundred feet. The good side of that is the power can be much lower and your phone can operate at much lower power. The further the away the tower is the more power (stronger signal) your phone uses to make the connection. So there are pros and cons to all this. Is it better to make an internet connection for several mins with 4G or a few seconds with 5G?

Is there a safe distance? Cell tower signals 3G/4G can travel for miles and as I said above the further away the tower the harder your phone 12” from your head has to work. 4G technology tower sends out a broad signal in a cone shape, your phone send out a signal 360º. 5G send out a board signal also but when it makes to connection to a 5G device it sends a focused beam at the device. If you do not have a 5G enabled device and no one close to you does then you will not get 5G “beamed” even if there is a 5G tower BUT if you live in a populated area there are lots of 5G devices all around you all the time.

According to [www.AntennaSearch.com](http://www.AntennaSearch.com) from our office in a town of 85K+/- there are _64 towers and 272 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius._ The closest is 1/2 mile away. If there are 40K people on our side of town how many cell phones are being used at any given time - 1K? 10K? How many “smart” meters, how many “smart” cars? How many WiFi signals? From my home - _6 towers and 12 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius and the closest is 1+ mile away._

Individual cell towers in big cities are bigger (more powerful) also:
Miami, FL - _370 towers and 1752 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius - how many cell phones?
Portland, OR - _251 towers and 2146 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius
Dallas, TX - _266 towers and 2564 antennas within a 3.0 mile radius___
Is there a safe distance? I would not want to live within 1200 feet of a (small) cell tower and 1/2 mile would be better - in a low density area. Even living in a small town if you have 50, 100, 500 homes within 1/2 mile of you, how many 4G/5G cell phone signals are there? There is a reason Jack tells us to move away from populated areas. I hope this helps you make an informed decision.

Measure and you will know - no guessing. Then what? . . . Make changes.

### Adamo — 2025-01-16T17:08:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-345284>

They are popping up on every major building / apartment complex, hidden in "flag poles" and street lights, farm houses have them on their barns (_assumably to render more monthly income because their farms are not doing as well as they should_), etc.

Has there been any deep research to see what the long-range effect is of all of this, in specific regards to:

\- If you are living in a rural area, but around that zone are some urbanized areas with a lot of towers, how "safe" are you really? (think a dot in a circle, or a dot in a triangle and the dot being your rural area)
\- How much of a distance is "good distance" being away from a major urban hub, in order to keep the nnEMFs down to a low range?
\- Does it even matter if they are placing devices above us and sending down information/signals? (I would assume this also requires massive energy output...)

I'm sure many of you may already know this, or have pondered this, but it seems anytime I go for a distance drive, I'm seeing new towers/relays/extenders/boosters etc.
Losing one's sanity to all of this can also be very detrimental, but it is good to know what we are up against and do the best that we can with what we can.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-18T00:13:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-tower-4g-5g-education.28205/post-346915>

> [michellejorge said:](/goto/post?id=346905)
>
> I found a lead on for a building biologist I'll reach out to him but any thoughts on the relatively safe reading per my Cornet despite the crazy 700 plus antenna and towers described above? Am I missing something? Thanks

Sometimes it works out that way. It depends on where the towers are pointing. You may be in a dead (quiet) zone between coverage areas. Take the reading at different times of the day. Readings can be high in a business area during the day but quiet at night OR in a residential area quite during the day and high once everyone gets home and turns on all the devices In the evening.

They opened the incision and packed it with silver tape/fabric, Staph infection went away. Back in the day they would put a silver dollar into a water cistern to keep the water fresh and stop it from going stagnant.

“Born with a silver spoon in the mouth” . . . Has come to mean wealthy but the expression comes from wealthier families who had actual silverware would put a silver spoon in the mouth of babies to help them with teething. It is also attributed as one of the reasons children of wealthy families having less sickness.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-09-26T21:05:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anybody-tried-the-dr-becker-silverlon-wound-care-patches.28207/post-326831>

If/when the “fecal matter hits the oscillator” having some silver will be a good idea for many reasons, preserving your stored water being one, putting on a cut being another. Other ways to make water safe to drink are chlorine of course (MMS), iodine (always good to have around) and studies show adding 25% red wine works as well as chlorine, so be sure to stock up on red wine also,  you’re welcome.

Having traveled to many “3rd world” countries and eaten lots of local food (Fruit Bat Curry in the Seychelles) in many questionable “eating establishments” including street vendors in India, in 5 years I never got food poisoning (except from a burger place in Australia). My golden rule was never eat on an empty stomach . . . always have a drink or two before going out to eat.

# Thread: Does or How does Light style influence the  Randle cycle?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-or-how-does-light-style-influence-the-randle-cycle.28208/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2023-09-26T18:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-or-how-does-light-style-influence-the-randle-cycle.28208/post-326824>

I have been wondering how eating outdoors gets signals to help the body best digest or handle the food I eat under outdoor light. The _**glucose fatty-acid cycle**_ must be involved. That led me to The Randle Cycle debates.

Am I right, to avoid eating sugars and protein together, or to avoid eating carbs and fat together....??? I am currently eating keto low carb and mostly animal foods but have issues with (carb, sugar and decaf coffee) cravings and constipation.

Reminds me of the 60's and 70's [_**Natural Hygiene Movement**_](https://www.healthscience.org/about/natural-hygiene-movement/) that believed in 'proper' food combining thinking that taught not to eat dairy or starches with meat (also biblical I think) and if fruit is eaten it is to be eaten alone. Reasoning was that the body produces different enzymes to break down different foods and the reasoning with the fruit was that it fermented if it is layered on top of slower more dense slow digesting foods it would ferment since it digests quickly. Well I generally ate that way since the 70's and it eliminated my indigestion / acid reflux that I used to suffer with as a teenager.

Also in 2005 I did a [_**Seven Day Adventist**_](https://hartlandwellness.com/program/) 11 day stint at one of their retreats and ate a pod of garlic with a huge fruit smorgasbord in the very early morning and a moderate plant food early lunch, a charcoal / grape juice smoothie in the early afternoon and no food the rest of the day only for dinner a little herbal tea. So a good time of 'intermittent' fasting. Walking and working out in a very warm therapy pool (I did not know and they must not have known either the dangers of fluoride and pool chemicals). However I felt great lost 10 lbs but did have strong cravings for animal foods and when I went back to eating non vegan shortly after I returned home I had the worst indigestion I have ever had in my life, before or since. When I called to ask the retreat folks they explained that my body had stopping making the **enzymes** to break down animal foods. I did tax my body because I choose to break my vegan experiment with a spicy lamb and veggie Kabob. Not wise at all. The whole 'retreat' and going back to the SAD diet took me backward from any benefit and shortly after I gained the weight back, developed hypothyroidism and in 2009 I was Dx with cancer.

Just reminiscing...

Are there absolutes for all humans?
Or are we all so uniquely different from each other due to our perceptions our hodgepodge of what we believe to be true.

### JanSz — 2023-09-26T19:16:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-or-how-does-light-style-influence-the-randle-cycle.28208/post-326825>

I got impression that to be healthy and able to consume
either high fat
or high glucose
**one must have body fat under 20%**
....
Eventually, secondarily, that comes with very low fasting insulin of no more than 5, but 1-3 would be better.
.....
When one set his/her mind to lose some body fat it should be done in a way that preserves muscle mass.
\=================

Fatty acids should be discussed widely.
Most important is Linoleic Acid, LA, Omega 6.

Situation of LA is beyond disaster.
LA is everywhere, it is hard to figure what to eat, because LA is everywhere in high amounts.
In the meantime, medical establishment is emphasizing that sufficient amount of LA is critical to our survival.

This is like worrying about sufficient hydration first when helping drowning person.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-1-to-4-1-la-ala-may-be-beneficial-for-health.28188/)

it is worth considering personal Nitric Oxide situation.

### NikkiYork — 2023-09-26T19:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-or-how-does-light-style-influence-the-randle-cycle.28208/post-326827>

I now eat like a great big white shark as much as I can

And I put on probably 10 pounds since, crossed the line to overweight. And I will deal with that later. Because the reason that I am overweight is low-ish redox. I am working on my melanin.

I’ve done probably all diets in the world. They don’t do shit in the long run. Of course you can lose weight with a lot of “tricks” and you can build a ton of muscle also, but it’s not sustainable, if the root cause of having a certain body stays the same.

I would ask myself: what is my goal? (Could be weight loss, could be health, could be weight gain, or getting as old as possible, finding a partner, not getting cancer, etc.)

Depending on what it is, you can pick a strategy - as best s you know.

Of course there are absolutes for all humans (define absolute, example, if you swim, you get wet, that’s a good absolute, and yes, you can come at me, with, but “what if”), and of course not one person is the same as another, not one environment is the same as another, myriads of variables make for your unique situation.

I eat with my family outdoors as often as possible and I thoroughly enjoy it  I’ll do that until a good reason not to presents itself.

### NikkiYork — 2023-09-28T21:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tanned-with-low-vitamin-d.28210/post-326886>

What is low vitamin d?
In a Krusean world low vitamin d is everything under 60ng/ml.

In centralized medicine you are “fine” between 20-40ng/ml.

That’s one point to consider.

Second point: there is tanning and tanning.
One person gets dark brown, the other a slight beige. There is a correlation of vitamin d levels.

A black person can however still be black and be vitamin d deficient. They will burn in the sun.

Low redox is also a gradual thing.
I have low redox in my opinion. Somebody else might have way lower redox and think they are fine.

I hope that answers it a bit.

### [Down on Vitamin D? It could be the cause of chronic inflammation](https://www.unisa.edu.au/media-centre/Releases/2022/down-on-vitamin-d-it-could-be-the-cause-of-chronic-inflammation/)

### JanSz — 2023-09-29T20:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tanned-with-low-vitamin-d.28210/post-326911>

Unfortunately, vitamin D deficiency is common in U.S. populations,11 and those with the lowest levels have a higher risk of cardiovascular disease.12 The kidneys have an important role in making vitamin D useful within the body as they convert it from supplements or from the sun into an active form.13 **However, those suffering with chronic kidney disease also experience low vitamin D levels, not from lack of absorption or sun exposure, but from lack of activation within the kidneys.**

In a study published in the American Journal of Kidney Diseases, researchers found low vitamin D levels may be able to predict early kidney disease. Those who were deficient were twice as likely to develop albuminuria, type of protein in the urine, over a period of five years. This condition is an early indication of kidney damage.

[https://www.healio.com/news/nephrology/20180226/low-vitamin-d-levels-linked-to-early-signs-of-kidn](https://www.healio.com/news/nephrology/20180226/low-vitamin-d-levels-linked-to-early-signs-of-kidn)

### [Albuminuria: Albumin in the Urine - NIDDK](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/kidney-disease/chronic-kidney-disease-ckd/tests-diagnosis/albuminuria-albumin-urine)

Defines albuminuria and discusses who is at risk. Reviews the signs of albuminuria and provides information about testing procedures and treatment options.

 www.niddk.nih.gov

### Kendall Toerner — 2023-09-29T07:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannot-convert-white-spots.28211/post-326898>

I have several white spots that will not produce melanin, and are irritated in sunlight, primarily on my upper chest and upper back.

I’m at a decent spot with my solar callus - I can lay in mid day sun in the summer in Ohio (UV 14) for an hour in one position and not burn (coming from someone of Finnish and German descent)

Every time these pure white spots, which are originally flat, become pink and swollen. Each are about 1-2 mm in size. Irritation lasts for 1-2 days after.

I’ve read many blogs and scoured much of the forums for an answer but none seem applicable.

I was convinced they were deuterium being extracted onto the skin with cholesterol, but have been consistently exfoliating for months with no changes.

They make maximizing my time in the sun very difficult.

Any thoughts? I would buy 5 lbs of oysters for anyone who can reverse this lol

### Marko Pollo — 2023-09-29T17:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cannot-convert-white-spots.28211/post-326905>

Kendall,
I had 2 bouts of white spots on my upper back and shoulders after embracing the sunshine that both resolved themselves without any intervention other than more sunshine. Haven't had any for over three years. Are you staying in the sun all morning until the UV shows up? The morning spectrums may build the callous on your white spots better than just coming out for an hour at mid day. We are designed for all day sunshine. I am also of Northern European ancestry as are all the dark brown Germans that pack the beaches in Spain. Good luck

I looked at EMR-tek and did not see a light with UVA/UVB just IR. I also notice Sperti have 2 options: The Fuji (75% UVA & 25% UVB) for tanning OR their UVB/VitD light (80%UVB & 20% UVA). Since our family tree is 2 parents, 4 grandparents, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024 etc. It doesn’t take too many generations to run out of people . . . Back in the 90’s a statistician did a study to find out how far back one would have to go for us to be descended from everyone. He calculated that everyone that was alive in Europe 1,000 years ago (that has any descendants alive today) due to inter marriage is related to EVERYONE of European heritage alive today. It is estimated that 20% of the people alive back then have no ancestors today because their line died out. In other words you are related to 100% of the people alive in Europe 1000 year ago whose line did not die out.

Then he took it to the next level and calculated how far back one need to go for everyone on the planet to be our ancestor - 3,500 years. That means for example: Abraham lived 3,600 years ago . . . everyone one on the planet (statistically) is a descendant of Abraham, Isaac, Jacob and ALL 12 boys.

Several year later a geneticist heard about this statistical hypothesis and decided to see if it was true, he found it was. Ergo - our differences are but percentages, Neanderthal included.

### NikkiYork — 2023-10-08T19:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vo2-max-testing.28231/post-327108>

Hi there,
I'm not in the mood to spend 100-200 bucks in a gym for VO2 max testing. There are a bunch of ways to do it by yourself. So I did two types of step testing so far. One with a 16inch stair case and oh wonder, I scored through the roof, like impossible, so I probably did it all wrong.

Just now I did a 12 inch box test and it seemed more reasonable with "above average". I'm thinking, it's morning and my pulse goes way higher in the mornings.
Did anybody do other testings, like the mile walk? I'm going to probably do all of those tests described online and average it out.

I'm grateful to have an amazing heart apparently. The covid area wasn't great for me though, I still can't excercise, but confident to get there. Back before Covid I had a personal trainer, and she said she couldn't believe my heart, and I was like, well, that's what I'm used to. I always hated Cardio and I kinda try not to do it, hahahaha. Maybe it's time to change.

### NikkiYork — 2023-10-13T00:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-the-lowest-emf-in-us.28242/post-327210>

I checked all the maps from all the cell phone carriers. You can find those online.
Then I also checked light pollution maps.

And I conclude you are sh\*\* out of luck. Alas, far from cities should be a hint. Not California… not Tenessee. Not anywhere northern because sun light…

I found the East is more lit than the west, but it probably varies from location to location and you can shield off some.

If you find a great spot, let me know. I’m hunting for a US and a Non-US spot.

### evan12321 — 2023-10-15T17:45:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-patient-locations-and-doctor-help.28249/post-327291>

Hi Mitochondriacs,

I'm a Long Covid patient, currently in the northeast, looking at options for a change in environment. Dr. Kruse had some suggestions and also suggested reaching out in the forum.

If you live in or are familiar with places in the southern US, especially the gulf states, with low population density but proximity to decent medical care, any advice/tips would be appreciated. If you know a GP who is staying up to date on Long Covid, even better (I have been checking the FLCCC).

### Sue-UK — 2023-10-16T13:59:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-patient-locations-and-doctor-help.28249/post-327311>

I've used chlorella with a high spermidine content, because natural spermidine levels in cells decrease with age. I've read that naked mole rats don't get the same spermidine age related decline as other rodents of the same size, and they live 10 times as long.

There seems to be a link between covid disrupting the spermidine pathways in cells. I'm a visual learner and found this quite a good video explaining the mechanisms behind it and the mechanisms by which spermidine, resveratrol and IF can help.

Nice video, thank you.
That video ends with two advices:
using drugs, spermidine and or resveratrol
and
natural ways: **intermittent fasting, IF.**
\-------------------------------------
**I would like to think and research about IF.**

I am in the process of studying mTOR.

[mTOR is a survival mechanism that signals to the body and cells that they should](https://youthandearth.com/blogs/blog/mtor-longevity-pathway)
[**grow during times of plenty**](https://youthandearth.com/blogs/blog/mtor-longevity-pathway)
[and](https://youthandearth.com/blogs/blog/mtor-longevity-pathway)
[**not grow when food is scarce**](https://youthandearth.com/blogs/blog/mtor-longevity-pathway)
\------
During food scarce time energy need to keep body alive is made using
body fat, and bad stuff which may be the stuff discussed in above video and additionally all kind of starting cancer cells, diabetes and other undesirables,
and when above is gone
using fat in the bone marrow to continue on living.
\---------
Above this type of discussion newer mentions
the amount of body fat body has in good times.
and the amounts of body fat during cleanup
also
that at certain point, when fasting lasts too long, body dies.
\---------
The pictures of fat people that we often see are misleading.
They show people morbidly obese; they are not healthy people, they are sick.

I am looking for a maximum body fat percentage of people **who are healthy but about ready to go for a fast.**

In my estimation that is about 10-15%
Anything above that should be considered morbidly obese and not desirable.

I have seen discussions stating that healthy Body Mass Index is about 18-24.
That does not say much about body fat, because some people have a more other less muscles.
But
in my personal case, until few days ago I was thinking of myself as a lean person, but no more.
I am 5' 8" 150#
my body fat varies from 15% to 13%
my BMI=22.8
if I would lose 10# my BMI would be 21.29
another 10#, so having 130# BMI=19.77
still far from 18
\===============
This part needs to be clarified better.
Because
250# or 200# person (many if not most here are those) may THINK THAT HE/SHE is fasting
while she really should lose another almost 100#
**before their mTOR is turned down, when using Intermittent Fasting.**

////////////////////////

Years ago, I knew a healthy, muscled volunteer fireman who was refused tandem skydiving because he was over the BMI. Someone skinny, less fit and perhaps with weaker bones and a higher risk of injury in the event of a bad landing technique, was allowed to do the jump. That exposed the flaws, so I'm not too interested in BMI. My body type holds more than the average subcutaneous fat when healthy, and my risk is greater from excess visceral fat. Within reason, I see my SQ fat as protective, and I can still get the benefits of IF.

_Jack: "The skin is a cholesterol laden insulator of fat with lipid raft antenna's designed to connect us to the sun.........."

"That external light determines the reality the mammal faces. When the solar cycles change so do the lipid rafts. This photoelectric change alters biochemistry in **mTOR**, PPP, glycolysis, the TCA cycle, and POMC cleavage. When the lipid content changes they induce changes in the semiconductive proteins embedded in them. This changes the physiologic ability. This is why the clock mechanism in mammals is linked to **light and temperature.** Both signals change to the surfaces of mammals."_

([https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/page-2#post-321555](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/page-2#post-321555))

So if I follow the sun, and experience the cold in winter (living in the cold lane and CT) I think I'll do better than starving myself to get closer to a BMI that suits any conventional mTOR paradigm. I know I don't fit with many people's ideas of what I should weigh, or how much weight I should lose, but if that's based on facade or BMI, they aren't going to be packing my parachute any time soon.

### Sue-UK — 2023-10-17T14:50:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-patient-locations-and-doctor-help.28249/post-327358>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)

_"I am looking for a maximum body fat percentage of people **who are healthy but about ready to go for a fast.**_

_In my estimation that is about 10-15%
Anything above that should be considered morbidly obese and not desirable."

"my body fat varies from 15% to 13%"

"I hate being hungry probably more than you do."_

I think there's a difference between inflammation or addiction driven hunger, and a hunger message that's a signal to replace lost mass.

In the case of covid, if the spike proteins disrupt the autophagy machinery, having a very low body fat percentage is not necessarily an advantage, it could just be a different risk, even in the young. In terms of recovery from covid and long covid, having extremely low levels of SQ fat could result in losing the _"...... lipid raft antenna's designed to connect us to the sun.........."_ with the subsequent photoelectric mTor connection. This could have knock on effects for anyone trying to use the sun to recover from Covid or long covid. or just using the sun to remain healthy, even if their environment in terms of nnEMF is good. If leptin resistance can occur in anorexia as well as obesity, I suspect there will be an n=1 point where fasting (other than overnight) for those with low body fat will be contra indicated. As fat is an endocrine organ, the healthy may become unhealthy in terms of natural vitamin D production, sex hormones and fertility etc etc if they try and reach an unrealistic body fat percentage. When losing too much mass, there's also potential for having insufficient deuterium which is used by immune cells. I think there is much more to it than estimating that 10 to 15% body fat is OK but anything over 15 per cent is morbidly obese. That doesn't even reach half truth status .....
